The Bazel 8.4.2 JVM spawns virtual-thread worker carriers during bzlmod extension eval and dies with 'java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: unable to create native thread: possibly out of memory or process/resource limits reached' (the cgroup pids cap, not heap). Reproduced: bazel test cli/... OOM-crashes at 256 pids regardless of memory; succeeds (Build completed successfully) at --pids-limit 4096. The crash also hangs ~75s in 'kill stale server', explaining the live container stuck Up 10+ min.

2. SECONDARY (config silently ignored): sandboxRunOpts() in internal/cli/scan.go threaded network/idle/timeout from config but DROPPED sandbox.cpus and sandbox.memory_mb, so every run used the backend defaults (2 CPUs / 2048 MB) ignoring aethon's configured 4 CPUs / 8192 MB. Introduced by 9d3bf31 which added timeout threading but missed memory/cpus; pids was never threadable at all.

NOTE: the '/vendor/bazel-external (Read-only file system)' ERROR is NON-fatal (bazel info exits 0 with it present) — a red herring, not the blocker.
design:
Fix = make every configured sandbox resource cap actually reach the backend, and add the missing pids knob.
- config.Sandbox gains PidsLimit (yaml pids_limit), Default()=4096, env BUGBOT_SANDBOX_PIDS_LIMIT, Validate requires >0 (mirrors cpus/memory_mb).
- sandboxRunOpts() now appends WithCPUs + WithMemoryMB + WithPidsLimit (guarded >0).
- 4096 chosen as default: proven sufficient for Bazel; still bounds fork-bombs given the container is also memory-capped, cpu-capped, network=none, cap-drop ALL, no-new-privileges, and timeout-reaped (pids is defense-in-depth, not the sole barrier).
- Existing configs auto-inherit 4096 because config.Load overlays YAML onto Default(), so an absent pids_limit keeps the default (aethon needs no edit).
- design_sandbox.candidateToSandboxConfig sets PidsLimit=defaultPidsLimit so emitted/merged YAML passes Validate; starter template + wizard emit the knob; doctor RunSandboxVerify honors it too.
Alternative rejected: raising NewCLI's hardcoded 256 globally — weakens the safety net for direct callers and hides the real config-driven design; keeping it conservative for direct callers while the scan path is config-driven is cleaner.
Alternative rejected: bumping only memory — proven insufficient (OOM persists at 8GB; the limit is pids).
acceptance_criteria: 1. config.Sandbox.PidsLimit exists, defaults 4096, env-overridable, Validate rejects <=0. 2. sandboxRunOpts emits WithCPUs/WithMemoryMB/WithPidsLimit from config (unit test). 3. A Bazel repo's reproducer no longer crashes with 'unable to create native thread'. 4. go build + go test ./... green; gofmt clean.
notes:
- The OOM message names BOTH memory and 'process/resource limits' — easy to misattribute to RAM; it was the pids cap. Always test pids in isolation.
- sandbox.Spec has no PidsLimit field, so per-run override is via the backend default only (set through NewCLI options); fine for current callers.
- A live scan binary won't pick up this fix — rebuild + rerun required.
- Verified: raw podman bazel test cli/... succeeds at 8192m/4cpu/4096pids (exit 4 'No test targets' = content, not crash); config.Load(aethon) yields CPUs=4 MemoryMB=8192 PidsLimit=4096; new unit test TestSandboxRunOpts_HonorsResourceCaps.
close_reason: Made sandbox resource caps config-driven: added config.Sandbox.PidsLimit (yaml pids_limit, default 4096, env override, Validate>0) and threaded WithCPUs/WithMemoryMB/WithPidsLimit through sandboxRunOpts (cpus/memory_mb were silently dropped; pids had no knob and was stuck at 256). 256 pids crashed the Bazel JVM mid-analysis ('unable to create native thread'); 4096 lets it complete (proven via raw podman). Existing configs auto-inherit 4096 via Load-over-Default. Also fixed design_sandbox emit, starter template, wizard, and doctor smoke path. Tests: TestSandboxRunOpts_HonorsResourceCaps + go test ./... green.

title: fix(ci): main red — stale miner self-mine assumption + staticcheck QF1002
description: CI on main failing: go test (internal/miner TestSeed_RealBugbotRepo) and golangci-lint (internal/repro/interpret.go QF1002).
design: Two root causes. (1) miner self-mine test: collectGoFiles walked ALL .go files, but production runContradictionSeed (cli/scan.go) feeds miner.Seed a snapshot filtered by cfg.Scan.Exclude whose default includes **/*_test.go. The enum-drift pass (241d70b) added testdata + scans _test.go; combined with config.go churn the real-repo lead count crept 9->12 over the maxLeadsAllowed=10 precision guard. 4 leads came from _test.go constraint sites and 2 more from doc sites living in _test.go. Chose to mirror the production exclude (skip *_test.go in collectGoFiles) over bumping the threshold: bumping hides real precision regressions and counts intentional test fixtures bugbot would never post in a real scan. Count drops 12->6. (2) QF1002: boolean switch on res.ExitCode -> tagged switch.
acceptance_criteria: go build/vet/test -race ./... green; golangci-lint run 0 issues.
close_reason: Fixed on branch fix/ci-main-failures (f14b5e0, d249dbf). Mirrored production *_test.go scan exclude in collectGoFiles (12->6 leads, threshold kept at 10) and converted interpret.go to a tagged switch. Verified: go build/vet/test -race ./... green, golangci-lint 0 issues.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text: CI surfaced a third (flaky) failure not seen locally: funnel TestHypothesize_TransportErrorBreaker asserted FinderRuns/FinderFailures == MaxParallel exactly, but the in-flight count at breaker-trip is scheduling-dependent (CI hit 5 vs local 4). Relaxed to breaker invariants (FinderRuns >= threshold, FinderFailures == FinderRuns). Fixed in ec3b5d7.

title: Lazy on-demand cartography pass
description: Convert the cartographer from an EAGER up-front pass (cartograph at run.go:545 LLM-summarizes ALL stale packages of all targets BEFORE the first finder runs) into a LAZY, on-demand, idempotent pass that materializes a package's summary the first time a finder unit spanning it initializes. Up-front cartography does not scale on large repos: huge up-front LLM fan-out + budget spend, much wasted when the finder stage is budget-truncated. The summary cache (store.PackageSummary, keyed (pkg,fingerprint)) is ALREADY a durable, content-addressed, idempotent pass substrate; this task converts only the DRIVER from eager to demand-pull with singleflight dedup. Generation stays SYNCHRONOUS/BLOCKING per unit (no streaming of partial summaries to the agent). See local/vpx-design.md section 7a for the full design.
design: Evolve cartography struct (cartographer.go:37) into a per-run lazy provider: add client/store/fps/budget/enabled, a mutex-guarded per-run memo summaries map, and a golang.org/x/sync/singleflight.Group keyed by package. Importer graph stays eager (pure-Go, cross-unit dependents). See local/vpx-design.md section 7a. Depends on .3 (shares run.go:540-547 mode guard + hypothesize.go); sequence AFTER .3.
acceptance_criteria: 1) run.go:545 eager cartograph replaced by a cheap newCartographer that builds ONLY the importer graph (pure-Go, no LLM) and returns nil when Features.Cartographer=false (byte-identical off path preserved); gated inside the mode==modeFull block from .3. 2) hypothesize.go:503 cart.contextFor -> cart.ensureContextFor(ctx,files): lazily GetPackageSummaries, generate+persist missing/stale via singleflight, budget-gated (finderOverHard skips generation, renders cached-only). 3) get_package_context tool generates-on-miss via the same provider. 4) A finder unit spanning an un-summarized package triggers exactly ONE summarizePackage call and persists it; a 2nd unit on the same package triggers ZERO; concurrent units on the same package trigger exactly ONE (singleflight). 5) RefreshCartography (bugbot cartography --run) still does the eager whole-repo refresh. 6) cartographer_test.go covers the above; existing cartographer tests pass; go build + go vet clean. Skip gates — orchestrator runs full suite at end.
close_reason: Cartography is now a lazy on-demand pass: eager cartograph() removed; newCartographer builds only the importer graph (no LLM, nil when feature off = byte-identical off path); ensureContextFor materializes a finder unit's package summaries on demand at unit init, singleflight-deduped across concurrent units, persisted idempotently via the (pkg,fingerprint) cache; budget-hard degrades to cached-only; get_package_context generates-on-miss via the same provider. RefreshCartography stays eager. 9 eager tests migrated to the lazy path + 6 new lazy tests (single-gen, memo, singleflight, budget-skip, off-path, store-seed). Scales: a budget-truncated sweep pays only for packages its finders reach. x/sync promoted to direct.

title: Design: pass-orchestration model & per-phase durability contract
description:
Foundation design task for the funnel re-architecture epic (bugbot-vpx). NO production code in this task — produce the written contract every implementation child builds on, recorded in this bead's --design.

Define: (1) the Pass abstraction — WorkRemaining(ctx) store query, Process(ctx,item) idempotent processor, and the terminal/skip marker that makes a reprocessed item a no-op; (2) per-phase mapping (hypothesize, triage, verify, repro, impact-sweep) onto that abstraction, citing the existing durable state each already has; (3) the scheduler contract — how daemon/run.go's schedule + Run loop drive passes to fixpoint with budget gating, and how oneshot `bugbot scan` picks up stranded work at start and runs passes to fixpoint; (4) interruption semantics — interruption may only DEFER an item to the next pass, never drop it; (5) the impact-sweep marker decision: reuse verdict_detail (reset-on-rediscovery = re-sweep intended) vs add a dedicated sweep_state column (migration) preserved across upsert like tier/repro_path/needs_human (findings.go:331-342); (6) confirm scope of cartography/leads (likely left as recomputed input passes).

Reference implementation to mirror: the repro backlog drain (internal/daemon/backlog.go OpenBacklog + runReproBacklog) — it is the only phase that already fully conforms.
design:
# bugbot-vpx.1 — Design: pass-orchestration model & per-phase durability contract

Foundation contract for the funnel re-architecture epic (bugbot-vpx). Implementation
children .2–.7 build on this; do not re-derive the model.

All file:line refs are against `feat/bugbot-vpx-idempotent-passes` @ 050c6df.

---

## 1. The Pass abstraction (conceptual contract, not a Go interface)

A **Pass** over durable store state is the triple:

| element | meaning |
|---|---|
| `WorkRemaining(ctx) []Item` | store query returning items not yet at a terminal marker |
| `Process(ctx, item)` | idempotent processor; advances one item toward its terminal marker |
| terminal marker | a durable per-item field whose presence makes re-`Process` a verified no-op |

We deliberately do **not** introduce a `Pass` Go interface. The phases differ too much
in item type (candidate row vs finding row) and execution shape (goroutine fanout vs
single-threaded batch) for a shared interface to pay rent — it would be an abstraction
that isn't pulling its weight. Instead each pass is a concrete method on `*funnel.Funnel`
(`VerifyDrain`, `SweepDrain`) plus a store-level `WorkRemaining` query, mirroring the
existing repro backlog (`daemon.OpenBacklog` + `Daemon.runReproBacklog` + `repro.PromoteAll`),
which is the only phase that already fully conforms and is the reference template.

**Invariant (interruption-defer):** interruption may only DEFER an item to the next pass,
never drop it. Concretely: every durable write that advances an item happens BEFORE the
in-memory state that depends on it. The terminal markers differ in their guarantee:
- **sweep**: `swept_at` is set in the SAME UPDATE as the severity re-rank (`UpdateFindingSeverity`)
  — genuinely ATOMIC with the work it records.
- **verify**: `UpsertFinding` (persist) and `DeletePendingCandidate` (WAL clear) are SEPARATE
  transactions. Safety here comes from IDEMPOTENCY, not atomicity: a lost delete (e.g.
  ctx-cancel between the two, by design in `helpers.go:118-128`) replays the row next pass →
  fingerprint-idempotent re-verify (`UpsertFinding` SELECT-then-UPDATE under the
  `idx_findings_fingerprint` UNIQUE index, `001_init.sql:31`) → delete again. No duplicate finding.
A ctx-cancel between items leaves earlier items terminal and later items still WorkRemaining-eligible.

**Load-bearing serialization fact:** within one daemon process every pass runs strictly
sequentially — the scheduler `Run` loop (`daemon/run.go:101-160`) fires exactly one timer
per iteration and each `d.runX` blocks to completion (no goroutines); `postCycle` is
single-threaded. So two passes NEVER touch `pending_candidates`/`findings` concurrently
in-process. The ONLY concurrency surface is daemon + a manual one-shot (`bugbot verify/sweep`),
which §7 closes with the advisory scan-lock. This serialization is what makes the composition safe.

---

## 2. Per-phase mapping

| phase | WorkRemaining query | terminal marker | idempotency key | status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| hypothesize | `ScanWatermarks` coverage stamps (`store.filestate.go:167 TouchScanCoverage`, `:208 ScanWatermarks`) — files whose content_hash is current & last_scanned_at non-epoch are skipped | per-unit coverage stamp (`hypothesize.go:718-731`) | (file, content_hash) | DURABLE+IDEMPOTENT. Gap: pickup coupled to run() start (the finder goroutine). Acceptable — not converted; the verify drain (below) decouples the *consumer* of its output. |
| cartography | **NEW (lazy)** packages spanned by a finder unit lacking a fresh `PackageSummary` (pulled on demand at unit init, not enumerated globally) | persisted `PackageSummary` with matching `Fingerprint` (`store/cartographer.go:88 UpsertPackageSummaries`) | (pkg, content fingerprint) | Substrate ALREADY conforms; **.7** converts the eager up-front driver (`run.go:545 cartograph`) to lazy demand-pull. WAS eager/non-scaling. |
| triage | operates on the WAL row in-flight; re-clustered on every replay | WAL row delete at terminal fate | recomputed `store.Fingerprint(lens,file,line,title)` | CONFORMS (re-anchored each replay). No new work. |
| verify | **NEW** `store.ListPendingCandidates` (`pending.go:109`) IS the durable queue | `DeletePendingCandidate` at terminal fate (already wired in `verify_stream.go` via `f.deletePending`) | candidate `PendingID` (WAL rowid) | Gap closed by **.3** standalone `VerifyDrain` (decouple from finder pass). |
| repro | `daemon.OpenBacklog` (`backlog.go:28`) | non-empty `repro_path` (or `needs_human`) preserved across upsert | finding fingerprint | FULLY CONFORMS — the template. Documented, not duplicated. |
| reverify | walks all open findings each postCycle (`postcycle.go:55`) | re-anchored content_hash | finding id | CONFORMS. No change. |
| impact-sweep | **NEW** `store.UnsweptOpenFindings` (status=open AND swept_at IS NULL) | **NEW** `swept_at` column set by `UpdateFindingSeverity` | finding id | Gap closed by **.2** (marker) + **.4** (`SweepDrain`). WAS non-conforming (terminal in-memory). |

**leads scope call:** OUT OF SCOPE (confirmed non-goal). Leads is a blackboard reseeded each
cycle by `seedAnalyzers`/`seedContradictions`; it holds no work that interruption strands.
Leave as-is.

**cartography scope call:** NOW IN SCOPE as a **lazy on-demand pass** (child .7, see §9a).
The summary cache (`store.PackageSummary`, keyed `(pkg, fingerprint)`) is ALREADY a durable,
content-addressed, idempotent pass substrate. The defect is timing: `cartograph` (`run.go:545`)
eagerly LLM-summarizes ALL stale packages of ALL targets BEFORE the first finder runs — which
does not scale on large repos (huge up-front LLM fan-out + budget spend, much wasted when the
finder stage is budget-truncated). The fix converts the eager driver into a lazy, demand-pulled,
singleflight-deduped one that materializes a package's summary at finder-unit init.

---

## 3. Impact-sweep marker decision: dedicated `swept_at` column (option b) — RESOLVED

**Decision: add `swept_at` (migration 014), preserved across UpsertFinding, reset on
file_hash change.** Rationale over reusing `verdict_detail=''`:

1. The acceptance criteria for .2/.4 require "re-discovery does not silently regress it"
   and "a second drain re-ranks nothing already swept (no redundant LLM adjudication)."
   `verdict_detail=''` is **clobbered to ''** on every re-upsert (`findings.go:285` INSERT,
   `:354/:363` UPDATE write `f.VerdictDetail`, which verify supplies as ""), so in
   production every re-scan re-marks every re-found finding as unswept → re-sweeps it →
   for ambiguous findings that is repeated LLM spend. That literally regresses the marker
   on re-discovery; option (a) fails the criteria.
2. `verdict_detail` doubles as human-readable rationale; conflating marker with display
   text is fragile. `classifyReachability` (`impact_sweep.go:156-250`) DOES return a
   non-empty rationale on every deterministic `keep`/`down` path (verified by reading all
   branches), so verdict_detail-as-marker would *mostly* work — but a dedicated timestamp
   is robust against any future empty-rationale path and is the explicit, legible choice.
3. `UpdateFindingSeverity` (`findings.go:527`) is called from EXACTLY two sites, both in
   `impactSweep` (`impact_sweep.go:83,127`) — confirmed by grep. So coupling
   `swept_at=now` into `UpdateFindingSeverity` makes it the sweep's exclusive terminal
   write with zero risk to other callers.

**Semantics:**
- New finding: `swept_at` NULL (unswept).
- Sweep persists a verdict via `UpdateFindingSeverity` → sets `swept_at=now` (terminal).
- Ambiguous-but-unadjudicated (no client / budget stopped, `impact_sweep.go:94-104`):
  `UpdateFindingSeverity` is NOT called → `swept_at` stays NULL → remains pickup-eligible
  for a later budgeted drain. ✓ (satisfies the "deferred ambiguous re-picked-up" criterion)
- Re-discovery via `UpsertFinding` with SAME file_hash → `swept_at` preserved (mirror the
  repro_path/needs_human CASE pattern at `findings.go:331-342,358,360`).
- Re-discovery with DIFFERENT file_hash → `swept_at` reset to NULL: the anchored code
  changed, reachability may have changed, so re-sweep is correct (the Finding struct is
  already "anchored to a code version through CommitSHA and FileHash" for exactly this
  re-evaluation intent — `findings.go:45-47`).

---

## 4. Concrete store changes (child .2)

`internal/store/migrations/014_finding_swept_at.sql`:
```sql
ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN swept_at TEXT;
```
(`migrate.go` tolerates sequential gaps; 013 is the current max.)

`findings.go`:
- `Finding` struct (+ after `VerdictDetail`): `SweptAt time.Time // zero = not swept (impact sweep marker)`.
- `findingColumns` (`:610`): add `swept_at` to the SELECT list (append at end, keep order in sync with `scanFinding`).
- `scanFinding` (`:633`): scan `swept_at` as nullable TEXT → `time.Time` (zero on NULL). Use the existing nullable-time idiom (see `parseTime`/`nullStr`; store as `sql.NullString` then `parseTime`).
- `UpsertFinding`:
  - INSERT branch (`:278-292`): add `swept_at` column + value. New finding is unswept → write NULL (`nullStr("")` or a nil time → NULL). Add to the column list and the VALUES placeholder count (currently 22 cols / 22 `?`).
  - SELECT-existing (`:266-268`): add `file_hash, swept_at` to the fetched columns.
  - UPDATE branch (`:352-371`): preserve-or-reset. Resolve in Go (mirror tier/repro_path resolution at `:331-342`) so the returned struct is accurate:
    ```go
    // swept_at: preserve across idempotent re-discovery; reset when the
    // anchored code (file_hash) changed so the sweep re-evaluates reachability.
    if f.FileHash == existingFileHash {
        f.SweptAt = existingSweptAt // preserve stored marker
    } else {
        f.SweptAt = time.Time{} // code changed → re-sweep eligible
    }
    ```
    and add `swept_at=?` to the UPDATE SET with the resolved value (`nullTime(f.SweptAt)`).
- `UpdateFindingSeverity` (`:543-546`): add `swept_at=?` to the SET clause, bound to `now` — this is the terminal marker write.

`UnsweptOpenFindings(ctx) ([]Finding, error)` — new store method (WorkRemaining for sweep):
```sql
... WHERE status = 'open' AND swept_at IS NULL ORDER BY updated_at ASC, id ASC
```
Oldest-updated-first for rotation parity with `OpenBacklog` (failed/deferred items touched →
move to back). Returns full `Finding` rows (sweep needs File/Line/Severity/Tier).

Verify-pass WorkRemaining = `ListPendingCandidates` (already exists, `pending.go:109`).
Repro WorkRemaining = `OpenBacklog` (already exists). Both DOCUMENTED as canonical; NO
duplicate query paths.

Tests (`internal/store/findings_test.go` or new `swept_test.go`): swept finding excluded
from `UnsweptOpenFindings`; unswept included; ambiguous-deferred (swept_at NULL) included;
`UpsertFinding` re-upsert with same file_hash preserves swept_at; with different file_hash
resets it; `UpdateFindingSeverity` sets swept_at.

---

## 5. Standalone verify drain (child .3) — reuse run() via a mode, do NOT fork

**Chosen approach: parametrize `run()` with a `runMode`** rather than hand-rolling a
second triage+verify pipeline. The pipeline (budget setup, spend recorder, slot fanout,
triage consumer, verify dispatcher, repro queue, late-fold, interrupt-safe finalize) is
~560 lines of intricate goroutine choreography (`run.go:331-889`); re-implementing it for
the drain IS forking the logic the bead forbids. The only difference a verify drain needs
is the candidate source: replay-only, no finder/cartographer.

`run.go`:
- Define `type runMode int; const ( modeFull runMode = iota; modeVerifyDrain )`.
- `run(ctx, kind, snap, targets, fps, touchCoverage, mode)` — add trailing `mode runMode`.
- In the hypothesize goroutine (`:513-566`): the resume replay (`:529-531`) runs in BOTH
  modes. Guard the finder stage so it is skipped in `modeVerifyDrain`:
  ```go
  for _, pc := range resume { emit(pendingToCandidate(pc)) }
  result.Stats.Resumed = len(resume)
  if mode == modeFull {
      seams := ingest.EnumerateSeams(snap)
      result.Stats.SeamsFound = len(seams)
      cart := f.cartograph(...)
      n, err := f.hypothesize(...)
      hypothesizedCount = n; hypothesizeErr = err
  }
  close(candCh)
  ```
  Net effect: in `modeVerifyDrain` the finder/cartographer clients are NEVER invoked
  (cartographer falls back to finder but `cartograph` is skipped), so candCh carries only
  replayed WAL candidates. Everything downstream is byte-identical.
- **REQUIRED (oracle #1): guard `budget.reserveForDownstream(f.opts.Budget.FinderBudgetShare)`
  (`run.go:387`) on `mode == modeFull`.** This line runs BEFORE the mode branch and is the
  one thing that assumes hypothesize ran: with `DefaultFinderBudgetShare=0.7` it caps the
  verify pool at 30% of `TokenBudget`, so an unguarded drain orphans every candidate as T3
  after spending 30% while 70% sits idle in the never-charged finder pool — defeating the
  drain under exactly the budget caps this epic targets. In `modeVerifyDrain` skip the
  reserve so `verifyPool` stays nil and `verifyOverHard/Soft` fall back to the total-budget
  gates (`funnel.go:1137-1142`), giving the drain the full budget.
- Guard the `StageStarted(hypothesize)` (`:505`) AND `StageFinished(hypothesize)` (`:557-565`)
  emits symmetrically on `mode == modeFull` (else the daemon pane — non-nil sink — shows an
  orphaned "started" hypothesize stage). Cosmetic but cheap; do both.
- Existing callers (`Sweep`/`Targeted`) pass `modeFull`.

New public method:
```go
// VerifyDrain verifies all pending_candidates left by interrupted runs WITHOUT
// invoking the finder/cartographer. ListPendingCandidates -> replay -> triage
// re-anchor -> runVerifyAndPersist -> DeletePendingCandidate (all via run()).
func (f *Funnel) VerifyDrain(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {
    // REQUIRED (oracle #4): cheap WAL pre-check BEFORE the full-repo snapshot, so an
    // empty-WAL drain is a single store query (the daemon fires this every postCycle +
    // hourly). Mirrors the OpenBacklog-first pattern of the repro template.
    pending, err := f.store.ListPendingCandidates(ctx)
    if err != nil { return nil, err }
    if len(pending) == 0 { return &Result{}, nil }
    snap, err := f.snapshot(ctx) // triage re-anchor needs the current snapshot
    if err != nil { return nil, err }
    return f.run(ctx, store.ScanVerifyDrain, snap, nil, nil, false, modeVerifyDrain)
}
```
(run() re-lists pending at `:498`; the extra pre-list is one cheap query and avoids a
full-repo snapshot+Fingerprints on the common empty-WAL path.)
- `targets=nil`, `fps=nil` (run() recomputes fps at `:419-426` — verify needs file_hash
  anchoring), `touchCoverage=false` (no finders, no coverage to stamp).
- New `store.ScanKind`: `ScanVerifyDrain ScanKind = "verify-drain"` (free-text kind column,
  NO migration). Add to `scanruns.go:13-23`.
- WAL deletes at terminal fates already happen inside `runVerifyAndPersist` →
  `f.deletePending(c.PendingID)` (`verify_stream.go`, helper `helpers.go:118-128`), and
  triage drops/merges delete too (`triage_streaming.go:303,434`). Nothing to add.
- Idempotency: second drain over a drained WAL has `ListPendingCandidates` empty → no-op.
- Re-anchoring precision preserved (same `ts.process` path as run-start replay): a pending
  candidate whose code vanished is dropped/killed exactly as today.

**Stage F note:** once .5 removes the terminal `impactSweep` call from `run()`, the verify
drain (which reuses run()) will not sweep — correct; sweep is its own drain.

---

## 6. Standalone impact-sweep drain (child .4)

`impactSweep(ctx, findings, repoRoot, verifierClient, budgetStopped, result)` already takes
a `[]store.Finding` — it does not care whether they came from the in-run slice or a store
query. So the drain is a thin wrapper:

```go
// SweepDrain re-ranks unswept open findings (this run's + any stranded by an
// interrupted/older run) by reachability/impact. Idempotent: swept findings are
// excluded by UnsweptOpenFindings; a second drain over already-swept rows is a no-op.
func (f *Funnel) SweepDrain(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {
    findings, err := f.store.UnsweptOpenFindings(ctx)
    if err != nil { return nil, err }
    result := &Result{}
    if len(findings) == 0 { return result, nil }
    // cap to a batch (config SweepDrainBatch) for budget bounding; rotation via
    // updated_at handled by the query order.
    scanRunID, err := f.store.BeginScanRun(ctx, store.ScanImpactSweep, "")
    ... wire spendRecorder + verifierClient(WithRecorder) + budget (mirror run() :342-388,
        the ~10 lines needed: rec, verifierClient, budget) ...
    f.impactSweep(ctx, findings, f.repo.Root(), verifierClient, budget.stopped.Load(), result)
    finalize(scanRunID, &result.Stats)
    return result, nil
}
```
- New `ScanImpactSweep ScanKind = "impact-sweep"`.
- The `swept_at` marker (set by `UpdateFindingSeverity` inside `impactSweep`) makes the
  terminal/idempotency contract hold. Ambiguous-deferred stay swept_at NULL → re-picked-up.
- Optional small extraction: a `f.newSpendBudget(ctx, scanRunID) (verifierClient, *budgetState, *spendRecorder)`
  helper shared by `run()` and `SweepDrain` to avoid copy-pasting the recorder/budget wiring.
  Keep it minimal; do not over-abstract.
- impactSweep body itself is UNCHANGED (only its caller/source changes).

Tests (`impact_sweep_test.go` extension): seed unswept open findings from a prior
"interrupted" run, run `SweepDrain`, assert severity re-ranked + swept_at set; second drain
is a no-op (no LLM adjudication — assert verifier call count unchanged); ambiguous-deferred
under nil client stays unswept and is re-picked-up by a budgeted drain.

---

## 7. Scheduler + run() composition (child .5)

`internal/daemon/run.go`:
- `schedule` struct (`:15-19`) gains `nextVerifyDrain, nextImpactSweep *time.Time` (nil =
  disabled). **Naming:** the existing `nextSweep`/`runSweep` is the FULL funnel scan
  (`cycle.go:167` → `f.Sweep`); the NEW impact-sweep drain is distinct — name it
  `nextImpactSweep`/`runImpactSweep` to avoid the collision. Document prominently.
- `earliest()` (`:23-39`): extend the deadline race + tie-break to include the two new
  timers. Keep existing priority (sweep>backlog>poll); slot verify/impact-sweep after
  backlog, before poll (low-priority maintenance drains).
- `Run` loop (`:137-159`): add `fireVerifyDrain`/`fireImpactSweep` cases, each day-budget
  gated exactly like backlog (`:144-152`): `d.dayBudgetExhausted` → skip+reschedule, else
  run + reschedule.
- `runVerifyDrain`/`runImpactSweep` (new, in cycle.go or backlog.go): `f, err := d.newFunnel()`
  (`daemon.go:298`); `defer f.Close()`; `f.VerifyDrain(ctx)` / `f.SweepDrain(ctx)`; record
  spend into cycleResult; log. Mirror `runReproBacklog` structure (`backlog.go:57`).

`internal/daemon/postcycle.go`:
- `postCycle` (`:25-41`): after `reverifyOpenFindings`, invoke the verify drain then the
  sweep drain so every cycle reconciles stranded work (cheap no-ops when nothing pending /
  all swept). Budget-gate.
- **REQUIRED (oracle #5): exact order is `reverify → verify-drain → sweep-drain → publisher
  → promoteNewFindings`.** Rationale: verify-drain BEFORE promote so newly-verified findings
  are promotable this cycle; sweep-drain BEFORE promote is SAFE because `promoteFinding`'s
  `UpsertFinding` preserves `swept_at` (file_hash unchanged) — confirmed by oracle. (Earlier
  draft text said "sweep AFTER promotion"; that was wrong — do NOT flip sweep and promote.)

`internal/funnel/run.go`:
- Remove the terminal `f.impactSweep(...)` call (`:828`) and its Stage-F comment block
  (`:825-828`). The sweep now runs only as a pass (daemon timer + postCycle + oneshot
  fixpoint). Adjust any stats/progress emits that assumed Stage F ran inline.

`internal/cli/scan.go` (oneshot fixpoint):
- After the `f.Sweep(ctx)` (oneshot), drive passes to fixpoint so a single interrupted-then-
  rerun scan converges: loop `{ VerifyDrain; SweepDrain }` until both report zero work
  remaining (ListPendingCandidates empty AND UnsweptOpenFindings empty) or budget stops.
  Bound the loop (e.g. max 3 rounds) so a pathological non-converging case can't spin.
  Since run() already replays the WAL at start, the "pick up stranded work at start" half is
  already satisfied; the new part is running verify+sweep drains to fixpoint at the end.
- **REQUIRED (oracle #3): after the fixpoint loop, re-read findings from the store before the
  summary print (`cli/scan.go:626-632`).** Relocating `impactSweep` out of `run()` means
  `f.Sweep().Findings` no longer carry re-ranked severities; without a re-read the oneshot
  prints PRE-sweep severities inconsistent with the store/published issues. Either re-read
  open findings (`st.ListFindings`) or have `SweepDrain` return the re-ranked set and merge
  into `res.Findings`.

Config (`internal/config/config.go`):
- `Daemon` struct (`:301-310`): `VerifyDrainInterval time.Duration yaml:"verify_drain_interval"`,
  `ImpactSweepInterval time.Duration yaml:"impact_sweep_interval"`.
- Batch sizes alongside `Repro.BacklogBatch`: `SweepDrainBatch int yaml:"sweep_drain_batch"`
  (verify drain processes the whole WAL — typically tiny — so a batch cap is optional; add
  `VerifyDrainBatch` only if needed).
- `Default()` (`:415-419`): `VerifyDrainInterval: 1h`, `ImpactSweepInterval: 6h`,
  `SweepDrainBatch: 100`. Plumb into `DaemonConfig` (`daemon.go:118-145`), `daemon.New`
  defaults (`:275-289`), and `newDaemonCmd` (`cli/daemon.go:121-128`).
- `bugbot doctor` must still PASS (new keys have defaults; no required config).

CLI one-shots (`internal/cli/verify.go`, `internal/cli/sweep.go`) — mirror `repro.go:27-80`:
- `cmdOpenStore` → `buildRoleClients` → `funnel.New` → `f.VerifyDrain(ctx)` /
  `f.SweepDrain(ctx)`. No progress sink (nil) to avoid status.json races with a running
  daemon. Register both in `cli/root.go:24-41` (`newVerifyCmd()`, `newSweepCmd()`).
- **REQUIRED (oracle #2): wire the advisory scan-lock + heartbeat, mirroring `bugbot scan`.**
  Call `checkScanLock(ctx, st, force, selfPID)` (`cli/scan.go:417`) before running and spawn
  `runHeartbeat` (`cli/scan.go:446`); add a `--force` flag. Without it a manual verify/sweep
  races a running daemon/scan: double LLM spend re-verifying the same `pending_candidates`,
  plus a spurious UNIQUE-violation abort path (`UpsertFinding` error → `setErr` →
  `run()` classifies Aborted). This preserves the bugbot-4zt advisory-lock protection.

---

## 7a. Lazy on-demand cartography (child .7)

**Goal:** stop eagerly LLM-summarizing every stale package up front; generate each package's
summary the first time a finder unit that spans it initializes. Same blocking call, same
injected block, same persisted cache — only the TIMING changes (eager → demand-pull). The
cartographer stays SYNCHRONOUS: a finder unit BLOCKS on summary generation before building
its task (per the requester — no streaming of partial summaries to the agent).

**What already exists and is reused unchanged:** `summarizePackage` (`cartographer.go:405`),
`UpsertPackageSummaries`/`GetPackageSummaries` (idempotent `(pkg,fingerprint)` cache),
`packagesSpanned`/`packageFingerprint`, `contextFor` rendering, the importer graph
(`PackageImportersScoped`, `ingest/blast.go:445`), and the cost bounds
(`DefaultCartographerMaxFiles=64`, `…HeadLines=400`, `…InputBytes=128k`).

**Design — evolve the `cartography` struct into a per-run lazy provider:**
- `cartography` (`cartographer.go:37`) gains: `client llm.Client`, `store`, `fps map[string]string`,
  `budget *budgetState`, `enabled bool`, a per-run memo `summaries map[string]string` (now
  populated lazily, guarded by a `sync.Mutex`), and a `golang.org/x/sync/singleflight.Group`
  keyed by package so concurrent finder units spanning the same package generate it ONCE
  (store upsert is idempotent, but singleflight avoids the double LLM spend). `importers`
  stays (built once, see below).
- Replace the eager `cartograph` (`run.go:545`) with a CHEAP constructor
  `f.newCartographer(ctx, snap, targets, fps, budget, client) *cartography` that:
  returns `nil` when `!Features.Cartographer` (byte-identical off path, unchanged);
  builds ONLY the importer graph (`PackageImportersScoped`, pure-Go, no LLM) and stores the
  deps. It does **NOT** summarize anything. (Importer graph kept eager because it is pure-Go,
  is needed across units to resolve cross-unit dependents, and is already O(spanned pkgs).
  If the graph build itself becomes a large-repo bottleneck, caching the import index in the
  store is a follow-up — out of scope here.)
- New method `func (c *cartography) ensureContextFor(ctx, files []string) string` replacing the
  call at `hypothesize.go:503` (`cart.contextFor(u.files)` → `cart.ensureContextFor(ctx, u.files)`):
  1. nil/disabled receiver → "" (unchanged off path).
  2. Compute own packages of `files` + direct dependents (from `importers`) — same set
     `contextFor` would render.
  3. For each package: memo hit → use; else `GetPackageSummaries` → fresh fingerprint match →
     memo+use; else (missing/stale) generate via `singleflight.Do(pkg, …summarizePackage…)`,
     `UpsertPackageSummaries` immediately (interrupt-safe), memo, use.
  4. Budget gate: if `budget != nil && budget.finderOverHard()`, SKIP generation and render
     only what is already cached/memoized — identical degradation to today's eager pass.
  5. Render via the existing `contextFor` logic over the now-materialized memo (extract the
     rendering half of `contextFor` so both the (now-lazy) path and tests reuse it).
- `get_package_context` finder tool (`hypothesize.go:468`): currently read-only
  (`GetPackageSummaries`, clean miss on absent). Upgrade it to generate-on-miss through the
  SAME singleflight/memo provider, so an explicit package query the finder issues also
  materializes (and caches) the summary. Keeps "lazy on demand" coherent across both the
  injected block and the pull tool.
- `package_graph` tool + `QueryGraph` (`hypothesize.go:482`): unchanged (graph is eager).

**Durability / idempotency:** each summary persists the instant it is produced (as today),
so an interrupted unit loses at most the one in-flight summary; the `(pkg,fingerprint)` cache
means the next run/unit reuses it. WorkRemaining (packages lacking a fresh summary) shrinks
monotonically; only genuinely-changed packages regenerate. A budget-truncated sweep over a
huge repo pays for ONLY the packages its finder units reached — the scaling fix.

**`RefreshCartography`** (`cartographer.go:578`, the `bugbot cartography --run` out-of-band
full refresh) stays as the eager whole-repo path — it is the explicit "summarize everything
now" entry point and is unaffected. `cartograph`'s eager body is removed; its reusable helpers
(`regenSummaries` etc.) stay for `RefreshCartography`.

**Interaction with .3 (verify drain):** in `modeVerifyDrain` no finder units run, so
`ensureContextFor` is never called and `newCartographer` need not even build the graph —
gate `newCartographer` inside the `mode == modeFull` block (the same block .3 introduces).
**Sequence .7 after .3** so the `mode == modeFull` guard already exists when .7 swaps
`cartograph` → `newCartographer` inside it (both touch `run.go:540-547`).

**Tests** (`cartographer_test.go` extension): a unit spanning an un-summarized package
triggers exactly one `summarizePackage` (assert client call count) and persists it; a second
unit spanning the same package generates ZERO new calls (memo/cache hit); two concurrent
units on the same package generate exactly one (singleflight); budget-hard skips generation
and renders cached-only; `Features.Cartographer=false` → newCartographer nil, zero calls,
byte-identical empty injection.

---

## 8. Interruption/resumability test matrix (child .6)

Extend `internal/funnel/interrupt_test.go` (fixtures: `openFixture`, `scriptedClient`,
`gatingClient`, `candJSON`, `realCand`). Matrix asserts BEHAVIOUR not plumbing:
- Interrupt at ≥4 boundaries (after finder unit emit / mid-triage / mid-verify / before
  sweep), then drive `VerifyDrain`+`SweepDrain` (NOT a full re-run) and assert stranded
  work picked up, no finding dropped/duplicated.
- "verify drain without finders": seed pending rows, `VerifyDrain` with a finder client
  that records calls (or panics) → assert finder call count == 0, findings persisted,
  pending rows deleted.
- "double drain": second `VerifyDrain`/`SweepDrain` over already-processed items is a no-op
  (WAL empty / swept_at set; verifier call count unchanged on the sweep).

---

## 9. Dependency / sequencing for implementation

`.2` (store: swept_at + UnsweptOpenFindings) is the foundation — do FIRST, alone.
`.3` (VerifyDrain) and `.4` (SweepDrain) are then parallelizable: disjoint funnel files
(`run.go` mode + a new `verifydrain.go` vs a new `sweepdrain.go`), disjoint CLI files
(`verify.go` vs `sweep.go`), both depend only on .2. Shared touch points: `store.ScanKind`
consts (both add one — pre-assign to avoid conflict: .3 adds `ScanVerifyDrain`, .4 adds
`ScanImpactSweep`), and the optional spend-budget helper (let .4 own it; .3 doesn't need it).
`.5` (scheduler + remove Stage F + config + oneshot fixpoint) depends on .3 AND .4.
`.7` (lazy cartography) depends on .3 (shares `run.go:540-547` mode guard + `hypothesize.go`);
it is disjoint from .4/.5 (funnel/cartographer.go + hypothesize.go vs daemon/cli/config), so
.7 may run alongside .5 once .3 has landed. Because .3/.5/.7 all touch `run.go` in different
regions, the orchestrator COMMITS between run.go-touching phases so line numbers are stable
(.3 first, then .5 and .7 on the committed base).
`.6` (test matrix) depends on .5 AND .7. Full `go test ./...` green gates epic closure.

Revised order: **.2 → (.3 ∥ .4) → commit → (.5 ∥ .7) → commit → .6 → full test → close.**
acceptance_criteria:
- This bead's --design records: the Pass abstraction signature/contract; a per-phase table mapping each phase to its WorkRemaining query + terminal marker + idempotency key; the scheduler integration plan (daemon timers + oneshot fixpoint); interruption-defer semantics; the resolved impact-sweep marker decision with rationale; and the cartography/leads scope call.
- The design names concrete store methods/columns to add (e.g. UnsweptOpenFindings, any migration) so children .2-.5 can be implemented without re-deriving the model.
- Reviewed/approved (human or oracle) before any implementation child starts.
notes: Full design at local/vpx-design.md. Oracle review (history://OracleReviewDesign) returned GO-WITH-CHANGES; all 5 required changes folded into design (reserveForDownstream guard in modeVerifyDrain, advisory scan-lock on one-shots, oneshot store re-read post-fixpoint, verify-drain WAL pre-check, postCycle order reverify->verify->sweep->publish->promote). Added child .7 (lazy cartography) per new requirement: up-front cartography does not scale; convert to lazy demand-pull pass. Revised order: .2 -> (.3||.4) -> commit -> (.5||.7) -> commit -> .6 -> full test.
close_reason: Foundation design complete and oracle-approved (GO-WITH-CHANGES, all 5 required changes incorporated). Decisions: (a) Pass = WorkRemaining query + idempotent Process + durable terminal marker, no shared Go interface (item types/exec shapes differ too much); repro backlog is the template. (b) impact-sweep marker = dedicated swept_at column (migration 014), preserved across UpsertFinding, reset on file_hash change, set by UpdateFindingSeverity (its only 2 callers are the sweep). (c) verify drain reuses run() via a runMode param (no fork), skipping finder+cartographer+reserveForDownstream in modeVerifyDrain. (d) sweep drain = thin wrapper over unchanged impactSweep fed by UnsweptOpenFindings. (e) cartography becomes a lazy on-demand pass (child .7). Design persisted to bead --design and local/vpx-design.md.

title: Funnel re-architecture: every phase an independently-schedulable idempotent pass (interrupt-safe pipeline)
description:
Problem: the funnel's phases hold durable intermediate state, but each phase's PICKUP is bolted to a fixed point in the monolithic run() lifecycle, so an interrupted scan strands or drops work. Production runs almost always get interrupted (budget cap / ctx cancel), so any phase whose pickup is terminal effectively NEVER runs.

Concrete failures (file:line evidence):
- Impact sweep (Stage F) is a one-shot terminal pass over the current run's IN-MEMORY findings slice: run.go:828 calls impactSweep (impact_sweep.go:57), which only sees `findings` from this run and only runs after the streaming pipeline drains. Interrupted run => never sweeps. Worse: UpsertFinding rewrites verdict_detail from the verify-time struct (findings.go:285 insert, :354/:363 update) where VerdictDetail=="", so every RE-DISCOVERY resets the sweep marker to ''. Net effect: severity re-ranking is effectively dead under real (interrupted) conditions. Live evidence in ../services-runtime/.bugbot/state.db: 36/36 open findings unswept (verdict_detail='').
- Finder hypotheses ARE durable + idempotent but pickup is coupled to run() START. Candidates are WAL-persisted per finder unit at hypothesize.go:705 (before emit at :713) plus a per-unit coverage stamp (:732); rows are deleted only at a terminal fate (triage_streaming.go:303/:434, helpers.go:125) and replayed at run start (run.go:498 load, :529 replay INSIDE the hypothesize goroutine). So a verifier cannot pick up a stranded hypothesis without launching a full run that ALSO re-runs finders => interrupted finder spend is only recoverable by paying for another finder pass.

Goal: treat run() as an orchestration of independently-schedulable, idempotent PASSES over durable store state. Each phase exposes (a) a store-queryable 'work-remaining' predicate, (b) a per-item claim / terminal marker, (c) an idempotent processor. The daemon scheduler AND oneshot scan drive passes to fixpoint; interruption only DEFERS work to the next pass, never drops it. Verify and sweep become free-standing drains (mirroring the existing repro backlog: backlog.go OpenBacklog + runReproBacklog) so any item can be picked up at any time, decoupled from the finder stage.
design:
Conformance audit (evidence-based) — which phases already follow durable-state + resumable-pickup + idempotent-recompute, and which do not:
  hypothesize : DURABLE pending_candidates WAL + coverage watermarks; pickup = replay at run START (run.go:529); idempotent (re-anchored). GAP: pickup coupled to finder pass.
  triage      : operates on the same WAL row; re-clustered on replay; idempotent. OK.
  verify      : finding upserted immediately, WAL row deleted at terminal fate; idempotent (UpsertFinding by fingerprint). GAP: no standalone verify-only drain — only runs inside run().
  repro       : durable repro_path/needs_human; standalone backlog drain (OpenBacklog + runReproBacklog) + daemon timer + claim guard. FULLY CONFORMS — use as the template.
  reverify    : walks ALL open findings every postCycle (postcycle.go:55). Conforms.
  impact-sweep: terminal in-memory pass; verdict_detail marker reset on re-upsert; NO cross-run pickup. NON-CONFORMING (worst).

Target abstraction: a Pass = { WorkRemaining(ctx) []Item ; Process(ctx, Item) (idempotent) ; terminal marker that makes a reprocessed item a no-op }. The daemon scheduler (daemon/run.go schedule struct + Run loop; today: poll/sweep/backlog timers) gains verify-drain and sweep-drain timers, budget-gated like backlog (run.go:141-155). Oneshot `bugbot scan` runs passes to fixpoint and picks up stranded work at start.

Marker design: impact sweep needs a durable sweep-state that survives re-discovery. verdict_detail='' as 'needs sweep' is ALMOST right but is clobbered by UpsertFinding on re-upsert (which is acceptable if re-sweep-on-rediscovery is intended, since reachability may have changed) — decide explicitly: either (a) treat re-discovery as 'needs re-sweep' (keep current reset, just make the pass resumable), or (b) add a dedicated swept_at/sweep_state column (migration 014) preserved across upsert like tier/repro_path/needs_human (findings.go:331-342). Ambiguous-but-unadjudicated findings (no client / budget stopped, impact_sweep.go:95-102) must remain pickup-eligible so a later budgeted pass retries the LLM adjudication.

Verify-drain: extract verify (runVerifyAndPersist) into a pass that drains ListPendingCandidates -> re-anchor (triage re-scope/dedup/suppress) -> verify -> persist -> DeletePendingCandidate, WITHOUT launching finders. Realizes 'verifier picks up a hypothesis at any time'. Reuse pendingToCandidate (run.go:897) + existing triageState/clusterReg.

Non-goals to confirm during design task: cartography pass durability (input enrichment, recomputed per run — may be left as-is); leads blackboard.
acceptance_criteria:
- Every finding-producing phase (hypothesize, triage, verify, repro, impact-sweep) has a durable, store-queryable 'work-remaining' predicate and an idempotent processor; re-running a pass over an already-processed item is a verified no-op.
- An interrupted scan drops NO work: on the next daemon cycle / oneshot invocation, stranded pending candidates are VERIFIED WITHOUT re-running finders, and unswept open findings are swept. Covered by an interrupt/resumability test matrix (extends internal/funnel/interrupt_test.go).
- Impact sweep runs to completion over open findings under interruption (not only at end-of-clean-run); a finding's sweep verdict is not silently lost.
- A verifier can pick up a pending hypothesis at any time via a standalone path (bugbot verify / daemon verify pass) independent of the finder stage.
- All child tasks closed; full `go test ./...` green; no terminal-only Stage F call remains in run().
notes: Originating session 2026-06-19: triggered by reviewing ../services-runtime impact-sweep output (36/36 open findings unswept). User operating fact: 'runs almost always get interrupted' — so terminal passes are effectively dead in prod; this is the core justification for the epic. Decomposition lives in child tasks .1-.6 with sibling dependency edges (children depend on siblings, not this epic). Repro backlog (internal/daemon/backlog.go) is the reference implementation to mirror for all new drains. Do NOT start implementation until the foundation design child is closed.
close_reason: Funnel re-architected into independently-schedulable idempotent passes. Delivered: (.2) swept_at marker + UnsweptOpenFindings; (.3) VerifyDrain via run() runMode (no finder, full budget) + bugbot verify; (.4) SweepDrain over store-queried unswept findings + bugbot sweep; (.5) daemon verify-drain/impact-sweep timers + postCycle composition + terminal Stage F removed + oneshot fixpoint with post-sweep re-read; (.6) interrupt/resumability matrix (B1-B4 + finder-never-called + double-drain idempotency); (.7) lazy on-demand cartography (scales to large repos). All acceptance criteria met: every finding-producing phase has a durable work-remaining predicate + idempotent processor; interrupted scans drop no work (stranded pending verified without finders, unswept findings swept); no terminal-only Stage F remains; full go test ./... green (20 packages). Oracle-reviewed design (GO-WITH-CHANGES, all 5 required changes incorporated).

title: [bug] reproducer hard-aborts on absolute/escaping repro file path instead of revising
description: When the reproducer agent's plan injects a file under an absolute or escaping path (observed: '/tmp/repro_test.cpp', a natural choice for a standalone C++ ASan compile), validatePlan does not catch it. The path reaches the sandbox applyWriteFiles -> sanitizeRelPath, which rejects it with 'path escapes workspace', and Attempt returns a hard error ('repro: execute finding <id>: sandbox: write file ...: path escapes workspace'), aborting the whole attempt. This blocks memory-safety repros that use the standalone-compile-in-/tmp approach. The patch prover already guards this (validatePatchPlan rule c, soft retry); the reproducer was the gap. Diagnosed against ../services-runtime (C++, custom image localhost/bugbot-services-runtime-gcc:latest). Confirmed empirically that ASan AND TSan are available and functional in that image (probe reports cpp.asan=true tsan=true ubsan=true; a real UAF aborts under ASan and a real race is reported under TSan), and that a later run promoted 3 memory-safety/leak findings to T1 -- so sanitizer support itself is working; the path-escape abort was the remaining blocker.
acceptance_criteria: validatePlan rejects absolute/escaping/empty/root file keys using the sandbox's own rule (no rule drift); such a plan is fed back to the agent as a recoverable revision (soft retry) and never reaches the sandbox; the corrected plan promotes; sandbox.ValidateWorkspacePath exported and shares sanitizeRelPath verbatim; planSchema files description states injected paths must be workspace-relative (runtime /tmp outputs allowed); repro+sandbox tests pass.
close_reason: Added sandbox.ValidateWorkspacePath (thin export over sanitizeRelPath, single source of truth) and used it in repro.validatePlan to reject absolute/escaping/empty/root injected file keys with an actionable message. validatePlan failures are already soft retries in Attempt, so an escaping path now feeds back to the agent and revises instead of hard-aborting. Clarified planSchema files description: injected paths must be workspace-relative; build outputs may still go to /tmp at run time. Tests: TestValidateWorkspacePath (sandbox), TestPromoteAll_AbsolutePathPlanRetries (repro). Patch prover already guarded this (validatePatchPlan rule c + soft retry); reproducer was the only gap. ASan/TSan confirmed working in the target image (probe cpp.asan/tsan/ubsan=true; later run promoted 3 memory-safety findings) -- not a sandbox-availability problem.

title: [bug] repro plan schema requires non-load-bearing 'expect', aborting attempts hard
description: Reproducer planSchema lists 'expect' in its required set. When a (weak) model returns a runnable files+cmd plan but omits the descriptive 'expect' string, RunJSON fails schema validation, the one repair round-trip also omits it, and Attempt returns a hard infrastructure error (repro: plan finding <id>: agent: model output did not parse as JSON after one repair: root object missing required field "expect"). This aborts the whole repro attempt even though 'expect' is never load-bearing: validatePlan checks only files+cmd, and every consumer (artifacts README, patch context) treats Expect as optional prose. Severity is inverted vs a missing load-bearing field (files/cmd), which only triggers a soft retry.
acceptance_criteria: planSchema.required == [files, cmd]; a plan omitting 'expect' parses on the first pass (no repair) and promotes; 'expect' stays in properties with minLength:1 so when present it must be non-empty; patch context guards empty Expect; repro+agent test packages pass.
close_reason: Dropped 'expect' from planSchema.required (now [files,cmd]); kept it in properties with minLength:1 so it's still requested and, when present, non-empty. Guarded patch.go's unconditional 'Expected failure:' write on empty Expect (artifacts.go already guarded). Added TestPromoteAll_PlanWithoutExpectPromotes proving an expect-less plan promotes in exactly one completion (no repair). This aligns the hard schema contract with the actual load-bearing requirements and with validatePlan. The other two repro failures in the report (environment_error, not_demonstrated) are legitimate non-code classifications, not defects.

title: Impact-assessment sweep: low-budget end-of-scan reachability/severity re-rank
description: PROPOSED (user, 2026-06-19) as the next step in weighting finding impact: a SEPARATE, low-budget impact-assessment agent sweep that runs at the END of a scan, after the finder/verifier/refuter stages (which work well). The 2026-06-19 services-runtime C++ dogfood run shipped ~14/31 findings in dead/uncalled code (add_effect 0 callers; Surface 2-arg ctor; JSON::write; RegisterableFuncType::bind callable only from its ctor; count_frames; PosixPoller; split) or platform code not compiled on the Linux target (WindowsFdSet under #ifdef _WIN32; macOS toolchain) at medium/high severity. The funnel verifies 'is this a real defect' but never asks 'does this code run, and who controls its inputs', so severity is defect-class-weighted not impact-weighted.
design: SEAM: add the stage in internal/funnel/run.go AFTER verifyFanout.wait() (~line 676) operating on the run's persisted `allFindings`, BEFORE finalize/return -- this gives parity for both `bugbot scan` (oneshot run()) and the daemon (Sweep/Targeted -> run()), exactly where in-run repro already hooks. Severity is a bare string on Candidate/Finding (funnel.go:621, set by the finder); the sweep rewrites it and stores a rationale. COST (M3 is input-dominated ~130:1, no prompt caching): compute reachability DETERMINISTICALLY first -- caller count via lsp references / grep, dead-code detection, test-only path, and build-tag/#ifdef platform gating -- so most findings are classified with ZERO LLM tokens; feed only the compact (finding, caller-facts) tuples to a SINGLE batched cheap-model adjudication that emits {severity, rationale}. RELATIONSHIP: this is the end-of-scan-sweep implementation of the goal diagnosed in bugbot-bar (which proposed reachability/trust-boundary SEATS inside the per-finding verifier/arbiter). Recommend the sweep over per-seat (cheaper: deterministic batch vs per-finding LLM seat); supersede-vs-keep bugbot-bar flagged for the user. Will `bd dep add bugbot-bar <this>`.
acceptance_criteria: (1) A new stage re-ranks each surviving finding's severity using a reachability/impact analysis and records the rationale in the verdict detail. (2) Replaying the services-runtime C++ run, the dead/uncalled/platform-gated findings (#7,#8,#9,#15,#20,#21,#22,#24,#25,#26,#27,#28, macOS #1) drop to low/latent with caller-trace rationale, while genuinely-reachable findings (#2/3 every-frame, #5/6 UAF, #13/14 lighting, #19 per-tile leak, #29 startup leak, #18 identity poison) retain high. (3) The sweep's added spend is a small fraction of a scan (dominated by deterministic analysis, at most one batched LLM adjudication call). (4) [ABSORBED FROM bugbot-bar -- Go dogfood corpus, a second-ecosystem regression check] Replaying the FIRST (Go) dogfood run, severity drops with recorded reachability/trust-boundary rationale: #52 (lsp Manager Close race) -> low/medium (every query goroutine is joined by wg.Wait before Funnel.Close; worst case one orphaned gopls dies on stdin EOF at process exit); #57 (ChangedFiles omits --end-of-options) -> low (every dash-capable input is the local operator's own CLI flag -- PR SHAs are 40-hex, git forbids dash-leading branch names -- no privilege boundary crossed); #55 (repro prompt-injection) -> info. (5) The analysis answers BOTH 'can the program reach this state' AND 'who controls the inputs' (trust boundary), and the stored severity rationale cites it.
notes: Trust-boundary axis (who controls the inputs) belongs here: e.g. TextureAtlas underflow #16 is only reachable with attacker-controlled atlas dims, but the sole call site passes constants -- impact LOW today. Keep the deterministic classifier conservative: 'dead' must mean zero non-test callers AND not an exported library entrypoint intended for external use. SUPERSEDES bugbot-bar (severity calibration): bar proposed reachability/trust-boundary SEATS inside the per-finding verifier/arbiter; this replaces that with a cheaper end-of-scan deterministic-classify + batched-adjudication sweep. Complements bugbot-aud (execute-the-claim: refuters are strong at 'is the code shaped as claimed', weak at dynamic semantics / threat-model context).
close_reason: Implemented (ed4/s5e, squash d017dec): end-of-scan impact sweep; deterministic tree-sitter reachability (EnclosingDefinition symbol, zero-caller/platform/test-only) downranks dead findings with rationale in new verdict_detail col (migration 012); C/C++ headers + ambiguous cases route to one batched LLM adjudication; reachable kept. Absorbs bugbot-bar. Oracle-verified. Merged to main.

title: funnel: persist in-flight candidates (WAL) so interrupted runs are not lost
description: Interrupting a oneshot (or daemon) scan permanently loses every hypothesis (funnel.Candidate) that had not reached a terminal fate. The streaming funnel (bugbot-280) made findings, per-unit coverage, and the scan_runs row durable, but candidates between hypothesize and verify live only in in-memory channels (candCh/verCh in run.go) and in-flight verify goroutines. On ctx cancel emit drops them (run.go:508) and the goroutines unwind. Compounding it: TouchScanCoverage already stamped those files at finder-unit completion (hypothesize.go:695), so the next sweep deprioritizes them (Group 2 stalest-first) and the candidates are never recovered. The expensive finder work is thrown away.
design: Write-ahead log of candidates. New store table pending_candidates (migration 009): one row per finder-emitted candidate, full Candidate fidelity (lens/file/line/title/description/severity/evidence/confidence/corroborating_lenses + scan_run_id + commit). (1) PERSIST at the finder emit site (hypothesize.go ~675), batched per finder unit, before candidates enter the volatile channel pipeline — same per-unit durability discipline as the coverage stamp. Candidate gains a PendingID field carrying the row PK. (2) DELETE at every terminal fate: triage drops (low-conf/out-of-scope/dup/suppressed) + cluster merge (triage_streaming.go), and verify survived/killed/orphaned (verify_stream.go). A clean run empties the WAL; only an interrupt leaves rows. Delete runs on the run ctx so a cancelled run does NOT delete in-flight rows (they persist). (3) REPLAY at run start (run.go): ListPendingCandidates from prior runs, emit into the same triage/verify pipeline BEFORE fresh hypothesize, re-anchored to the current snapshot — skips re-hypothesize, verifier re-checks against current code (stale candidates get killed; precision-safe). Always-on (no config knob). Crash-safe (WAL survives SIGKILL, not just SIGINT). Stats.Resumed counts replayed candidates. No retention prune needed: replay self-cleans out-of-scope rows via the triage drop+delete.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Interrupt a sweep after a finder emits candidates but before verify completes: pending_candidates table has the un-verified candidate rows (test via gated verifier + watchdog cancel). 2) A second run on the same store replays those rows: they are verified and persisted as findings, and the pending_candidates table is drained to zero. 3) A clean (uninterrupted) run leaves pending_candidates empty at finalize (every candidate deleted at terminal fate). 4) Persist/delete/replay are best-effort: a store error is f.note-logged and never aborts the scan. 5) store unit tests for AddPendingCandidates (batch)/ListPendingCandidates/DeletePendingCandidate incl. empty-id no-op. 6) make test green.
close_reason: Implemented a candidate write-ahead log (pending_candidates table, migration 009). Finder-emitted candidates are persisted per-unit BEFORE entering the volatile channel pipeline (hypothesize.go) and deleted at every terminal fate — triage drops/merge (triage_streaming.go) and verify survived/killed/orphaned (verify_stream.go via Funnel.deletePending). run() replays surviving rows from prior interrupted runs into triage/verify before fresh hypothesize (pendingToCandidate), re-anchored to the current snapshot so stale candidates are dropped/killed precision-safely. Always-on (correctness, no config knob); crash-safe (survives SIGKILL, not just SIGINT). Stats.Resumed counts replayed candidates and is surfaced in the CLI scan summary. Delete runs on the run ctx so an interrupted run keeps its in-flight rows. Tests: store round-trip + empty-id/empty-batch no-ops; funnel interrupt->resume (candidate survives interrupt, next run verifies it, WAL drains), clean-run-drains-WAL. Full suite (18 pkg), go vet, gofmt all green.

title: Deep schema enforcement at every LLM phase boundary (strengthen jwd's shallow validation)
description: Follow-up to closed bugbot-jwd/gd3/w88. Those wired native structured-output (ResponseSchema) and a HARNESS-side shape check, but the harness check (agent.validateRootShape) is deliberately shallow: it only verifies the root JSON type and top-level required keys. It does NOT recurse into array items or nested object properties, and ignores enum/minItems/minProperties/minLength/minimum/additionalProperties. So when a provider's StructuredOutput capability is OFF (the dogfood MiniMax-M3 openai-compatible path is opt-in), the ONLY real enforcement is a typed json.Unmarshal — which silently tolerates bad enums (severity='blocker'), missing NESTED required fields (a candidate with no evidence), empty required strings, and empty files maps (coerced/defaulted downstream, e.g. normalizeSeverity maps unknown->'medium'). Every LLM phase output is the next phase's input, so a loose output schema = loose input contract. Make the schema strong and well-defined: a full (subset) JSON-Schema validator applied to the parsed body at the single RunJSON chokepoint, plus tightened schema definitions.
design: Hand-rolled validator over a closed in-repo schema set (5 schemas) instead of a new JSON-Schema library dependency: zero new deps (project values minimal deps), validates exactly the constructs used, emits repair-prompt-tuned path-qualified errors. Parse schema per call (matches existing validateRootShape; negligible vs LLM latency). Root-level error phrasing preserved verbatim ('root JSON type ...', 'root object missing required field ...') so existing tests and any caller classification stay stable. Behavior change: strict enum now triggers a repair round-trip where normalizeSeverity/normalizeConfidence previously silently coerced — intended (precision lever); bounded to one repair; absent entirely when native StructuredOutput cap is on (grammar prevents violations). additionalProperties:false enforced for parity with native decoding.
acceptance_criteria: agent.validateSchema replaces validateRootShape and recursively enforces type (incl. integer vs number), required (nested), properties, additionalProperties (false rejects unknowns; schema validates values), items, enum, minItems, minProperties, minLength, maxLength, minimum with path-qualified errors; RunJSON routes any violation through the existing single repair round-trip and re-validates the repair; schema defs tightened where genuinely invariant (planSchema files minProperties:1, required free-text minLength:1, candidate line minimum:1) WITHOUT adding constraints that reject the valid 'found nothing' empty-candidates output; go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/funnel/... ./internal/repro/... ./internal/llm/... green; scripted eval gate still precision=1.000.
close_reason: Replaced agent.validateRootShape (shallow: root type + top-level required only) with a deep recursive validateSchema enforcing the full JSON-Schema subset the phase schemas use — type/integer, nested required, properties, additionalProperties (false-reject + subschema-validate), items, enum, minItems/minProperties/minLength/maxLength/minimum — with path-qualified errors routed through RunJSON's existing single repair round-trip (re-validated on repair). Hand-rolled, zero new deps, over the closed 5-schema set. Tightened the schemas themselves: candidate line minimum:1 + minLength:1 on file/title/description/evidence, refuter reasoning minLength:1, plan files minProperties:1 + expect minLength:1, patch summary minLength:1, cartographer summary minLength:1 — deliberately NOT adding minItems to candidates (empty=found-nothing). 18-case TestValidateSchema + TestRunJSON_DeepValidationTriggersRepair (a wrong-enum body that the old shallow check + typed unmarshal accepted now repairs). go test ./... green (18 pkg ok); scripted eval gate precision=1.000 recall=1.000; gofmt+vet clean.

title: cartographer: parallelize summary generation + persist each summary on the fly
description: Two coupled large-repo problems in the cartographer regen loop (funnel cartograph + RefreshCartography): (1) summaries are generated SEQUENTIALLY, one blocking client.Complete per package, so a large repo's uncached packages serialize; finders already parallelize via the funnel slot pool. (2) summaries are accumulated into a batch and UpsertPackageSummaries is called ONCE at the end — and the in-scan path calls that final upsert with the (possibly cancelled) scan ctx — so an interrupted or budget-stopped pass discards EVERY computed summary, wasting all prior LLM spend.
design: Add Funnel.regenSummaries helper: launches one goroutine per uncached package bounded by the funnel-wide slot pool (slotLow, same class/bound as finders; cartographer runs before hypothesize so the pool is free), and PERSISTS each summary the instant it is produced via a single-row UpsertPackageSummaries — never batched. Persist uses context.WithoutCancel(ctx)+timeout so a summary produced just before cancellation is still saved. Optional budget gating mirrors the finder stage (stop launching + in-flight bail on finderOverHard). Launch order follows the sorted regen list (reproducible prefix under a budget cutoff); completion order is unspecified. onResult callback is serialized so callers need no locking. cartograph and RefreshCartography both call it: cartograph updates the summaries map + notes persist failures; RefreshCartography counts Summarized/Failed and returns a persist error if any row failed (successes still saved). Bug-findings already persist incrementally via UpsertFinding, so scope is the cartographer only.
acceptance_criteria: Cartographer summaries generate concurrently (bounded by MaxParallel) and each is in the DB before the next starts/finishes; an interrupted pass keeps all summaries produced so far (no end-of-run batch). Both cartograph and RefreshCartography use the shared helper. Tests: concurrent persist-on-the-fly writes each row as produced; a cancelled-mid-pass run still has the already-produced summaries in the store; spend/budget accounting unchanged. go test ./internal/funnel green; vet+lint+gofmt clean.
close_reason: Added Funnel.regenSummaries: summarizes uncached packages CONCURRENTLY bounded by the funnel slot pool (slotLow, same class/bound as finders; cartographer runs before hypothesize so the pool is free) and persists EACH summary the instant it is produced via a single-row UpsertPackageSummaries — never batched. Persist uses context.WithoutCancel(ctx)+30s timeout so a summary produced just before cancellation is still saved. Optional budget gating mirrors the finder stage (stop launching + in-flight re-check on finderOverHard). Both cartograph (in-scan, with budget) and RefreshCartography (manual, no budget) now call it. Replaces the two sequential loops + end-of-pass batch upsert (the in-scan one used the cancelled ctx, so an interrupted large-repo pass previously discarded every computed summary). Tests: interrupted pass persists exactly the already-produced summary (old code: 0); full concurrent pass persists all; barrier client proves real concurrency (sequential regression deadlocks to the ctx deadline). go test ./... green (17 pkgs); vet+lint+gofmt clean.

title: bazel reproduction fails silently under network=none — make the limitation loud
description: repro runs bazel test //... for bazel repos, but bugbot has no bazel dependency strategy (recommendDepStrategy only handles Go/Python module caches) and the sandbox runs network=none, so bazel cannot fetch external repos (deps.MODULE.bazel, requirements_lock wheels, npm, patches). When bazel is missing (127) or deps cannot be fetched, interpret() emits a generic environment_error and the finding silently stays unreproduced.
design: Convert the silent failure into a clear, actionable outcome. In repro interpret/verdict (internal/repro/interpret.go), when the suite command launcher is bazel and the run shows exit 127 or fetch/network errors, produce a distinct specific message that names bazel + offline as the cause and tells the user to bake a custom image with a prefetched repository cache or disable repro for bazel repos. Also update prime recommendDepStrategy guidance for bazel repos to state this. Do NOT implement an actual bazel dep-prefetch strategy (large feature; separate bead).
acceptance_criteria: A synthetic sandbox.Result with cmd [bazel test //...] and ExitCode 127 yields a verdict whose message clearly attributes failure to bazel/offline (not a generic env error); prime dep_strategy guidance for a bazel repo mentions the offline-bazel limitation; tests added; go test ./internal/repro/ and ./internal/cli/ pass.
close_reason: prime dep-strategy note + doctor warn make the no-bazel-strategy/offline limitation explicit; repro interpret surfaces a bazel-specific environment_error summary+feedback and a precision-first bazel ecosystem entry

title: funnel: reserve downstream (verify/repro) budget so finders can't starve them
description: SYMPTOM (user 2026-06-17): triage+repro are effectively always ignored because finders blow through the per-cycle token limit. ROOT CAUSE: the per-run TokenBudget is a SINGLE shared agent.BudgetPool charged by all roles. budgetState.overHard/overSoft compare CUMULATIVE TOTAL chargeable spend against the single budget. The finder stage (Stage A, hypothesize) launches first and in bulk, so finders drain the whole pool before any candidate reaches Stage C (verify); verify's gate (verify_stream.go:67 budget.overHard) then sees total>=budget and orphans every candidate as T3. No T2 survivors => repro (which depends on T2 input; repro spend is on a SEPARATE per-day ledger per bugbot-3lt) never has anything to reproduce. bugbot-3lt live evidence: 769 finder units skipped_hard_budget, 0 T1 promotions across 15 runs.
design: FIX = partition the per-run TokenBudget into a finder sub-budget (share*budget) and a reserved verify sub-budget (the remainder), each gated independently by its role so verify keeps full 3-refuter strength WITHIN its reserve instead of degrading the instant finders fill their share. Implementation (dual-mode, opt-in so direct-budgetState unit tests stay legacy): budgetState keeps the existing total 'pool' (legacy single-pool mode, unchanged) and gains finderPool/verifyPool + finderBudget/verifyBudget, populated only by a new reserveForDownstream(share) called from run.go with the resolved Options.FinderBudgetShare (default DefaultFinderBudgetShare=0.7 => reserve 30%). spendRecorder routes each completion's chargeable tokens to finderPool when ev.Role=='finder' else verifyPool (nil-safe), in addition to the total pool. Gates: hypothesize finder gate -> finderOverHard/finderOverSoft (finderPool vs finderBudget); verify_stream verify gate -> verifyOverHard/verifyOverSoft (verifyPool vs verifyBudget); both fall back to legacy overHard/overSoft when their sub-pool is nil. runnerLimits refactored to runnerLimitsForPool(base,pool); finder uses finderRunnerLimits, verify+arbiter use verifyRunnerLimits. share>=1 or unlimited budget => reserveForDownstream is a no-op (legacy single pool). Config: budgets.finder_budget_share (0..1; 0=>funnel default), env BUGBOT_BUDGETS_FINDER_BUDGET_SHARE, validated 0..1, wired through cli/scan.go. Repro budgeting UNCHANGED (separate per-day ledger by design, bugbot-3lt).
acceptance_criteria: 1) New funnel test proves a Sweep that would (legacy) exhaust the pool on finders now VERIFIES at least one candidate to T2 under the default reservation. 2) budgetState unit tests: reserveForDownstream partition math, recorder role routing into finderPool/verifyPool, finderOverHard/verifyOverHard thresholds. 3) Verify-budget-exhaustion still orphans as T3 (reworked TestSweep_BudgetOrphanPersistsAsTier3). 4) Existing finder-degradation tests preserved by FinderBudgetShare=1.0 (legacy mode). 5) config: finder_budget_share parses, env-overrides, validates 0..1; default 0 defers to funnel. 6) go build + go test ./internal/funnel/ ./internal/config/ ./internal/cli/ green.
notes: Config knobs: budgets.finder_token_claim / budgets.verifier_token_claim (env BUGBOT_BUDGETS_FINDER_TOKEN_CLAIM / _VERIFIER_TOKEN_CLAIM), Options.FinderTokenClaim/VerifierTokenClaim, default funnel.DefaultTokenClaim=1_000_000; 0=default, negative=disable per-task cap. Wired through scan/daemon/review CLI. Core change: internal/funnel/funnel.go runnerLimitsForPool(base,pool,claim) + finder/verifyRunnerLimits pass the role claim; run.go sets budget.finderClaim/verifyClaim. Tests (internal/funnel): reworked TestSweep_BudgetOrphanPersistsAsTier3 (2 cands, share 0.5, verify reserve exhausted by cand1's panel -> cand1 survives T2, cand2 orphaned T3 at verifyOverHard gate); TestBudgetReserve_VerifyGateIndependentOfFinderSpend (gate-level prioritization); TestRunnerLimits_ClaimCap; TestSpendRecorder_ClaimRefundIsAutomatic; TestResolve_TokenClaimDefaults; config TestEnvOverride_TokenClaims. go build + go test ./internal/... green (17 pkgs), -race green on budget tests.
close_reason: Implemented the claimant budget system: per-task token claim (default 1M, configurable per role) caps each finder/verifier run; unspent budget is automatically returned to the per-role pool because only actual spend is charged. Downstream prioritization preserved via the existing per-role reserve (FinderBudgetShare). Rejected the explicit reserve/refund ledger on oracle review (same-role starvation + sentinel misclassification, no benefit). All acceptance criteria met; tests green incl. -race.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: Delivered the claimant budget system (user directive 2026-06-17: finders/verifiers claim a per-task budget, default 1M, unspent returned to pool). Final design after oracle review: KEEP the spend-based per-role reservation (finderPool/verifyPool sized by FinderBudgetShare; gates finderOverHard/verifyOverHard; BudgetCheck->TruncBudgetPool orphan) — this is the prioritization that stops finders starving verify. ADD a per-task CLAIM as a per-run cap in budgetState.runnerLimitsForPool: each finder/verifier run's TokenBudget = min(role claim, base, sub-pool remaining). 'Return to pool on closure' is AUTOMATIC: the recorder charges the sub-pool only for tokens ACTUALLY spent, so the unspent part of a claim is never removed and stays available to siblings. REJECTED the explicit reserve-and-refund ledger (reservable counter + acquire/release): oracle confirmed it adds a same-role starvation bug (first verifier reserves the whole verify reserve -> concurrent candidates denied -> FEWER T2 survivors) and a verifier sentinel misclassification, while buying nothing the spend pool doesn't already give. Net: ~per-run cap + config knobs, zero launch-site rewires, no new concurrency state.

title: Umbrella: --repro inconsistent / low-yield — decomposed root causes (live dogfood evidence)
description:
SYMPTOM (user-reported 2026-06-17): `bugbot scan --repro` produces inconsistent and low-yield results; reporter suspected a sandbox issue but also a token/budgeting/pipelining issue. Investigation against the LIVE dogfood state DB (.bugbot/state.db, 15 scan runs) decomposes the symptom into distinct, independently-fixable causes. '--repro inconsistent' is mostly the visible tip of an upstream finder-reliability + budget problem, plus a latent repro-correctness hazard and a sandbox/image capability gap. This is a TRACKING issue: it depends on the child causes below.

LIVE EVIDENCE (cumulative, 15 runs):
- findings: 4 total, ALL tier-2 open, 0 promoted to T1 (repro_path empty, needs_human=0).
- reproducer agent_units: 1 exhausted, 1 infra_error, 0 reproduced — repro effectively never succeeds.
- finder agent_units: 700 parse_failed, 769 skipped_hard_budget, 48 skipped_degraded, 24 budget_stopped, 81 ok. Several individual runs show 125/125 finders parse_failed with 0 ok.
- sandbox image (bugbot.yaml) = debian:stable-slim: NO Go toolchain, so the reproducer cannot build/run the target (Go) repo's tests.

CONTRIBUTING CAUSES (each its own bead):
1. bugbot-vig (P1) — interpret() (internal/repro/interpret.go:50) treats ANY non-zero exit not matching a Go-ONLY marker blacklist as 'demonstrated'. Latent in this DB (nothing reached repro) but the moment a finding does, an env/build failure's non-zero exit is inconsistently promoted to T1. This is the core 'inconsistent' code hazard.
2. bugbot-5au (P2) — no per-ecosystem sandbox capability probe; debian:stable-slim has no `go`, the reproducer emits Go invocations that exit 127 -> environment_error -> exhausted/infra_error, with no upfront 'image cannot run this ecosystem' signal. This is the 'sandbox issue' the reporter sensed.
3. bugbot-1q0 / bugbot-rwe (P1/P2) — mass finder parse_failed (700 cumulative; per-run 125/125) from MiniMax-M3 think/output-token exhaustion mid-JSON. Starves repro of T2 findings (only 4 ever reached verify-survived).
4. bugbot-v3z (P1) — capability profile inert: ContextWindow never scales chunk/read/compaction; combined with whole-repo `**/*` scope + 10M per-cycle budget this yields 769 hard-stop skips. The 'token/budgeting/pipelining' the reporter suspected.
5. bugbot-dj7 (P1, incidental) — a long --repro run aborted with SQLITE_IOERR_SHORT_READ (522), a write-volume/WAL-checkpoint race; '--repro inconsistent' may also surface as the scan crashing mid-run.
