PIPELINING WAS CHECKED AND IS NOT A CAUSE: the in-run repro queue (reproQueue buffer=16 + overflow drain, repro_hook.go), the IDLE-slot consumer, the claim check (ReproPath/NeedsHuman re-read before and after slot acquisition), and the post-verify catch-up drain (reproAttempted sync.Map) were traced in run.go:575-641 and repro_hook.go:33-123 — delivery is exactly-once and the worker join (reproWg.Wait after reproQ.ch close) is correct. Repro LLM spend is ledgered via a SEPARATE recorder (cli/scan.go newLedgerRecorder) so it counts toward per_day totals but NOT the funnel's per-run TokenBudget pool — this is documented design, not a defect. Record this so the next agent does not re-investigate the pipeline.
design: Tracking/umbrella only — no code lands here. Add `bd dep add <this> <child>` for each contributing cause so this issue shows blocked-by the real fixes and `bd ready` surfaces the children first. Suggested fix ORDER for a future agent: (1) bugbot-vig — make repro outcomes trustworthy/consistent (cheap, self-contained, highest leverage on the 'inconsistent' symptom); (2) bugbot-1q0/bugbot-rwe + bugbot-v3z — restore finder yield so repro has T2 input; (3) bugbot-5au — capability probe so the reproducer stops emitting impossible invocations; (4) bugbot-dj7 — store write-volume hardening for long runs. Operational note for reproducing the symptom: dogfood bugbot.yaml uses debian:stable-slim (no Go); switch to a golang image (integration tests use golang:1.26-alpine) before expecting any Go finding to reach genuine T1.
acceptance_criteria: Each child cause (bugbot-vig, bugbot-5au, bugbot-1q0/bugbot-rwe, bugbot-v3z, bugbot-dj7) is either fixed or carries an explicit determination. End state: a `--repro` run on a toolchain-capable image yields DETERMINISTIC T1 promotions for genuinely reproducible findings and an honest 'could not reproduce (environment)' otherwise — never a non-deterministic T1 from a bare non-zero exit. Finder parse-failure rate on the bugbot self-scan corpus is measured and bounded so repro receives a non-trivial T2 stream.

title: Blackboard leads: consumed leads are never deleted (zombie rows persist on the blackboard)
description: ConsumeLeads (internal/store/leads.go) only flips status='posted'->'consumed' and stamps consumed_at; the row is never deleted. Migration 004 + cli/leads.go treat consumed rows as permanent 'history'. Live dogfood state.db (.bugbot/state.db) shows 3 api-contract-misuse leads, ALL status=consumed, 0 pending — they persist indefinitely. The deterministic miner re-posts the identical (target_lens,file,line) every scan; AddLead's ON CONFLICT flips consumed->posted, the funnel re-consumes, so the same rows oscillate forever and never leave the blackboard. User expectation: a lead should be consumed AND deleted once its target lens has triaged it.
design:
FIX DIRECTION (confirmed with user 2026-06-17): DELETE-ON-CONSUME. ConsumeLeads must DELETE the claimed rows, not flip status='consumed'. The funnel already copies pending leads into the in-memory leadsByLens map BEFORE the finder runs (internal/funnel/hypothesize.go ~185-216), so deleting the DB row does not affect lead injection into finder tasks. Failure semantics are identical to today's consume-at-claim trade-off: a finder that dies after its lead is claimed loses the claim; the deterministic miner/analyzer re-post next cycle via a fresh AddLead INSERT (the old row is gone).

CLEAN CUTOVER (no shims, migrate every caller):
- internal/store/leads.go: ConsumeLeads -> `DELETE FROM leads WHERE id IN (...)` in the existing single transaction. The consumed->posted flip branch of AddLead's ON CONFLICT (and the consumed_at=NULL reset) becomes dead for the consume path; simplify so ON CONFLICT only refreshes note/poster/scan_run_id for a STILL-PENDING re-post.
- Lead.Status / Lead.ConsumedAt and scanLead()'s consumed_at handling lose their only writer. Recommend removing the consumed-row CONCEPT: drop Status/ConsumedAt from the struct + scanLead + ListLeads selects. Schema: leave the status/consumed_at columns in place (modernc.org/sqlite has limited ALTER DROP COLUMN; a no-op column is harmless) OR add 008_*.sql if a clean drop is wanted.
- internal/cli/leads.go: renderLeads currently renders consumed rows as 'history' and prints 'N lead(s), M pending' + consumedNote()/age(); collapse to pending-only output, update the command long-help ('consumed rows are the history') and the package doc comment.
- internal/cli/worldstate.go: already uses ListLeads(pendingOnly=true) -> unaffected.

TESTS TO MIGRATE:
- internal/store/leads_test.go TestLeads_ConsumeLeads: assert the rows are DELETED (PendingLeads + ListLeads both empty) instead of status='consumed'.
- internal/cli/leads_test.go: drop consumed-history rendering assertions; assert pending-only output.
- internal/funnel/leads_test.go: PendingLeads==0 after Sweep and Stats.LeadsConsumed still hold (no change needed).
- internal/analyzer + internal/miner tests use PendingLeads only -> unaffected.
acceptance_criteria: A lead consumed by its target lens during a run is removed from the blackboard after that run's triage (not left as a status='consumed' zombie). bugbot leads / bugbot status no longer surface already-triaged leads. Pending leads (posted, not yet claimed by an active lens) are unaffected. Tests cover: consumed lead absent post-run; pending lead for an inactive lens survives; re-post after deletion re-raises cleanly.
notes: Evidence pulled from live state.db 2026-06-17: leads table = 3 rows all consumed (api-contract-misuse, poster miner:doc-contradiction). Consume happens at CLAIM time in funnel/hypothesize.go (pre-finder), copied into in-memory leadsByLens, so deleting the DB row does not affect injection into the finder task. Failure semantics of delete-on-consume == current consume-at-claim (a failed finder loses the claim; miner re-posts next cycle). cli/leads.go renderLeads + worldstate.go + store/leads_test.go + cli/leads_test.go reference consumed-row history and must be migrated on a clean cutover.
close_reason: Delete-on-consume implemented: ConsumeLeads now DELETEs claimed rows in its single tx (was status='consumed' flip); AddLead ON CONFLICT simplified to refresh note/poster/scan_run_id only; Lead.Status/ConsumedAt concept removed (columns left inert in schema, no migration); cli renderLeads collapsed to pending-only. Tests assert deletion (gone from PendingLeads+ListLeads), inactive-lens survival, and clean re-post. Merged to main (--no-ff).

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: RELATED FOLLOW-UP (not in scope here, do NOT lose it): delete-on-consume removes the zombie ROWS but does NOT stop the deterministic doc-contradiction miner (internal/miner) and analyzer seed from RE-RAISING the identical (target_lens,file,line) lead every single scan — the finder will keep getting the same lead injected and re-investigating it each cycle (wasted finder budget). User explicitly chose delete-on-consume over 'stop the miner re-raising' for THIS pass, accepting the re-raise churn for now. If that churn becomes a budget problem, file a follow-up to make the seeders skip a suspicion that was already claimed for unchanged code (e.g. key on content hash).

title: eval: live-model scorecard (weak-vs-strong precision/recall/cost) behind keys
description: Eval measures pipeline discrimination but cannot A/B a real weak vs strong model on the same corpus. Add a key-gated live mode that runs the real funnel per configured model over the corpus and emits a per-model scorecard: recall, FP-rate, cost-per-true-bug, tokens. Grow corpus toward real historical bugs.
acceptance_criteria: bugbot eval --live (key-gated) runs >=1 model over BuiltinCases and prints a per-model scorecard with precision/recall/cost-per-true-bug; offline gate unaffected.

title: llm/funnel: wire WithSerializedToolCalls + capability-driven chunk/read/compaction scaling
description: Capabilities is inert in production: WithSerializedToolCalls is never called (only tests), and ContextWindow never scales chunk size/read caps/compaction threshold. One-size-fits-all pipeline silently overflows small-context weak models. Wire serialization in registry.NewClient; scale DefaultChunkSize/finder read caps/history compaction from ContextWindow.
acceptance_criteria: Non-parallel-capable clients are serialized in production; finder chunk size and read caps derive from ContextWindow when known; unit tests cover the scaling and serialization wrapping.
close_reason: WithSerializedToolCalls now wired as outermost decorator in registry.NewClient (no-op for parallel-capable providers; serializes openai-compatible in production). New funnel.scaleFinderForContext derives finder chunk size + read caps from finder ContextWindow when >0, strict no-op when unknown(0)/large(>=64k) so existing behavior is stable; explicit Options values preserved. Unit tests cover serialization wrapping + scaling. Merged to main.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: LIVE EVIDENCE (.bugbot/state.db, 2026-06-17): inert capability scaling manifests as 769 skipped_hard_budget finder units across 15 runs — whole-repo '**/*' scope + fixed chunk/read caps + 10M per-cycle budget on MiniMax-M3 (no caching) exhausts the pool and sheds most units. The 'token/budgeting' half of the user's '--repro inconsistent' report. Umbrella: bugbot-3lt.

title: llm: native structured-output (Request.ResponseSchema) across all adapters + config opt-in
description: Add ResponseSchema (and ResponseSchemaName) to llm.Request. OpenAI/openai-compatible: set response_format json_schema (honor even with tools). Google: responseJsonSchema + application/json, only when no tools (Gemini cannot combine with function-calling). Anthropic: synthetic forced output tool (tool_choice=tool, input_schema=schema) only when no tools, surface tool input as Response.Text. Gate via Capabilities.StructuredOutput; add config Provider.structured_output override so openai-compatible (MiniMax-M3) can opt in.
acceptance_criteria: Each adapter has a unit test (fake server) asserting the request carries the schema in the right field and that tools-present requests behave safely; openai-compatible caps overridable from config; go vet + go test ./internal/llm/... green.
close_reason: Added llm.Request.ResponseSchema + ResponseSchemaName. OpenAI/openai-compatible: response_format json_schema (honored even with tools). Google: responseJsonSchema + application/json (only when no tools). Anthropic: synthetic forced-output tool + tool_choice, forced tool input surfaced as Response.Text (only when no tools). All gated on Capabilities.StructuredOutput. Added Provider.structured_output *bool config override (registry applies it, e.g. MiniMax openai-compatible opt-in). 14 fake-server unit tests across all adapters incl. tools-present-safe + cap-gating + config override. go vet + go test ./internal/llm/... green. Merged.

title: agent: thread answer schema through runner/RunJSON + shallow shape validation + constrained repair
description: Thread RunJSON schema into llm.Request.ResponseSchema (capability-gated). Apply on completions; adapters guard tool-presence. Add shallow root-level schema validation (type + required props, lenient on items/additionalProperties) to RunJSON accept gate so wrong-shape-but-valid-JSON (bare array, missing refuted) is caught. On parse/validate failure, repair via an immediate tools-less natively-constrained finalization completion (run with maxIterations=0).
acceptance_criteria: RunJSON sends ResponseSchema only when StructuredOutput cap is set; validation routes missing-required to repair; repair completion is tools-less + schema-bearing; existing runjson_test cases stay green; new tests cover validation-triggered repair and the no-cap passthrough path.
close_reason: RunJSON now threads its schema natively into llm.Request.ResponseSchema through run/completeOnce/complete, gated at the agent layer on Capabilities().StructuredOutput (no-cap path sends no schema = legacy behavior). Added shallow root-shape validation (root type + required keys, lenient) to the accept gate so wrong-shape-but-valid-JSON routes to repair. Repair is now a single tools-less, schema-bearing completion (final=true drops tools) so capable adapters apply native grammar-constrained decoding. A budget-truncated run SKIPS the repair and returns its budget outcome, preserving bugbot-1q0's budget-stop classification + overshoot bound. Merged: overseer salvaged the cancelled fixer's complete+passing-but-uncommitted implementation and added the budget-skip guard that fixed a funnel TestRunFinder_BudgetStopNotParseFailure regression the agent's agent-only test scope missed.

title: Schema-constrained (grammar) output at every agent/phase boundary
description: North-star lever for weak models: force every agent->phase JSON boundary (finder candidates, refuter/arbiter refutation, reproducer plan, patch plan) to fit its declared JSON schema via provider-native structured output (grammar-constrained decoding) plus a harness-side shape guarantee. Today candidatesSchema/refutationSchema/planSchema/patchSchema are passed to RunJSON but only embedded in the prompt; the StructuredOutput capability is inert. Malformed/wrong-shape final answers are the dominant weak-model (MiniMax-M3) failure and burn repair round-trips.
acceptance_criteria: llm.Request carries ResponseSchema; OpenAI/openai-compatible/Google/Anthropic adapters honor it natively; RunJSON sets it and validates top-level shape, repairing under native constraint; openai-compatible opt-in via config; go test ./internal/llm/... ./internal/agent/... green; scripted eval gate still 1.0 precision.
close_reason: Integration capstone: gd3 (adapters) + w88 (RunJSON) already wire every boundary that calls RunJSON; jwd proves it end-to-end. New boundary integration tests assert each declared schema reaches the wire as ResponseSchema when the client has the StructuredOutput cap (finder=candidatesSchema, refuter+arbiter=refutationSchema, reproducer=planSchema, patch=patchSchema), is ABSENT when cap off (no-cap passthrough), and that the constrained repair is tools-less + schema-bearing at the finder/reproducer/patch boundaries. Surfaced the openai-compatible structured_output opt-in in the config template. Scripted eval gate verified still precision=1.000. Merged: overseer salvaged the cancelled fixer's boundary tests + fake-client extensions and added the template opt-in + eval-gate verification.

title: scan aborts with SQLITE_IOERR_SHORT_READ (disk I/O error 522)
description:
A `bugbot scan --repro` run aborted with a fatal `bugbot: disk I/O error (522)` after the hypothesize stage. The full scan (3m35s, 5.2M tokens) was lost.

## Observed

```
bugbot oneshot  commit a9dd455277ec  elapsed 3m35s
  (no active agents)
  stages: hypothesized=1 triaged=0 verified=0 killed=0
  spend:  in=5184647 out=34474 total=5219121 tokens (cached 4260880)
  budget: hard budget reached: skipped finder lens "api-contract-misuse" on 2 f…
  last:   stage done: hypothesize
bugbot: disk I/O error (522)
```

## What 522 is

`522 = SQLITE_IOERR (10) | (2<<8)` = **SQLITE_IOERR_SHORT_READ**. The driver is pure-Go `modernc.org/sqlite` v1.52.0 (`internal/store/store.go:5`). A short read means a `read()` returned fewer bytes than requested at the VFS layer.

CRITICAL: this is NOT `SQLITE_BUSY` (code 5). Lock contention is already handled by `busy_timeout(5000)` + WAL (`store.go:91-95`). A short read is a different failure class — file/VFS/checkpoint level, not lock starvation.

## Why the obvious "too many connections" theory is wrong

Concurrency is low: `DefaultMaxParallel = 4` (`funnel.go:58`). Surviving-candidate count (1) is irrelevant to connection count, but even the real concurrency driver is small: up to 4 finder agents + per-completion spend writes. 4 concurrent connections against a WAL DB is a normal, well-supported workload. So pool size alone does not explain a SHORT_READ.

## Write-volume context (matters for the leading hypothesis)

`spendRecorder.Record` issues a synchronous `RecordSpend` INSERT on EVERY LLM completion (`funnel.go:709`), from each agent goroutine, with the DB write OUTSIDE the totals mutex. 5.2M tokens implies hundreds of completions -> hundreds of spend-ledger INSERTs in ~3.5 min -> a large, fast-growing WAL. Plus `AddAgentUnit` per unit and `UpsertFinding` per survivor.

## Timing

The error fired at/after `stage done: hypothesize`, i.e. the hypothesize -> triage transition (triaged=0). At that boundary the triage path does store reads while finder goroutines may still be flushing spend/agent_unit writes.

## --repro is incidental

No finding reached verify (`verified=0`), so the repro stage never ran on a finding. The fault is in the finder/triage DB path, not the reproducer. `--repro` likely just lengthened the run / raised spend volume.
design:
# Leading hypothesis

WAL autocheckpoint racing a concurrent read. The heavy spend-ledger write volume pushes the WAL past SQLite's default autocheckpoint threshold (1000 pages); a checkpoint runs (truncating/resetting the WAL and writing back to the main db) concurrently with a read on another pooled connection during the hypothesize->triage transition. modernc.org/sqlite's VFS surfaces a read that lands past the just-shrunk file size as SQLITE_IOERR_SHORT_READ. Concurrency of 4 is sufficient because this is a checkpoint-vs-read race, not lock saturation, and the run generated unusually high write volume.

This fits every observation: low concurrency, huge write volume, failure at a stage boundary where reads and writes overlap, and a SHORT_READ (not BUSY).

## Alternative hypotheses (must be ruled in/out, not assumed)

1. Environmental: state DB on overlay/NFS/tmpfs or a near-full / flaky disk. SHORT_READ is literally an OS read returning short; a misbehaving FS produces it directly. Check `cfg.Storage.Path` and its filesystem in the failing environment.
2. Pre-existing truncation/corruption: a prior run killed abruptly (OOM under 5.2M-token contexts, or hard-budget abort mid-write) left the db/WAL truncated; this run hit the bad page. Distinguishable because it would recur on reopen / `PRAGMA quick_check`.
3. A genuine modernc.org/sqlite concurrency/WAL VFS bug independent of checkpointing.

# Investigation + fix plan

## 1. Diagnostics FIRST (so the next occurrence is self-explaining)
- Wrap every store error with the DB path AND the SQLite extended result code so SHORT_READ (522) is distinguishable from BUSY (5) / FULL (13) in logs. modernc exposes the code via its `*sqlite.Error` type (`.Code()`); add a helper in `internal/store` that annotates returned errors.
- On this error class, run `PRAGMA quick_check` (or `integrity_check`) and log the result, and log whether reopening the db succeeds. Recurrence on reopen => corruption (hyp 2); clean on reopen => transient race (hyp 1/3).

## 2. Mitigation candidate (cheap; verify, do not assume it cures)
- Bound the writer pool: `db.SetMaxOpenConns(1)` in `store.Open` (currently UNSET -> unbounded). This serializes checkpoint vs reads and removes the concurrency variable entirely. For a low-throughput control-plane DB this is acceptable. If read latency matters, use a 1-writer + N-reader split instead. Mark as mitigation pending root-cause confirmation.
- Consider WAL checkpoint tuning: explicit `wal_autocheckpoint` value, or `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` at safe stage boundaries instead of mid-flight autocheckpoints.

## 3. Reduce write amplification (addresses the trigger regardless of root cause)
- Batch spend-ledger inserts: buffer per-completion spend and flush once per agent-run (or on a short timer) instead of one INSERT per completion (`funnel.go:709`). Shrinks WAL growth and checkpoint frequency. Must keep in-memory budget totals authoritative and reconcile the persisted ledger with totals.

## 4. Reproduction harness (gates the fix)
- A store stress test: N goroutines (N>=4) hammering `RecordSpend` + interleaved reads against a real on-disk WAL store with a forced-low `wal_autocheckpoint`, asserting no 522. If it cannot be reproduced, document that and lean on the environmental determination from step 1.

## Files in scope
- `internal/store/store.go` (Open: pool config, pragmas, error wrapping)
- `internal/store/spend.go` (RecordSpend; optional batching)
- `internal/funnel/funnel.go` (spendRecorder.Record call site, funnel.go:709)
- New: `internal/store` stress test + error-annotation helper
acceptance_criteria: 1) Root cause is identified with evidence and classified as one of: checkpoint/VFS race, environmental (filesystem/disk), or pre-existing corruption -- not assumed. 2) Store errors are wrapped with the db path AND the SQLite extended result code, so a future 522 is distinguishable from BUSY(5)/FULL(13) in logs (regression-tested via a forced sqlite error or unit test on the annotation helper). 3) On the IOERR class, the store runs and logs PRAGMA quick_check and a reopen attempt so transient-vs-corruption is decidable from logs. 4) Either a stress test reproduces the 522 (>=4 concurrent RecordSpend + reads on a real WAL store with low wal_autocheckpoint) and then passes after the fix, OR there is a documented determination that the cause is environmental with the supporting evidence. 5) A scan under the original conditions (heavy spend volume, MaxParallel=4, --repro) completes without 522, confirmed by re-run or the stress test. 6) If SetMaxOpenConns/pool bounding is adopted, a test asserts the pool config; if spend batching is adopted, persisted ledger rows still reconcile with in-memory budget totals.
notes: Confirmed facts from investigation: driver is modernc.org/sqlite v1.52.0 (store.go:5); Open sets WAL/foreign_keys/busy_timeout(5000)/synchronous(NORMAL)/_txlock=immediate but NO SetMaxOpenConns -> unbounded pool (store.go:91-95). 522 = SQLITE_IOERR(10)|(2<<8) = SQLITE_IOERR_SHORT_READ, NOT BUSY(5) -- so this is a VFS/file-level short read, not lock contention (already covered by busy_timeout+WAL). Concurrency is only DefaultMaxParallel=4 (funnel.go:58), so 'too many connections' is the WRONG framing; the real trigger is write VOLUME: spendRecorder.Record does a synchronous RecordSpend INSERT per LLM completion outside the totals mutex (funnel.go:709), and 5.2M tokens => hundreds of INSERTs => large fast-growing WAL crossing autocheckpoint mid-run. Failure fired at hypothesize->triage boundary (hypothesized=1 triaged=0). --repro is incidental: verified=0 so repro stage never ran. Do diagnostics BEFORE picking a fix; pool-bounding is a mitigation, not a confirmed cure.
close_reason: Hardened store against SQLITE_IOERR_SHORT_READ(522). Mitigation: SetMaxOpenConns(1) removes the checkpoint-vs-read concurrency variable. Diagnostics: errors annotated with db path + extended SQLite result code (IsIOErr/IsIOErrShortRead/SQLiteCode predicates); Diagnose() runs quick_check + separate-handle reopen and is WIRED into cli/scan.go's IOERR error path (logs transient-race vs corruption). Stress regression guard (>=6 writers/4 readers, low wal_autocheckpoint) stays green on the bounded pool; could not deterministically reproduce 522 in 3s on SSD tmpdir -> documented environmental determination (acceptance #4 alt). Merged after one overseer rework (Diagnose wiring + de-mutation).

title: Instrument finder no-parseable-output failures with a postmortem artifact (raw output, error class, finish reason, usage)
description: The reasoning-model reliability cluster (bugbot-ci0/3b0/8xp/1q0) all share one root problem: when a finder produces no parseable output, the failure path DISCARDS the evidence needed to diagnose it. At internal/funnel/hypothesize.go:625, runFinderWithPrompt returns finderParseFailed and drops BOTH the err from RunJSON (which carries the classified provider error: 429 + Retry-After via openai.go normalizeErr, or 'empty model output') AND outcome.FinalText (the raw unparseable text). The operator only ever sees 'finder lens X produced no parseable output ... LOST' (hypothesize.go:450). We have been reverse-engineering root causes from that single symptom string. Instrument the failure path to persist a postmortem artifact per no-parse failure so future occurrences are diagnosable from real data instead of hypothesis. Capture: raw FinalText (or its head + length + emptiness flag + whether it contained a <think> span or looked like a separate-reasoning-field drop), the err string/classification (rate-limit vs parse vs empty), finish/truncation reason, and token usage from outcome. This is doable now with NO provider key — it records whatever the next real failing run produces. Directly resolves 8xp's core complaint (rate-limit reason discarded), supplies bugbot-3b0 the real M2.7 response shape to confirm the ExtraFields/reasoning_content hypothesis, and gives bugbot-1q0 the budget/finalization evidence it asks for. NOTE: stripThinkBlocks already exists in agent/runjson.go, so inline <think> is already handled — pointing the finger at the separate-field (3b0) or rate-limit (8xp) shapes, which this instrumentation will confirm.
acceptance_criteria: Every finder no-parse failure (finderParseFailed AND finderBudgetStopped) records a postmortem artifact carrying: raw model output (head-capped, with empty + had-think + length flags), the underlying err string and a coarse classification (rate-limited / empty-output / unparseable / budget-stop), truncation/finish reason, and in/out token usage. Stored where an operator can retrieve it post-run (agent_units row field or a sibling table; pick and justify in --design) and surfaced in the reliability warning enough to point at the cause. Best-effort: recording failure never aborts the scan. err is no longer silently dropped at hypothesize.go:625. Covered by a test that feeds a finder error (rate-limit-shaped and empty-output-shaped) and asserts the artifact captures the classification + raw output. go build/vet/test and gofmt clean. Independently reviewed and merged.
close_reason: Implemented & merged (PR #69, commit 2747944). finderPostmortem captured on every no-parse failure (finderParseFailed + finderBudgetStopped): coarse class (rate-limited/empty-output/unparseable/budget-stop via errors.Is on llm.ErrRateLimited), underlying err string (no longer dropped), head-capped raw output with empty/had-think/len flags, truncation reason, token usage. Stored in existing agent_units.Detail column (no migration; retrievable via 'report units' CLI) — chosen over a new table because grain is already one row per unit. Class surfaced in the operator reliability warning. Best-effort recording never aborts the scan. Reviewed (skeptical: caught + fixed a dead-code golangci-lint failure on the orphaned recordFinderUnitWithTime wrapper before merge). Full CI green. Unblocks bugbot-3b0 and bugbot-8xp.

title: High concurrency (16) exhausts finder retries on provider rate-limits -> false 'no parseable output' reliability failure
description: With --concurrency 16 against MiniMax (openai-compatible), finder agents complete in 1-2s (vs 90s+ normal) producing zero candidates, and the scan reports the SCAN RELIABILITY WARNING. Disappears at low concurrency. Mechanism: 16 simultaneous finder LLM requests exceed the provider's rate limit; each finder's retry budget (RetryConfig.MaxAttempts=4) is exhausted FAST on instant 429/connection errors (not slow stalls), RunJSON returns an error, and internal/funnel/hypothesize.go runFinderWithPrompt classifies any non-ctx, non-budget error as finderParseFailed (hypothesize.go ~line 31 in the 595-660 block) = 'findings LOST' reliability failure. NOT a hang (the per-attempt timeout fix from bugbot-ci0 is in place and working) and NOT caused by that fix (its diff is purely retry.go). NOT the reasoning-model token issue (that is concurrency-independent). Confirmed concurrency-dependent by the operator: reproduces at 16, gone at lower concurrency. Unconfirmed live (no MINIMAX_API_KEY in agent env) — operator observed the behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A high-concurrency run against a rate-limiting provider does NOT surface as a reliability failure with lost findings. Options to evaluate: (a) global concurrency cap / client-side rate limiter for the openai-compatible provider; (b) distinguish 'retries exhausted on rate-limit' from 'genuine parse failure' so a rate-limited finder is a retryable/degraded state, not finderParseFailed (lost); (c) honor Retry-After more aggressively / increase MaxAttempts under contention; (d) document a safe max --concurrency per provider. Pick and justify; add a test for the rate-limit-exhaustion classification path.
notes: Operator insight: MiniMax M2.7 is the FIRST thinking model run through bugbot. A thinking model holds each request open far longer and burns far more tokens/request than prior non-thinking models, so at --concurrency 16 it likely trips the provider's TOKEN-per-minute (TPM) limit, not just request-per-minute (RPM). Explains why this surfaces now and not on earlier models at the same concurrency, and implies the safe concurrency ceiling for a thinking model is much lower (token throughput, not request count, is the binding constraint; a big repo could trip it even at concurrency 4-8). Evidence: openai.go normalizeErr DOES preserve the 429 message + Retry-After into APIError, but runFinderWithPrompt collapses retries-exhausted into finderParseFailed and DISCARDS that detail — operator only sees 'no parseable output', never the rate-limit reason. Highest-value fix: stop classifying rate-limit-exhaustion as finderParseFailed (lost); surface a distinct degraded/retryable state carrying provider message + Retry-After. Then: client-side throttle/concurrency cap keyed to provider, ideally TPM-aware; honor Retry-After across the whole finder fleet. Likely RELATED to bugbot-1q0 (empty finder output under budget pressure / reliability regression at scale) — check whether that is the same swallowed-degradation path.
close_reason: Picked acceptance option (b): rate-limit exhaustion is now classified as a distinct finderRateLimited status (errors.Is(err, llm.ErrRateLimited) in runFinderWithPrompt), counted on its own Stats.FinderRateLimited axis, EXCLUDED from FinderFailures so it no longer trips the SCAN RELIABILITY WARNING / 'findings LOST' path. FinderReliable()/MostFindersFailed() stay keyed on FinderFailures only (rate-limited-only run = reliable-but-coverage-incomplete). Surfaced in cli/scan.go printResult. Retry-After already honored by retry client; no new limiter added. Test TestRunFinder_RateLimitNotParseFailure proves the classification; existing parse-failure/budget-stop tests unchanged. Merged to main (--no-ff). go build/vet/test + gofmt clean across all 16 packages.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: UNBLOCKED by bugbot-hp4 (merged, commit 2747944). Rate-limit exhaustion is now classified as class=rate-limited in the postmortem (errors.Is on llm.ErrRateLimited) and surfaced in the reliability warning — so the discarded-429 complaint is addressed for visibility. Remaining 8xp scope: the CLASSIFICATION-as-finderParseFailed-LOST decision and the throttle/Retry-After-honoring fix. A high-concurrency run will now show class=rate-limited in 'report units' to confirm the mechanism before picking fix (a)-(d).

title: UpsertFinding wipes ReproPath and demotes tier on re-scan: every sweep destroys T1 repro artifacts
description: Pre-existing bug, reproduced by the bugbot-591 review (2026-06-12) with a throwaway test: after a finding is promoted (Tier 1 + repro_path set), a subsequent UpsertFinding of the same fingerprint — exactly what runVerifyAndPersist builds on the next sweep: a Tier-2 Finding with empty ReproPath — UNCONDITIONALLY overwrites repro_path='' and tier=2 (internal/store/findings.go UPDATE sets both unconditionally, ~lines 196-208). Observed: 'AFTER PROMOTE: tier=1 repro=/artifacts/...' then 'AFTER RESCAN: tier=2 repro=empty'. The very next sweep of the same file destroys the repro artifact and T1 promotion the pipeline paid sandbox time to produce — and the repro backlog then re-attempts it, burning the cost again on every sweep cycle. Severity amplified now that bugbot-591 produces artifacts in-run: they get wiped sooner. Fix direction: UpsertFinding's conflict UPDATE must PRESERVE promotion state — never lower tier from 1, never clear non-empty repro_path/needs_human with incoming empty values (CASE expressions like filestate.go's hash preservation); re-verification freshness fields (reasoning, anchoring, updated_at) still update.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Test reproducing the review scenario: promote, re-upsert same fingerprint as fresh T2, tier stays 1 and repro_path preserved while reasoning/anchor update. (2) Demotion remains possible through EXPLICIT paths (status changes, MarkFixed) — only the implicit re-scan upsert is guarded. (3) Repro backlog no longer re-attempts promoted findings after a sweep. (4) Gates clean.
close_reason: Fixed & merged (PR #71, commit 8553740). UpsertFinding's implicit re-scan update now preserves promotion: tier=MIN(stored,incoming) (lower=stronger), non-empty stored repro_path never cleared by empty incoming, needs_human sticky once set by patch-prover. Freshness fields still update. Enforced in Go (returned struct accurate for downstream callers) AND SQL CASE (atomic vs concurrent writer between SELECT/UPDATE). Confirmed explicit demotion paths (UpdateStatus/MarkFixed) bypass this branch and are unaffected. Tests: promote-then-rescan preservation, explicit-demotion-still-works, tier/repro edge cases. Gates + CI green.

title: Verifier passed a false positive with 3/3 refuter consensus (parseSARIF multi-run cap) — add execute-the-claim verification
description: Dogfood scan (commit 1d5f34b, MiniMax-M3) verified 'parseSARIF cap is bypassed by multi-run SARIF documents' (GH #64) and all three refuter seats (reachability/semantics/guards) failed to refute. The finding is FALSE: the cap guard at internal/analyzer/analyzer.go:567 checks global len(out) at the top of every inner-loop iteration, so once 200 results accumulate, every subsequent run's loop breaks on its first iteration. Empirically refuted with a 5-run x 200-result document → exactly 200 results. One refuter even wrote the correct semantics mid-trace ('per-run nothing is appended beyond the cap') then concluded the opposite. Root cause: all refuters use the same model and the same static re-reading method — consensus is correlated, not independent. For pure-function claims (parser caps, boundary math), static re-reading is the weakest verification available while a 20-line unit test refutes instantly.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Verifier pipeline detects findings whose claim is about a pure/deterministic function and routes at least one seat to write+execute a falsification test in the sandbox instead of re-reading code. (2) The dogfood parseSARIF finding, replayed, gets killed by that seat. (3) Sandbox exec policy supports this (currently sandbox_min_severity=high + max_execs=2 meant a medium FP never touched the sandbox).
notes: GH issue #64 closed as not-a-bug with refutation. Related: bugbot-jou (debate verification), bugbot-8a4 (FP memory). Optional clarity refactor: move the cap check to the outer loop / labeled break so the code reads as the global cap it is.
close_reason: Added execute-the-claim verification. (1) isExecutableClaim(Candidate) heuristic (internal/funnel/claim_class.go) classifies deterministic/pure-function claims via curated det-marker set minus env/IO/concurrency exclusions (precision-first; false-negatives safe, false-positives only offer the tool). (2) buildSandboxTool bypasses the sandbox_min_severity floor for executable claims so a medium FP reaches the sandbox (the gating that hid the parseSARIF FP); default config unchanged. (3) New executorSeat assigned to panel position 0 via seatForCandidate when n>=2 && hasSandbox && isExecutableClaim; instructs WRITE+RUN a falsification test instead of correlated static re-reads. n<=1 byte-identity and reachability/semantics/guards diversity preserved. Replay test TestReplay_ParseSARIFCap_RefutedByExecutor drives f.Sweep end-to-end: the medium parseSARIF-cap FP is killed (0 findings survive, Stats.Killed>=1, sandbox exec'd). Merged to main (--no-ff); full gates clean.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: GH #64 closed as not-planned with refutation comment (user-authorized 2026-06-12). The premature note in this bead is now accurate.

title: Strategy axis for finder units: injectable search strategies (sweep-wide, contract-trace-deep) orthogonal to lens
description: Investigation outcome (2026-06-11, follows the bugbot-ig7 post-mortem): depth search is NOT a tool gap — finders already run multi-turn loops with repo-wide grep and LSP navigation (find_references/find_definition/read_symbol, internal/funnel/helpers.go:44-71) and the system prompt licenses reading beyond the chunk (prompt.go:25). The gap is the OBJECTIVE: finderTask says 'audit these target files' (prompt.go:167-172), so discovery happens by staring at the chunk and traversal is licensed only to CONFIRM an already-formed suspicion; cross-file joins like ig7 never form the suspicion. Tight read caps + 20-turn cap + precision-first framing further penalize exploratory wandering. Proposal: add a strategy axis orthogonal to lens — finder unit becomes (lens x strategy x seed) where current behavior is named 'sweep-wide'. First new strategy 'contract-trace-deep': same chunk as SEED, but the objective is (1) enumerate contracts declared in seed files that have a far end — validated config/options fields, sentinel-documented parameters ('0 = unlimited'), exported invariants — then (2) chase each via find_references to every consumption/documentation site, hunting belief asymmetries between what is enforced here and assumed there. Budget shape differs from wide (many small navigation reads vs few full-file reads) and fits the existing tightened read caps. Strategies are data like lens manifestations (mi5.2 pattern), yield-ranked alongside lenses so existing degradation economics carry over; mi5.10 unit records gain a strategy column so (lens x strategy) yield is learnable. Ecosystem-general: traversal is LSP/grep-driven, per-language only via existing manifestation tables. Held-out eval: bugbot-ig7 stays unfixed in-tree; this capability finding it organically (seeded from either side — config.go or daemon.go, both reach the join via references on the budget fields) is the acceptance evidence. Note traversal works from both directions, which doubles seeding luck.
design: Why this over alternatives as next step: mi5.6 seam units need a deterministic enumerator per seam type (engineering per class); mi5.11 is class-specific and overfits the ig7 benchmark. The strategy axis buys generality by letting the LLM do both contract enumeration and traversal with tools it already has — one mechanism covers config knobs, option structs, sentinel params, and contracts nobody enumerated. Deep-vs-wide should NOT be mere persona flavor over the same objective: a 'go deep' instruction atop 'audit these files' still anchors discovery to the chunk. The strategy must replace the task objective and the budget-spend guidance. Risks: (a) deep agents wander — cap navigation fan-out in the prompt (e.g. trace at most N contracts, prefer validated/documented ones), measure via mi5.10; (b) duplicate findings across strategies — existing fingerprint dedup should absorb; (c) yield table gains a dimension — seed hand-estimates, let mi5.10 data correct them.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Finder unit carries a strategy field; existing behavior preserved verbatim as 'sweep-wide' (no eval regression on current fixtures). (2) 'contract-trace-deep' strategy implemented as data + task template, no new tools. (3) Per-strategy iteration/token tuning supported (deep may need a different turn cap). (4) mi5.10-style unit records include strategy (coordinate with mi5.10 if unmerged). (5) HELD-OUT TEST: a daemon run over bugbot's own repo surfaces bugbot-ig7 (config.go:584 vs daemon.go:154 contradiction) as a verified finding via this strategy, with mi5.11 NOT yet implemented. (6) Strategy participates in budget degradation ranking.
close_reason: Shipped (PR #45) and empirically validated as a capability: strategy axis works end-to-end, contract-trace-deep out-yielded api-contract-misuse@sweep-wide 2:0 across a full dogfood sweep and produced a verified documented-contract-vs-code finding (change.go:44) — the exact defect class it was built for. Acceptance #5 (organic ig7 discovery) FAILED: the deep units over config.go's and daemon.go's chunks ran clean with zero candidates at 110M tokens. Conclusion recorded on the bead: LLM traversal adds real recall for cross-file contract bugs but is not reliable enough to be the only defense for lexically-self-revealing contradictions — mi5.11's deterministic miner unblocks now, ig7 stays open as ITS acceptance fixture, and the deep-unit autopsy continues in the follow-up bead.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Implemented: PR #45 (CI green, squash-merge pending Dustin's review approval — branch protection requires human review, auto-merge disabled). Sonnet implementer + adversarial review round: no MUST-FIX; applied two SHOULD-FIXes before PR (degradation tiebreak switched from accidental-alphabetical to stability-over-lensesByYield-input-order, preserving exact pre-strategy equal-yield semantics against future yield retunes; byte-identity prompt tests now drive the extracted production helper composeFinderSystemPrompt instead of re-deriving the expression). Reviewer verified survivor-set equality across all 127 language mixes and that lens@strategy labels reach only presentation sinks (fingerprints/leads/eval routing get bare lens names). Side effect: the budget retune in degradation tests fixes the pre-existing flake (see flaky-test bead) — 20x hammer clean. ACCEPTANCE #5 (ig7 found organically by live daemon run) deliberately NOT claimed: needs a dogfood run after merge, with mi5.11 still unimplemented.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Merged: PR #45 squashed to main (e984bf9). Bead stays OPEN solely for acceptance #5: the held-out test — a live daemon run over bugbot's own repo surfacing bugbot-ig7 via contract-trace-deep, with mi5.11 still unimplemented. Everything else delivered and verified.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: HELD-OUT TEST CONCLUDED (2026-06-12 dogfood, scan over bugbot@1d5f34b, MiniMax-M3, 110M input tokens, full sweep, no degradation): ig7 NOT FOUND. The chunks containing internal/config/config.go and internal/daemon/daemon.go were each covered by api-contract-misuse@contract-trace-deep units that ran finderOK and emitted ZERO candidates — the deep strategy had the exact seeds and whiffed. Counter-signal in the same run: deep out-produced its own lens's wide mode 2 candidates to 0 (every api-contract candidate all run came from traversal; one — ChangedFiles omitting the --end-of-options defense 'claimed as package-wide convention', change.go:44 — IS a documented-promise-vs-code contradiction, ig7's exact class, found and verified via deep). So the capability is real but unreliable on the specific target. Acceptance #5 therefore FAILS as stated; closing with the capability shipped and the miss honestly recorded — the autopsy and prompt iteration continue in a follow-up bead, and per the standing decision bugbot-mi5.11 (deterministic contradiction miner) now UNBLOCKS: this defect class belongs to the deterministic tier.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: CORRECTION to the close reason (checked against findings provenance): the change.go:44 contradiction finding came from injection/input-validation@sweep-wide, NOT the deep strategy — and neither of contract-trace-deep's 2 candidates survived verification (both among the run's 5 kills). Honest final tally for the deep axis this run: candidate-level recall 2:0 over its lens's wide mode, VERIFIED yield 0. The capability evidence is therefore weaker than the close reason states: deep produces traversal-born hypotheses wide does not, but none proved real this run. The autopsy bead matters more, not less.

title: Sweep coverage starvation: heat ordering has no cursor, budget-truncated sweeps re-scan the same hot head and never reach the cold tail
description: Structural seeding flaw, independent of lens design, found during the bugbot-ig7 post-mortem. Sweeps heat-order targets by churn-weighted recency before chunking (internal/funnel/run.go:19-46, applyHeatOrder at :61), with alphabetical tiebreak — fully deterministic. There is no persisted coverage cursor: backlog rotation exists for findings (internal/daemon/backlog.go) but nothing rotates FILE coverage across sweep cycles. When the per-cycle token budget truncates a sweep (hypothesize.go:312 'stopped by budget ... partial coverage'), the unscanned cold tail is identical next cycle, so cold stable files can be starved indefinitely while hot files are re-scanned every cycle. This is the likely shape of the 45M-token miss: spend concentrated on the same hot head repeatedly. Cannot currently be confirmed from data because per-unit records are not persisted (bugbot-mi5.10). Fix direction: persist per-file (or per-chunk) last-scanned info and blend staleness into the heat score, or resume each sweep from the previous truncation point — so repeated truncated sweeps converge to full coverage instead of fixating. Ecosystem-general: the ordering operates on the language-agnostic target list.
acceptance_criteria: (1) A repeated budget-truncated sweep over an unchanged repo provably advances coverage: files unscanned in sweep N are prioritized in sweep N+1 (test with a small budget and a fixture repo, asserting union of scanned files grows to the full set). (2) Staleness/cursor state persists across daemon restarts. (3) Heat ordering still front-loads churned files on the first sweep after a commit. (4) Run stats report cumulative coverage fraction so starvation is visible.
notes: PR #47 open (CI pending), behind #45/#46 in the approval queue. Review round found and fixed: vacuous acceptance test (replaced with deterministic 5-file boundary-straddling-chunk design, vacuity-checked) and dead fps param (hash-changed files now join group 1; TouchScanCoverage records hashes so CLI-only sweeps keep a truthful group 2). Known minor: targeted scans deliberately do not stamp coverage (documented asymmetry, occasional redundant re-scan).
close_reason: Merged in PR #47 (35335c2). Shipped: truthful per-file coverage out of the finder stage (finderOK units only), TouchScanCoverage with content hashes (CLI-only sweeps keep a truthful group 2), RefreshContentHashes preserving last_scanned_at, and two-group anti-starvation ordering (never-scanned/hash-changed heat-ordered first, clean files stalest-first). Acceptance: (1) rotation proven by a deterministic 5-file test whose sweep-2 head chunk exists only under correct ordering — vacuity-checked by breaking the ordering; (2) cursor persists in sqlite across funnel instances (exercised by the rotation test reusing one store); (3) commit-churned files lead via hash-mismatch group-1 membership, heat-ordered; (4) per-sweep stats (SweepNeverScanned/SweepChangedSinceScan/CoveredFiles) make starvation visible — cumulative coverage FRACTION deferred to bugbot-mi5.10's unit table as noted in its comments. Known deliberate gap: targeted scans do not stamp coverage (documented asymmetry, costs occasional redundant re-scan).

title: Deterministic doc-contradiction miner: extract documented sentinel semantics, join against validation sites, post mismatch leads
description: Motivated by the bugbot-ig7 miss: a doc/validator contradiction ('0 = unlimited' comments vs 'must be > 0' validator) that 45M input tokens of LLM finder search missed but two greps find in seconds. The defect class is lexically self-revealing: codebases in every ecosystem document sentinel semantics in comments/docstrings using a small phrase family ('0 = unlimited', 'zero means', 'negative disables', 'empty = all', '-1 for no limit'), and validation/rejection sites use an equally small family ('must be > 0', 'must be positive', 'cannot be empty', raise/return-error on <= 0). A deterministic pre-pass — treesitter comment extraction per grammar (or plain regex to start) — mines (entity, documented-sentinel) pairs and (entity, rejected-range) pairs repo-wide, joins them on the entity (config key name, struct field, parameter), and posts contradictions as leads to the existing analyzer-seeding blackboard (bugbot-mi5.3 architecture: this is just a new registry entry that is not an external tool). The LLM's job becomes assessing a named contradiction with both sites quoted — cross-file, near-zero tokens to find, high precision to verify. Language-general by construction: comments and docstrings exist in every ecosystem; only the comment-extraction query is per-grammar. Go motivating instance: bugbot's own config.go:584 vs daemon.go:154.
design: Start regex-only over comment lines (cheap, no grammar work), upgrade to treesitter comment queries when precision demands it. Join key is the hard part: nearest declared identifier to the comment (field name, const, config key string literal). Prefer high precision over recall — every lead costs verifier tokens. Relationship to mi5.6: this is the lexical/deterministic cousin of contract-seam discovery; a config knob (definition + validator + consumers) is an intra-language seam — commented on bugbot-mi5.6.
acceptance_criteria: (1) A miner produces (entity, doc-claimed semantics, doc site) and (entity, enforced constraint, code site) records over a snapshot. (2) Joined contradictions are posted as leads with both file:line sites named, routed to an appropriate lens. (3) Eval fixture seeded with the ig7 pattern (0-documented-unlimited + >0 validator in different files) is caught. (4) bugbot's own repo run posts the ig7 lead. (5) False-positive rate sane on a clean fixture: no lead when doc and validator agree.
close_reason: Shipped deterministic doc-contradiction miner (internal/miner): pure-Go in-process pre-pass, always-on in scan + daemon poll/sweep. Real-repo run finds the ig7 per_cycle_tokens/per_day_tokens contradiction (config.go:585/588) as 2 true leads out of 204 doc-sites + 131 constraint-sites. Merged in PR #72.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Sequencing decision (2026-06-11): blocked behind bugbot-mi5.12. Dustin is keeping bugbot-ig7 unfixed as a held-out benchmark for the agentic traversal capability; this miner was designed from ig7's shape and would find it by construction — running it first contaminates the benchmark. Land after mi5.12's held-out test passes (or is conclusively abandoned), then ig7's pattern graduates into the eval fixture corpus.

title: Config validator rejects budgets=0 while daemon/funnel/CLI docs define 0 = unlimited
description: Contradiction between the config validation layer and every consumer's documented contract. internal/config/config.go:584-588 rejects per_cycle_tokens <= 0 and per_day_tokens <= 0, but internal/daemon/daemon.go:154-160, internal/daemon/cycle.go:238, internal/cli/worldstate.go:30, and internal/funnel/funnel.go:24 all document 0 as unlimited for these same knobs (worldstate.go even names the config key: 'budgets.per_day_tokens (0 = unlimited)'). A user who reads any consumer doc and sets 0 gets a startup validation error. Found manually by Dustin 2026-06-11; the daemon burned ~45M input tokens on internal/**/*.go without finding it (post-mortem on bugbot-mi5 comments). Decide the intended contract: either the validator should accept 0/negative as unlimited (consumers already implement this — budget.go pools treat <=0 as unlimited), or unlimited-via-config is deliberately disallowed and all consumer docs must stop advertising 0=unlimited. Note the PerCycleTokens > PerDayTokens cross-check at config.go:590 needs a guard if 0 becomes legal (0 day + positive cycle must not be rejected as cycle>day).
acceptance_criteria: (1) One contract chosen and recorded in the close reason. (2) Validator and all doc comments (daemon.go, cycle.go, worldstate.go, funnel.go, config template) agree with it. (3) A config_test case covers budgets=0 asserting the chosen behavior, including the cycle-vs-day cross-check interaction. (4) gates clean.
notes: DECISION (Dustin, 2026-06-11): do NOT fix yet — keep this bug live in the tree as a held-out benchmark until bugbot finds it organically. Benchmark-honesty caveat: bugbot-mi5.11 (lexical contradiction miner) was designed FROM this bug and would find it trivially — running it first is training on the test set. Sequencing: evaluate the general traversal capability (bugbot-mi5.12 strategy axis) against this bug first; mi5.11 lands after, and ig7's pattern then moves into the eval fixture corpus as a permanent regression case. Only after bugbot finds it does the fix decision in the description get made.
close_reason: Contract chosen: budgets.per_cycle_tokens/per_day_tokens treat <=0 as UNLIMITED, matching every consumer; validator (config.go) drops the >0 rejections and only applies the cycle>day cross-check when BOTH are positive; doc comments in daemon.go/cycle.go/funnel.go + config template aligned; config_test covers budgets=0 and the cycle-vs-day interactions. Clean cutover also updated internal/miner TestSeed_RealBugbotRepo (its real-repo fixture WAS the live ig7 contradiction; positive miner coverage preserved by the synthetic testdata/ig7_contradiction fixture). NOTE the stale 'held-out benchmark, do not fix' note in the bead was lifted per user. Merged after one overseer rework (miner test).

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Organic held-out test concluded: NOT found by the full-sweep dogfood run (110M tokens, deep units covered both relevant chunks finderOK, zero candidates — measured via agent_units, not inferred). Per the standing sequencing decision, bugbot-mi5.11 is now unblocked and this bead becomes ITS acceptance fixture: the contradiction miner must surface this bug as a lead and the funnel must verify it. The fix decision still waits until bugbot finds it.

title: Repro/patch outcome interpretation: require positive per-ecosystem test-ran evidence, never infer 'demonstrated' from bare non-zero exit
description: GENERAL INTENT: for ANY target ecosystem, interpret() and patchVerdict() must distinguish three outcomes of a repro/patch run: (a) the test RAN and FAILED — a demonstration; (b) the test ran and passed; (c) the test NEVER RAN (toolchain refusal, build failure, environment failure) — which proves nothing in either direction. The current design infers (a) from any non-zero exit not matching Go-only marker blacklists (buildFailureMarkers/environmentFailureMarkers in interpret.go) — brittle, already burned twice ('failed to initialize build cache', now 'go: -race requires cgo'), and meaningless for Python/JS/Rust/C++ repos where none of the markers apply: every non-Go ecosystem currently inherits the worst behavior (any non-zero exit = T1 evidence). DESIGN DIRECTION: move interpretation into the per-ecosystem registry (same table pattern as sandbox/deps.go and the planned mi5.2 manifestation tables). Each ecosystem entry carries: positive ran-markers (Go: '--- FAIL'/'FAIL\t'/'panic:'/'WARNING: DATA RACE'; pytest: 'FAILED'/'AssertionError'/'short test summary'; jest/vitest, cargo test, ctest analogues), toolchain-error shapes (Go: 'go: ' prefix; pip/npm/cargo resolution errors), and env-failure markers. Rule: a non-zero exit WITHOUT ran-evidence is classified env/build failure in every ecosystem — positive evidence required, blacklists only refine the failure subtype. patchVerdict() shares the same classification so the prover reports 'environment cannot run repro' instead of 'possibly misdiagnosed'. MOTIVATING INSTANCE (Go): finding 0000019eb867 — go test -race exited 2 with 'go: -race requires cgo' before compiling; interpret() minted T1 from it; patch-prover then discredited a likely-correct fix with the same blind spot.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Outcome interpretation is table-driven per ecosystem; Go is the first entry; a non-zero exit lacking that ecosystem's ran-evidence is NEVER a demonstration. (2) The cgo refusal transcript (exit 2, single 'go:' line) yields no T1 — regression test. (3) patchVerdict shares the classification and the prover's report distinguishes env-failure from fix-rejected. (4) Adding a new ecosystem's interpretation requires only a table entry plus output fixtures (verified by adding a second ecosystem, e.g. pytest, with fixture transcripts). (5) Existing recorded-corpus verdicts unchanged for Go.
close_reason: interpret() and patchVerdict() now require POSITIVE per-ecosystem ran-evidence; a non-zero exit without it is NEVER a demonstration. New table-driven ecosystem.go (Go first entry, pytest second); interpret(res,cmd) detects ecosystem from the repro command. cgo-refusal regression test (exit 2 'go: -race requires cgo' -> not demonstrated); Go recorded-corpus verdicts unchanged (eval suite green); patchVerdict distinguishes env-failure from fix-rejected. Merged to main.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: LIVE EVIDENCE (.bugbot/state.db, 2026-06-17): hazard is currently LATENT but real — 0 of 4 T2 findings reached a successful repro (reproducer agent_units: 1 exhausted, 1 infra_error, 0 reproduced), so interpret()'s default 'non-zero exit => demonstrated' branch never fired here. It WILL fire once finder yield improves. Dogfood image debian:stable-slim has no Go toolchain, so most repro plans exit 127 -> environment_error (correctly NOT demonstrated); but any non-127 non-zero exit (e.g. the 'go: -race requires cgo' exit-2 case in this bead) would be a false T1. Umbrella: bugbot-3lt.

title: verify.go: shared tools slice race — three-index cap fix from hypothesize.go missing in verify stage
description: Dogfood finding 0000019eb8670be95749fc3f6fed3a01 (fingerprint d971ff23..., commit b3c336b). verify.go:117 does candTools := tools, then :121 appends sbTool inside each per-candidate goroutine. tools comes from f.readOnlyTools (helpers.go: append of a cap-5 literal grown by nav.Tools() to len=9 cap=10), so spare capacity exists and every parallel goroutine's append writes the SAME backing-array slot at index len(tools) — refuters can get the wrong candidate's sandbox tool, a torn interface value, or none. This is the exact race already fixed in hypothesize.go:184 with baseTools[:len(baseTools):len(baseTools)] and covered there by TestVerify-style race test; verify.go never got the fix. The refuter trace on the finding is correct and thorough — the bug is real.
acceptance_criteria: (1) verify.go forces a fresh backing array per goroutine (three-index slice or per-goroutine copy) before appending sbTool. (2) A -race test mirroring TestHypothesize_MultiLens_NoRace covers the verify stage and fails without the fix. (3) go test -race ./internal/funnel/ clean. (4) Audit for any OTHER append-on-shared-slice in per-goroutine paths (triage, repro) while in there.
close_reason: RESOLVED as already-fixed + regression-guarded (PR #70, commit 62c14ab). The shared-slice race was incidentally fixed by the streaming-funnel refactor (#66/#68): readOnlyTools is now called INSIDE runVerifyAndPersist (verify_stream.go:103), so each per-candidate goroutine owns a freshly-allocated tools slice and the sbTool append at :114 is private — no shared backing array. Verified by revert-experiment (reintroducing a shared slice trips DATA RACE under -race). Did NOT add a spurious three-index fix to safe code; instead added TestVerify_MultiCandidate_NoRace (sandbox-enabled, concurrent candidates) to lock the invariant and fail if a future edit hoists readOnlyTools back outside the goroutine. Audited triage/repro/arbiter — no other shared-slice appends. go test -race clean.

title: Analyzer-seeding registry: run per-ecosystem static analyzers in sandbox, post SARIF hits as leads
description: Deterministic tools become recall sources feeding the existing leads table; LLM agents shift from finding to assessing reachability/severity, which is what verify is good at. Build a per-ecosystem analyzer registry mirroring sandbox/deps.go's table: detect (repo markers) -> invoke (in the existing sandbox, network none) -> parse -> AddLead. Key simplification: modern analyzers (clang-tidy, ruff, eslint, staticcheck, clippy) emit SARIF, which bugbot already speaks on the OUTPUT side — parse SARIF as the single ingestion format so per-tool work shrinks to a detection+invocation table row. Map SARIF rule categories to target lenses (e.g. a staticcheck SA2xxx concurrency hit posts to the concurrency lens). Analyzer absent in the sandbox image = skip silently; analyzers are recall aids, never blockers.
design: Settled design (orchestrator): new package internal/analyzer with a registry mirroring sandbox/deps.go's ecosystem table: rows {name, detect(repoDir) via root markers, argv producing SARIF on stdout or a file, ruleLens mapping, timeout}. v1 rows: staticcheck (-f sarif; chosen over go vet which cannot emit SARIF) and ruff (check --output-format=sarif). Single minimal SARIF ingestion parser (runs[].results[]: ruleId, message.text, locations[0].physicalLocation uri+startLine) — the only ingestion path, symmetric with report's SARIF output. Rule→lens mapping is per-analyzer data (prefix tables, e.g. staticcheck SA2xxx→concurrency) with a per-analyzer fallback lens. Invocation: sandbox.Exec, network none, per-analyzer timeout (default 5m); analyzer binary absent in image (exit 125/126/127 or command-not-found) → silent skip with a note; nonzero exit WITH parseable SARIF is normal (findings); nonzero without SARIF → note, never scan failure. Hits land via store.AddLead with the poster identifying the tool ('analyzer:staticcheck'), target_lens set by the mapping — consumed by the matching lens's NEXT finder run through the existing leads path (no same-run injection; keeps funnel untouched). Wiring: a seed step in the scan/daemon path before the funnel run, always-on with graceful-skip semantics (no new config in v1). Runtime counted in run stats via the existing progress/notes mechanism.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Registry with at least go vet (or staticcheck) plus one non-Go analyzer (e.g. ruff) working end-to-end. (2) Single SARIF parser is the only ingestion path. (3) Hits land as leads with poster identifying the tool, consumed by the matching lens's next finder run. (4) Missing analyzer or non-zero analyzer exit degrades gracefully with a run note, never fails the scan. (5) Analyzer runtime is bounded (timeout) and counted in run stats.
close_reason: Merged (PR head bugbot-mi5-3-analyzer-seeding, content verified on main post-merge: internal/analyzer package + scan/daemon seed wiring). Decisions: registry is deps.go-shaped data ({name, detect, cmd, ruleLens, timeout}); staticcheck -f sarif chosen over go vet (vet cannot emit SARIF — SARIF symmetry was the point); ruff as the non-Go row. One minimal SARIF parser is the sole ingestion path (ruleId, message.text, first location uri+startLine; absent fields tolerated and counted; 200-results/analyzer cap so a pathological run cannot flood leads). Rule→lens prefix tables skip style families outright (staticcheck S1*/ST1*/QF*, ruff pycodestyle E*/W*, isort/docstyle/naming) — leads stay signal-dense; SA2*→concurrency, ruff S* (bandit)→injection, B023→concurrency (loop-capture analogue), defaults→nil-safety/error-handling. Leads post with PosterLens='analyzer:<name>' — attribution only; consumption is keyed by target_lens via the existing PendingLeads path, so the funnel needed zero changes and Lead needed no migration (poster_lens existed since migration 004). Never-block semantics throughout; only store errors propagate. Wiring: change-gated daemon polls + sweeps via a Deps.SeedAnalyzers hook (keeps daemon free of analyzer/sandbox imports), and bugbot scan pre-funnel; no runtime → silent skip; no config knob in v1. Real-container integration test (ghcr.io/astral-sh/ruff on a pickle fixture → injection lead) surfaced and fixed two empirical issues: the ruff image invokes via ENTRYPOINT (cmd is args-only) and SARIF file:///workspace/ URIs need prefix-stripping to repo-relative paths. Follow-on candidates for the epic: clang-tidy/clippy/eslint rows are now single-table additions.

title: Lens generalization: universal defect ontology + per-language manifestation tables
description: Keystone for language generality. Split each lens into (a) a universal core stating the failure mode ('acquired resources must be released on every path') and (b) a manifestation table keyed by ingest.Language supplying concrete idioms (Go: defers-in-loops, leaked goroutines; C++: missing RAII, new without delete; Python: handles outside with-blocks, un-awaited coroutines). Compose into the prompt exactly as persona already is — language-conditioned clause + fixed precision contract. Manifestations are DATA in a registry shaped like sandbox/deps.go's ecosystem table. Yield becomes a (lens x language-profile) table: current rankings explicitly encode Go (lens.go comment) and drive budget degradation, so non-Go repos currently shed the wrong lenses under budget pressure. Includes language-affinity chunking: group files by language before chunking so each finder unit gets one coherent manifestation block. Long-term follow-on (not this bead): learn per-language yields from eval recorded corpora.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Finder prompts on a Python-only fixture contain zero Go idioms, and vice versa. (2) Manifestation tables are data, addable without touching prompt-composition code. (3) Degradation order is derived from the (lens x language) yield table per repo profile. (4) Chunks are language-homogeneous where possible; mixed chunks get the union manifestation set. (5) Existing Go eval results do not regress. (6) Lens.Name stability preserved (dedup fingerprints unaffected).
close_reason: Merged (PR head bugbot-mi5-2-lens-generalization; content verified on main: lens_manifestations.go + Core field in lens.go). The keystone: every lens = universal Core (language-free failure mode) + per-language manifestation rows as pure data (deps.go-shaped registry; adding a language/lens touches only the table); prompts compose Core + per-chunk-language manifestation blocks (union for mixed, omission for row-less, Core-only first-class). Go content extracted from the original Specializations with clause-by-clause preservation verified in adversarial review (no drops, no weakening — Go-only prompts carry the same hunting guidance). Manifestations authored rich for Python and C++ (deployment targets), solid for JS/TS, lean for Rust/C; reviewer verified factual accuracy per language. Yields: (lens x language) table; Go column = exact historical numbers (Go-repo degradation byte-identical: {nil-safety, concurrency}); non-Go columns reasoned priors documented as pending learned yields (epic follow-on) — Python keeps {nil-safety, injection} under degradation, C++ {boundary, nil-safety}, C {boundary, resource-leaks}. Degradation survivors = language-ranked order FILTERED to lenses that emitted units this run (composes with diff-intent: competes on commit runs at anyLanguage:95, never steals a sweep slot — the review's C5 hazard, resolved in the reconciliation commit). Language-affinity chunking groups targets by DetectLanguage with unit-count ceiling unchanged (<=ceil(n/size), 2000-trial verified) and chunks re-sorted by hottest-member input rank so churn-heat (bugbot-sro) survives grouping (review finding 1, fixed not documented-away). Implementer was the full-strength model per the judgment-heavy-work rule — manifestation prose is finder-prompt content driving recall. Unblocks mi5.5 and mi5.6. Follow-on recorded on epic: learn per-language yields from eval corpora; tie-break nudge if C++ survivor pair should be {boundary, resource-leaks} (review finding 3, cosmetic).

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Empirical input now has a pipeline: bugbot-mi5.10 (per-unit finder observability) is the data feed for this bead's long-term goal of LEARNED per-language yield tables. Early dogfood signal (2026-06-11, n=3 hypotheses over 3 runs): the hand-coded yield ranking mispredicted — the only verified finding (verify.go tools race, a concurrency bug) was caught by api-contract-misuse (yield 50, 5th of 6) while the concurrency lens (yield 90) ran the same files earlier and found nothing. Small n, but it suggests lens assignment is leaky and rankings should be validated against unit-level data before being trusted for budget degradation order.

title: Diff-intent regression lens: hunt gaps between a commit's stated intent and its diff
description: New finder lens whose unit of work is the triggering CHANGE, not a file chunk. The task message carries the commit message and diff hunks; the agent infers intent, checks the diff achieves it, and checks which existing callers' assumptions the change breaks (blast radius gives the caller set). Zero language coupling — works on every repo from day one. This is bugbot's structural advantage as a commit-triggered daemon: generic scanners never see the change context. Plumbing: ingest/change.go already detects commits; the diff + message need to flow into the funnel's finder task for this lens only. Skipped on periodic sweeps (no triggering change).
design: Settled design (orchestrator): (1) ingest gains Repo.CommitMessage(ctx, commit) via git log -1 --format=%B (change.go already has ChangedFiles/UnifiedDiff/BlastRadius). (2) Funnel stays git-free: Options gain an optional ChangeContext{FromCommit, ToCommit, Message string, Diff []byte, ChangedFiles, BlastFiles []string} populated by the commit-triggered callers (daemon change path, cli scan commit path); nil on sweeps. (3) New Lens 'diff-intent' appended to the table, Yield 95 (above concurrency 90 — it is the unique-advantage lens — below correctness 100); activates ONLY when kind==commit scan AND ChangeContext non-nil; on sweeps it contributes zero tasks so degradation ordering is unaffected. (4) One finder task per commit-triggered run: prompt embeds commit message, unified diff bounded to a byte cap with explicit truncation marker (~48KB), and the blast-radius caller list; persona/precision contract composed exactly as existing lens tasks. (5) Candidates flow through triage/verify/repro untouched (lens name rides the existing fingerprint path). (6) Eval fixture: seeded commit whose diff contradicts its message / breaks a caller assumption, asserted caught by diff-intent and missed by taxonomy lenses, following the eval package's existing fixture pattern.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Lens activates only on commit-triggered scans, never sweeps. (2) Finder task embeds commit message + diff hunks + blast-radius caller list. (3) Candidates flow through existing triage/verify/repro unchanged. (4) An eval fixture with a seeded regression (commit whose diff contradicts its message / breaks a caller assumption) is caught by this lens and missed by taxonomy lenses. (5) Lens participates in yield-ranked budget degradation.
close_reason: Merged (PR head bugbot-mi5-1-diff-intent-lens-review; content verified on main: lens.go diff-intent entry + funnel.go ChangeContext). The change-scoped lens: commit-triggered callers (daemon poll, scan --since) populate Options.ChangeContext{FromCommit,ToCommit,Message,Diff,ChangedFiles}; gating is strictly kind==ScanTargeted && cc!=nil (the adversarial review proved Funnel.Sweep runs as ScanOneshot, so the original isCommitKind helper was a latent never-on-sweeps violation one careless caller away — deleted); one finder task per commit run embedding 4KB-capped message, 48KB-capped diff, and REAL blast-radius dependents (review fix: targets at hypothesize are already the BlastRadius-expanded set, so the task derives dependents from targets minus changed — the original cc.BlastFiles carried the changed list under a lying label and was removed). Yield 95; participates in degradation on commit runs, and the review's slot-theft finding produced degradedLensNamesFor(lenses-with-units-this-run) so a zero-unit diff-intent cannot demote a taxonomy lens on degraded sweeps (pinned: degraded sweep keeps {nil-safety, concurrency}). diff-intent excluded from lead consumption and post_lead targets (change-scoped lens has no file/line lead semantics; review caught consumed-and-dropped leads). ingest gains CommitMessage (--end-of-options guarded). Eval fixture: seeded intent-vs-diff regression caught by the lens, asserted by Finding.Lens; harness limitation (RunCase sweeps only) documented in the test — negative taxonomy-miss assertion deferred. runFinder refactored to take a prebuilt task; reviewer verified the leads path byte-equivalent. NOTE for mi5.2 reconciliation: apply C1-C5 checklist recorded in this session (Specialization→Core, lensYields row {anyLanguage:95}, thread lenses-with-units through the new ranked degradedLensNames).

title: Two finders emitted empty output under budget pressure (reliability regression at scale)
description: DOGFOOD FINDING (bugbot-9bd): two finder agents ended 'model output did not parse as JSON after one repair: empty model output' — the bugbot-210 failure mode resurfacing when agents run long/near budget limits on real files. Honest-failure stats correctly surfaced it ('stopped by budget before emitting parseable output — partial coverage'), so it is visible, not silent. Determine whether forced-finalization fires before budget hard-stop cuts the agent off, and whether a near-budget finder should be given a cheap finalize turn.
acceptance_criteria: Repro the empty-output path under budget pressure; ensure forced finalization gets a chance before hard-stop, or the unit is cleanly marked failed not silently empty; finder failure rate on the bugbot-core corpus measured.
close_reason: Budget-cut finders now get the reserved finalization turn. New runner.finalizeAndTruncate helper applied to all three budget-stop paths (pre-turn overBudget, shared-pool BudgetCheck exhaustion, post-tool overBudget) in addition to the iteration cap; guarded by finalizePrompt!='' so the public Run path adds NO extra model call (budget overshoot-bound invariant preserved, budget_test.go still green). If the finalize turn still yields empty output the outcome keeps its budget TruncationReason so the funnel classifies it as budget_stopped, not a silent parse failure. 4 new agent tests. Finder-failure-rate measured via deterministic test signal (live-corpus measurement needs real LLM spend, documented). Merged to main.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: LIVE EVIDENCE (.bugbot/state.db, 2026-06-17, 15 runs): finder agent_units = 700 parse_failed, 769 skipped_hard_budget, 48 skipped_degraded, 24 budget_stopped, 81 ok. Several whole runs show 125/125 finders parse_failed, 0 ok. Dominant signal behind the user's '--repro inconsistent / low-yield' report: only 4 T2 findings ever produced, so repro has almost nothing to promote. Umbrella: bugbot-3lt.

title: Finder token burn per unit is high on real files (~60k input/turn x many turns)
description: DOGFOOD FINDING (bugbot-9bd): even discounting cache, finder agents on real multi-file chunks made ~8-9 tool-call turns each at ~60k input/turn (206 completions for ~24 units). Uncached input alone was ~0.93M for a partial run. The code-nav + read_file loop re-sends growing context each turn. Investigate: tighter finder iteration caps, smaller default chunk size for large files, summarizing/pruning tool-result history between turns, or per-file token ceilings. Lower priority than the cache-weighting bug but compounds it.
acceptance_criteria: Measured finder input-per-unit reduced materially on the bugbot-core corpus without recall loss on the eval suite; approach documented; chunk-size/iteration defaults revisited.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #23 (commit 8105914) + lint fix #24. KEY MEASURED FINDING (contradicts the bead's leading hypothesis): threshold history compaction is a CACHE-WEIGHTED LOSS (-43% on the harness; mutating the message prefix forfeits 0.1x cache hits worth more than the bytes reclaimed) — shipped opt-in/OFF (Limits.HistoryTokenBudget, for weak/no-cache providers only). Shipped DEFAULT lever: finder per-read_file caps 800 lines/96KB via agent.ReadCaps (cache-safe, append-only); synthetic best-case upper bound ~60% cache-weighted reduction — explicitly NOT a corpus measurement (recorded fixtures sit below the caps). Review must-fixes applied: tool schema now states instance's real caps; truncation notes carry actionable offset/limit continuation (the recall seam offline eval can't catch). ChunkSize 8 unchanged (calibration comment holds). Offline measurement harness lives at internal/eval/compact_measure_test.go. Follow-up filed: bugbot-6pu (read_symbol tool).

title: Dogfood: real-repo scan of bugbot itself with all features
description: First real-codebase run (production-readiness gate, follows bugbot-7ib toy validation): scan bugbot's core packages (funnel/repro/store) with MiniMax-M3, all 6 lenses, sandbox_exec + --repro + patch-prover enabled, deps vendored for offline sandbox builds. Goals: multi-chunk finder reliability at real scale, honest precision on unseeded code, leads behavior with full context, repro/patch on a real module, and live confirmation that PR #16's spend fix records reproducer rows.
acceptance_criteria: Scan completes without finder-reliability failures across multiple chunks; every reported finding manually triaged as real/FP with verdicts recorded here; spend table contains reproducer-role rows (PR 16 validation); total spend recorded; sandbox offline-build behavior observed and module-cache strategy bead filed if vendoring proves necessary.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: DOGFOOD VERDICT: scan of bugbot's 27-file core EXHAUSTED the 6M per_cycle budget and produced ZERO findings — finders consumed input=6.12M (85% cache reads) before completing even half the (lens,chunk) units. Root cause = budget counts cached input at full weight (filed P0 bugbot-16k). Secondary: high per-unit finder burn (bugbot-3nf), 2 empty-output finders under budget pressure (bugbot-1q0), sandbox needs vendored deps (bugbot-2ry). NOT a clean dogfood — the tool cannot yet scan a real repo to completion under a sane budget. Production verdict: NOT READY; the budget-weighting bug is the gating fix. Re-run this dogfood after bugbot-16k lands.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: DOGFOOD MILESTONE (user-run with adequate budget): bugbot found a REAL bug in its own code — T1 'DSN injection via unvalidated database path overrides SQLite pragmas' at internal/store/store.go:78 — reproduced (T1) and published to GitHub end-to-end. e2e flow PROVEN for Go. Token reality: finder alone burned 30.2M input / 911 completions (84% cache) on the 27-file core — we WILDLY underestimated from the 3-file toy run (~0.3M). Confirms bugbot-16k (cache-weight) is necessary but NOT sufficient: raw scale is the bigger story; bugbot-3nf (per-unit finder burn) is now P1-critical not secondary. User feedback bead filed: repro artifacts should ship inside the gh issue (bugbot-<new>). The store.go:78 finding should itself be triaged/fixed.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: CACHE-WEIGHTING VALIDATION (PR #18, weighted binary, same 6M budget): BEFORE fix = hard-stopped at ~10/24 units (degraded+stopped), most lenses unrun, 0 findings. AFTER fix = ALL 24 finder units ran to completion, ZERO budget-degraded/stopped lines; 15.5M raw input (12.9M cached, 84%) processed under 6M budget because weighted charge ~3.5M.
