(3) A recorded 7-split corpus exists and replays offline; the recommendation (flip rate, no false demotion) is recorded on mi5.17. (4) Aggregate recorded-corpus precision does not regress (mi5.17 AC5).
notes: Refs: internal/eval/eval.go (Case/NewScriptedCase/NewRecordedCase, ModeScripted/ModeRecorded), internal/eval/score.go, internal/eval/testdata/recorded/, live-eval-invocations memory (record corpus: go test -tags record -run TestRecordCorpus ./internal/eval/, REAL spend ~15min). Blocks bugbot-mi5.17 acceptance. Source repo for the real splits: ../the_cloud (controller repo, C++/shell/python).
close_reason: Primary decided deliverable DONE+merged: the self-contained SCRIPTED CI gate (PR #96, cbc21df) — split-arbiter-refutes (clean code, arbiter REFUTES => 0 findings; also a regression gate vs the pre-mi5.17 one-shot arbiter via the REQUIRED evidence field) + split-arbiter-keeps (real reachable nil-deref survives a 1-refute split). Confirmed passing 2026-06-24: TestBenchmarkSuite aggregate precision=1.000, clean-fp=0, split-arbiter-keeps tp=1 (AC1, AC2, AC4). The one-time live capture happened (real MiniMax-M3, transcripts /tmp/arbiter-live-transcripts; build-tagged harness internal/funnel/arbiter_live_test.go), and the recommendation (flip rate + no false demotion — wheel_jog preserved) is recorded on mi5.17. AC3's 'offline ModeRecorded 7-split corpus' is SUPERSEDED by bugbot-mi5.22: the mi5.21 adjudication falsified the original FP labels (speed_config is a real bug, not an FP), so recording the original labels would bake in wrong ground truth; the corrected labeled split ground-truth set is scoped as mi5.22 AC3 (offline eval fixtures for all four mechanisms). Harness is corrected (expectations now match adjudication) and awaits a commit/PR decision.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text: Scripted CI gate landed in PR #96 (squash cbc21df): internal/eval/cases.go adds split-arbiter-refutes (clean code, split panel, arbiter REFUTES with evidence -> 0 findings; false-positive canary AND a regression gate vs the pre-mi5.17 one-shot arbiter — the now-REQUIRED evidence field is rejected by the old arbiterSchema, falling back to majorityRefuted so the 1-1 split survives as an FP) and split-arbiter-keeps (real bug, split panel, arbiter KEEPS -> seeded bug survives a 1-refute split). Both wired into BuiltinCases / the eval Gate / TestBenchmarkSuite with per-case expectations; zero API spend. Recorded-replay plumbing already exists (recorded_corpus.go / NewRecordedCase). REMAINING (this bead stays OPEN): the live ModeRecorded capture of the 7 real controller-repo splits (speed_config.cc:29, remote_provision.sh:17, dac121c.cc:25, pwm_pin.cc:21 should flip to refuted; wheel_jog_controller.cc:31 must stay kept) — requires real MiniMax-M3 spend + the sibling repo ../the_cloud, so it was NOT run autonomously. That capture is the live-model proof of mi5.17 AC4 / mi5.19 AC3-4; run it manually (go test -tags record ... with the key + sibling repo) and record the flip-rate recommendation here.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text:
LIVE CAPTURE DONE (2026-06-24, real MiniMax-M3, authorized spend ~1.5M tokens). Harness: internal/funnel/arbiter_live_test.go (build tag `arbiterlive`) replays each stored split's EXACT original panel verdicts (parsed from the finding's trace) through the NEW arbiter against the real ~/code/controller source. Replay isolates the arbiter (refuters unchanged). Transcripts: /tmp/arbiter-live-transcripts.

RESULT — FP FLIP RATE 0/3; legit keep preserved (1/1):
- common/speed_config.cc:29 -> KEPT (high). Arbiter read the .cc + .h, found NLOHMANN_DEFINE_TYPE_NON_INTRUSIVE_WITH_DEFAULT -> j.value(field,default) SILENTLY defaults missing keys (does NOT throw), traced 4 callers + the test, concluded the bug stands. Tokens 993k (used the larger budget). This CONTRADICTS the bead's FP premise ('operator>> throws').
- tools/remote_provision.sh:17 -> KEPT (high). Concluded set -e IS exempt in an if-body so the creds guard doesn't exit -> bug stands. Tokens 5.5k.
- peripherals/pwm_pin.cc:21 -> KEPT (high). Traced callers + hardware_factory; GpioInterfacePtr is shared_ptr, constructor takes possibly-null ref with no guard -> bug stands. Tokens 307k.
- control/wheel_jog_controller.cc:31 -> KEPT (high) = CORRECT (legit bug preserved; deep C++ virtual-inheritance analysis). Tokens 227k.
- encoder_tool.py:49 -> not run (matcher path bug, since fixed: file is setup/encoder_tool/encoder_tool.py). Trivial case.

INTERPRETATION: AC1/AC2/AC3/AC5/AC6 are CONFIRMED LIVE — the arbiter genuinely drives to ground (reads code, traces callers across many files, cites file:line evidence, uses the larger budget) instead of one-shot rubber-stamping. BUT AC4 ('flip the FP splits') is NOT demonstrated: the grounded arbiter judged all 3 labeled FPs to be REAL bugs with specific cited evidence. Either the FP labels were wrong, or the arbiter is biased toward 'bug real'. The evidence it cites looks sound (esp. the nlohmann WITH_DEFAULT and pwm shared_ptr analyses), so the FP labels are now in question. See follow-ups bugbot-mi5.20 (evidence-integrity: fabricated probe) and bugbot-mi5.21 (re-examine FP labels). Harness NOT yet committed (build-tagged; pending decision).

title: Arbiter should drive split verdicts to ground (agentic, evidence-verifying), not one-shot read on a refuter's budget
description:
## Problem
The split-verdict arbiter (added in bugbot-mi5.4 / PR #46) is a one-shot READER: runArbiter (internal/funnel/verify.go:111) gets the SAME per-run budget as a single refuter (verify.go:63, budget.verifyRunnerLimits(VerifierLimits)) and merely reads the two sides' written verdicts plus the code, then votes. Its task is strictly harder than a refuter's, so starved of budget/tool-reach it cannot finish the diagnosis and falls back to survival-biased defaults (orphan-on-budget-stop; majorityRefuted on unparseable; not-refuted on nil) — split panels get KEPT by default, including the false positives the arbiter exists to catch.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run a scan (dogfood config, MiniMax-M3) over a non-trivial target; e.g. the sibling controller repo.
2. Collect findings whose trace reads 'split panel decided by arbitration'.
3. Manually verify each split finding against source.

## Expected
The arbiter resolves contested splits on the merits — refuting false positives and keeping real bugs.

## Actual
The arbiter kept all 7 split findings ('not-refuted' every time); 6/7 should not have survived (4 false, 2 trivial). In one trace it admits it 'could NOT verify the library behavior directly' because an external dep was unreadable within its budget — then defaulted to keep.

## Fix direction (decided 2026-06-24)
Make the arbiter an AGENTIC investigator that DRIVES each split to ground rather than a one-shot reader: it issues targeted, evidence-demanding probes and VERIFIES decisive claims with its OWN tool calls (read the cited code/dep, trace all callers) before deciding. It drives — the seats are NOT placed in a free-form multi-agent debate. Back it with a distinct, much larger budget and broader tool reach; the higher per-split cost is acceptable because splits are rare.
design:
DECIDED approach (2026-06-24, with dpoage): arbiter becomes an agentic investigator that drives the split to ground, not a one-shot reader.

1. Active grounding loop: the arbiter issues targeted follow-up probes that demand evidence in file:line / dependency-source form ('produce the caller that passes null'; 'show the line where >> swallows the parse error') and then VERIFIES the answer with its own tool calls rather than trusting it. Only then does it issue the deciding verdict, which must cite the concrete evidence it confirmed.
2. It DRIVES; seats do not free-form argue. Symmetric multi-agent debate is REJECTED: the 7 observed split failures were grounding failures, not argument failures — the correct refutation was already written in the dissenting seat's verdict; the arbiter just failed to verify/credit it. Debate adds persuasion rounds, breaks refuter independence (verify.go:106-108, the core FP killer), and invites herding/last-word bias without adding ground truth.
3. Budget + reach: add opts.Limits.ArbiterLimits (token+iteration), set materially larger than VerifierLimits (a one-shot read shares VerifierLimits today at verify.go:111 == :63), and give the arbiter tool reach to read external deps and trace all callers. A 5-10x arbiter is fine: splits are rare — a handful per few hundred findings observed so far — so total scan spend barely moves (consistent with the mi5.4 'splits rare -> marginal' premise).
4. Keep unanimous-panel behavior byte/behavior-identical (mi5.4 acceptance #2). Cheaply falsifiable: prototype on the 7 labeled splits + recorded corpus before committing.
acceptance_criteria: (1) On a split, the arbiter runs as an agent with a distinct, configurable budget (ArbiterLimits) materially larger than a refuter's, plus tool reach to read external deps and trace all callers. (2) The arbiter actively drives to ground: it can demand evidence and MUST verify the decisive claim with its own tool calls; its recorded verdict cites concrete evidence (file:line / dep source), not just 'I read both sides'. (3) Seats are NOT placed in a multi-agent debate; refuter independence is preserved (no seat sees another seat's reasoning). (4) On the recorded corpus / known split set the arbiter flips the false-positive splits to refuted (speed_config.cc:29, remote_provision.sh:17, dac121c.cc:25, pwm_pin.cc:21) WITHOUT demoting the legitimate keep (wheel_jog_controller.cc:31). (5) Unanimous-panel verification stays behavior-identical and recorded-corpus precision does not regress. (6) Stats expose arbiter token spend + budget-stop rate so cost/starvation is observable.
notes: Evidence (2026-06-24, controller-repo dogfood scan, MiniMax-M3): 7 findings were decided by split-panel arbitration; the arbiter KEPT all 7 (always 'not-refuted'). Independent source review: 4 false (speed_config.cc:29 — nlohmann operator>> throws, does not swallow; remote_provision.sh:17 — set -e is NOT exempt in an if-body, and a tailscale guard exits anyway; dac121c.cc:25 & pwm_pin.cc:21 — null-deref unreachable, every caller passes make_shared/never-null GetDevice), 2 true-but-trivial (can_battery_pack.h:25 doc nit; encoder_tool.py:49 fd leak in a short-lived CLI), only 1 a correct keep (wheel_jog_controller.cc:31). So 'split panel decided by arbitration' was a ~85% low-quality signal — the arbiter rubber-stamped survival. Refs: internal/funnel/verify.go:98-134 (runArbiter), :44-96 (runRefuters), internal/funnel/agent_runners.go:45-49, opts.Limits.VerifierLimits. Follow-up to bugbot-mi5.4 (PR #46).
close_reason: Implemented and merged in PR #96 (squash cbc21df). The split arbiter is now an agentic investigator with a DISTINCT budget (StageLimits.ArbiterLimits + BudgetConfig.ArbiterTokenClaim ~5x refuter, default 5M; arbiterRunnerLimits draws from the verify pool at the larger claim; DefaultArbiterMaxIterations=50) — refuter budget (verifyClaim/VerifierLimits) provably untouched (AC1/AC5). Arbiter-only read_file dep-source reach (GOROOT/src + Go module cache via mi5.18 DepSourceRoots, existence-aware after oracle MUST-FIX); refuters stay repo-rooted. arbiterSystemPrompt rewritten to drive-to-ground + MANDATORY verify-the-decisive-claim; REQUIRED structured evidence field on arbiterSchema, surfaced in the verification trace (AC2). Refuter independence preserved, no debate, post_lead absent (AC3). Stats.ArbiterTokens + ArbiterBudgetStops (AC6). Oracle review APPROVE (1 MUST-FIX + 6 nits all fixed). CI green (build/vet/test, golangci-lint, CodeQL). AC4 machinery is in place and pipeline-gated by mi5.19's scripted fixtures; the live-model flip proof on the 7 controller-repo splits is the recorded-corpus step tracked on mi5.19.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text: Decision (2026-06-24, with dpoage): reframed the fix from 'bigger budget' to 'agentic arbiter that DRIVES the split to ground'. The arbiter issues evidence-demanding probes and verifies the decisive claim with its own tool calls before voting; it drives, seats are NOT put into a free-form debate (preserves refuter independence, verify.go:106-108). Symmetric multi-agent debate explicitly rejected — the 7 observed split failures were grounding failures, not argument failures (the correct refutation was already in the dissenting seat's written verdict, the arbiter just failed to verify it); debate adds persuasion + herding + cost without adding ground truth. A more expensive arbiter is accepted by the owner: splits are rare (a handful per few hundred findings so far), so a 5-10x arbiter barely moves total spend. Validate on the 7 labeled splits + recorded corpus before building.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text:
Code-grounded scope (2026-06-24 investigation). Confirmed the diagnosis against source:

CURRENT STATE
- runArbiter (verify.go:109-134): single RunJSON with arbiterSystemPrompt + arbiterTask + arbiterSchema. Budget = budget.verifyRunnerLimits(opts.Limits.VerifierLimits) — BYTE-IDENTICAL to a refuter (verify.go:63). VerifierLimits is never config-wired (zero agent.Limits) -> MaxIterations=20, per-run TokenBudget=min(verifyClaim=1M, verify-pool remaining). So the arbiter is starved exactly as filed.
- Tools (candTools): readOnlyTools (read_file/list_dir/grep/git_blame/git_log + LSP find_definition/find_references/find_implementations/read_symbol/find_usages/outline) + sandbox_exec + run_tests + status_note; post_lead absent. 'Trace all callers' (find_references) is ALREADY present.
- CONFIRMED GAP for 'read external deps': read_file is rooted at repo.Root() and traversal-protected (no ..). GOROOT + module cache ($GOPATH/pkg/mod) live OUTSIDE the repo -> unreadable. In-repo vendor/ is reachable; system/3rd-party C/C++/python deps are NOT. verifierToolParagraph (prompt.go:235) CLAIMS GOROOT/module source is readable — false for non-vendored deps. This is the cited 'unreadable external dep' failure.
- Survival bias paths (verify_stream.go:202-210): split + arbiter nil/failed -> majorityRefuted; 1-refute split is a tie -> survives; budget-stop -> orphan kept T3. Defaults keep.

TOUCHPOINTS
- AC1 budget: add StageLimits.ArbiterLimits (agent.Limits, larger MaxIterations) + resolve(); add BudgetConfig.ArbiterTokenClaim + budget.arbiterClaim + budget.arbiterRunnerLimits(base) drawing from verifyPool with the larger claim; wire run.go:399-400 and config.Budgets + bootstrap.go:155. runArbiter switches to arbiterRunnerLimits.
- AC1 reach: external-dep read mechanism is an OPEN DECISION (widen read_file to a 2nd RO root for GOROOT/module-cache/system-includes vs lean on sandbox_exec vs new read_dep tool).
- AC2 grounding+citation: rewrite arbiterSystemPrompt (prompt.go:363) to drive-to-ground + mandatory verify-the-decisive-claim; add required structured evidence field to arbiterSchema (file:line/dep-source) so citation is testable; surface it in arbiterReasoning (verify_stream.go:155) + buildReasoning.
- AC3 independence: already preserved; debate rejected; add a guard test that the arbiter sees only written verdicts (no seat-to-seat reasoning) and post_lead stays absent.
- AC5 unanimous-identical: runArbiter only fires on isSplitVerdict; ensure ArbiterLimits/claim wiring does NOT touch VerifierLimits/verifyClaim. Pin with existing structured_output + verify_soundness tests.
- AC6 stats: add Stats.ArbiterTokens (split from combined panel tokens at verify_stream.go:141) + Stats.ArbiterBudgetStops (aggregate the existing local arbiterBudgetStopped).

VERIFICATION (AC4/AC5): the 7 labeled controller-repo splits (../the_cloud/.bugbot/state.db) are not in-repo. Need either ModeRecorded transcripts of those 7 panels OR self-contained scripted eval fixtures reproducing the 4 FP mechanisms (nlohmann operator>> throws not swallows; set -e not exempt in if-body; null-deref unreachable via make_shared/GetDevice callers x2) + the 1 legit keep + 2 trivial. This fixture set is a distinct work item and the design's 'cheaply falsifiable' gate.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text:
Scoping decisions LOCKED (2026-06-24, with dpoage) + decomposition:

DECISIONS
- Reach (AC1): SECOND READ-ONLY ROOT for external deps (GOROOT/module-cache/system-includes), not sandbox-only. Split out to bugbot-mi5.18 (reusable agent-package capability; also fixes the false verifierToolParagraph claim and serves bugbot-c45). mi5.17 depends on mi5.18.
- Evidence (AC2): REQUIRED STRUCTURED evidence field on arbiterSchema (file:line / dep-source), surfaced in the trace and asserted in eval — not prompt-only self-citation.
- Verification (AC4/AC5): BOTH — scripted in-tree fixtures as the zero-spend CI gate + a one-time recorded capture of the 7 real splits. Split out to bugbot-mi5.19 (depends on mi5.17).

mi5.17 CORE SCOPE (this bead, one PR in internal/funnel):
1. Budget knobs: StageLimits.ArbiterLimits (agent.Limits, larger MaxIterations) + resolve() default; BudgetConfig.ArbiterTokenClaim (~5x DefaultTokenClaim) + budget.arbiterClaim + budget.arbiterRunnerLimits(base) drawing from verifyPool; wire run.go:399-400, config.Budgets, bootstrap.go:155. runArbiter (verify.go:111) switches off verifyRunnerLimits to arbiterRunnerLimits. MUST NOT alter VerifierLimits/verifyClaim (refuters unchanged).
2. Prompt: rewrite arbiterSystemPrompt (prompt.go:363) from one-shot reader to active grounding driver — demand evidence in file:line/dep-source form, MUST verify the decisive claim with its own tool calls before voting. Keep verifierToolParagraph + verifierRefutationCriteria shared (no drift). Wire the mi5.18 dep-read reach into candTools.
3. Schema: add required evidence field to arbiterSchema (refuters keep refutationSchema); add field to refutation struct; surface in arbiterReasoning (verify_stream.go:155) + buildReasoning.
4. Independence (AC3): no debate; add guard test that arbiter sees only written verdicts + post_lead absent.
5. Stats (AC6): Stats.ArbiterTokens (split arbiter spend from the combined panel total at verify_stream.go:141) + Stats.ArbiterBudgetStops (aggregate the existing local arbiterBudgetStopped flag).
6. Unanimous-identical (AC5): pinned by existing structured_output + verify_soundness tests; arbiter only fires on isSplitVerdict.

DAG: mi5.18 (reach) -> mi5.17 (core) -> mi5.19 (verification). bd ready surfaces mi5.18 first.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text: AC4 RESOLVED by bugbot-mi5.21 human adjudication (2026-06-24). AC4's premise (4 controller splits are FPs the arbiter should flip) was 2-ways wrong: (a) speed_config.cc:29 is a REAL bug — speed_config.h:20-21 documents throw-on-missing-fields but NLOHMANN_DEFINE_TYPE_NON_INTRUSIVE_WITH_DEFAULT silently defaults missing keys; the arbiter's grounded KEEP is correct, the FP rationale conflated the malformed-json path (operator>> throws) with the missing-fields path (WITH_DEFAULT defaults). (b) The arbiter did NOT flip the two genuine FPs: remote_provision.sh:17 (set -e is NOT exempt in an if-body — verified empirically; arbiter over-kept on a fabricated+wrong probe, see mi5.20) and pwm_pin.cc:21 (unreachable null; all callers make_shared). NET: arbiter GROUNDING is validated (correct keeps on speed_config + the wheel_jog negative control), but an OVER-KEEP bias on execution-only/reachability decisive claims remains — filed as bugbot-mi5.22. AC4 as written is retired; the grounding objective (AC1/AC2/AC3) is a PASS.

title: Flaky: TestSweep_InterruptThenResume_PendingCandidates / TestInterruptMatrix B1 under -race load
description: internal/funnel/interrupt_test.go TestSweep_InterruptThenResume_PendingCandidates (line 233) and TestInterruptMatrix/B1_InterruptedBeforeVerify_DrainFindsAndClearsWAL (line 444) intermittently fail with 'after interrupt: 2 pending candidates, want 1 (the un-verified hypothesis)'. Reproduced on PRISTINE main ceed4dd (commit #94 'durable cross-lens dedup via locus-key fold', bugbot-ef2) with NO other changes: 2/4 failures running the full funnel package under 'go test -race -count=1 ./internal/funnel/'; 8/8 PASS when the two tests run in isolation. So the flake only surfaces under full-package parallel/-race load. Surfaced by CI on PR #95 (bugbot-p9q) but is unrelated to that PR (prompt-text only).
design: Likely a timing/ordering race in the pending-candidate WAL accounting introduced or exposed by the durable cross-lens dedup change (#94, bugbot-ef2): under concurrent load an extra pending candidate row is observed after interrupt (2 vs expected 1), suggesting the dedup/locus-key fold and the pending-WAL write/delete are not serialized w.r.t. the interrupt snapshot. Investigate ordering between AddLead/dedup fold and pending_candidates WAL delete on interrupt; check whether the test's expected count assumes dedup has already collapsed siblings before the interrupt snapshot is taken.
acceptance_criteria: go test -race -count=20 ./internal/funnel/ (full package) passes with zero TestSweep_InterruptThenResume_PendingCandidates / TestInterruptMatrix failures; root cause identified as either a test-synchronization gap or a real dedup/WAL race and fixed at the source (not by relaxing the assertion).
close_reason: Same flaky test as bugbot-303; fixed by the same change in PR #101 (commit da93801). Acceptance met: go test -race -count=20 ./internal/funnel/ passes with zero TestSweep_InterruptThenResume_PendingCandidates / TestInterruptMatrix B1 failures. Root cause was chunk-axis duplicate WAL rows (two files -> two chunks both matching the lens-only finder fake), fixed at the source by anchoring the fake to the candidate's file.

title: Durable cross-lens dedup via locus-key point-lookup in triage
description: Cross-lens duplicate findings survive when an interrupted run resumes: triage's cross-lens collapse lives only in in-memory cluster state (ts.clusters), which is NOT reconstructed from already-persisted findings on replay. A WAL-replayed candidate whose sibling (same enclosing-symbol locus, different lens) was persisted by the prior run becomes a second primary instead of folding in. Observed in a real controller run: reader_writer Reader() (resource-leaks+concurrency, jaccard 0.241) and encoder_tool.py:64 (api-contract-misuse+dynamic-typing, jaccard 0.309) both cleared the merge predicate yet persisted twice. The durable fingerprint cannot catch them because it intentionally includes lens.
design: Add a lens-independent locus key = sha256(normFile, locus) (the fingerprint minus lens), persisted+indexed on findings (migration 015). In triage process(), after in-memory clustering (step 5/5b/5c) finds nothing and before registering a new primary, do a durable indexed point-lookup OpenFindingsByLocusKey(locusKey); on a hit with a DIFFERENT lens and description-jaccard >= mergeSimilarityThreshold, fold via the existing AddCorroboratingLenses + AppendFindingSites instead of forwarding a new primary. Per-candidate, idempotent (lens/site sets dedup), one indexed lookup — same pipelined shape as the existing step-3 fingerprint/suppression point-lookups; NOT a whole-DB sweep. Similarity guard is mandatory because enclosing-symbol locus is shared by distinct bugs in one function. graph_transfer 13/14 (line-fallback locus, jaccard 0.176) is OUT of scope — that is a threshold case, separate follow-up.
acceptance_criteria: 1) New migration adds findings.locus_key TEXT + index. 2) store.LocusKey() + store.OpenFindingsByLocusKey(). 3) triage process() folds a cross-lens locus hit as corroboration. 4) Test: a finding persisted by a prior run, then a fresh-triageState replay of a cross-lens candidate at the same locus, yields ONE finding carrying the second lens as CorroboratingLenses and a second Site (no second primary forwarded). 5) go build/vet/test -race and golangci-lint green.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #94 (squash ceed4dd). Durable cross-lens dedup: lens-independent locus_key (sha256(normFile,locus), migration 015, indexed) + per-candidate point-lookup OpenFindingsByLocusKey in triage process(); on a different-lens hit gated by SimilarFinding it folds as corroboration (AddCorroboratingLenses+AppendFindingSites) instead of forwarding a duplicate primary. Pipelined+idempotent, no DB sweep; fixes the interrupted-run resume gap where in-memory cluster state is not rebuilt from persisted findings. Catches the reader_writer + encoder_tool:64 real dups; graph_transfer 13/14 (line-fallback locus, jaccard 0.176) remains out of scope (threshold case). Oracle APPROVE-WITH-NITS (nits addressed). NOTE: TestInterruptMatrix/B1 is pre-existing flaky on main (interrupt-timing WAL count), unrelated to this change.

title: design-sandbox: outside-in --guide mode emits designer brief for the calling LLM
description: design-sandbox today only externalizes its reasoning when a provider is configured (--agent, inside-out: bugbot calls its own LLM). When the operator IS an LLM driving bugbot with no provider wired, the verify-failure path dead-ends at 'rerun with --agent'. Add --guide (outside-in): print the same designer brief the internal agent tier consumes — verify verdict, current candidate sandbox block, mined repo artifacts, the sandbox-proposal JSON schema/contract, the constraint rules, and the convergence loop (edit bugbot.yaml sandbox block, then 'bugbot doctor --verify-sandbox' until PASS). No provider required; never runs the host's commands.
design: Reuse single sources of truth: designSandboxSystemPrompt() for rules, renderSandboxBlock(candidateToSandboxConfig(candidate)) for the YAML, sandboxProposalSchema for the contract, and shared writeMinedArtifacts() for the raw context (also refactored into buildAgentTask). Control flow: --guide and --agent are mutually exclusive; the agent/error branch is gated on !guideMode; after the verify block a single 'if guideMode { return printDesignGuide(...) }' emits the brief for all verdict outcomes (OK / failed / not-run). Convergence path is doctor --verify-sandbox because synthesisSandbox/verifyCandidate ignore the existing bugbot.yaml block — re-running design-sandbox would not pick up the caller's edits.
acceptance_criteria: (1) 'bugbot design-sandbox --guide --agent' errors (mutually exclusive). (2) --guide on a failing candidate prints verdict+candidate+artifacts+schema+apply-loop and returns nil (no provider call, no error). (3) --guide on a passing candidate prints a verified brief. (4) failure hint without --agent also mentions --guide. (5) renderDesignGuide is a pure, unit-tested function.
close_reason: Added 'design-sandbox --guide' (outside-in): emits the full designer brief — verdict, candidate sandbox block (YAML), mined repo evidence, the sandbox-proposal JSON schema, the constraint rules, and a convergence loop (edit bugbot.yaml sandbox block, then 'bugbot doctor --verify-sandbox' until PASS) — for the LLM driving bugbot, with no provider required. --guide and --agent are mutually exclusive (cobra MarkFlagsMutuallyExclusive); the agent/error branch is gated on !guideMode and a single post-verify 'if guideMode { return printDesignGuide(...) }' covers OK/failed/not-run verdicts. Convergence routes through doctor --verify-sandbox because synthesisSandbox/verifyCandidate ignore the existing block. renderDesignGuide is a pure function; rules/evidence/schema reuse the inside-out single sources of truth (designSandboxSystemPrompt, new shared writeMinedArtifacts with sorted labels, sandboxProposalSchema). The failure hint now mentions both --agent and --guide. Tests: TestRenderDesignGuide (3 verdicts + invariants + sorted evidence), TestRenderDesignGuideNoEvidence, TestDesignGuideMutuallyExclusiveWithAgent, TestDesignGuideEmitsBriefOnVerifySkip. Full ./internal/cli/ suite + go vet green; smoke-tested via the root binary.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text: Code review (approve-with-nits) fixes applied: (1) P2 — apply-loop convergence steps diverged when repoDir != invocation cwd (edit used configPath relative to cwd; verify cd'd into repoDir and doctor read repoDir/bugbot.yaml). Fixed by anchoring the whole loop on one file: 'cd <repoDir>' then edit/verify ./bugbot.yaml. Dropped the misleading configPath param from renderDesignGuide/printDesignGuide. (2) P3 — 'rationale' was listed as a sandbox: field though it is JSON-proposal-only and absent from config.Sandbox; brief now states it explains the choice and is NOT written to bugbot.yaml. Decisions on non-blocking notes: content[:4000] rune-split left as-is (pre-existing, moved verbatim, at most garbles one char at an already-marked truncation boundary in a prompt); '--guide --write' left to take guide precedence (return-early) rather than adding a second mutual-exclusivity rule — guide vs write are distinct intents and writing the possibly-failing deterministic candidate under --guide is undesirable anyway. Tests updated for new signature; full ./internal/cli/ suite + vet green; re-smoke-tested via binary.

title: Flaky: TestSweep_InterruptThenResume_PendingCandidates (~30% fail under -race)
description: internal/funnel/interrupt_test.go:233 TestSweep_InterruptThenResume_PendingCandidates fails nondeterministically: 'after interrupt: 2 pending candidates, want 1 (the un-verified hypothesis)'. Reproduced 3/10 under 'go test -race ./internal/funnel/ -run TestSweep_InterruptThenResume_PendingCandidates -count=1'. Pre-existing since #82 (657df79, funnel re-architecture); unrelated to bugbot-2tl (which only touched detectTestCmd). Surfaced because CI runs go test -race ./... and one of the two duplicate runs (push+PR triggers) on the same SHA failed while the other passed.
design: Either the interrupt fires at a nondeterministic point leaving a candidate mid-flight (test-timing fragility -> deterministic synchronization needed), or a real race in the sweep interrupt/resume pending-candidate accounting. Needs investigation of how the sweep checkpoints pending vs in-flight candidates on interrupt.
acceptance_criteria: Test passes deterministically (e.g. 100/100 under -race), with either a synchronization fix in the test or a fix to the sweep interrupt accounting if it is a real race.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #101 (commit da93801). ROOT CAUSE (empirical, not the strategy-axis hypothesis): openFixture's repo has two files (bug.go, clean.go) so ChunkSize=1 yields TWO sweep-wide finder chunks for the single lens (deep strategies' AppliesTo excludes nil-safety). The lens-only finder fake fired on both chunks -> both persisted an identical pending_candidates WAL row -> triage dedups to 1 but the mid-interrupt snapshot raced the dedup window -> '2, want 1'. Fix: anchor the finder fake to the chunk whose task lists bug.go; test-only, no assertion relaxed. Verified -race -count=100 focused + -race -count=20 full package.

title: [bug] bugbot verify --suspected is silent during the (slow) re-verify pass — looks dead
description: Follow-up to bugbot-db2. `bugbot verify --suspected` builds the funnel with opts.Progress=nil (deliberate, to avoid racing a daemon's status.json snapshot). The pending-drain pass is fast, but the new ReverifySuspected pass runs the full verifier panel on every open Tier-3 finding and can take minutes. With a nil progress sink it emits ZERO output until the final summary, so it looks hung: a user observed only 'Verify drain: no pending candidates.', assumed nothing happened, and killed a run that had already promoted 1 of 4 T3 findings to T2 (which had in turn enqueued a repro agent). Fix: under --suspected, attach progress.NewLogRenderer(stdout) (plain log lines, NOT a status.json snapshot, so no daemon race) so the pass streams stage:/verified:/killed: lines per finding, plus an upfront 'Re-verifying open Tier-3 suspected findings (this can take a few minutes)...' announcement. Plain `bugbot verify` (no flag) output stays byte-identical (sink remains nil).
acceptance_criteria: Plain `bugbot verify` output unchanged. `bugbot verify --suspected` prints an upfront announcement before the pass and streams per-finding progress (verified:/killed:) during it, then the final 'Re-verify suspected: N re-judged...' summary. No status.json is written by `bugbot verify` (no daemon race). Verified: smoke test on a 0-finding store shows announcement + summary; build/vet/golangci-lint/cli-tests green.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #88 (squash 649ce7d). Under --suspected, bugbot verify now attaches progress.NewLogRenderer(stdout) (plain log lines, no status.json -> no daemon race) so the slow re-verify pass streams stage/verified/killed lines per finding, plus an upfront announcement. Plain `bugbot verify` keeps the nil sink and byte-identical output. Root cause was the diagnosis of a live aethon run: the pass was working (had promoted 1 of 4 T3->T2, which enqueued a repro agent) but emitted nothing until completion, so it looked dead. Verified via smoke test on a 0-finding store (announcement + summary print; plain verify unchanged); build/vet/golangci-lint/cli-tests green.

title: Bazel-monorepo reproduction never reaches T1: agent runs 'bazel test //...' (can't demonstrate by design) instead of direct/targeted repro; exec budget too tight; read-only disk_cache
design:
Verified live (bugbot repro --max 1 against ../aethon, fixed binary with bugbot-1gm pids fix). The pids OOM is GONE: bazel ran 147s, compiled 1130/2530 actions with 21 parallel actions, no 'unable to create native thread'. But the finding (Python KeyError on missing PATTERN_INSTANCE_ID, tools/encoder_tool.py) returned not_demonstrated, repro_path still NULL. Layered causes:
1. STRATEGY: the reproducer agent used its 3 run_tests calls as: exit4 (~5s, no test targets / bad pattern), bazel test //... (exit1, 147s, whole-repo build), exit4 (~5s), then 'budget exhausted (3/3)'. For a Python CLI bug the demonstrating command is just `python3 tools/encoder_tool.py` with the env var unset (-> KeyError traceback -> demonstrated via python/unknown ecosystem ranMarkers). The agent picked the worst possible tool.
2. DESIGN DEAD-END: internal/repro/ecosystem.go EcosystemBazel has ranMarkers:[] on purpose ('a bazel non-zero exit is NEVER enough to demonstrate ... bugbot does not support offline bazel repro'). So even a correctly-targeted `bazel test //pkg:py_test` that fails will NOT promote unless output carries sanitizer/assert markers interpret() trusts independently. Routing repro through bazel is structurally a dead end on this repo.
3. BUDGET: cfg.Repro.SandboxMaxExecs default 3 (DefaultSandboxMaxExecs) is too tight for a bazel monorepo where the agent burns calls discovering target patterns (exit 4) and one slow //... build.
4. CACHE: bazel disk_cache at /bazel-cache is on the sandbox read-only root -> flood of 'java.io.IOException: /bazel-cache/cas/... (Read-only file system)' (non-fatal) -> no incremental cache writes -> every build recompiles forward from the baked cache (the 147s cost).
Highest leverage: teach the reproducer to prefer direct/targeted invocation (python3 entrypoint, direct gtest binary) over `bazel test //...`, especially since bazel results can't demonstrate. Secondary: per-ecosystem exec budget; writable bazel cache mount or relocate disk_cache under /tmp.
acceptance_criteria: 1. For a directly-runnable entrypoint (python/script/binary) the reproducer prefers a targeted direct invocation over `bazel test //...`. 2. A bazel-monorepo finding with a clear runtime failure (e.g. KeyError traceback) reaches T1. 3. Exec budget is sufficient/per-ecosystem for bazel target discovery. 4. (optional) bazel disk_cache is writable in-sandbox (no read-only IOException flood).
notes: RESOLVED via commit 0946b9e. interpret()/patchVerdict() now classify bazel by exit code (3=demonstrated), with a dedicated branch placed BEFORE the generic env cascade so the ubiquitous read-only /bazel-cache warnings no longer false-positive as environment_error (this was the hidden second blocker, found via ground-truth). Reproducer prompt gained bazelGuidance() (direct-run-first, else targeted bazel test //pkg:target, never //..., exit-3 contract). Exec budget (3) left unchanged: with the targeted guidance the agent reproduced within budget (smart_arena used 1 targeted build). Read-only disk_cache left as a perf follow-up (non-fatal; ~25s builds).
close_reason: Bazel monorepo reproduction now works end-to-end, proven against ../aethon (bazel 8.4.2): 0 -> 2 Tier-1 reproductions in one `bugbot repro` run. cli/deploy.sh reproduced via a DIRECT shell invocation (new direct-run-first guidance); memory/smart_arena.h (double-free) reproduced via a TARGETED `bazel test --test_output=errors //memory/tests:smart_arena_repro_test` returning exit 3, which interpret() correctly demonstrated despite the read-only-cache warning flood. Root causes fixed: (1) interpret() empty bazel ranMarkers / no exit-3 handling; (2) read-only-cache warnings misread as env failure; (3) agent ran //... instead of targeted/direct. Unit tests (interpret_bazel_test.go + updated contract) + empirical ground-truth back it.

title: [bug] T3-suspected findings orphaned by hard budget have no re-verification path (VerifyDrain skips them; only a full re-scan can re-verify)
description: When the verify stage hits the HARD token budget, the candidate's refuter panel never runs and the candidate is persisted as Tier 3 suspected (internal/funnel/verify_stream.go:65-91, persistOrphan + budgetStoppedReasoning). The recorded reasoning instructs the user to 'Re-run the scan with more budget to verify it.' But there is NO targeted re-verification path: the hard-budget orphan DELETES its pending_candidates WAL row once durably persisted (verify_stream.go:82, f.deletePending). funnel.VerifyDrain only re-verifies pending_candidates (internal/funnel/verifydrain.go via store.ListPendingCandidates), so it can never pick a budget-orphaned T3 back up. SweepDrain only re-ranks SEVERITY (impactSweep), never re-runs refuters. The only recovery is a full re-scan whose finder must re-discover the same candidate (non-deterministic, full finder cost) before verification can re-run — so a budget-orphaned T3 is effectively stuck unverified unless rediscovered. The same gap applies to the verify-failed orphan path (verify_stream.go:249-259, verifyFailedReasoning, recordStatus orphaned_verify_failed), which also deletes the WAL row. User report: 'when against a hard deadline the verification refuter agents don't run and a hypothesis is promoted to T3. This is OK - but there's no way to rerun validation.'
design: Options: (a) RECOMMENDED — extend VerifyDrain (or add a sibling drain) to also enumerate open Tier-3 suspected findings (status=open, tier=3; store.ListFindings already supports the Status+Tier filter, see funnel_test.go:889) and replay them through the verify path: reconstruct a Candidate from the durable Finding (file/line/title/description/lens), run the refuter panel + arbiter, and re-persist tier (survivors -> T2, refuted -> killed). Keep the finder UN-invoked (the whole point). Idempotent + budget-gated; the daemon already fires VerifyDrain on cadence so orphans would auto-recover. (b) Do NOT delete the pending WAL row on a budget orphan so VerifyDrain re-verifies it naturally — tradeoff: WAL grows and the current 'durably persisted -> drop the row' self-heal semantics change. (c) A dedicated 'bugbot verify --suspected' CLI mode. Prefer (a): the durable Finding is the single source of truth, no WAL bloat, and reconstruction is straightforward from stored fields.
acceptance_criteria: A user can re-run verification on existing open Tier-3 suspected findings WITHOUT re-running the finder/cartographer (finder.callCount()==0). The drain/command reconstructs the Candidate from the stored Finding, runs the refuter panel, and promotes survivors to T2 / kills refuted ones, re-persisting tier and reasoning. Idempotent (a second pass over already-verified findings is a no-op), budget-gated, and produces no duplicate findings. Regression test: seed an open T3-suspected finding (budget-orphaned), run the re-verify drain with a scripted not-refuting verifier, assert it becomes T2 open and the finder was never called.
notes: Code: internal/funnel/verify_stream.go:65-91 (hard-budget orphan: persistOrphan + deletePending) and :249-259 (mid-verify budget stop / verify-failed orphan, same deletePending); internal/funnel/persist.go (budgetStoppedReasoning + verifyFailedReasoning, both say 're-run the scan'); internal/funnel/verifydrain.go (modeVerifyDrain; ONLY ListPendingCandidates); internal/funnel/sweepdrain.go (SweepDrain = severity only); store.UnsweptOpenFindings drives SweepDrain; store.ListFindings(Status:open, Tier:3) enumerates the orphans. Stats.Suspected counts these. Related: bugbot-8rd (refuter parse failure fail-open -> T3), bugbot-cvc (persist killed hypotheses for auditing).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #87 (squash 1032b32). New Funnel.ReverifySuspected + modeReverify replays open Tier-3 suspected findings through triage+verify WITHOUT the finder; findingToCandidate reconstructs from the durable finding (Reverify=true, PendingID="", Confidence=High to dodge triage step-1 drop). Survivors promote T3->T2 via UpsertFinding; refuted ones dismissed in the verify kill path (durable open row must leave the open set; UpdateStatus(dismissed) also suppresses, intended for a refuted reverify). Exposed as 'bugbot verify --suspected' (chose a dedicated path over overloading VerifyDrain, to keep oneshot drainToFixpoint from re-burning budget on just-orphaned findings). Tests: TestReverifySuspected_{PromotesSurvivor,DismissesRefuted,Empty}. CI green.

title: [bug] Severity validation (impact sweep) is a bulk drain, not pipelined into live verify — findings stream/publish at un-validated finder severity
description: The impact sweep (reachability/impact severity re-rank, internal/funnel/impact_sweep.go) was decoupled from the inline pipeline: the terminal Stage F "no longer re-ranks inline" (internal/cli/scan.go:281, comment + applyReranked). Severity validation now runs ONLY as a post-scan BULK drain (SweepDrain -> impactSweep via drainToFixpoint, scan.go:279) and on a 6h daemon cadence (Daemon.ImpactSweepInterval, default 6h). Consequence: during a live scan, candidates that survive verification persist/stream from the verify stage (verify_stream.go) carrying their RAW finder-assigned severity; severity is only "validated" afterward, in bulk, over UnsweptOpenFindings. In the oneshot path scan.go:284 applyReranked refreshes res.Findings before the summary/publish, but the validation step itself is not pipelined — it is a separate bulk pass, so the live status pane, streaming consumers, and the daemon observe pre-sweep severities until the bulk drain runs. User report: 'verify sweep seems to not run. This is a bulk action but should be pipelined in normal behavior (validate report severity live).' Want: pipeline per-finding severity validation into the live verify stage so reported severity is already validated at emit time, keeping the bulk drain only as the reconciliation/recovery path.
design: Options: (a) Run severity validation per-finding inside the verify path (verify_stream.go) just before a survivor is persisted as T2, so the persisted Finding already carries validated severity + swept_at. Keep SweepDrain only for stranded/interrupted findings and the daemon cadence. Tradeoff: per-finding adjudication loses impactSweep's single-batched-LLM-call efficiency (adjudicateImpact batches ALL ambiguous findings into one call). Mitigation: the deterministic classifyReachability ladder is already per-finding and ZERO-LLM for the common cases (dead code, test-only, platform-only, exported-Go-keep), so only genuinely ambiguous findings would need an inline LLM call; those could be micro-batched within a verify flush window. (b) Keep the bulk pass but run it BEFORE publish/stream rather than after. Either way, the deliberate decoupling rationale (independent daemon cadence + interrupt-safety) must be preserved for the recovery path.
acceptance_criteria: Severity re-rank runs per-finding inline in the verify stream as each candidate is promoted, so a finding's persisted AND streamed severity is already validated at emit time (no waiting for the post-scan drain). Bulk SweepDrain remains ONLY as reconciliation/recovery for stranded (interrupted) findings and the daemon cadence. Live status pane and any live publish reflect validated severity. A regression test asserts a freshly-persisted T2 survivor's stored severity equals the reachability-adjusted value (e.g. dead-code finding persists as low), without invoking SweepDrain.
notes: Key code: internal/funnel/impact_sweep.go (impactSweep Stage-F re-ranker; classifyReachability deterministic ladder; adjudicateImpact = at most ONE batched LLM call); internal/funnel/sweepdrain.go (SweepDrain over store.UnsweptOpenFindings, opens ScanImpactSweep run); internal/cli/scan.go:271-295 (drainToFixpoint + applyReranked + the 'terminal Stage F no longer re-ranks inline' comment); internal/funnel/verify_stream.go (survivors persist as T2 — the pipeline insertion point); internal/daemon (ImpactSweepInterval timer, default 6h; runImpactSweep -> SweepDrain). Related bead: bugbot-bar (severity calibration: verifier reachability/trust-boundary seats).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #86 (squash 015db78). validateSeverityInline (impact_sweep.go) runs the reachability/impact ladder per-finding inside runVerifyAndPersist so a T2 survivor is persisted with its validated severity + swept_at stamp; deterministic cases are zero-LLM, ambiguous cases do a single-finding adjudication when budget allows else defer to the bulk drain (swept_at NULL). SweepDrain kept only as the stranded/interrupted reconciliation path (chose option (a): inline at persist over moving the bulk pass before publish). Test: TestVerifyStream_InlineSeverityDownrank. CI green (build/vet/test, golangci-lint, CodeQL).

title: repro+funnel: cmake/meson suite-command detection for C/C++
description: detectSuiteCmd (repro/patch.go) and its in-sync duplicate detectTestCmd (funnel/helpers.go) return nil for cmake/meson though ingest detects them — so C/C++ gets no suite cmd (run_tests unavailable, patch-prover skips, smokeCmd degrades). Map cmake/meson to a compound bash -c configure+build+test argv. Child of bugbot-696 (stream 2).
acceptance_criteria: cmake -> bash -c 'cmake -S . -B build ... && cmake --build build ... && ctest --test-dir build --output-on-failure'; meson -> bash -c 'meson setup build && meson test -C build --print-errorlogs'; both detectSuiteCmd and detectTestCmd updated identically and kept in sync; tests in patch_test.go and helpers_run_tests_test.go; a test asserts detectEcosystem classifies the wrapped cmake command as cpp. Project-wide gates run by orchestrator.
close_reason: Landed on main (squash 312e9a6): detectSuiteCmd/detectTestCmd map cmake->'bash -c cmake configure+build(--parallel 4)+ctest --no-tests=ignore' and meson->'bash -c meson setup+test', byte-identical across both detectors. Oracle fixes folded in: bounded build parallelism (OOM), ctest no-tests-ignore, meson routed to cpp ecosystem in detectEcosystem, smokeCmd cheap --version probe. Tests in patch_test/helpers_run_tests/interpret_cpp all green.

title: repro: wire budgeted run_tests tool into reproducer agent
description: Give the reproducer agent the existing agent.RunTestsTool so it can run the repo's existing test suite in the sandbox during investigation, instead of planning blind. Reuses detectSuiteCmd(repoDir) for the base argv and r.deps for the dep strategy. Child of bugbot-696 (stream 1).
acceptance_criteria: newRunner attaches a run_tests tool when a build system is detected; budget is config-driven (repro.sandbox_max_execs, default 3, env BUGBOT_REPRO_SANDBOX_MAX_EXECS, validated >=1); system prompt tells the agent the tool exists and to use it to orient before writing the repro; unit tests cover tool presence + budget; scan.go and daemon.go pass the new budget. Project-wide gates run by orchestrator.
close_reason: Landed on main (squash 029464b): reproducer newRunner attaches agent.RunTestsTool when a build system is detected, budget from repro.sandbox_max_execs (default 3, env override, validated >=1), shared across revision rounds. Oracle fixes: offline-vs-online note in guidance, detectSuiteCmdFor reuses cached buildSystems, de-coupled test assertions. config+repro tests green.

title: scope: give reproducer agent in-sandbox build/test capability ('bugbot build/test')
description: Feasibility scoping (no code) for extending bugbot so the reproducer agent can BUILD/TEST in the sandbox instead of planning blind. Today the reproducer uses read-only tools only (repro/repro.go:447-461) and emits a single Plan{Files,Cmd} executed exactly once per attempt (execute(), repro/repro.go:465-489), revised up to MaxAttempts. The dominant documented failure mode (see bugbot-3lt, pkgContextGuidance) is the agent burning budget rediscovering how the repo builds/tests and emitting malformed/impossible commands. Two readings: (A) an in-loop AGENT TOOL (reuse agent.RunTestsTool / agent.SandboxExecTool — already used by funnel finder/refuter, funnel/helpers.go:254-278) wired into repro.newRunner; (B) an operator CLI subcommand 'bugbot build'/'test' (repro.VerifySandbox/RunSandboxVerify in verify_sandbox.go is ~80% of it, already behind doctor --verify-sandbox).
design: (A) is a clean drop-in: Reproducer already holds sb, resolved deps (ROMounts/Env/SetupCmds), repoDir, Image/Timeout, buildSystems (repro/repro.go:181-255); the agent loop is multi-turn tool-use before the JSON plan (planFor->RunJSON). Add run_tests to newRunner's tool slice + a repro.sandbox_max_execs budget knob (mirror verify.sandbox_max_execs default 3). KEY GAPS found: (1) detectSuiteCmd (repro/patch.go:202-244) and its duplicate funnel.detectTestCmd (helpers.go:221-247) map only Bazel/Go/JS/Cargo/NPM/Python and return NIL for cmake/meson/make/maven/gradle/dotnet, though ingest.DetectBuildSystems detects them (ingest/buildsys.go:37-50) — so C/C++ has no suite cmd: run_tests unavailable, patch-prover skips, smokeCmd falls back to version probe. C/C++ is the top pain locus (bugbot-dmy P0, bugbot-gav). (2) 'build' != 'test': no build-only path exists anywhere; a real 'bugbot build' is NEW logic (go build ./..., cargo build, cmake -S . -B build && cmake --build build, npm run build) and is highest-value for C/C++ configure+build. (3) Cost: each sandbox run copies whole repo (repro.go:43-45) -> maxExec budget mandatory. (4) False-T1 hazard: new tool output MUST route through the positive-ran-evidence classifier (interpret()/ecosystem.go), preserving bugbot-vig. RECOMMENDATION: ship (A) run_tests-for-reproducer for already-supported ecosystems first (small, independent); treat cmake/meson build-cmd detection + a compile-only 'bugbot build' for C/C++ as the larger, separately-scoped follow-up; (B) CLI is a thin front on the same core, optional since doctor --verify-sandbox overlaps. Consolidate the duplicated detectSuiteCmd/detectTestCmd while here.
acceptance_criteria: This bead is a scoping artifact. Done when: the two interpretations (agent tool vs CLI subcommand) are recorded with their wiring points and tradeoffs; the C/C++ no-suite-cmd gap and the build-vs-test distinction are captured as concrete follow-up work; and a go/no-go + ordering decision is recorded (recommended: run_tests-for-reproducer first, C/C++ build detection second). Implementation is tracked in separate child beads, not here.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: Both primary streams landed on main: bugbot-ziq (run_tests tool in reproducer, squash 029464b) and bugbot-p4l (cmake/meson detection, squash 312e9a6), after oracle review. Deferred one follow-up: bugbot-ee8 (buildArgv pkg/run narrowing for the bash-wrapped C/C++ compound). The larger compile-only 'bugbot build' CLI subcommand idea from the original scope remains unbuilt and optional.

title: [bug] repro: logic/config bugs demonstrated via ad-hoc assert binary aren't recognized as ran-evidence (not_demonstrated)
description:
SYMPTOM (user 2026-06-19, live `bugbot repro --max 3` on ~/code/personal/services-runtime): two findings -> not_demonstrated although the reproducer wrote a CORRECT repro that exits non-zero when the bug is present:
- 'macOS toolchain never defines SDL2_LIB_NAME / SDL2_IMAGE_LIB_NAME' -> the agent wrote `repro_macos_toolchain.sh` (cmd: bash repro_macos_toolchain.sh) that inspects .cmake/toolchains/x86_64-macos.cmake and asserts (exit 1) that the consumer-facing vars are unset.
- 'passthrough.frag uIntensity uniform is a no-op' -> the agent wrote test/src/graphics/PassthroughShaderTest.cpp, a bare main() compiled with `g++ ... && /tmp/bin`, that reads the .frag and asserts (exit non-zero) the no-op `mix(color,color,uIntensity)` pattern is absent.

ROOT CAUSE: these are NON-crash, non-runtime bug classes (build-system config; shader/asset semantics) for which a runtime test framework is a poor fit, so the agent reasonably signals via a custom assert program / shell script. But detectEcosystem maps `bash <script>.sh` and a bare `./bin` to EcosystemUnknown, whose ran-marker set is intentionally conservative (bugbot-vig: a BARE non-zero exit from an ad-hoc command is NOT trusted as positive ran-evidence, because it is indistinguishable from a build/env failure). So interpret() correctly-but-unhelpfully returns not_demonstrated. The precision gate that protects against false T1 from broken builds also rejects legitimate assertion-style repros that have no recognized framework marker. This is the inverse failure of bugbot-vig: a real demonstration the harness cannot TRUST.
design:
PROPOSED, not implemented. Two candidate directions, tradeoffs:
(a) GUIDANCE-ONLY (cheapest, lowest risk): strengthen systemPrompt to require wrapping every assertion — even a static file-content or config check — in the repo's standard test framework run through its standard runner (gtest+ctest, go test, pytest), so the existing per-ecosystem ran-markers fire. The agent went off-piste with a bare main()/shell script; for the shader case a gtest reading the .frag + EXPECT_FALSE would have produced '[ FAILED ]' -> demonstrated. Limitation: build-system/config bugs (the macOS toolchain case) genuinely have no runtime test; cmake configure assertions still land in unknown.
(b) SENTINEL PROTOCOL (more robust, small new surface): define a unique token (e.g. BUGBOT_REPRO_DEMONSTRATED) the agent is instructed to print to stdout ONLY on the bug-present branch right before exit(1); add it to a trusted global ran-marker (like sanitizerReportMarkers, but agent-asserted). Risk: the token is agent-controlled, so it must be paired with a non-zero exit AND must never be the ONLY signal for a build/env failure — gate it so the env/toolchain/build markers still win first (token checked at step ~4 ran-evidence, not step 0). Probably (a) first (guidance, free) then (b) if logic/config classes keep missing.
acceptance_criteria: A non-crash bug (build-config, logic, or asset/semantic) demonstrated by an assertion program/script that exits non-zero on the buggy code and zero when fixed is promotable to T1 WITHOUT weakening bugbot-vig (a broken build / env failure / bare non-zero from an unrelated command must STILL never demonstrate). Concretely: either (a) the reproducer is guided to emit positive ran-evidence the harness already trusts (wrap the assert in the repo's actual test framework so a gtest/ctest/go/pytest ran-marker fires) OR (b) a narrow, explicit demonstration protocol is introduced (e.g. the agent prints a unique bugbot sentinel line immediately before exiting non-zero, and interpret() treats that exact sentinel as positive ran-evidence) — with a unit test proving a broken build that happens to exit non-zero is still not_demonstrated. Decide between (a)/(b) and implement with regression tests for both the true-positive and the false-positive guard.
notes: Distinct from bugbot-bo2 (exit-code masking of SANITIZER findings, fixed) — these two findings did NOT mask the exit code; their cmds were `bash script.sh` and `g++ ... && ./bin` with no tail/echo. The blocker is ecosystem=unknown + bugbot-vig refusing a bare non-zero. New live evidence for the bugbot-3lt umbrella (C++ class). detectEcosystem unknown-ran-marker conservatism is intentional (interpret.go step 5 comment). Consider whether 'unknown ecosystem' should ever be promotable at all, or only via (a)/(b).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #99 (commit cfdf06e). Added a trusted sentinel token BUGBOT_REPRO_DEMONSTRATED the reproducer prints on the bug-present branch before exiting non-zero; interpret() treats it as positive ran-evidence but checks it AFTER the env/toolchain/build gates (step 3.5), so a broken build/env failure emitting the token still classifies as the failure (bugbot-vig preserved). Reproducer prompt gained the escape-hatch guidance (fallback behind standard test frameworks). Oracle-reviewed (RevExpr APPROVE), guard test proves build_error wins over the sentinel.

title: [bug] repro interpret() misclassifies sanitizer repro as exit_zero when agent masks the exit code
description:
SYMPTOM (user 2026-06-19, live `bugbot repro --max 3` on ~/code/personal/services-runtime C++/SDL, runtime=podman): finding 'Use-after-free in SubscriberList::publish when cleanup runs concurrently' -> exit_zero (0 promoted of 3). Transcript forensics (.bugbot/transcripts 20260619T231757 round1, T232007 round2): the reproducer correctly followed bugbot-gav ASan guidance — BOTH rounds built the repro with -fsanitize=address and ran it; round 2's test is a DETERMINISTIC single-threaded heap-use-after-free (subscribe in a scope, let SubscriptionHandle destruct -> EventSubscription freed but the atomic slot keeps the dangling raw ptr, then publish() derefs it) that ASan catches every run.

ROOT CAUSE: the agent appended exit-code-masking shell plumbing to the plan cmd: round 2 ended `... /tmp/repro_uaf 2>&1 | tail -40; echo "EXIT_CODE=$?"` and round 1 ended `... run_tests ... | tail -30`. A bash list's status is its LAST command's: a trailing `; echo` always exits 0, and a `| tail` pipeline's status is tail's (0). So the sandbox recorded ExitCode==0 even though ASan aborted and printed `AddressSanitizer: heap-use-after-free` into the captured output. interpret() (internal/repro/interpret.go) had a `case res.ExitCode == 0` short-circuit that returned exit_zero BEFORE the runtimeFailureMarkers (sanitizer/valgrind) check at step 0 — so the dispositive ASan report was never inspected. A genuine T1 reproduction was discarded as exit_zero. Language-agnostic; also covers the valgrind-without-error-exitcode=1 leak case (program exits 0 but 'definitely lost' is printed).
design: interpret.go: split runtimeFailureMarkers into (a) sanitizerReportMarkers — the unambiguous tool headers that appear ONLY on a detected violation ('sanitizer:', 'detected memory leaks', 'definitely lost', 'invalid read of size', 'invalid write of size') — checked BEFORE the exit-code switch so a masked exit-0 sanitizer abort still demonstrates; and (b) runtimeFailureMarkers = those PLUS the looser 'runtime error:'/'data race' phrases, checked only after the non-zero gate (step 0b) to avoid false positives from benign prose on a passing run. runtimeFailureMarkers is defined as append([looser], sanitizerReportMarkers...) for a single source of truth. agent.go: cmakeGuidance/mesonGuidance gain a -fsanitize=thread clause for concurrency/data-race findings (TSan reports the race deterministically; a single ASan run only fails if the UAF window fires); systemPrompt gains a global bullet: make the test the FINAL command, do not append `| tail`/`| head`/`; echo` (they mask the exit code).
acceptance_criteria: (1) A sandbox result whose output contains an unambiguous sanitizer/valgrind report header (AddressSanitizer:/LeakSanitizer:/ThreadSanitizer:/MemorySanitizer: via 'sanitizer:', 'detected memory leaks', valgrind 'definitely lost'/'invalid read|write of size') is classified DEMONSTRATED even when the recorded ExitCode is 0 (masked by `| tail`/`| head`/`; echo`). (2) SAFETY: a genuinely passing run (exit 0) whose prose merely mentions a LOOSE phrase ('data race', 'runtime error:') is NOT promoted — those phrases stay gated behind a non-zero exit. (3) bugbot-vig preserved: a bare non-zero exit with no marker still never demonstrates; compile/env/build errors still classify correctly. (4) Reproducer guidance steers ThreadSanitizer for concurrency/data-race findings and tells the agent not to mask the final command's exit code. (5) repro test pkg green incl. existing precision regressions.
notes: Files: internal/repro/ecosystem.go (marker split), internal/repro/interpret.go (0a pre-exit-code sanitizer check + 0b post-exit-code full set), internal/repro/agent.go (TSan + no-mask guidance), internal/repro/interpret_cpp_test.go (TestInterpret_MaskedExitCode_SanitizerStillDemonstrates: masked-exit0 ASan + valgrind demonstrate; exit0 loose-phrase prose does NOT; non-zero loose data-race still does). The systemPrompt bullet uses double-quotes not backticks (it lives inside a backtick raw-string literal). The interpret fix is the robust safety net; the no-mask guidance is the source-side complement (helps NON-sanitizer findings, where masked exit-0 still hides per-ecosystem ran-evidence). The other two findings in the run (macOS-toolchain SDL2_LIB_NAME, passthrough.frag uIntensity) are a DIFFERENT class filed separately.
close_reason: Fixed. interpret() now checks an unambiguous sanitizerReportMarkers subset BEFORE the exit-code switch, so a sanitizer/valgrind report (AddressSanitizer:/LeakSanitizer:/ThreadSanitizer:/MemorySanitizer: via 'sanitizer:', 'detected memory leaks', valgrind 'definitely lost'/'invalid read|write of size') demonstrates even when the agent masked the exit code to 0 via `| tail`/`; echo EXIT=$?`. The looser 'runtime error:'/'data race' phrases stay gated behind a non-zero exit (step 0b, full runtimeFailureMarkers = looser ++ subset) so a passing run that merely mentions them is never falsely promoted. bugbot-vig intact (bare non-zero/zero without a marker still never demonstrates; compile/env/build still classify). Guidance: cmake/meson now steer -fsanitize=thread for concurrency/data-race findings; systemPrompt forbids trailing `| tail`/`| head`/`; echo` after the test (exit-code masking) and requires the test be the final cmd. Tests: TestInterpret_MaskedExitCode_SanitizerStillDemonstrates (masked-exit0 ASan + valgrind leak demonstrate; exit0 loose-phrase prose does NOT; non-zero helgrind data race does). Full repro suite + vet + gofmt green. This would have promoted the SubscriberList UAF finding (deterministic ASan-catchable UAF lost only to the trailing echo).

title: [bug] repro Attempt hard-aborts on unparseable/schema-violating plan instead of revising
description:
SYMPTOM (user 2026-06-19, live `bugbot repro --transcript-dir ... --max 3` on ~/code/personal/services-runtime, an SDL/C++ project, runtime=podman): 1 of 3 attempts HARD-ERRORED instead of producing a verdict:

  [T2] LightingManager2D allocated with new and never freed (leaked) (error: repro: plan finding 0000019ee069bce84869efe9625ac296: agent: model output did not parse as JSON after one repair: root object missing required field "cmd")

Transcript forensics (the 6 captured .jsonl): for this finding, ROUND 1 (revision=false) produced a VALID, runnable plan (files+cmd) that executed in the sandbox and did NOT demonstrate (exit_zero). The revision round (ROUND 2) the MiniMax model degenerated and emitted a broken serialized tool-call blob as its FINAL text (']<]minimax[>[<tool_call>...<invoke name=read_file>...'), which is not JSON; the repair round-trip also failed, so RunJSON returned 'agent: model output did not parse as JSON after one repair: ...'.

ROOT CAUSE: repro.Attempt (internal/repro/repro.go) propagated ANY planFor error as a hard infrastructure abort (`return nil, fmt.Errorf("repro: plan finding %s: %w", ...)`). A RunJSON parse/schema-validation failure is NOT infrastructure — it is the model producing a malformed plan, exactly like a structurally-invalid plan that validatePlan already feeds back for a recoverable revision. The asymmetry: validatePlan failure and a non-demonstrating run -> soft retry; an unparseable plan -> hard abort that ALSO discarded round 1's already-executed honest non-demonstration verdict, reporting `error: ...` instead of `(not reproduced)`. Same hard-abort CLASS as the already-closed bugbot-wb2 (required 'expect') and bugbot-xli (trailing content), but a distinct trigger (a degenerate revision round) and the one load-bearing-field ('cmd') case.
design:
Two-part fix.
(a) internal/agent/runjson.go: add exported sentinel `var ErrUnparseableOutput = errors.New("model output did not parse as JSON")`. Rewrap the 4 parse-failure returns (budget-stop branch + 3 post-repair branches) using multiple %w so the sentinel joins the error tree while the rendered message is identical (e.g. `fmt.Errorf("agent: %w after one repair%s: %w", ErrUnparseableOutput, truncationNote(o), cause)`). Go 1.25 multi-%w => errors.Is/As traverse the tree; single errors.Unwrap returns nil but nothing relies on it (only store pkg uses Unwrap). r.run/r.repair infra errors stay returned UNWRAPPED, so they never match the sentinel.
(b) internal/repro/repro.go Attempt loop: when planFor returns perr, if errors.Is(perr, agent.ErrUnparseableOutput) -> set feedback + att.Reason='unparseable plan: '+perr, continue (mirrors the existing validatePlan recoverable branch); else hard-abort as before. classifyFinderErr (funnel) checks budgetStopped(outcome) BEFORE err, so wrapping the budget branch doesn't change finder budget classification.
acceptance_criteria: (1) A planFor/RunJSON parse-or-schema failure (unparseable JSON, missing required field, wrong shape, even after the repair round) is treated as a recoverable revision in repro.Attempt: feedback is fed back, the loop continues, and on exhaustion the finding settles Promoted=false with a non-error Reason ('unparseable plan: ...') and outcome.Err==nil — NOT a hard `error:` abort. (2) A genuine infrastructure failure (LLM transport, context cancellation, sandbox launch) STILL aborts with an error. (3) RunJSON wraps its model-output parse/schema/unmarshal failures with a sentinel (agent.ErrUnparseableOutput) that callers test via errors.Is; the historical error message strings are byte-for-byte unchanged (funnel classifyFinderErr + tests unaffected). (4) Unit tests: agent-level (sentinel set on parse + schema paths, NOT on infra error) and repro-level (valid round 1 non-demonstration + unparseable round 2 -> Tier-2, Err==nil, both rounds attempted, only round 1 hits sandbox). (5) gofmt/vet/full suite green.
notes: Files: internal/agent/runjson.go (sentinel + 4 wrap sites), internal/repro/repro.go (Attempt recoverable branch), internal/agent/runjson_test.go (TestRunJSON_ParseFailureWrapsSentinel, +errors import, fixed a stale 'errors.Unwrap' doc comment), internal/repro/repro_test.go (TestPromoteAll_UnparseablePlanDoesNotAbort). The underlying MiniMax adapter emitting a broken ']<]minimax[>[<tool_call>' blob as final text (tool-call serialization leaking into the answer) is a SEPARATE adapter-quality concern not fixed here — the agent JSON boundary now degrades gracefully regardless of which model misbehaves. Other two findings in the run (split -> not_demonstrated, SDL_PeepEvents -> build_error) are legitimate non-reproductions, not defects.
close_reason: Fixed. (a) agent.ErrUnparseableOutput sentinel added in internal/agent/runjson.go; the 4 RunJSON model-output parse/schema/unmarshal failure returns now wrap it via multi-%w with byte-identical messages (funnel classifyFinderErr + existing string-matching tests unaffected); r.run/r.repair infra errors stay unwrapped. (b) repro.Attempt now treats errors.Is(perr, agent.ErrUnparseableOutput) as a recoverable revision (feedback + 'unparseable plan: ...' reason + continue), mirroring the validatePlan branch, so a degenerate revision round no longer hard-aborts the finding or discards a prior round's executed verdict; genuine infra errors still abort. Tests: TestRunJSON_ParseFailureWrapsSentinel (parse + schema wrap the sentinel; infra error does not) and TestPromoteAll_UnparseablePlanDoesNotAbort (valid round-1 non-demonstration + unparseable round-2 -> Tier-2, outcome.Err==nil, Reason 'unparseable plan:', both rounds attempted, only round 1 reached the sandbox). gofmt/vet/full suite (20 pkg) green. The MiniMax broken-tool-call-blob-as-final-text adapter issue is a separate, unfiled adapter-quality concern; the agent JSON boundary now degrades gracefully regardless.

title: repro: JS ecosystem ran-markers share the same bare 'failed'/'fail ' weakness
description: EcosystemJS.ranMarkers = [fail , failed, ✗, tests failed, ×]. Same class of bug as bugbot-dmy: bare 'failed'/'fail ' can match build/tooling noise. Lower risk than C/C++ because JS buildMarkers (cannot find module, syntaxerror, unexpected token, ...) run first and jest/vitest are real runners, but the markers should be tightened to runner-anchored evidence (jest 'Tests:  N failed', '● ' failure bullet; vitest 'FAIL ' / '⎯⎯ Failed Tests'). No observed false positive yet — hardening only.
acceptance_criteria: JS ran-markers tightened to jest/vitest runner-anchored evidence; a webpack/tsc-style build failure whose output contains 'failed' but no JS build marker does not demonstrate; genuine jest/vitest failures still demonstrate; regression tests added.
close_reason: Merged to main (squash b424221). EcosystemJS ran-markers tightened to jest/vitest runner-anchored evidence; dropped bare failed/fail. Also fixed latent bug: old jest glyph U+2717 never matched real jest U+2715. Oracle APPROVE.

title: repro/validatePlan: reject plan.Cmd carrying raw shell operators as literal argv
description: Finding 0000019ee035a3dd... (JSON silent-failure) produced a plan.Cmd of the form [cmake, ..., '&&', cmake, '--build', ..., '&&', 'cd', 'build', '&&', './tests/...']. The sandbox runs plan.Cmd as raw argv (no shell unless the agent wraps in bash -c), so '&&'/'cd'/'2>&1' were passed as literal arguments to the first cmake, which errored ('Unknown argument'). The repro could never run. validatePlan should detect shell control operators (&&, ||, |, ;, >, <, 2>&1, cd) as bare argv elements and feed back guidance to wrap the command in bash -c, instead of spending a sandbox run on an uninterpretable command.
acceptance_criteria: validatePlan flags a plan whose Cmd contains a bare shell operator argv element and returns a recoverable revision message instructing the agent to wrap in 'bash -c'; unit test covers the JSON-finding command shape.
close_reason: Merged to main (squash a031782). validatePlan rejects bare shell-operator argv elements (&&,||,|,;,2>&1,>,>>,<,&,cd) with recoverable bash -c guidance; correctly-wrapped and normal plans pass (whole-element match, no substring). Oracle APPROVE.

title: status pane reports day-budget % on raw spend while daemon gates on chargeable
description: bugbot status 'spend today ... X% of day budget' (internal/cli/worldstate.go renderWorldState) computed the percentage from raw input+output, but the daemon's day-budget gate (internal/daemon/cycle.go dayBudgetExhausted) compares cache-discounted CHARGEABLE spend (SpendTotals.Chargeable(cache_read_weight)) against per_day_tokens. On cache-heavy runs (services-runtime: ~104M raw, 85.8M cache reads, weight 0.1, cap 100M) the pane showed ~104% 'of day budget' while the gate sat at ~27%, so users read the cap as violated though it was honored on chargeable cost (the documented contract). Fix: render the percentage on chargeable spend using cfg.Budgets.CacheReadWeight (added worldState.CacheReadWeight), keep raw in/out/total honest, and surface the chargeable figure when it diverges from raw.
acceptance_criteria: renderWorldState computes the day-budget percentage from SpendTotals.Chargeable(cache_read_weight), not raw in+out; raw in/out/total still shown; chargeable figure surfaced when != raw; new test TestRenderWorldState_DayBudgetUsesChargeable passes; live 'bugbot status' in services-runtime shows ~27% not ~104%.
close_reason: Fixed in internal/cli/worldstate.go: added worldState.CacheReadWeight (from cfg.Budgets.CacheReadWeight); renderWorldState now bases the 'X% of day budget' figure on SpendTotals.Chargeable(weight) — the same accounting daemon.dayBudgetExhausted gates on — and surfaces 'chargeable N' when it diverges from raw total. Raw in/out/total kept honest. Verified: TestRenderWorldState (no-cache, unchanged 30.0%) and new TestRenderWorldState_DayBudgetUsesChargeable pass; live 'bugbot status' in services-runtime now reports 27.6% of day budget (was 106.1%). Not a gate change: the cap was already honored on chargeable cost per the documented cache_read_weight contract; the display was the lie.

title: Interruption/resumability test matrix: prove no phase drops work
description:
End-to-end proof that the re-architected funnel drops no work under interruption and that every pass is idempotent. Extends internal/funnel/interrupt_test.go.

Scope: parametrized matrix that interrupts a run at each phase boundary (after finder unit emit / mid-triage / mid-verify / before sweep), then runs the daemon cycle (or oneshot rerun) and asserts: stranded pending candidates get verified WITHOUT a finder call; unswept findings get swept; re-running any pass is a no-op. Assert behaviour (no dropped/duplicated findings, idempotent markers), not plumbing.
acceptance_criteria:
- Test interrupts at >=4 phase boundaries; each asserts the stranded work is picked up on the next pass and no finding is dropped or duplicated.
- A 'verify drain without finders' test asserts the finder client is never invoked while pending candidates are drained.
- A 'double drain' test asserts the second sweep/verify pass over already-processed items is a no-op.
- `go test ./internal/funnel/... ./internal/daemon/...` green; full `go test ./...` green for epic closure.
close_reason: Interruption/resumability matrix added to interrupt_test.go: B1 interrupted-before-verify (VerifyDrain recovers WAL, finder never called), B2 interrupted-mid-verify multi-candidate (no duplicate; narrow vacuous-race t.Skip mirroring existing pattern), B3 verify-complete-no-sweep (SweepDrain re-ranks unswept), B4 combined-stranded fixpoint converges; plus end-to-end verify-drain-without-finders and double-drain (verify+sweep) idempotency. Full go test ./... green (20 packages) on clean feat HEAD.

title: Scheduler + run() composition: drive passes to fixpoint; remove terminal Stage F
description:
Wire the new standalone passes (verify drain .3, sweep drain .4) into the daemon scheduler and recompose run() so the funnel is an orchestration of passes.

Scope: (1) daemon/run.go: extend the schedule struct + earliest() + Run loop (today: poll/sweep/backlog timers at run.go:137-159) with verify-drain and sweep-drain deadlines, budget-gated like backlog (:141-155); add intervals to config. (2) Make oneshot `bugbot scan` (cli/scan.go:406 -> Sweep) pick up stranded work at start and run passes to fixpoint, so a single interrupted-then-rerun scan converges. (3) Remove the terminal-only impactSweep call (run.go:828); the sweep now runs as a pass. (4) postCycle (daemon/postcycle.go) invokes the sweep + verify drains alongside reverifyOpenFindings so every cycle reconciles stranded work.
acceptance_criteria:
- Daemon scheduler fires verify-drain and sweep-drain on their own budget-gated cadence; cycle accounting/logging covers them.
- run() no longer contains a terminal-only Stage F call; impact sweep is driven as a pass.
- A oneshot scan that was interrupted, then re-run, converges: prior pending candidates verified and prior findings swept without manual intervention (covered by .6).
- Config keys for new intervals documented; `bugbot doctor` still PASS.
- `go test ./internal/daemon/... ./internal/cli/... ./internal/funnel/...` green.
close_reason: Scheduler composition done: terminal Stage F removed from run() (sweep is now only a pass); daemon schedule gained verify-drain (1h) + impact-sweep (6h) timers, earliest() refactored to a priority-ordered candidate list, both day-budget gated like backlog; runVerifyDrain/runImpactSweep mirror runReproBacklog; postCycle order reverify->verify-drain->sweep-drain->publisher->promote; config keys + env overrides + Validate; oneshot scan drives verify+sweep to fixpoint and re-reads re-ranked severities before printing (oracle #3). Tests: daemon drain cadence + budget gating + earliest priority; config; applyReranked. All gates green.

title: Standalone impact-sweep drain (decouple from terminal Stage F)
description:
The originating bug: impact sweep (run.go:828, impact_sweep.go:57) is a one-shot terminal pass over the current run's in-memory findings slice, so under interruption (the common case) it never runs. Rework it into an idempotent drain over the store's unswept-open-findings query (bugbot-vpx.2).

Scope: (1) refactor impactSweep to take findings from the work-remaining query (this run's + any stranded by earlier interrupted/older runs), not only the in-memory slice; keep classifyReachability + batched adjudicateImpact unchanged. (2) Persisted verdict via UpdateFindingSeverity remains the terminal marker; honor the .1 marker decision so re-discovery semantics are intentional. (3) Ambiguous-but-unadjudicated findings (no client / budget stopped: impact_sweep.go:95-102) stay pickup-eligible for a later budgeted pass. (4) Expose as a daemon scheduler pass + `bugbot sweep` one-shot drain (mirror runReproBacklog) and a run-boundary pickup so even oneshot scans self-heal stranded findings. The terminal-only Stage F call is removed in .5.
acceptance_criteria:
- Impact sweep processes unswept OPEN findings queried from the store, not just the current run's slice; integration test: seed unswept findings from a prior 'interrupted' run, run the drain, assert they are swept (severity re-ranked + verdict_detail set).
- Idempotent: a second drain re-ranks nothing already swept (no redundant LLM adjudication for already-resolved findings).
- Ambiguous findings deferred under no-client/budget-stopped are re-picked-up by a later budgeted drain.
- `bugbot sweep` one-shot exists; daemon schedules the sweep pass budget-gated.
- `go test ./internal/funnel/...` green (impact_sweep_test.go extended).
close_reason: SweepDrain implemented: idempotent drain over UnsweptOpenFindings (store-queried, not in-memory slice), reusing the unchanged impactSweep with its own spend recorder+budget. swept_at (set by UpdateFindingSeverity) is the terminal marker -> second drain is a no-op (tested), ambiguous-deferred under budget-stopped stays pickup-eligible (tested). bugbot sweep one-shot wires advisory lock+heartbeat. Terminal Stage F removal + daemon scheduling in .5.

title: Standalone verify drain: verifier picks up pending hypotheses without re-running finders
description:
Extract verification into a free-standing, idempotent pass that drains the pending_candidates WAL independent of the finder stage — realizing 'a verifier can pick up a hypothesis at any time'.

Today the replay loop lives INSIDE the hypothesize goroutine (run.go:498 load, :529 replay), so stranded candidates are only verified by launching a full run that also pays for a fresh finder pass. New pass: ListPendingCandidates -> pendingToCandidate (run.go:897) -> triage re-anchor (re-scope/dedup/suppress via existing triageState/clusterReg) -> runVerifyAndPersist -> DeletePendingCandidate at terminal fate. Reuse the existing triage/verify machinery; do not fork its logic.
