Expose as a daemon scheduler pass and a `bugbot verify` one-shot drain (mirror `bugbot repro` and runReproBacklog in internal/daemon/backlog.go). Budget-gated like the backlog timer (daemon/run.go:141-155).
acceptance_criteria:
- A standalone entrypoint verifies all pending_candidates with NO finder/hypothesize invocation; integration test: seed pending rows, run the drain, assert findings persisted + pending rows deleted, and finder client never called.
- Idempotent: running the drain twice over the same WAL is a no-op the second time (rows already consumed).
- Re-anchoring preserved: a pending candidate whose code changed/vanished is correctly dropped/killed (precision parity with run-start replay).
- `bugbot verify` one-shot exists and is documented; daemon schedules the pass on its own cadence, budget-gated.
- `go test ./internal/funnel/... ./internal/daemon/...` green.
close_reason: VerifyDrain implemented: run() gains a runMode param; modeVerifyDrain replays the pending_candidates WAL through the existing triage+verify+persist topology with zero finder/cartographer invocation (proven by test: finder.callCount()==0) and skips reserveForDownstream so the drain gets full budget (oracle fix #1). VerifyDrain pre-checks ListPendingCandidates before snapshot (oracle #4). bugbot verify one-shot wires advisory lock+heartbeat (oracle #2). Idempotent + out-of-scope re-anchor drop tested. Daemon scheduling wired in .5.

title: Durable per-phase progress markers + work-remaining store queries
description:
Implement the durable markers and store-side 'work-remaining' queries the Pass abstraction (bugbot-vpx.1) requires, so verify (.3) and sweep (.4) drains can query exactly what is left to do.

Scope: (1) impact-sweep marker per the .1 decision — either a store query UnsweptOpenFindings(ctx) over verdict_detail='' AND status=open, or a dedicated sweep_state/swept_at column (migration 014, preserved across UpsertFinding like tier/repro_path/needs_human at findings.go:331-342). (2) Confirm/encapsulate the verify-remaining query: ListPendingCandidates (store/pending.go:109) already is the durable queue — expose it as the verify pass's WorkRemaining. (3) Confirm hypothesize coverage watermarks + repro backlog (OpenBacklog) already serve as their phases' work-remaining queries; document, don't duplicate. Mirror OpenBacklog's pattern (query open, filter in Go) where a SQL predicate is awkward.
acceptance_criteria:
- A store-level work-remaining query exists for the impact sweep (unswept open findings) with a unit test proving swept findings are excluded and ambiguous-deferred ones are included.
- If a new column is chosen: migration added (next sequential number), preserved across UpsertFinding (re-discovery does not silently regress it), with a store test.
- Verify-pass work-remaining (pending candidates) and repro work-remaining (OpenBacklog) are documented as the canonical queries; no duplicate query paths introduced.
- `go test ./internal/store/...` green.
close_reason: swept_at marker (migration 014) + UnsweptOpenFindings work-remaining query implemented. swept_at set atomically by UpdateFindingSeverity (its only callers are the sweep), preserved across UpsertFinding via a CASE matching the tier/repro_path atomicity pattern, reset on file_hash change. ListPendingCandidates (verify) and OpenBacklog (repro) documented as canonical work-remaining queries; no duplicates added. 6 behavioral store tests + full store suite green.

title: Extend agent statusline (activity/observability) to cartographer, reproducer, patch-prover, severity sweep
description: The finder and verifier agents surface live activity in 'bugbot status' (and the pane) via KindAgentStarted/Activity/Finished events + the runner's WithActivitySink + the status_note tool. The extra-features agents (cartographer, reproducer, patch-prover, impact/severity sweep) do NOT participate, so 'bugbot status' shows nothing while they run. Extend the same observability to all four via a SHARED, opt-in seam rather than copy-pasting the funnel's per-Funnel wiring.
design: Introduce progress.AgentScope: a value bundling (sink, role, label) with Start()/Activity()/ActivitySink()/Finish(tokens,dur,err). It is the single shared bridge between agent.WithActivitySink/agent.NewStatusNoteTool (agent layer, pure callbacks) and progress's KindAgentStarted/Activity/Finished events. Migrate the funnel's activitySinkFor/emitAgentFinished/maybeStatusNoteTool + inline KindAgentStarted to delegate to it (one convention). Add roles cartographer/reproducer/patch-prover/severity. Wire the four agents: cartographer (bracket per-package summary, no tools), severity (bracket batched adjudication, no tools), reproducer + patch-prover (bracket per-finding run + WithActivitySink + status_note gated by StatusNotes). Thread progress.EventSink + StatusNotes into repro.Options from daemon buildReproducer and scan repro.New.
acceptance_criteria: (1) progress.AgentScope exists with unit tests covering Start/Activity(empty-drop)/Finish/ActivitySink emission. (2) cartographer, reproducer, patch-prover, and severity-sweep agents each emit KindAgentStarted+Finished so they appear in 'bugbot status' ActiveAgents while running. (3) reproducer and patch-prover wire WithActivitySink (tool-call activity) and the status_note tool gated by Scan.StatusNotes. (4) funnel finder/verifier wiring delegates to AgentScope (no duplicated emit logic). (5) repro.Options carries Progress+StatusNotes, threaded from both daemon and scan. (6) go build ./... and go test ./internal/progress/... ./internal/funnel/... ./internal/repro/... pass.
close_reason: Implemented progress.AgentScope as the single shared opt-in seam (NewAgentScope/Start/Activity/ActivitySink/Finish) bridging agent.WithActivitySink + status_note to KindAgentStarted/Activity/Finished. Migrated funnel finder/verifier wiring to delegate to it (removed dup emit logic + dead errString). Wired cartographer (per-package bracket), severity sweep (batched-adjudication bracket), and reproducer + patch-prover (per-finding bracket + WithActivitySink + status_note gated by Scan.StatusNotes, token usage accumulated across attempts). repro.Options gained Progress+StatusNotes threaded from daemon and scan; one-shot 'bugbot repro' passes nil to avoid racing the daemon's single-writer status.json. Verified: go build ./... + go test ./... (20 pkgs ok) including new AgentScope, severity-bracket, and reproducer/patch-prover bracket+gating tests.

title: Verifier soundness: fold refuter corrections into the published finding description before persist
description: VERIFIER SOUNDNESS BUG found in the 2026-06-19 services-runtime dogfood run. When a finding SURVIVES but its refuters CORRECT a sub-claim, the correction is not folded back into the published description. Finding #5 (LockFreeEventBus) still publishes the mechanism 'cleanup_dead_subscriptions ... release() ... destroys the object', but a refuter correctly established release() is a no-op and the object is freed by the sole shared_ptr in ~SubscriptionHandle -- conclusion (UAF) right, published mechanism wrong. Separately, finding #6's refuter-2 HALLUCINATED a nonexistent `subscriber_storage_` registry and a self-held shared_ptr (which, had it existed, would have made the code SAFE) yet still concluded 'could not refute'; the arbiter caught it, but a refuter asserting code that does not exist is a quality risk.
acceptance_criteria: (1) On a surviving finding, refuter-identified corrections to the description's mechanism are reconciled into the published description/title before persist, so the shipped mechanism matches the arbiter's conclusion. (2) Replaying #5 yields a description whose mechanism states the shared_ptr in ~SubscriptionHandle frees the object (not cleanup's release()). (3) Refuter claims that assert the existence of code not present in the cited files are detectable/flagged (guard against hallucinated 'safe' rebuttals like #6's subscriber_storage_).
notes: Cheapest form: the arbiter already reads all seat outputs to reach a verdict; have it emit a reconciled description/mechanism for survivors as part of its existing structured output, rather than passing the finder's original description through unchanged. No extra LLM round-trip.
close_reason: Implemented (ed4/verifier, squash afa4d56): arbiter emits corrected_description+hallucinated_rebuttal; on unanimous-survive panels bestRefuterCorrection folds the highest-confidence examined refuter's correction (no extra LLM call). Oracle-verified incl. the unanimous-correction blocker. Merged to main.

title: Verifier soundness: a refuter that cannot read the cited code must ABSTAIN, not count as 'could not refute'
description: VERIFIER SOUNDNESS BUG found in the 2026-06-19 services-runtime dogfood run. Finding #28 (uninitialized WindowsFdSet::max_fd) survived its adversarial panel even though refuter-3 explicitly reported (confidence=low) 'I tried listing the repository structure and searching for these files ... the search did not return matching file paths ... I cannot verify ... Because the evidence is not refutable from the code I could access ... I must return that I could not refute it.' Inability to ACCESS the code was counted as 'could not refute' and folded into the survive majority. Inability to read != inability to refute.
acceptance_criteria: (1) A refuter seat that signals it could not read/locate the cited code is treated as ABSTAIN and is EXCLUDED from the survive/kill quorum (quorum computed only over seats that actually examined the code). (2) If too few seats examined the code (below quorum), the candidate is re-seated or flagged needs_human rather than silently surviving. (3) Replaying #28: with the no-access seat abstaining, the panel outcome is recomputed from the remaining seats (and the finding does not survive on the strength of a seat that never saw the file).
notes: Needs a structured 'could_not_read_code'/abstain signal in the refuter output schema (agent.RunJSON phase schema), distinct from 'could not refute'. Today the prose 'could not refute' is the only channel, so an abstaining seat is indistinguishable from a confident non-refutation. Low-cost, high-trust fix.
close_reason: Implemented (ed4/verifier, squash afa4d56): refuters emit could_not_read_code; examinedVerdicts excludes abstainers from majorityRefuted/isSplitVerdict; below-quorum survivors flagged NeedsHuman. Oracle-verified (abstainer can't flip kill→survive; all-abstain→NeedsHuman). Merged to main.

title: Dogfood run finding improvements: impact-weighting, root-cause dedup, verifier soundness (services-runtime C++ run 2026-06-19)
description: Umbrella for quality improvements surfaced by the 2026-06-19 dogfood scan of ~/code/personal/services-runtime (SDL/OpenGL/zmq C++ engine). The run produced 31 tier-2 'verified' findings. Independent re-verification against source this session: ALL 31 are real defects (0 false positives) and the finder/verifier/refuter reasoning is human-grade -- the engine under the reports is sound. The deficiency is EDITORIAL, in three axes: (a) NO IMPACT WEIGHTING -- ~14/31 (~45%) are in dead/uncalled code (add_effect 0 callers; Surface 2-arg ctor; JSON::write; RegisterableFuncType::bind callable only from its own ctor so the broken state is unobservable; count_frames; PosixPoller; split empty-delim) or platform code NOT compiled on the Linux target (WindowsFdSet under #ifdef _WIN32; macOS toolchain), yet they ship at medium/high. (b) 5 REDUNDANT root-cause clusters survived dedup (stats dropped_duplicate=0): #2==#3 (same Engine.cpp:128, diff lens), #5~=#6 (same LockFreeEventBus UAF), #10==#11 (RenderSystem leak: .cpp alloc vs .hpp decl -- CROSS-FILE), #27==#28 (WindowsFdSet max_fd: .cpp vs .hpp -- CROSS-FILE), #25+#26 (same broken PosixPoller ctor). (c) two VERIFIER-SOUNDNESS gaps (see children). Net: 31 raw findings collapse to ~10 high-signal reachable + ~12 latent/dead + ~9 redundant.
design: Diagnosis/tracking only; fixes land on children. The finder/verifier/refuter stages are NOT the problem and should not be retuned here. Children: NEW -- (1) low-budget end-of-scan impact-assessment sweep; (2) abstain refuters that could not read the cited code; (3) reconcile published description with refuter corrections. ADOPTED existing -- (4) bugbot-bar (severity calibration via reachability), (5) bugbot-wf1 (root-cause merge in triage). Per umbrella convention (cf bugbot-3lt), this epic depends-on each child so `bd ready` surfaces the children first.
acceptance_criteria: After the children land, replaying this run's 31 findings yields: (1) dead/uncalled/platform-gated findings carry low/latent severity with a recorded caller-trace/reachability rationale, while reachable ones (SDL_PeepEvents #2/3, LockFreeEventBus UAF #5/6, lighting packing #13/14, Tileset clone leak #19, LightingManager2D leak #29, NetworkIdentity bind #18) keep high; (2) the 5 clusters publish as merged single findings; (3) no surviving finding counted a no-access refuter toward survival; (4) published descriptions match the arbiter's conclusion (no stale refuted mechanism). Net published count for this corpus drops from 31 to ~22 unique with impact-weighted severity.
notes: Evidence corpus: ~/code/personal/services-runtime/.bugbot/state.db -- 31 findings (all tier-2 open), 6 scan_runs; the headline run is the 92-min oneshot 2026-06-19T13:53-15:25Z (verifier_runs=105, arbiter_kills=11, merged_within_lens=2, merged_cross_lens=5, dropped_duplicate=0). Full per-finding VALID/OVERSTATED verdicts produced this session. Repro low-yield (reproducer ran but 0 T1 promotions this run) is ALREADY tracked under bugbot-3lt + bugbot-gav -- intentionally NOT duplicated here. Cost note (memory host-env-and-m3-notes): MiniMax-M3 spend is input-dominated ~130:1 with no prompt caching, so any new sweep must minimize INPUT tokens.
close_reason: All children landed + oracle-verified + full-CI-green, squash-merged to main (afa4d56 dox/nn3, d017dec s5e, 4f2f120 wf1; bugbot-bar absorbed by s5e). Mechanisms for impact-weighting, root-cause dedup, and verifier soundness are in place and unit-tested; live dogfood replay validation tracked separately. Follow-ups filed: bugbot-sw7 (NeedsHuman copy), bugbot-g13 (scan.go merged_root_cause display).

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: Cross-reference: the repro low-yield observed in this run (the reproducer ran but produced 0 Tier-1 promotions; all 31 findings stayed T2) is NOT tracked here -- it is already owned by the repro umbrella bugbot-3lt and its C/C++ child bugbot-gav (no sanitizer path; C/C++ memory-safety/leak findings structurally unreprovable). This epic is scoped to report QUALITY of the T2 findings themselves (impact-weighting, dedup, verifier soundness), not repro yield.

title: Repro integration build broken: writeFile redeclared (integration_test.go vs verify_sandbox_test.go)
description: internal/repro/integration_test.go (//go:build integration) declares writeFile(t,dir,name,content) and internal/repro/verify_sandbox_test.go (untagged) declares writeFile(path,[]byte) error. Under 'go test -tags integration ./internal/repro/' both compile -> redeclared, breaking the entire repro integration suite (incl. patch-prover and verify-sandbox integration tests). Pre-existing, same class as bugbot-emv (sandbox mustMkdir). Found while verifying the language wave's repro/agent.go guidance changes.
acceptance_criteria: go vet -tags integration ./internal/repro/ passes; the byte-form helper is renamed (writeFileBytes) with all callers updated; repro integration suite compiles and runs green.
close_reason: Renamed verify_sandbox_test.go's writeFile(path,[]byte)error to writeFileBytes (7 callers updated across verify_sandbox_test.go + verify_sandbox_integration_test.go); the 4-arg integration_test.go helper keeps the writeFile name. go vet + repro integration suite green (65s).

title: repro: C/C++ data-race findings — ThreadSanitizer (-fsanitize=thread) repro guidance
description:
GAP (inspected 2026-06-19, follow-up to the C++ services-runtime dogfood): bugbot can demonstrate Go data races (`go test -race`) but has NO ThreadSanitizer path for C/C++ data-race findings. A data race never reliably fails a plain assertion test, so a C/C++ concurrency finding either exits 0 (the race did not manifest this run) or crashes nondeterministically — never a clean T1.

CURRENT STATE (code):
- Go: SUPPORTED end-to-end. capabilityGuidance (internal/repro/agent.go:189-196) renders `go test -race` AVAILABLE/UNAVAILABLE from goCapabilityProbe (internal/sandbox/capabilities.go); the Go ecosystem ranMarker 'warning: data race' (internal/repro/ecosystem.go:61) recognizes the TSan-runtime output so a race demonstrates; the cgo refusal is classified toolchain_error (bugbot-vig).
- C/C++: UNSUPPORTED. (a) No cpp capability probe — capabilityEcosystems (capabilities.go:78) holds only goCapabilityProbe, so the agent cannot tell whether the image has a TSan-capable compiler. (b) capabilityGuidance renders ONLY the 'go' ecosystem; nothing instructs the agent to use -fsanitize=thread for a C/C++ race finding. (c) cmakeGuidance/mesonGuidance/genericGuidance carry no concurrency/race strategy. (d) GATING: the cpp ecosystem ranMarkers (failed/fail/assertion failed, ecosystem.go:160-177) do NOT include the C/C++ TSan signature 'WARNING: ThreadSanitizer: data race' / 'data race', so even a correct -fsanitize=thread repro falls through to not_demonstrated.

This bead = the TSan repro STRATEGY guidance for C/C++ (the analog of the Go `-race` guidance). It complements, not duplicates: bugbot-93z.13 (adds the cpp capability PROBE + capabilityGuidance rendering for asan/ubsan/tsan availability) and bugbot-gav (C/C++ MEMORY repro: leaks/UAF/uninit via asan/msan/valgrind; gav's proposed language-agnostic runtimeFailureMarkers set already lists ThreadSanitizer:/'data race', which is the gating half this bead needs).
design: Mirror the existing Go precedent. capabilityGuidance (internal/repro/agent.go) already branches on caps["go"]["race"]; add a parallel caps["cpp"]["tsan"] branch emitting e.g. 'C/C++ ThreadSanitizer: AVAILABLE. You MAY build the target with -fsanitize=thread -g and run the racing scenario; a WARNING: ThreadSanitizer: data race is the demonstration.' with the UNAVAILABLE/deterministic-fallback counterpart. The cpp tsan probe row is owned by bugbot-93z.13 (a trivial compile check, e.g. `printf 'int main(){}' | c++ -fsanitize=thread -x c++ - -o /dev/null`); this bead CONSUMES it (hard dependency). GATING: the C/C++ TSan signature must live in the dispositive runtime-marker set; bugbot-gav already proposes a package-level runtimeFailureMarkers (incl. ThreadSanitizer:/'data race') checked before build/env markers — land it there and this bead only adds guidance + tests (do NOT introduce a second marker list). Suggested sequence: bugbot-93z.13 (probe) -> bugbot-gav (gating) -> this (guidance).
acceptance_criteria: (1) For a C/C++ data-race finding on a TSan-capable image, the reproducer is guided to build the racing target/test with -fsanitize=thread and run it (including any repeat/stress needed to surface the race), and a resulting 'WARNING: ThreadSanitizer: data race' non-zero run is promoted to T1. (2) The TSan guidance is CAPABILITY-GATED: offered only when the cpp tsan probe reports available, and explicitly withheld (with a deterministic-fallback note) when unavailable — mirroring the Go -race AVAILABLE/UNAVAILABLE pattern in capabilityGuidance. (3) Gating recognizes the C/C++ ThreadSanitizer signature ('threadsanitizer:'/'data race') as ran-and-failed evidence, coordinated with bugbot-gav's runtimeFailureMarkers so the marker list is not duplicated. (4) Unit tests: guidance rendered when tsan available and withheld when not; interpret() demonstrates on a C/C++ TSan data-race output and still does NOT demonstrate on a bare non-zero with no race marker. (5) Other ecosystems' guidance/gating byte-for-byte unchanged. (6) Gates clean.
notes: Data races are NONDETERMINISTIC — unlike a leak/uninit read, a single TSan run may not trip. Guidance should tell the agent to make the racing test deterministic where possible (force concurrent entry: e.g. two threads + a barrier) and/or run it repeatedly; TSAN_OPTIONS=halt_on_error=1 makes the first race abort non-zero (TSan's default exitcode is 66 — non-zero, so interpret()'s existing non-zero path handles it; no exit-code special-casing needed). Image dependency: -fsanitize=thread ships with gcc/clang (libtsan); a TSan-capable image is the prerequisite the cpp probe (93z.13) detects. MUTUAL EXCLUSION: -fsanitize=thread cannot be combined with -fsanitize=address in one build — guidance must pick one per finding class (race -> TSan, leak/UAF -> ASan).
close_reason: Implemented in main 554a2d4 (squash, with bugbot-gav). cppCapabilityProbe (asan/tsan/ubsan) added to capabilityEcosystems (internal/sandbox/capabilities.go); capabilityGuidance renders the cpp ecosystem mirroring the Go -race pattern: TSan AVAILABLE offers -fsanitize=thread -g + TSAN_OPTIONS=halt_on_error=1 and names 'WARNING: ThreadSanitizer: data race' as the demonstration; UNAVAILABLE withholds it with a deterministic fallback; ASan likewise; both note TSan/ASan mutual exclusion. Gating recognizes the TSan signature via runtimeFailureMarkers. Capability-gated as designed; tests TestCapabilityGuidance_Cpp + TestCppProbeInterpret + the TSan interpret case all green. Force-closed: the cpp slice of the bugbot-93z.13 dependency it needed is delivered here; 93z.13's rust/js/python probes remain open (commented there).

title: repro: C/C++ memory-safety findings are unreprovable — no sanitizer path (guidance + gating + detection)
description:
SYMPTOM (user 2026-06-19, live `bugbot repro` on ~/code/personal/services-runtime, an SDL/C++ project, runtime=podman): 0 of 3 eligible findings promoted to T1. All three are memory-safety bugs:
- 'Tileset::from_index leaks a cloned texture on every call' -> exit_zero (the generated test ran and exited 0).
- 'Iterating uninitialized tail of SDL_PeepEvents output array' -> not_demonstrated (non-zero exit, no ran-evidence marker).
- 'Leaked SDL_Surface in two-arg Surface constructor' -> agent JSON error (filed separately as bugbot-xli).

ROOT CAUSE: bugbot has no path to DEMONSTRATE C/C++ memory-safety bugs — the dominant C++ bug class. A memory leak never makes a process exit non-zero; an uninitialized read is UB that may not trip any assertion. So a plain CTest/GoogleTest assertion test exits 0 (leak) or passes nondeterministically (uninit read). The only mechanical detectors are sanitizers / valgrind, and repro supports neither. Three interlocking sub-causes (must ship together to be useful):

(a) GUIDANCE — internal/repro/agent.go cmakeGuidance / mesonGuidance / genericGuidance never mention sanitizers. The reproducer system prompt tells the agent to 'encode the bug as an explicit assertion,' which is impossible for a leak/uninit read. It needs: build with -fsanitize=address (ASan+LeakSanitizer) for leaks/use-after-free/overflows; -fsanitize=memory (clang) or valgrind memcheck --error-exitcode=1 for uninitialized reads; -fsanitize=undefined for UB. Partially overlaps bugbot-93z.13 (which adds the capability PROBE/render for cpp asan/ubsan/tsan); THIS is the repro STRATEGY guidance telling the agent to actually use them for memory findings.

(b) GATING — internal/repro/ecosystem.go + interpret.go have no sanitizer/valgrind ran-markers, and the order is hostile: the cpp buildMarkers entry "error: " matches AddressSanitizer's report line (==PID==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: heap-use-after-free; lowercased contains 'error: '), and interpret() checks buildMarkers (step 3) BEFORE ran-evidence (step 4) — so even a CORRECT ASan repro is misclassified build_error, not demonstrated. Need dispositive runtime-instrumentation markers (AddressSanitizer:/LeakSanitizer:/MemorySanitizer:/ThreadSanitizer:/UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer:/'runtime error:'/'detected memory leaks'/valgrind 'definitely lost'/'invalid read of size') checked with PRECEDENCE over build/env/toolchain.

(c) DETECTION — detectEcosystem maps only `ctest` to the cpp ecosystem, but cmakeGuidance itself offers running the test binary directly (./build/tests/<bin>). A direct-binary cmd falls through to the 'unknown' ecosystem, which has neither cpp nor sanitizer markers -> a sanitizer abort becomes not_demonstrated. Sanitizer markers should be global/language-agnostic (preferred) or detection must recognize direct C/C++ binaries.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. On a C/C++ project with memory-safety findings at Tier-2 (a leak and/or an uninitialized read), run `bugbot repro` against a C/C++ toolchain image.
2. The reproducer writes a plain CTest/GoogleTest assertion test (no sanitizer build).
3. Observe: the leak finding exits 0 -> verdict exit_zero; the uninitialized-read finding exits non-zero without a recognized ran-marker -> verdict not_demonstrated. Neither promotes to T1 (observed live 2026-06-19: 0/3 promoted).
design: Sanitizer reports are dispositive positive ran-and-failed evidence that can ONLY occur post-build+run, so they may outrank the build/env/toolchain blacklists WITHOUT violating the bugbot-vig invariant (a bare non-zero exit still never demonstrates — positive evidence is still required). Suggested shape: a package-level runtimeFailureMarkers set in ecosystem.go (language-agnostic — fixes both the ctest and direct-binary detection cases at once), checked as step 0 in interpret() AFTER the exit-code switch and BEFORE env/toolchain/build. Keep the exit-0 short-circuit (a passing sanitizer run is not a demonstration; ASan/LSan abort non-zero by default; valgrind needs --error-exitcode=1, hence the guidance). Coordinate the guidance sub-cause with bugbot-93z.13 (capability probe/render) so the prompt never advertises a sanitizer the probed image lacks. Suggested fix order: (b) gating + (c) detection first (small, self-contained, unblocks ANY sanitizer repro the model already tries), then (a) guidance to make the model reliably emit sanitizer builds.
acceptance_criteria: (1) A C/C++ leak / use-after-free / uninitialized-read finding, reproduced with a sanitizer (or valgrind --error-exitcode=1) build that exits non-zero and emits a sanitizer/valgrind report, is promoted to T1 deterministically — via EITHER `ctest ...` OR a direct `./build/.../<bin>` command. (2) A plain C/C++ compile error still classifies as build_error (no false-reproduction regression); other ecosystems' classification is byte-for-byte unchanged (bugbot-vig invariant: a BARE non-zero exit never demonstrates — positive evidence still required). (3) The reproducer prompt instructs sanitizer usage for memory-safety findings (cmake + meson paths at least). (4) Unit tests: sanitizer-output-demonstrates, compile-error-still-build_error, direct-binary path detected/handled, Go/bazel ran-marker behavior unaffected. (5) feedback() for exit_zero / not_demonstrated on a C/C++ finding nudges toward a sanitizer build. (6) Gates clean.
notes: Image dependency: ASan/LSan/MSan/UBSan ship with gcc/clang (compiler-rt / libsanitizer), so they work in a standard C/C++ toolchain image under network=none; valgrind is a separate package and may be absent — prefer the compiler sanitizers as the default recommendation. New live evidence for the bugbot-3lt umbrella (C++ class, distinct from its Go-centric children). Companion bug at the agent JSON boundary: bugbot-xli. detectEcosystem 'ctest' mapping is at internal/repro/ecosystem.go:292; cpp ecosystem entry at ecosystem.go:160-177; interpret() ordering at interpret.go:64-107.
close_reason: Fixed in main 554a2d4 (squash). Added package-level runtimeFailureMarkers (sanitizer/valgrind signatures) checked as a dispositive step-0 gate in interpret() BEFORE build/env/toolchain markers — language-agnostic, so it also fixes the direct-test-binary 'unknown' ecosystem case and stops the cpp 'error: ' build-marker from swallowing AddressSanitizer's ERROR: line. cmake/meson guidance now instruct -fsanitize=address / -Db_sanitize=address (leaks/UAF/overflow) and -fsanitize=memory / valgrind --error-exitcode=1 (uninit reads); genericGuidance untouched so HasGuidance membership is unchanged. Regression-guarded: plain compile error still build_error, bare non-zero still not_demonstrated (bugbot-vig invariant intact). Unit tests in interpret_test.go cover LSan-via-ctest, ASan-direct-binary, TSan-data-race, compile-error, bare-nonzero.

title: agent: RunJSON aborts the whole attempt on trailing content after a complete JSON value
description:
SYMPTOM (user 2026-06-19, live `bugbot repro` on a C++ project ~/code/personal/services-runtime, runtime=podman): one of three repro attempts HARD-ERRORED instead of producing a verdict:

  [T2] Leaked SDL_Surface in two-arg Surface constructor (error: repro: plan finding 0000019eddf68b3606d4a515718c36bf: agent: model output did not parse as JSON after one repair: body is not valid JSON: invalid character ',' after top-level value)

The other two attempts produced normal non-demonstrating verdicts (exit_zero, not_demonstrated); this one aborted the finding before any sandbox run.

ROOT CAUSE: internal/agent/runjson.go stripBody() strips <think> blocks and a single surrounding markdown fence (stripFences) but does NOT extract the first complete JSON value. The encoding/json error 'invalid character "," after top-level value' is Go rejecting trailing bytes AFTER a syntactically COMPLETE leading value — i.e. the model emitted a valid plan object followed by a trailing comma and/or a second value (e.g. `{...plan...},{...}` or `{...plan...},`). validateSchema and json.Unmarshal both run over the whole body and fail; RunJSON's single repair round-trip produced the same trailing-content shape, so Attempt (repro.go:274-276) returned the agent error and PromoteAll recorded it as 'error: ...'.

DISTINCT FROM bugbot-rwe / bugbot-1q0: those are output-token-exhaustion / truncation mid-JSON (empty or incomplete output). This is the OPPOSITE — a COMPLETE leading value with extra trailing bytes. stripFences removes only ONE surrounding fence, so a model that closes its JSON then appends a comma / second block / prose is not recovered.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Have the reproducer (or any RunJSON-using role) model emit a final answer that is a valid JSON value followed by trailing content — a trailing comma or a second value, e.g. `{...validplan...},{...}` or `{...validplan...},`.
2. stripBody strips fences/think-blocks but keeps the trailing bytes; validateSchema + json.Unmarshal both fail with 'invalid character , after top-level value', and the single repair round-trip reproduces the same shape.
3. RunJSON returns the agent error; repro Attempt aborts the finding with 'error: ...' instead of a verdict (observed live 2026-06-19 on the SDL_Surface finding).
design: Fix direction (NOT implemented yet): in stripBody (or in RunJSON before validate/unmarshal), decode exactly one JSON value via json.NewDecoder(strings.NewReader(body)).Decode(&raw) into a json.RawMessage and, on success, use raw's bytes for BOTH validateSchema and json.Unmarshal. encoding/json's decoder respects string/escape boundaries, so it never mis-splits on braces or commas inside strings. On decode error (no complete leading value), fall back to the cleaned body so the existing precise parse error still drives the repair round-trip and the budget/truncation branch (runjson.go:76-80) is unchanged. This is strictly MORE lenient: it only rescues bodies that previously hard-failed on trailing content; it never makes a previously-passing body fail.
acceptance_criteria: (1) A model final answer consisting of a valid leading JSON value followed by trailing content (trailing comma, a second JSON value, or appended prose the fence-stripper missed) parses to the leading value and round-trips through RunJSON WITHOUT hitting the repair path. (2) A truncated/incomplete body (no complete leading value) still errors — no false rescue that would mask a real truncation (bugbot-1q0 budget-stop classification preserved). (3) Unit test in internal/agent/runjson_test.go covers the trailing-comma case, the double-value case, and the truncated negative case. (4) Gates clean.
notes: Surfaced on a real C++/SDL repro but the bug is language-agnostic — it lives at the shared agent JSON boundary (internal/agent/runjson.go) used by finder/triage/verify/repro/patch. Same file as bugbot-rwe's truncation issue but a DIFFERENT failure mode; fixing one does not fix the other. The companion C/C++ repro gap (leaks/uninit reads needing sanitizers) is filed separately.
close_reason: Fixed in main e1ab896 (squash). stripBody (internal/agent/runjson.go) now extracts the first complete JSON value via json.Decoder, so trailing content after a valid value (trailing comma / second value / appended prose) parses instead of aborting the attempt; truncated/incomplete leading values still error (no false rescue), preserving the budget/truncation branch. 4 unit tests: TrailingCommaNoRepair (proves NO repair round-trip via single queued completion), DoubleValueParsesFirst, TruncatedLeadingValueErrors, EmptyBodyErrors. gofmt/vet/full-suite green.

title: Sandbox integration build broken: mustMkdir redeclared (workspace_test.go vs local_mounts_integration_test.go)
description: internal/sandbox/workspace_test.go (untagged) and internal/sandbox/local_mounts_integration_test.go (//go:build integration) both declared func mustMkdir, so 'go test -tags integration ./internal/sandbox/' failed to COMPILE (redeclared in this block). This silently disabled the ENTIRE sandbox integration suite (incl. the Python wheelhouse tests from 93z.11) on main. Found while wiring the Rust/JS dep-strategy integration tests (93z.9/.10).
acceptance_criteria: go vet -tags integration ./internal/sandbox/ passes; the duplicate helper is removed (untagged workspace_test.go version is canonical); integration suite compiles and runs.
close_reason: Removed the redundant integration-tagged mustMkdir in local_mounts_integration_test.go (+ now-unused os import); the untagged workspace_test.go helper is canonical and identical. go vet -tags integration ./internal/sandbox/ green. Committed on main.

title: Java Maven/Gradle sandbox dep strategy: ~/.m2 or ~/.gradle cache mount + go-offline prefetch
description: Companion to 93z.4 (Java pipeline) and 93z.8. Plug Maven and Gradle into internal/sandbox/deps.go so non-vendored JVM repos build+test under network-none. Per 93z.4 comment: Maven ~/.m2/repository ro-mount + 'mvn dependency:go-offline' prefetch; Gradle ~/.gradle/caches ro-mount + 'gradle --refresh-dependencies' prefetch. CAUTION: Gradle's cache is lock-heavy and may fight a read-only mount — verify empirically (may need copy-to-writable like the npm cache). Mirror pythonEcosystem. Unique container paths. Requires a JDK+maven/gradle image.
acceptance_criteria: A non-vendored Maven repo and a non-vendored Gradle repo each build+test under network-none via prefetch (integration-tagged, skip if no image); Gradle RO-cache contention resolved and documented; sentinel keyed on the lock/build file; unit tests for detection+mount+env; gates green.

title: C# NuGet sandbox dep strategy: ~/.nuget/packages mount + dotnet restore prefetch
description: Companion to 93z.3 (C# pipeline) and 93z.8 (per-ecosystem resolver). Plug NuGet into internal/sandbox/deps.go ecosystems table so non-vendored C# repos restore + test under network-none. Per 93z.3 comment: ~/.nuget/packages ro-mount + 'dotnet restore' prefetch keyed on packages.lock.json (or restore in-sandbox from the mount). Mirror pythonEcosystem/cargoEcosystem. Unique container path. Requires a dotnet SDK image for the integration test.
acceptance_criteria: A non-vendored C# repo with one external NuGet package passes 'dotnet test' under network-none via prefetch+offline restore (integration-tagged, skip if no SDK image); sentinel keyed on packages.lock.json/csproj; unit tests for detection+mount+env; gates green.

title: Capability probes + repro-guidance rendering for non-Go ecosystems (Rust/JS/Python/C++)
description: bugbot-5au landed a per-ecosystem capability-probe registry (internal/sandbox/capabilities.go capabilityEcosystems) and a repro prompt section (internal/repro/agent.go capabilityGuidance) but ONLY the Go cgo/-race probe exists and capabilityGuidance only renders the 'go' ecosystem. The language-support wave (93z.3/.4/.7/.9/.10) added new languages/ecosystems WITHOUT capability probes, so the reproducer cannot tell whether the image can run their gated diagnostic modes (Rust toolchain components / cargo; JS test runner jest/vitest/node --test + node version; Python pytest/plugins; C/C++ sanitizers asan/ubsan/tsan). Add probeEntry rows for these ecosystems and extend capabilityGuidance to enumerate their AVAILABLE/UNAVAILABLE modes. Mirror goCapabilityProbe (cheap probe argv, interpret func, network-none, cached per image, failure degrades to all-false).
acceptance_criteria: capabilityEcosystems has entries for rust/js/python/cpp (at least one concrete probed mode each); capabilityGuidance renders each ecosystem's modes when probed; probe failure degrades to unavailable and never blocks; unit tests for each probe interpret + prompt both ways; new ecosystem = one probeEntry row.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: cpp slice delivered out-of-band by bugbot-mhd + bugbot-gav (main 554a2d4): cppCapabilityProbe with modes asan/tsan/ubsan appended to capabilityEcosystems, and capabilityGuidance now renders the cpp ecosystem (TSan + ASan AVAILABLE/UNAVAILABLE, mirroring the Go -race branch). Sandbox/repro unit tests cover the cpp probe interpret + cpp guidance rendering. REMAINING 93z.13 scope: rust / js / python probeEntry rows + their capabilityGuidance rendering.

title: config: sandbox.setup_cmds surface for offline pre-run setup
description: config.Sandbox (internal/config/config.go:158) exposes backend/runtime/image/cpus/memory/timeout/idle_timeout/network/dep_strategy but offers NO way to express pre-run setup commands. SetupCmds exist in the sandbox layer (sandbox.Spec.SetupCmds, Resolution.SetupCmds) but are only COMPUTED per-ecosystem in deps.go (e.g. pip offline install) -- an operator cannot add their own (apt-get install libpq-dev, protoc, ...), which is exactly the knob edge-case repos need and the sandbox designer's agent tier wants to emit. Add sandbox.setup_cmds to config and thread it into the run Spec.
design: The SetupCmds plumbing already exists end-to-end; this is a config surface + a merge, not new sandbox machinery. ORDER decision: operator cmds first (system libs), then ecosystem offline install. Keep ORTHOGONAL to dep_strategy. NETWORK limit: setup_cmds run in the SAME network-none run, so anything needing the network must be baked into the image or the fetch prefetch -- document this so operators do not put `apt-get update` (network) in setup_cmds expecting it to work offline.
acceptance_criteria:
- New field sandbox.setup_cmds ([][]string argv list) in config.Sandbox with yaml tag, doc comment, empty default.
- Validated in config.Validate alongside the dep_strategy check: each entry a non-empty argv; clear error otherwise.
- Threaded into the run: merged into Resolution.SetupCmds / Spec.SetupCmds at the sites that consume deps (repro promote.go/patch.go, funnel helpers.go) in a DEFINED order -- operator setup_cmds run BEFORE the per-ecosystem offline-install setup so system libs are present for any ecosystem install; document the order.
- Reuses the existing SetupCmds plumbing + the /bin/sh wrapping and exit-125-on-setup-failure semantics (sandbox.go SetupCmds doc) so a failed operator setup is classified env_error, never a bug demonstration.
- StarterYAML + applyEnvOverrides updated to match if the sibling fields are env-overridable.
- Tests: config parse/validate (valid; empty-argv invalid); resolver/Spec merge places operator cmds before ecosystem cmds; existing ecosystem setup still present.
notes: sandbox.Spec.SetupCmds + Resolution.SetupCmds (internal/sandbox/deps.go, sandbox.go); consumed at promote.go:248 / patch.go:487 / funnel helpers.go:179. config.Sandbox at config.go:158; Validate dep_strategy switch ~682; applyEnvOverrides ~505; StarterYAML in config package.
close_reason: Added config.Sandbox.SetupCmds [][]string with validation (non-empty argv) and threaded it operator-cmds-BEFORE-ecosystem-offline-install at the repro+funnel ResolveDeps sites; reuses the existing SetupCmds /bin/sh wrap + exit-125 env_error semantics. Wired into scan/daemon CLI entry points so the config actually reaches the run.

title: cli: design-sandbox command (detect->synthesize->verify->write, tiered)
description: The command that closes the recommend->write->verify loop. DETECT (gatherPrimeFacts + mine repo env artifacts) -> SYNTHESIZE a concrete sandbox spec (deterministic heuristic tier reusing recommendImage/recommendDepStrategy; agent tier for edge cases) -> VERIFY offline via the child smoke-test verifier -> PROPOSE a diff and merge the sandbox: block into bugbot.yaml on --write. Tiered like the codenav navigator: deterministic + smoke-test resolves the common case at zero LLM spend; the agent engages only when the heuristic spec fails the smoke test or artifact mining surfaces edge cases (system libs, pinned toolchain, services).
design: Tier order: heuristic -> smoke test -> agent (exception only). Reuse gatherPrimeFacts + recommendImage/recommendDepStrategy (prime.go), the child verifier, the child setup_cmds surface, agent.Runner + RunJSON (schema-constrained spec output like every other phase). The agent's job is mining + proposing, NOT executing. Keep prime/init intact. local_mounts emission depends on bugbot-ixu's config surface (related link, soft): ship image/dep_strategy/setup_cmds first, add local_mounts when ixu lands.
acceptance_criteria:
- New `bugbot design-sandbox` command (register in root.go) with flags: --target (repo dir, default .), --agent (enable edge-case agent tier, default off), --verify (smoke test, default ON), --write (merge result into bugbot.yaml; default off => print proposal + diff only).
- Artifact miner (read-only): scan .github/workflows/*, .gitlab-ci.yml, Dockerfile*, .devcontainer/devcontainer.json, Makefile, tox.ini, noxfile.py, flake.nix; extract base image, system deps, toolchain version, test-command hints. Pure parsing in the deterministic tier (no LLM); raw artifact text is fed to the agent tier when enabled.
- Deterministic tier: produce a candidate sandbox block (image, dep_strategy, network, resources, setup_cmds from mined system deps, local_mounts when monorepo/local-path deps detected -- gated on bugbot-ixu) from gatherPrimeFacts + miner output. No LLM.
- Verify tier: run the child verifier on the candidate under network=none; on env_error, either report an actionable failure (no --agent) or hand the failure + artifacts to the agent tier (--agent).
- Agent tier (--agent): agent.Runner with the finder read-only tools (read_file/grep/glob, NO post_lead), RunJSON against a sandbox-spec schema; reads mined artifacts + smoke feedback, proposes image+setup_cmds(+local_mounts), re-verifies in a bounded loop (cap iterations + budget). Proposes config ONLY -- never executes arbitrary host commands; all execution is the hardened sandbox smoke test.
- Output: prints proposed sandbox: block + rationale; with --write merges into existing bugbot.yaml without clobbering unrelated keys (show a diff; mirror init's no-clobber care but this is a MERGE).
- Trust: PROPOSE-not-auto-apply; never writes without --write, never runs mined commands outside the sandbox.
- Tests: deterministic synthesis from synthetic facts+artifacts (pure, like renderPrime); miner parsing unit tests per artifact type; agent tier with a fake llm.Client (scripted spec) + sandbox.Mock verifier (env_error then ok) proving the iterate loop; --write merge/no-clobber test.
notes: Depends on the verifier child + the setup_cmds child; related-to bugbot-ixu (produces local_mounts). recommendImage/recommendDepStrategy/gatherPrimeFacts in internal/cli/prime.go; finder read-only toolset assembly pattern in internal/funnel/hypothesize.go (tools minus post_lead); RunJSON deep-schema validation (internal/agent/runjson.go). init's O_EXCL no-clobber pattern (internal/cli/init.go) informs the --write path, but here it is a structured MERGE of the sandbox: key, not a fresh-file create.
close_reason: Implemented bugbot design-sandbox: tiered detect(miner+gatherPrimeFacts)->synthesize(deterministic)->verify(ftd.1 verifier)->agent(RunJSON bounded<=3 loop, propose-only)->write(yaml.Node splice preserving unrelated keys, diff shown). PROPOSE-not-auto-apply: --write required, mined commands never executed outside the sandbox. v1 scope: image/dep_strategy/setup_cmds(+agent-proposed local_mounts); no Dockerfile gen.

title: sandbox: offline toolchain smoke-test verifier (+ wire into doctor)
description: The single most common silent sandbox misconfiguration is a toolchain-less or wrong image: under network=none, repro/verify run the toolchain INSIDE the sandbox, and a missing toolchain makes every finding silently stay unreproduced with an environment_error (prime.go header comment). Nothing today executes the toolchain to catch this -- doctor's checkImageToolchain only string-matches the image NAME against langImageHints (internal/cli/doctor.go). Build a verifier that PROVES a candidate sandbox spec works by running the repo's toolchain inside the image under network=none and classifying the result. Reusable by both the sandbox designer (its verify tier) and doctor (a real check instead of a name match).
design:
Thin orchestration over existing primitives: sandbox.Exec + sandbox.Mock for execution, ecosystem.go (defaultEnvMarkers, hasAnyMarker, the 125/126/127 convention) for classification. Verdict categories mirror the reproducer's interpret.go vocabulary so designer/doctor speak the same language as the real repro pipeline.
PLACEMENT decision: the sandbox package is stdlib-only by invariant (deps.go header). The verifier needs internal/repro (ecosystem.go) and internal/ingest (detectSuiteCmd), so it MUST live OUTSIDE internal/sandbox -- put it in internal/repro (next to interpret.go) or a new small package that may import sandbox+repro+ingest. Do NOT add non-stdlib imports to internal/sandbox.
acceptance_criteria:
- New verifier function taking repoDir + a candidate sandbox Spec/Resolution, running a bounded smoke command under network=none via sandbox.Exec, returning a structured verdict {ok bool, category (ok|env_error|toolchain_missing|dep_missing|timeout), detail string}.
- Smoke-command derivation per ecosystem: prefer a cheap toolchain-liveness probe (e.g. `pytest --collect-only`, `cargo check`/`cargo metadata`, `go build ./...`-lite or `go vet`, node `--version` + offline install dry-run); fall back to a bare version probe (`go version`, `python --version`) when no suite cmd resolves. Reuse detectSuiteCmd (internal/repro/patch.go) and the ecosystem table where possible.
- Classification REUSES internal/repro/ecosystem.go: defaultEnvMarkers + exit 125/126/127 => env_error/toolchain_missing; a clean exit OR a genuine compile/test failure => ok (the toolchain RAN). MUST NOT misclassify a real test failure as a toolchain problem.
- Honors the dep Resolution (mounts/env/setup) so verifying a fetch/host/local-mount spec mounts exactly what the real run would.
- doctor gains a check behind a flag (e.g. --verify-sandbox, default OFF because it needs the runtime + an image pull) that runs the verifier for the configured image and emits PASS/FAIL + category. Keep the cheap checkImageToolchain name-match warn; the verifier is the authoritative check.
- Tests: classification unit tests with scripted sandbox.Mock results (ok, env markers, 125/126/127, timeout, real-failure-not-misread); integration test (podman build-tag) running a trivial Go smoke in golang image (pass) and in a toolchain-less image like debian-slim/alpine (env_error).
notes: Sources of truth: prime.go header documents the failure mode; ecosystem.go defaultEnvMarkers + hasAnyMarker + the 125/126/127 env-error convention (sandbox.go SetupCmds doc, repro interpret.go/patch.go); detectSuiteCmd is in internal/repro/patch.go. doctor check sites: checkSandbox/checkImageToolchain in internal/cli/doctor.go; doctorEnv has injectable seams for tests.
close_reason: Implemented repro.VerifySandbox/RunSandboxVerify: runs a per-ecosystem cheap toolchain liveness probe under network=none, classifying via the existing ecosystem.go markers + 125/126/127 convention into ok|env_error|toolchain_missing|dep_missing|timeout. Real compile/test failures correctly classify as ok (toolchain ran) — proven by TestClassifySmoke_RealFailureNotMisread. Honors the dep Resolution mounts/env/setup. doctor gains --verify-sandbox (default off) running the verifier; cheap name-match warn preserved.

title: Sandbox designer: detect -> synthesize -> verify -> write sandbox config
description:
The sandbox-config lifecycle today is recommend -> write -> validate, with three holes:
- RECOMMEND: `bugbot prime` (recommendImage/recommendDepStrategy in internal/cli/prime.go) is a static lookup switch. It cannot handle edge cases that actually break repro: system libs (libpq/openssl/protoc), pinned non-default toolchains, services, a Makefile that needs apt-get preinstall, multi-language repos.
- WRITE: `bugbot init` writes a generic StarterYAML that is NOT repo-aware and ignores prime's findings. Nothing turns detected facts into a written sandbox: block -- the loop is open.
- VALIDATE: `bugbot doctor` (checkImageToolchain) only STRING-MATCHES the image name against langImageHints. It never RUNS the toolchain offline, so the single most common silent misconfiguration -- a toolchain-less/wrong image under network=none -> every repro environment_errors and findings stay unreproduced (see prime.go header comment) -- is invisible.

Build a sandbox designer that closes the loop: DETECT (gatherPrimeFacts + mine the env artifacts the repo already ships: .github/workflows, .gitlab-ci.yml, Dockerfile*, .devcontainer/devcontainer.json, Makefile, tox.ini, noxfile.py, flake.nix) -> SYNTHESIZE a concrete sandbox spec (deterministic heuristic tier first, agent tier for edge cases) -> VERIFY by running the toolchain in the candidate image under network=none and classifying with the existing ecosystem env-error markers -> PROPOSE a diff and write the sandbox: block into bugbot.yaml on approval. Tiered like the codenav navigator: deterministic + smoke-test resolves the common case at zero LLM spend; the agent engages only on the exception path.
design:
Cross-cutting decisions (children carry the detail):
- DETERMINISM-FIRST: heuristic + smoke-test tiers resolve the common case with zero LLM spend; the agent is the exception path only (failed smoke test or mined edge cases).
- PROPOSE-NEVER-AUTO-APPLY: mining an untrusted repo's CI/Dockerfiles to derive image+commands is a prompt-injection surface. The command shows a diff, requires --write, and NEVER runs mined commands outside the hardened sandbox.
- REUSE OVER NEW INFRA: gatherPrimeFacts + recommendImage/recommendDepStrategy (prime.go), sandbox.Exec + sandbox.Mock, the ecosystem env-error classification (internal/repro/ecosystem.go), and agent.Runner + RunJSON with the finder read-only toolset minus post_lead.
- SCOPE v1: public image + setup_cmds. Dockerfile generation + custom image build (the deepest system-lib edge cases) is a deliberate FAST-FOLLOW, not in this epic.
- LEAVE prime (pure guidance) and init (bare template) intact; optionally make init call the deterministic tier later so its sandbox: block is repo-aware (follow-up).

RELATIONSHIP TO bugbot-ixu: the designer is the natural PRODUCER of local_mounts entries when it detects monorepo/local-path deps. local_mounts emission is gated on ixu's config surface, so child-3 is linked related-to ixu (soft, not a hard blocker -- the command ships emitting image/dep_strategy/setup_cmds first, local_mounts when ixu lands).
notes:
Registered commands (internal/cli/root.go): doctor, init, prime, scan, repro, review, daemon, status, report, metrics, cartography, leads, eval, publish. No design/sandbox command exists.
config.Sandbox (internal/config/config.go:158) exposes backend/runtime/image/cpus/memory/timeout/idle_timeout/network/dep_strategy and has NO setup_cmds/container_opts field (-> child-2).
Children: (1) offline smoke-test verifier (+doctor wiring), (2) sandbox.setup_cmds config surface, (3) design-sandbox command (deterministic + agent tiers). Child-3 depends on child-1 and child-2; child-3 related-to bugbot-ixu.
close_reason: Epic complete: all three children (ftd.1 verifier, ftd.2 setup_cmds, ftd.3 design-sandbox command) landed plus ixu's local_mounts surface. End-to-end: design-sandbox detects build systems + mines env artifacts, emits a concrete sandbox block, smoke-tests it offline, and merges into bugbot.yaml only with --write. Merged to main, full module build+vet+test green.

title: agent: on-demand cartography pull tools (get_package_context + package_graph)
description:
Cartography context is currently FRONT-LOADED: cart.contextFor(u.files) renders one bounded block (the unit's own packages + their direct dependents, capped by cartographyInjectMaxPkgs/cartographyInjectMaxBytes) into the finder prompt ONCE (internal/funnel/hypothesize.go:473). The instant a finder follows find_definition/read_symbol/read_file into a package OUTSIDE that seed, it is blind to the map and must re-read files to rediscover what a 120-word summary already states.

Add two PULL-based tools so a deep-traversing agent can fetch context on demand for a block it stepped into:

1. get_package_context(pkg) -> the cached cartographer summary for an arbitrary package. The token-cheap alternative to reading N files just to orient in a package the agent navigated into.
2. package_graph(pkg) -> who imports this package / what it imports, from the importer graph ALREADY built and held in the cartography struct (internal/funnel/cartographer.go:36-39, the same graph contextFor uses for 'direct dependents'). Lets the agent reason about blast radius mid-traversal.

This is the static-seed -> pull-based-retrieval shift: keep the small up-front seed for the common local case, add pull primitives for when traversal escapes it. Low up-front tokens, unbounded reach.
design:
PULL vs PUSH: contextFor stays as the cheap static seed (own pkg + direct dependents); these tools serve the traversal tail. Do NOT remove the seed -- the common case is local and the seed avoids a tool round-trip.

COVERAGE (the main gotcha): cartograph() only summarizes packagesSpanned(targets) -- the packages the finder was ASSIGNED. A package the finder traverses INTO is usually not a target, so GetPackageSummaries returns no row. Resolution for v1: serve cached-or-miss and rely on whole-repo RefreshCartography (already exists, internal/funnel/cartographer.go) to populate summaries repo-wide. On-demand generation (call summarizePackage on a miss) is a DELIBERATE FAST-FOLLOW filed as a separate bead: a nested LLM call inside a tool call needs budget gating (budget.finderRunnerLimits exists), recursion guarding, and breaks tool determinism for tests -- not worth bundling into v1.

REFUTER INDEPENDENCE (explicit decision): post_lead and the cartography injection are finder-ONLY because shared/derived state contaminates the independent verification that kills false positives (comment in hypothesize.go where refuter tools are assembled). get_package_context surfaces cartographer LLM OUTPUT (a description that can carry framing/bias) -> keep it FINDER-ONLY. package_graph is pure code-derived (no model opinion) -> ELIGIBLE for refuters; v1 may still wire it finder-only to keep one assembly site, with refuter exposure as a trivial follow-up. State this in the tool doc comments.

CALLBACK SEAM: both tools take a callback at construction (onLookup / graph query) injected by the funnel. This keeps internal/agent free of store and funnel-graph imports and makes both tools unit-testable with a fake -- the established PostLeadTool/SandboxExecTool pattern.

CONTAINMENT/BOUNDS: pkg args are repo-relative and validated; no filesystem escape (these read the store/graph, not arbitrary host paths). Cap output bytes/list length like codeNavMaxFileBytes / gitBlameMaxBytes / maxLeadNoteLen.
acceptance_criteria:
get_package_context:
- New Tool in internal/agent (e.g. tools_package_context.go) implementing the Tool interface; arg {pkg: string}. Accepts a package dir OR a file path (derive dir via path.Dir, matching packagesSpanned); reject the repo root (path.Dir==".") with a clear tool error.
- Backed by an injected onLookup callback (e.g. func(pkg string) (summary string, found bool, err error)) so the agent package takes NO store import -- same seam as PostLeadTool.onPost / SandboxExecTool.onExec.
- Funnel wires onLookup to store.GetPackageSummaries (cached-or-miss). v1 does NOT generate on a miss: it returns a deterministic 'no cached summary for <pkg>' result (NOT a Run error that implies retry) so the model cleanly falls back to reading.
- Output bounded defensively (summary is <=120 words by construction; still cap).
- Offered to FINDER agents ONLY (see design: refuter independence). Assembled at the same site as postLeadTool in hypothesize.go (~437-473).
- Unit tests with a fake onLookup: hit, miss, lookup error, bad/empty args, file-path->dir derivation, repo-root rejection.

package_graph:
- New Tool; arg {pkg: string, direction?: "importers"|"imports"|"both" (default "both")}.
- Backed by an injected graph-query callback so the agent package stays free of the funnel's graph type.
- Deterministic ordering (sorted) and a bounded result count.
- Unit tests: importers, imports, both, unknown pkg (empty result, not error), bad args.

Both:
- Defs advertise terse, model-facing descriptions; args validated -> tool error (recoverable), never a loop abort.
- No regression: existing static contextFor injection stays; these are additive.
notes:
Symbols/anchors:
- Tool interface + newToolSet: internal/agent/tool.go. Existing callback-seam tools: tools_post_lead.go (onPost), tools_sandbox_exec.go (onExec).
- Store read: store.GetPackageSummaries(ctx, []string{pkg}) -> map[string]PackageSummary (internal/store/cartographer.go). PackageSummary.Summary is the text.
- Importer graph lives in the cartography struct (internal/funnel/cartographer.go:36-39); expose a small query method on it for the package_graph callback rather than leaking the map.
- Assembly + cartography plumbing: hypothesize.go ~437-473 (postLeadTool built, then task = finderTask(u.files, u.leads, cart.contextFor(u.files))). cart may be nil ('feature off') -> the get_package_context callback must handle a nil cartography/store gracefully (return miss, do not panic), mirroring contextFor's nil-receiver handling.
- packagesSpanned (cartographer.go) is the canonical file->package (path.Dir, root skipped) rule; reuse it for the file-path->dir derivation so behavior matches the cache keys.

Separate fast-follow beads to file later (do NOT bundle here): (1) on-demand summary generation on cache miss (budget+recursion gated); (2) outline_file (tree-sitter declaration skeleton, reuses tsBodyBackend); (3) lookup_symbol (LSP workspace/symbol jump-by-name, reuses the CodeNav LSP manager).
close_reason: Added get_package_context (cached cartographer summary, FINDER-ONLY for refuter independence, deterministic miss-message not error) and package_graph (importers/imports via new cartography.QueryGraph, sorted+bounded). Callback-seam construction keeps internal/agent free of store/funnel imports; nil-cart safe; wired finder-only in hypothesize.go beside post_lead.

title: sandbox: composable local-mount dep layer for on-disk/monorepo deps
description:
Bugbot's sandbox copies ONLY the scanned repo (copyTree -> writable /workspace, internal/sandbox/workspace.go). Anything a build references via a host filesystem path that escapes the repo root (monorepo siblings, locally-checked-out path deps, a parent workspace root) is absent under --network=none. The existing dep strategies off/host/fetch (internal/sandbox/deps.go) only make REGISTRY/package-manager deps present (caches); they do NOTHING for local on-disk deps.

This is a cross-ecosystem gap, NOT Go-specific: JS file:../sibling, Rust path="../sibling", Python editable installs (-e ../lib), Go replace/go.work use, C/C++ add_subdirectory(../other), and the general 'scan one subdir of a monorepo' case.

Add a composable layer that mounts operator-specified host directories READ-ONLY into the sandbox so those local references resolve offline. It is ORTHOGONAL to off/host/fetch (a monorepo can need a sibling mount AND a registry cache), so it is NOT a 4th mutually-exclusive DepStrategy value -- it is an additive layer merged into Resolution, like isVendored 'applies in every mode' and like resolveWith's multi-ecosystem ROMounts merge.
design:
ORTHOGONAL AXIS: off/host/fetch resolve REGISTRY deps; this resolves LOCAL-PATH deps. Model as additive ROMounts merged into Resolution, NOT a new exclusive enum. resolveWith already merges ROMounts across ecosystems; the config-driven mounts can be appended in ResolveDeps regardless of which ecosystems match (or none match at all).

CONTAINER PATH MAPPING: preserve the relative path under /workspace so the build tool's own relative resolution works unchanged (host ../sibling -> container /sibling, since /workspace's parent is /). This is the language-agnostic sweet spot: the package-under-test stays in the WRITABLE /workspace copy (WriteFiles test injection still works), only out-of-tree deps are RO. Absolute-path references inside manifests would need per-ecosystem rewriting -- out of scope for v1.

SECURITY (the crux, why v1 is config-only): the sandbox runs untrusted LLM-driven code against untrusted repos; sandbox.go package doc mandates RO mounts expose only public/cache content, NEVER secrets/private trees. off/host/fetch mount KNOWN locations (modcache, bugbot-owned cache). A local mount exposes an arbitrary host tree. If the path were auto-derived from an in-repo manifest, a crafted manifest (use /home/me/.ssh, ../../../) is an exfiltration vector -- even at network=none the agent can echo contents into findings/output. So v1 takes paths ONLY from operator config (trust boundary intact). Auto-derive-from-manifest is a deliberate FAST-FOLLOW, gated on a containment check (target must stay within an allowed ancestor of the scan root; reject absolute escapes).

REUSE: ROMount type + Shared semantics (the HOST modcache mount is the precedent for Shared=true), validateMounts uniqueness/absolute-path checks, and the Resolution merge in resolveWith.
acceptance_criteria:
- New config knob (e.g. sandbox.local_mounts: [{host, container}]) parsed in internal/config, env-overridable, and validated: absolute host+container paths required; container paths unique across registry mounts (reuse/extend the validateMounts uniqueness invariant); missing host dir -> clear error before podman emits an opaque bind-mount failure.
- Resolved into Resolution.ROMounts (deps.go) INDEPENDENT of ecosystem detection, so it composes with off/host/fetch (e.g. fetch + local mounts both present in one Spec).
- Mounts are READ-ONLY and set ROMount.Shared=true for operator dirs (no :Z relabel of user source); honored by the CLI backend's -v host:ctr:ro rendering.
- Plumbed through BOTH consumers: reproducer (internal/repro patch.go/promote.go depMounts) and funnel refuter (internal/funnel helpers.go f.deps.ROMounts).
- v1: paths come ONLY from trusted operator config -- NO parsing of in-repo manifests (go.work/Cargo.toml/package.json), because those are untrusted input (see design).
- Tests: config validation (valid/invalid/duplicate/missing-dir); resolver merge composes with host AND fetch; integration test (podman, build-tag) proving a package whose dep lives in a mounted sibling dir builds/tests offline.
- Name avoids the 'workspace' collision (workspaceMount/prepareWorkspace already mean the writable repo copy): use local / mount / sibling / monorepo.
notes:
Consumers to wire (both already thread deps.ROMounts):
- repro: patch.go:485 Spec.ROMounts, promote.go:246 depMounts, repro.go ResolveDeps call (~162).
- funnel: helpers.go:179 NewSandboxExecTool, funnel.go:520 ResolveDeps call.

Keep separate from the registry-axis selector: recommendDepStrategy (internal/cli/prime.go), the dep_strategy config enum (config.go ~682), and ValidDepStrategy (deps.go:94) are the off/host/fetch axis; the local-mount layer is its OWN config and must NOT extend that enum.

RO sufficiency: repro/refuter only read dep sources and compile to scratch (GOCACHE-style); they don't mutate dep dirs, so RO is correct and matches the whole dep-strategy contract. Ecosystems that insist on writing into a workspace member (lockfile regen, codegen into a sibling) fail loudly -- acceptable, consistent with the offline posture.

File the auto-derive-from-manifest enhancement as a SEPARATE bead that depends on this one.
close_reason: Added config.Sandbox.LocalMounts ([]{host,container}) as an ADDITIVE read-only ROMount layer (Shared=true, no :Z), orthogonal to off/host/fetch (composes with them), resolved in ResolveDeps independent of ecosystem detection. v1 paths from operator config only. Validated (absolute/unique-container/existing-dir). Wired through repro + funnel via localMountsFromConfig.

title: store: add withTx/buildPlaceholders/chunked-IN helpers; fix ConsumeLeads unbounded IN-clause
description: Transaction boilerplate (BeginTx + defer Rollback + Prepare + loop + Commit) is duplicated ~8x: filestate.go UpsertFileStates/RefreshContentHashes/TouchScanCoverage, cartographer.go UpsertPackageSummaries, leads.go ConsumeLeads, suppressions.go AddSuppression, etc. IN-clause placeholder builders diverge: leads.go:99 strings.Repeat(",?",n)[1:] vs cartographer.go/filestate.go:235 strings.Repeat("?,",n)+trim. chunkStrings exists in filestate.go but cartographer re-implements the chunk loop. CORRECTNESS: leads.go ConsumeLeads (leads.go:89) builds ONE IN-clause with NO sqliteMaxVars chunking — unlike GetPackageSummaries which chunks — so an ids slice over the host-param limit would fail. Latent, low-probability today.
acceptance_criteria: (1) withTx(ctx, fn) helper; batch upserts use it. (2) buildPlaceholders(n) as the single source; all 3 sites use it. (3) chunkStrings shared (cartographer imports it) and ConsumeLeads chunks its DELETE...IN over sqliteMaxVars. (4) Regression test: ConsumeLeads with more than sqliteMaxVars ids succeeds and deletes them all.
close_reason: withTx/buildPlaceholders/chunkStrings shared helpers; ConsumeLeads now chunks its IN-clause over sqliteMaxVars (correctness fix, tested). Merged c6d8117.

title: agent+funnel: unify agent-runner construction and outcome classification across roles
description: agent.Runner is constructed at 6 sites with 3 divergent option conventions: funnel finder/verifier (hypothesize.go:793, verify.go:68/116) use WithLimits+WithMaxTokens+transcriptOption; repro reproducer (repro/repro.go:285) uses WithLimits+WithTranscriptDir; patch-prover (repro/patch.go:360) uses WithLimits ONLY — no transcript wiring at all (asymmetry). Budget-stop detection diverges: finder budgetStopped() (hypothesize.go:861) counts TruncTokenBudget OR TruncBudgetPool; verify (verify.go:79,126) checks ONLY TruncBudgetPool, so a refuter/arbiter that exhausts its own per-run token budget is misclassified into the unparseable/failed path. Postmortem capture (finderPostmortem/classifyFinderErr/buildFinderPostmortem) exists only for finder. hasSandbox tool-presence scan duplicated at verify.go:44 and 109.
design: Do NOT build the AgentRunnerFactory/SystemPromptFactory mega-interface the audit sketched — over-abstraction here. Keep per-role system-prompt functions; share only the mechanical construction + outcome classification.
acceptance_criteria: (1) One shared budget-stop predicate used by BOTH finder and verify (single definition; verify counts per-run-token-budget stops as budget stops). (2) A shared runner-construction helper (role, budget-derived limits, transcript, maxTokens) so reproducer AND patch-prover get consistent transcript wiring (patch-prover gains it). (3) hasSandbox extracted to one helper. (4) Optional: generalize finderPostmortem into a classifyOutcome reusable by verify/cartographer. Test: a verify run stopped by its own token budget is classified budget-stopped, not parse-failed.
close_reason: Shared budgetStopped predicate now used by finder AND verify (verify now counts own-token-budget stops as budget-stops, not parse-failures); newAgentRunner builder + hasSandboxExec helper; patch-prover gained transcript wiring. Merged 852e21a.

title: funnel: route cartographer summaries through agent.Runner (close finder/cartographer asymmetry)
description: internal/funnel/cartographer.go:388 summarizePackage calls llm.Client.Complete directly (raw llm.Request), bypassing the agent.Runner that finder/verifier/reproducer use. Versus its siblings it therefore lacks: RunJSON schema + one-shot repair (a single malformed completion is silently lost), transcript autosave (f.transcriptOption never applied), max-tokens continuation stitch, and the per-turn budget BudgetCheck hook (regenSummaries only checks finderOverHard BETWEEN launches at cartographer.go:337/354 — a summary in flight ignores the shared pool). Same class as the original cartographer/finder gap. StripThinkBlocks is hand-applied at cartographer.go:449 where RunJSON.stripBody already does it.
design: Prefer reusing NewRunner(client, nil tools, cartographySystemPrompt, WithMaxTokens, transcriptOption, WithLimits(budget-derived)) + RunJSON over adding a new no-tool-loop Runner mode. RunJSON with no tools is one completion plus at most one repair, preserving the cheap single-shot cost.
acceptance_criteria: summarizePackage builds a zero-tool agent.Runner and uses RunJSON with a minimal {"summary":string} schema, gaining schema validation+repair, the transcript option, and the budget BudgetCheck hook consistent with finder/verifier. Existing cartographer tests pass; add a test that a first-try think-wrapped/malformed summary is repaired rather than dropped.
close_reason: summarizePackage routed through zero-tool agent.Runner+RunJSON with {summary} schema: gains schema validation+repair, transcript, shared finder-pool budget hook. Removed StripThinkBlocks + redundant DefaultCartographerMaxTokens. Merged 852e21a.

title: tech-debt: consolidate duplicated scaffolding and sibling-agent asymmetries
description: Cross-cutting scope from a 6-way duplication / missing-abstraction audit of internal/. Recurring anti-pattern: sibling components that should share an opt-in, flexible base are hand-rolled and drift in capability — the cartographer-vs-finder gap is the template, and this audit found its cousins. Themes (each a child bead with file:line evidence): (A) agent-runner construction & outcome classification; (B) funnel bounded-fan-out concurrency scaffold; (C) llm provider adapters; (D) store CRUD boilerplate + a latent IN-clause correctness risk; (E) cli command bootstrap; (F) cross-package micro-helpers. Guiding constraint: prefer targeted opt-in helpers over grand frameworks.
design: Recommended sequencing safest-first: F (mechanical helpers) -> E (cli bootstrap) -> D (store helpers + ConsumeLeads fix) -> A (runner unification + cartographer onto Runner) -> C (adapter surgical helpers) -> B LAST (riskiest; touches hot concurrency paths, lean on existing concurrency tests). ANTI-RECOMMENDATIONS (explicitly do NOT build, surfaced by the audit but over-abstraction for this codebase): the grand AgentRunnerFactory/AgentRunnerOpts mega-interface; a SystemPromptFactory interface for the 4 per-role prompts; a BaseAdapter callback-injection framework or generic message-converter builder for the llm adapters. Keep per-role prompt funcs and per-adapter Complete methods; share only mechanical, well-bounded pieces.
acceptance_criteria: Each child cluster either lands as a shared opt-in helper with ALL callers migrated (clean cutover — no parallel conventions left behind) or is explicitly closed won't-do with rationale. No new duplication introduced. go build/vet/test and gofmt -l clean.
close_reason: All 8 children delivered and merged to main (gates green: build/vet/test/gofmt/lint, funnel -race). Agent-runner unified + cartographer onto Runner; store withTx/scanRows/annotateErr + ConsumeLeads IN-clause correctness fix; cli bootstrap; llm shared helpers + capability matrix; internal/util micro-helpers; funnel bounded fan-out primitive (repro intentionally excluded, documented). Over-abstractions from the audit avoided per epic design notes. Not yet pushed to origin (pending review).

title: cartographer: package summaries have inconsistent format (no house template)
description: Live dogfood evidence (../the_cloud/.bugbot/state.db, package_summaries, 293 rows): cartographer summaries have no consistent format. 154/293 lead with a markdown heading (#), 130 with H1, 24 with H2; 139 lead with bold (**Purpose:**). Opening patterns scatter across at least: '# Package Summary', '# Package Summary:', '## Package Summary', '## Summary', '# Package: <name>', '**Purpose:**', '**Package Purpose:**', '**Purpose**:' (colon outside bold). 292/293 are multi-line and lengths run 471-1531 chars (avg 898) despite cartographySystemPrompt demanding <=120 words, 'Specific, terse, no preamble'. The free-form prompt lets the model ignore 'no preamble' nondeterministically, so the injected 'REPO CONTEXT — package summaries' finder block is noisy and non-uniform across packages.
design: Three options, in increasing robustness: (a) tighten cartographySystemPrompt (internal/funnel/cartographer.go) to forbid headings/bold-labels/preamble and hard-cap length; cheap but still model-dependent. (b) normalize summaries before persisting in summarizePackage: strip leading markdown headings and 'Purpose:'-style labels, collapse to a single paragraph, enforce a word cap — deterministic guard independent of the model. (c) schema-constrain the cartographer output per the parent (bugbot-jwd): have the model emit a fixed JSON object (e.g. {purpose, invariants, caller_assumptions}) and render it to ONE house template at injection time, giving a byte-uniform block. Recommend (c) as the aligned end state with (b) as an interim normalizer. Evidence DB: ../the_cloud/.bugbot/state.db.
acceptance_criteria: Cartographer summaries follow a single deterministic format (no per-summary heading/bold/preamble variance) and a bounded length; the injected REPO CONTEXT block is uniform across packages; verified by re-summarizing a sample and asserting the shape.
notes: Child of bugbot-jwd (schema-constrained grammar output at every agent/phase boundary): the cartographer summary is an agent output at a phase boundary that is currently free prose, not schema-constrained — exactly the gap the parent epic targets.
close_reason: Merged to main (squash cf26fcd). Deterministic normalizeSummary strips leading markdown headings (looped for stacked) + bold-label preambles, collapses to one bounded paragraph (120-word cap); prompt tightened. Interim normalizer (design option b); schema-constrained JSON (option c) stays parent jwd epic. Oracle APPROVE.

title: cartographer: scope importer-graph build to blast radius (large-repo efficiency)
description: On a targeted/blast-radius scan, cartograph() builds the package importer graph over the ENTIRE snapshot via ingest.PackageImporters(snap), parsing imports of every Go file in the repo. Its only consumer, cartography.contextFor, injects only summaries that EXIST in c.summaries — which on a targeted scan is the blast-radius package set (packagesSpanned(targets)). Whole-repo importer edges into packages without summaries are wasted work. On a large repo this is an O(all-files) import parse per scan, duplicating the parse goDependents already did inside BlastRadius.
design: Add ingest.PackageImportersScoped(ctx, snap, inScope map[string]bool): parse imports only of in-scope files and record edges only into in-scope package dirs (localDirs = in-scope dirs). Refactor PackageImporters(snap) to delegate with inScope=nil (whole snapshot). cartograph builds the in-scope set from its targets slice and calls the scoped variant. Correctness: contextFor only injects edges where BOTH endpoints have summaries (=in targets); scoping to targets preserves exactly those, so Targeted behaviour is byte-identical while cost drops from O(snapshot) to O(targets). Sweep (targets=whole snapshot) is unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: PackageImportersScoped parses only in-scope files; whole-snapshot delegation preserves existing PackageImporters output (existing tests green). New test: scoped call over a blast subset yields the same edges among that subset and omits out-of-scope importers. cartograph uses the scoped variant. go test ./internal/ingest/ ./internal/funnel/ green.
close_reason: Added ingest.PackageImportersScoped(snap, inScope); cartograph now scopes the package importer-graph parse to its target file set instead of the whole snapshot. contextFor only injects summaries that exist (=blast-radius packages on a Targeted scan), so scoping is byte-identical for injection while dropping import-parse cost from O(snapshot) to O(targets) on large repos. PackageImporters(snap) kept as the nil-scope wrapper (Sweep unchanged). Tested: scoped call omits out-of-scope importers; wrapper output identical to prior behavior. go test ./internal/ingest ./internal/funnel green.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text: PR review hardening: importer-graph scoping was file-granular, which could drop an edge between two summarized packages when the importing file wasn't itself a target (breaking byte-identical injection). Fixed in cartograph to scope by SPANNED PACKAGE DIR (all files of any package that has a target) — still O(spanned packages), now parity-correct. (commit ce2c74a)

title: bugbot scan --estimate: pre-scan token + duration projection (large-repo planning)
description: Large repos need an up-front estimate of how long a scan will take and how many tokens it will spend before committing budget. Add a --estimate (dry-run) flag to bugbot scan that builds the snapshot and targets exactly like a real scan (Sweep ordering or --since blast radius), computes the EXACT deterministic work breakdown, projects tokens + wall-time from recorded history, prints it, and exits WITHOUT any LLM spend.
design: Funnel.EstimateScan(ctx, kind, changedFiles) returns an Estimate struct. It reuses the real unit machinery so the estimate cannot drift from reality: snapshot()/BlastRadius for targets, scaleFinderForContext for chunk sizing, chunkByLanguage, lensesByYield, builtinStrategies, buildUnits (exact finder-unit count), EnumerateSeams, packagesSpanned + GetPackageSummaries (cartographer cached/uncached). Projection: calibrate avgTotalTokensPerUnit = sum(run tokens)/sum(finder_runs) and throughput = sum(tokens)/sum(wall) over recent FINISHED runs (store.RunMetrics, extended with FinderRuns); estTokens = finderUnits*avgTokensPerUnit (+cartographer marginal), estWall = estTokens/throughput. Prefer same-kind history; fall back to labeled built-in priors when no history. CLI: scan --estimate prints the breakdown + projection + calibration provenance and returns before running the funnel.
