acceptance_criteria: bugbot scan --estimate prints scope (files/packages/chunks/lenses), exact finder-unit count, cartographer cached/uncached, projected tokens + duration, and calibration source; makes ZERO LLM calls. EstimateScan unit-tested: exact unit count matches buildUnits for a fixture; calibration from seeded scan_runs yields finite token/time; empty history falls back to priors with provenance flagged. store.RunMetrics exposes FinderRuns. go test ./internal/funnel/ ./internal/store/ ./internal/cli/ green.
close_reason: Added bugbot scan --estimate: Funnel.EstimateScan(ctx,kind,changed) reuses the production builders (BlastRadius, scaleFinderForContext, chunkByLanguage, lensesByYield, buildUnits, EnumerateSeams, packagesSpanned + GetPackageSummaries) so the work breakdown is EXACT and cannot drift — anti-drift test asserts estimate FinderUnits == actual Sweep Stats.FinderRuns. Token/time projected from store.RunMetrics (extended with FinderRuns): avgTokens/finder-unit and tokens/sec throughput over recent finished runs, preferring same-kind+cartographer-mode sample; labeled built-in priors when no history (duration reported unknown). CLI prints scope/units/cartographer + projection + provenance and returns before any LLM call (verified by e2e smoke: sweep 9 units, targeted blast-radius 10 units incl diff-intent, exit 0, 0 spend). Tests green in funnel/store/cli; lint+vet+gofmt clean.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text: PR review hardening: (1) the estimate chunked targets in snapshot order while Sweep heat-orders before chunking, so the finder-unit count could drift on polyglot repos (chunkByLanguage packs per-language tails by order). Fixed by sharing Sweep's ordering via a new orderSweepTargets helper; added a polyglot anti-drift test. (2) Mirror hypothesize's empty-targets+nil-ChangeContext early return so seam units aren't overcounted. (3) humanCount 999_500..999_999 now renders 1.0M not 1000k. (commit ce2c74a)

title: image recommendation and doctor ignore polyglot — single-language image passes validation silently
description: For a polyglot repo (the_cloud: TS 1534 > Py 593 > Go 400) prime picks the image by build-system priority (go_module) not language dominance, and bugbot doctor checks image presence but never cross-checks the configured image toolchains against detected languages, so a Go image for a TS/Py/Go repo passes validation.
design: In doctor (internal/cli/doctor.go) add a WARN check: map each detected dominant language to expected image-name hints (go->golang, python->python, typescript/javascript->node, rust->rust, c/cpp->gcc, etc.); if a detected language has no matching hint in the configured sandbox.image, warn that repro/verify of that language findings will fail in this image. Also warn when build system is bazel and network=none (offline bazel repro unsupported). Heuristic by image-name substring (no container introspection).
acceptance_criteria: doctor with image golang:1.25-alpine on a TS/Py/Go repo emits a WARN naming the uncovered languages; a matching image suppresses the warn; doctor tests added; go test ./internal/cli/ passes.
close_reason: doctor cross-checks sandbox image vs detected languages and warns on bazel+network=none; verified on the_cloud: WARNs typescript, python, and offline-bazel

title: Elixir source is unclassified (LangOther) — add language detection and persona
description: the_cloud has 182 .ex/.exs files (a flight-pipeline atom) but ingest classifies them as LangOther: no entry in extLang (internal/ingest/lang.go), so no persona/lens manifestation and excluded from DominantLanguages.
design: Add LangElixir to the Language vocabulary, map .ex and .exs to LangElixir in extLang, add displayName Elixir in internal/ingest/profile.go. Update any Language maps/switches found via lsp references. SCOPE: classification + persona ONLY. Tree-sitter grammar for Elixir code-nav and an Elixir LSP server are OUT OF SCOPE (separate follow-up): correct Elixir tags queries are non-trivial (def/defp/defmodule are macro call nodes) and risk wrong navigation.
acceptance_criteria: DetectLanguage of x.ex and x.exs is LangElixir; ExtensionsForLanguage(LangElixir) returns [.ex .exs]; PersonaLanguages includes Elixir; lang/profile tests updated; go test ./internal/ingest/ passes.
close_reason: .ex/.exs classify as LangElixir with 'Elixir' persona; tree-sitter grammar + LSP intentionally left as follow-up per design

title: go install bare module path: relocate main from cmd/bugbot to module root
description: `go install github.com/dpoage/bugbot@latest` failed with 'module ... found but does not contain package github.com/dpoage/bugbot' because package main lived at cmd/bugbot/, leaving the module root with no installable package. Users instinctively `go install` the bare module path; for a single-binary CLI the main package belongs at the module root so the module path IS the install path.
design: Considered keeping cmd/bugbot and only documenting the /cmd/bugbot@latest path, but that just re-documents a papercut users keep hitting. Bugbot ships exactly one binary, so root-level main is the idiomatic, friendliest layout. Clean cutover (no shim): git mv cmd/bugbot/main.go -> ./main.go, removed cmd/, Makefile PKG := ., updated README + backend.go comment to the bare path. Also aligned CI setup-go go-version 1.26 -> 1.25 so the lowered module floor (bugbot-cwk: go 1.25.0) is actually exercised in CI rather than silently always built on 1.26.
acceptance_criteria: `go list -f {{.Name}} github.com/dpoage/bugbot` prints 'main'; `go install github.com/dpoage/bugbot@latest` (and local `go install .`) produces a binary named bugbot that runs self-contained; `make build` still produces the ~65MB subset binary; treesitter/cli tests pass; CI builds/vets/tests on Go 1.25.
notes: Builds on bugbot-cwk (go directive lowered to 1.25.0). Verified under GOTOOLCHAIN=local go1.25.10. main.go content unchanged (package main importing internal/cli). The pre-existing pseudo-version users saw (v0.0.0-...-862410b) predates this layout; @latest will resolve to the new commit once pushed.
close_reason: Moved package main from cmd/bugbot/ to the module root so `go install github.com/dpoage/bugbot@latest` resolves (was failing: module found but no package at root). Clean cutover: removed cmd/, Makefile PKG=., README + backend.go updated to the bare path, CI go-version 1.26->1.25. Verified `go list .` name=main, `go install .` -> bugbot binary runs, make build -> subset, tests pass under go1.25.10.

title: Make repo go-install-friendly: lower gratuitous go 1.26.4 directive to 1.25.0
description: go.mod pinned `go 1.26.4`, a patch-level, gratuitously-high floor that made `go install github.com/dpoage/bugbot/cmd/bugbot@latest` unfriendly: under GOTOOLCHAIN=auto (default) every user below 1.26.4 is forced to download a 1.26.4 toolchain; under GOTOOLCHAIN=local (CI/corporate) it hard-fails ('go.mod requires go >= 1.26.4'). The code never needed 1.26 — it compiles and tests pass on go1.25.10, and the dependency-graph floor is 1.25.0 (modernc.org/sqlite/libc/x/sys et al.). The grammar -tags are NOT a blocker: a no-tags `go install` already works and embeds every grammar (~86MB full binary), which is the intended/supported install output per user direction (default install = full install).
design: Considered making the lean grammar_subset binary the go-install default: impossible — build tags cannot be defaulted-on for remote `go install` (no -tags, and GOTREESITTER_GRAMMAR_SET is runtime-only, does not shrink the binary). Per user direction the full all-grammars binary IS the supported install output, so make build stays the opt-in size optimization. Real fix is the go directive: lowered go.mod `go 1.26.4` -> `go 1.25.0` (true minimum: deps cap at 1.25.0, bugbot code compiles on local go1.25.10). go.sum and all require blocks unchanged; entire dep diff is one line. Verified under GOTOOLCHAIN=local go1.25.10 (a 1.25 user with no download available).
acceptance_criteria: go.mod `go` directive is 1.25.0; go.sum byte-identical to prior HEAD; `GOTOOLCHAIN=local go build ./...` and `go install ./cmd/bugbot` succeed on go1.25.x and produce the full ~86MB binary that runs self-contained (bugbot --help and bugbot init work outside any checkout); treesitter/cli/store tests pass under 1.25.x; `make build` still produces the ~65MB subset binary; README documents `go install ...@latest` as the full-build install path needing only Go 1.25+.
notes: Only in-module go:embed is store/migrations/*.sql; bugbot init writes compiled-in config.StarterYAML — binary is fully self-contained, safe for go install. backend.go + Makefile comments now warn that the subset cannot be selected via `go install` (no tags), so future maintainers don't try to force it. prime.go tests reference '1.26' but those parse TARGET repos' go.mod, unrelated to bugbot's own directive.
close_reason: Lowered go.mod `go 1.26.4` -> `go 1.25.0` (the true minimum; code compiles on go1.25.10, deps cap at 1.25.0). Any Go 1.25.x user now `go install`s with zero toolchain download; GOTOOLCHAIN=local no longer hard-fails. Default install remains the full all-grammars binary per direction. go.sum unchanged; README/Makefile/backend.go docs updated. Verified build+install+tests under GOTOOLCHAIN=local go1.25.10 and subset `make build`.

title: reproducer emits non-terminating repro tests (idle-killed) for string-truncation findings
description: During live dogfood `bugbot repro` (2026-06-18, image golang:1.26-alpine, AFTER the build-scratch + dynamic-idle-timeout fixes in PR #78), the Tier-2 string-truncation findings (e.g. 'Repro total-cap slice without UTF-8 rune boundary check', 'FixPatch byte-cap slice ...', 'Reasoning/Description byte-cap slice ...') consistently fail to reproduce with verdict 'timeout'. With the new idle watchdog (output+fs+CPU signals) the BUILD phase is correctly kept alive (CPU busy), but the generated test then makes NO progress (no output, no fs writes, no CPU) and is idle-killed at idle_timeout_seconds (120s). The reproducer model is emitting tests that hang / never terminate for this finding class, not a fast failing assertion. This is a reproducer-agent quality issue, distinct from the now-fixed sandbox infra causes.
design: Root cause is the reproducer agent's generated test, not the sandbox. The dynamic idle watchdog (PR #78, internal/sandbox/cli.go) now bounds the waste at idle_timeout_seconds instead of the old fixed 600s and distinguishes a live build (CPU-alive) from a hung test (fully idle). Investigate why the model writes blocking tests (waiting on stdin, unbounded sleep, deadlock) and add guardrails: pass `go test -timeout` in the emitted plan, constrain the reproducer prompt to self-contained terminating tests, and/or a post-generation static check. Complements bugbot-vig (outcome interpretation already prevents mis-promoting a timeout to T1).
acceptance_criteria: For the string-truncation finding class the reproducer either produces a TERMINATING test that demonstrates the bug (non-zero exit with ran-evidence -> T1) or fails fast with a real verdict; never an idle-timeout. Verified on a live --repro run against the bugbot self-scan corpus on a Go-toolchain image. A representative generated repro test for one such finding terminates in < a few seconds.
notes: Evidence, 3 live runs this session (golang:1.26-alpine, dep_strategy off/vendored, timeout_seconds 1800, idle 120): max=3 -> 1 T1 + 2 timeout; max=2 -> 357s, 2 timeout; max=2 post-CPU-fallback -> ~9m, 2 timeout (each attempt = compile kept alive by CPU signal + ~120s idle on the hung test). The one finding that DID reach T1 ('FunctionResponse Name set to call ID') produced a fast terminating test, confirming the pipeline works when the generated test terminates.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text: Guardrails landed + squash-merged in PR #103 (commit 8b3a34e): (1) reproducer systemPrompt now has a 'Self-terminating' hard requirement naming each runner's timeout flag; (2) validatePlan requires -timeout on 'go test' repros (unwrapShell handles bash -c; hasCmdFlag matches -flag and -flag=value), feeding the Attempt revision loop; goodPlan + affected unit/integration plans updated. Unit tests TestValidatePlan_GoTestRequiresTimeout + TestSystemPrompt_TerminationGuidance pass; real-podman TestIntegration_RealSandboxPromotion/_PatchProver pass. OUTSTANDING ACCEPTANCE: the live 'bugbot repro' run on a Go-toolchain image over the self-scan corpus (string-truncation finding class) was NOT performed (needs network + API spend); reopened for that verification step.

title: Cartographer: live-eval measurement + default-on decision
description: Closes the open acceptance criterion (#3) of bugbot-mi5.7, which shipped the Cartographer feature behind Options.Cartographer / scan.cartographer DEFAULT OFF (commit c866b74). Run the eval/dogfood with cartographer ON vs OFF on the bugbot-core corpus to measure finder tokens-per-unit and recall, then decide whether to flip the default to ON. Also evaluate whether the deep strategies (contractTraceDeep/stateTraceDeep) should carry the summary block (currently only the default sweep-wide finderTask does).
acceptance_criteria: Eval run comparing cartographer on/off: report finder input-tokens-per-unit delta and recall/precision delta on the eval suite (neither regresses). If a net win, flip scan.cartographer default to ON and document; else keep opt-in with the measured rationale recorded here. Decision on deep-strategy injection recorded.
notes: Cost model + harness: internal/eval (see compact_measure_test.go pattern used for bugbot-3nf). Cartographer spend is ledgered under the finder role and charged to the finder pool, so per-unit finder token deltas are directly comparable in the agent_units table.
close_reason: Closes mi5.7 AC3. Default-on decision MADE and shipped: scan.cartographer defaults true (PR #75, 13905e6/7c08260). The owner's call reframed the metric from raw-token-reduction (wrong lens for an additive agent) to valid-findings-per-token: per-run instrumentation persists cartographer_enabled + verified + input/output tokens into scan_runs.stats_json (sliceable on/off), and 'bugbot metrics' (store.RunMetrics) prints the verified/1k series + pooled on/off comparison. Prerequisite think-strip + budget fix shipped (PR #76, 2148f0c — cartographer was injecting raw <think> reasoning as the summary). Deep-strategy injection decision: only the default sweep-wide finderTask carries the summary block (documented known-limitation on mi5.7; contractTraceDeep/stateTraceDeep unchanged). Ongoing validation = watch the on/off verified/1k trend across real dogfood runs (a continuous series, not a one-shot gate), so it is not a close blocker.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: Model knob shipped: PR #75 (branch feat/cartographer-model-config) adds an optional [roles.cartographer] config role — selects the summary model, falls back to finder when unset. Spend tagged 'cartographer' but charged to the finder pool, so per-unit finder token deltas remain comparable. This unblocks the on/off eval (criterion 3). NOTE: dogfood config (bugbot.yaml) is MiniMax-M3 for every role, so the immediate experiment is cartographer ON vs OFF (does amortized package context cut finder tokens-per-unit?), not a cheaper-model swap. MiniMax has NO prompt caching (cache_read_weight 1.0), so any finder input reduction is a raw-token win. Recall/precision axis still needs a labeled live run (the recorded eval corpus can't measure cartographer's added calls without re-recording).

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text:
LIVE BOUNDED EVAL (MiniMax-M3, scope=internal/progress 5 files, 2 lenses, isolated /tmp DBs, PR #75 binary). RESULT: feature validated, token win NOT demonstrated on this scope; mi5.16 stays OPEN.

Data — OFF: finder 64 completions / 1,383,193 input; 0 cartographer; 0 summary rows. ON: finder 62 completions / 1,392,433 input; cartographer 1 call (6,074 in / 284 out); 1 package_summaries row (internal/progress, 1212-char summary, fingerprint persisted = cache-ready). Net input OFF 1,383,193 vs ON 1,398,507 (+1.1%, within noise).

WORKS: role resolution to a configured cartographer client, one bounded Complete (well under the 24KB/400-tok caps), fingerprint-keyed persistence, ledgered as 'cartographer' role. OFF path byte-identical.

WHY NO WIN HERE: wrong scope for the mechanism. A 5-file package the finder reads in full gains ~nothing from a ~300-token summary; Cartographer targets LARGER/cross-package sweeps where the agent would otherwise tool-call to discover neighbors. n=2-3 finder agents is also swamped by MiniMax-M3 variance, and the ON run had 1/3 lenses fail unparseable (orthogonal reliability issue, bugbot-rwe/210) — totals non-comparable.

NEXT (for a real verdict): larger multi-package sweep (~20-30 files spanning several packages, the original firehose scenario from bugbot-9bd), several runs to beat variance, and inject cross-package direct-dependent summaries (already implemented) where they matter. Until then: default stays OFF; do NOT flip on this evidence.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text:
DECISION (owner's call): default cartographer ON. Rationale: it's a new agent, so it ADDS tokens by construction — raw token reduction is the wrong lens; valid-findings-per-token is the metric that decides its worth. Shipped in PR #75: scan.cartographer defaults true (config.Default + StarterYAML); BUGBOT_SCAN_CARTOGRAPHER=false / scan.cartographer:false opts out.

INSTRUMENTATION (PR #75): the metric is now a fully-derivable per-run time series. Stats already persisted Verified (numerator) + InputTokens/OutputTokens (denominator) into scan_runs.stats_json; added Stats.CartographerEnabled so the series is SLICEABLE by on/off. The pass's own cost is isolable via spend WHERE role='cartographer' GROUP BY scan_run_id. Time axis = scan_runs.started_at. No migration (stats_json is the established per-run counter blob).

Query (verified per 1k tokens, per run, sliced by cartographer):
  SELECT started_at, kind,
    json_extract(stats_json,'$.cartographer_enabled') carto,
    json_extract(stats_json,'$.verified') verified,
    json_extract(stats_json,'$.input_tokens')+json_extract(stats_json,'$.output_tokens') tok,
    ROUND(1000.0*json_extract(stats_json,'$.verified')/NULLIF(json_extract(stats_json,'$.input_tokens')+json_extract(stats_json,'$.output_tokens'),0),4) verified_per_1k
  FROM scan_runs WHERE finished_at IS NOT NULL ORDER BY started_at DESC;

REMAINING: optional read surface (CLI). Validation is now ongoing (compare the series across real dogfood runs) rather than a one-shot eval. mi5.16 closeable on PR #75 merge.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: Read surface shipped in PR #75: 'bugbot metrics' (store.RunMetrics) prints the per-run verified/1k series + pooled cartographer on/off comparison. All three deliverables (default-on decision, per-run instrumentation, read surface) are now in PR #75 with tests + clean gates. mi5.16 is closeable on merge; ongoing validation = watch the on-vs-off verified/1k trend across real dogfood runs.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: PREREQUISITE FIX (PR #76): the default-on cartographer was storing/injecting MiniMax-M3's raw <think>...</think> reasoning as the 'summary' (summarizePackage never stripped it; the finder JSON path did). It also truncated on large packages (MaxTokens=400 caps reasoning+summary combined). Until PR #76, any findings-per-token on/off comparison would be measuring finders fed reasoning garbage — not a real test. PR #76: moves the stripper to llm.StripThinkBlocks, raises cartographer budgets (out 400->8192, input 24K->128K), surfaces the previously-swallowed upsert error, and adds 'bugbot cartography' (view + --run out-of-band). Verified live on MiniMax: clean summary persisted, cache reuse on re-run. Re-run the findings-per-token eval only after #76 merges.

title: analyzer/miner: add semgrep/clippy/gosec analyzers + AST contradiction miners

title: funnel: self-consistency multi-sample finder voting
description: Weak models are cheap and noisy. Run a lens N times at temp>0 and keep candidates that recur across samples (agreement = signal). Trades abundant tokens for scarce reasoning reliability.
acceptance_criteria: A configurable sample count drives N finder passes per (lens,chunk); only candidates recurring in >=K samples survive to triage; precision on scripted eval unaffected; unit test covers the voting.

title: funnel: mixed-tier escalation to strong model on refuter split/disagreement
description: Today the arbiter on a split panel is the same (weak) verifier model. Escalate only the ambiguous candidates to a stronger model, spending the expensive model on the ~5% that need it.
acceptance_criteria: On split verdict, the arbiter resolves via a configured stronger role/model when present; falls back to current behavior when unset; test covers escalation path.

title: Persist killed hypotheses for verifier-precision auditing
description:
Persist killed hypotheses (verifier-refuted candidates) so verifier precision is auditable on production scans.

## Problem

A "hypothesis" is a `funnel.Candidate` (finder-proposed bug). It has four terminal fates, wildly asymmetric in what they persist:

- **Survived** -> `findings` row, T2 open (`verify_stream.go:253`). Full content.
- **Budget-orphaned** -> `findings` row, T3 suspected (`persistOrphan`, `verify_stream.go:302`). Full content.
- **Killed by verifier** -> thin `agent_units` row only (`recordVerifierUnit`, `observability.go:89`). Carries lens + file + `refuted=2/3 arbiter=refuted`. NO title, line, description, severity, or kill reasoning.
- **Triage-dropped** -> a `Stats` counter increment only. Nothing about the claim.

The architecture is precision-first and names the verifier as THE precision lever (`funnel.go:6-8`). Today that lever runs as a black box on real repos: you get a `killed` count but cannot distinguish a good kill (false positive correctly removed) from a bad kill (real bug suppressed = a verify-stage false negative). The eval harness exposes "where seeded bugs died" but ONLY on seeded corpora -- never on production scans.

## Scope

IN: persist killed candidates + their kill reasoning trace.
OUT: full persistence of triage drops (keep as counters; high volume, low signal). Out-of-scope-drop sampling is explicitly a possible later follow-up, NOT part of this work.

## Two facts that make this cheap and safe

1. The kill reasoning is computed-then-discarded. At the kill site (`verify_stream.go:199`, branch `:239-243`) `verdicts` + `seatNames` are in scope; `buildReasoning(verdicts, seatNames, arbiterReasoning, arbiterRan)` -- already called for survivors at `:250` -- would produce the identical trace. The killed branch returns at `:239-243` BEFORE building it. Data exists in memory; it is thrown away.

2. `agent_units.Detail` is deliberately free-text-free (`observability.go:104-106`, `:77-78`: "No model-authored free text -- only structured counts and names that are safe for long-term storage"). Killed-candidate prose MUST NOT go there.
design:
# Recommended design: dedicated bounded `dead_hypotheses` table

Mirror the existing orphan path. Add a `persistKilled()` next to `persistOrphan()` (`verify_stream.go:302`); it is nearly free because the verdicts/seatNames are already in scope at the kill site.

## Why a dedicated table, not a `findings` tier

The `findings` table carries suppression memory and fingerprint identity:
- `UpsertFinding` forces `StatusDismissed` for any fingerprint that was ever suppressed (`store/findings.go:127-151`).
- Fingerprint (`lens+file+line+title`) is the dedup/upsert key.

If killed candidates were written into `findings`, a later genuine verification of the same fingerprint would collide with the dead record, and a "killed" status risks poisoning suppression memory so a future real bug never resurfaces. A separate store decouples the audit log from the live "real bugs" surface and from suppression identity entirely.

Schema sketch (`dead_hypotheses`): id, scan_run_id, fingerprint, lens, file, line, title, severity, kill verdict breakdown (seats + refuted count + arbiter verdict, structured), reasoning trace (free text, acceptable here for the same reason `findings` stores free text -- a deliberately-scoped store, NOT injection-sensitive `agent_units.Detail`), created_at.

## Retention

Bounded, exactly like `agent_units`: killed >> survived under precision-first, so this grows fast. Reuse the `PruneAgentUnits` pattern (`store/agentunits.go:114`, `keepRuns=200`) -- add a sibling `PruneDeadHypotheses` (or fold both into one prune pass keyed on the same recent-scan_runs set) and wire it into the same call site that already prunes agent_units.

## Lighter alternative (documented, NOT recommended)

A non-reportable `findings` tier ("refuted"). Smaller diff, reuses upsert machinery, BUT: must be persisted `StatusOpen` (never dismissed) to avoid poisoning suppression, and every findings reader must filter the new tier or the precision surface is muddied. The fingerprint-collision risk above is why the dedicated table is preferred.

## Call-site changes

- `verify_stream.go` `candKilled` branch (`:239-243`): before returning, build the kill trace and call `persistKilled(...)`. Keep `reg.SignalPersisted(c.Fingerprint, false)` semantics (a killed primary still discards staged corroboration). Best-effort: a failed insert is `f.note(...)`-logged and never aborts the scan, matching `persistOrphan`/`recordVerifierUnit`.
- Add the store table + migration under `internal/store/migrations/`, an `AddDeadHypothesis` writer, a `ListDeadHypotheses(scanRunID)` reader, and `scanDeadHypothesis` row scanner -- mirror `agentunits.go` structure.
- Optionally surface in the `report`/CLI path that already reads `agent_units` so an operator can inspect kills (mirror the existing "report units" path referenced in `hypothesize.go:788`). Treat CLI surfacing as a stretch goal if it balloons scope.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Killed candidates (candKilled branch in verify_stream.go) are persisted to a bounded dead_hypotheses store carrying fingerprint, lens, file, line, title, severity, structured kill-verdict breakdown (seats+refuted count+arbiter verdict), and the reasoning trace from buildReasoning. 2) Kill prose is NOT written to agent_units.Detail (free-text-free invariant preserved). 3) Records are scoped to scan_run_id and pruned with the same recent-runs retention as PruneAgentUnits (keepRuns). 4) Persistence is best-effort: a failed insert is f.note-logged and never aborts the scan (matches persistOrphan). 5) reg.SignalPersisted(fingerprint,false) semantics on the killed path are unchanged (staged corroboration still discarded). 6) Triage drops remain counters only -- no per-drop persistence. 7) New store writer/reader/scanner + migration land under internal/store with tests; a unit test asserts a killed candidate produces a dead_hypotheses row with the kill trace and that no findings row is created for it.
notes: Verdict from design discussion: YES improve, but ONLY for killed candidates -- triage drops stay counters (high volume, low signal). Key cheap-and-safe facts: kill reasoning is already computable at the kill site (verdicts/seatNames in scope, buildReasoning identical to survivor path) but the killed branch returns before building it; agent_units.Detail is deliberately free-text-free so kill prose must live in a separate store. Asymmetry to fix: budget-orphaned (a less interesting outcome) persists a full T3 finding, but killed -- the verifier's core output -- vanishes. Prefer a dedicated dead_hypotheses table over a findings tier to avoid fingerprint-collision and suppression-memory poisoning (UpsertFinding forces StatusDismissed for ever-suppressed fingerprints). Mirror agentunits.go for store structure and PruneAgentUnits for retention.
close_reason: Merged to main (squash de8d546). New bounded dead_hypotheses store (migration 016) persists verifier-killed candidates: structured kill-verdict breakdown + buildReasoning trace; persistKilled at candKilled branch (best-effort, agent_units.Detail free-text invariant preserved); PruneAgentUnits-style retention. Oracle APPROVE.

title: openai-compatible adapter: surface reasoning_content from JSON.ExtraFields when Content is empty
description: internal/llm/openai.go toResponse() reads only choice.Message.Content. The openai-go SDK ChatCompletionMessage struct has no reasoning_content field; reasoning models that emit a SEPARATE reasoning field (DeepSeek-R1 style reasoning_content, MiniMax reasoning_details under reasoning_split) land in JSON.ExtraFields and are silently dropped — yielding empty resp.Text and the same 'no parseable output' symptom the token-budget fix (bugbot-ci0) addressed from the other direction. Blocked on confirming (via the live-investigation bead) whether MiniMax actually uses this shape; if it does, this is the real fix and the budget raise only masks it.
acceptance_criteria: If a supported reasoning model emits reasoning in a separate field, toResponse surfaces it into resp.Text when Content is empty (or strips/handles it appropriately), covered by a conformance test using a recorded response with empty content + populated reasoning field. Otherwise close with rationale that no in-use provider needs it.
close_reason: Not applicable: live investigation (bead comments 2026-06-17) confirmed MiniMax emits reasoning inline as <think> in content, not a separate reasoning_content/reasoning_details field; no in-use openai-compatible provider needs ExtraFields surfacing. Reopen if a provider with separate-field reasoning is adopted.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: UNBLOCKED by bugbot-hp4 (merged, commit 2747944). The finder failure path now records a finderPostmortem to agent_units.Detail with raw model output head + had_think flag + class. To confirm the M2.7 ExtraFields/reasoning_content hypothesis: run a finder against a reasoning model that fails to parse, then 'report units' and inspect the Detail field — raw_head will show whether Content was empty while reasoning landed elsewhere. Confirm from THAT data before implementing, rather than SDK-struct guessing.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Live finding (2026-06-17, from bugbot-ci0): MiniMax-M2.7 does NOT use a separate reasoning_content/reasoning_details field — message object keys are content/role/name/audio_content only, and reasoning is inline as <think>...</think> in content (already handled by agent/runjson.go parseInto). So the ExtraFields/reasoning_content surfacing this bead proposes is NOT needed for MiniMax. Keep 3b0 only if a DIFFERENT openai-compatible provider is observed to put reasoning in a separate JSON field; otherwise it can be closed as not-applicable.

title: Confirm MiniMax reasoning-transport shape against live API + verify 32768 clears truncation
description: The bugbot-ci0 fix is robust regardless of mechanism but the precise cause was never confirmed live (no MINIMAX_API_KEY at fix time). Run a live finder against MiniMax M2.7 with BUGBOT_TRANSCRIPT_DIR set and inspect a finder transcript: does CoT arrive inline as <think> in message.content, or via a separate reasoning_content/reasoning_details field that internal/llm/openai.go toResponse drops (it reads only choice.Message.Content)? Also confirm max_output_tokens=32768 actually clears the truncation on a real M2.7 run.
acceptance_criteria: Live M2.7 transcript inspected; reasoning-transport shape documented on this bead; confirmed whether 32768 clears the failure or a higher value is needed. If CoT comes via a dropped field, file/escalate the openai.go adapter fix (read reasoning_content from JSON.ExtraFields when Content is empty).

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Reasoning-transport shape CONFIRMED live (2026-06-17, via bugbot-ci0): MiniMax-M2.7 emits inline <think>...</think> in message.content, no reasoning_content field; production adapter path verified by 'go test -tags live -run TestLive' (LLM_LIVE_BASE_URL=https://api.minimax.io/v1 LLM_LIVE_MODEL=MiniMax-M2.7) — completion + tool round-trip + usage all pass. The remaining mnr scope is the token-budget half: verify max_output_tokens=32768 clears truncation for reasoning-heavy finders (still operator-held per ci0).

title: ingest: --end-of-options in ChangedFiles AND UnifiedDiff (finder missed the latter) + parseHeat scanner limit (GH #57 #60)
description: (1) change.go:44 — ChangedFiles passes fromCommit/toCommit without --end-of-options while CommitMessage (change.go:141-150) documents it as the package convention. No trust boundary crossed (operator's own --since flag; PR SHAs are 40-hex; git forbids dash-leading branch names) so this is hardening/low, not the published medium injection — but the convention claim in the doc comment is currently false. (2) NEW finding from diagnosis, missed by the scanner which wrongly cleared it: UnifiedDiff (change.go:130) joins from+"..."+to into a single argv that still leads with '-' if from does — e.g. from="--output=/tmp/x" yields arg "--output=/tmp/x...HEAD" which git parses as an option that writes the diff to a file; the user --since flag DOES flow there via buildScanChangeContext (scan.go:454). Harden identically (--end-of-options is positional, works before the joined arg). (3) heat.go:113-138 — bufio.Scanner with default 64KiB token limit and no sc.Err() check; a long line silently truncates the heat map. Near-unreachable (lines are timestamps + repo paths) and within ChurnHeat's documented best-effort contract, but the repo's own convention elsewhere (prdiff.go, transcript.go, tools_grep.go) raises buffers and checks Err.
acceptance_criteria: (1) --end-of-options inserted before refs in ChangedFiles and before the joined range arg in UnifiedDiff; tests mirroring TestCommitMessage's dash-injection guard for both. (2) parseHeat: replace scanner with strings.Split over the in-memory data (no token limit) or sc.Buffer sized to input; behavior stays error-free per the best-effort contract. (3) GH #57 #60 closed.
close_reason: Merged to main in PR #81 (commit 37c72ab, 'batch of 8 bounded bugfixes'). Dolt was initialized after this work and never recorded the close; reconciled against committed bead state + verified code on main.

title: agent: bound renderBodyRange first line + flush SaveJSONL buffer on encode error (GH #59 #63)
description: (1) tools_read_symbol.go:287 — the 'i > startLine' clause in the byte-budget guard is an intentional at-least-one-line progress guarantee, but it's unbounded: the only outer cap is codeNavMaxFileBytes (5 MiB), so a minified/generated one-line file emits up to ~5 MiB into the tool result. runTool (runner.go:372) returns tool output verbatim — no downstream cap — so a giant first line blows out the prompt (1-2M tokens → API rejection kills the agent step; sub-limit cases silently inflate cost x2 via transcript EventRequest). Also: a single-line range that's oversized gets NO truncation marker today (byteTruncated stays false). Medium is right; common in real repos. (2) transcript.go:130 — encode error returns without bw.Flush(), and because bufio flushes at 4KB byte boundaries the file likely ends mid-JSON-line, which LoadJSONL (transcript.go:151-153) hard-fails on: the transcript isn't just truncated, it's unloadable. The comment at runner.go:419-420 claiming Close flushes is wrong. Realistic trigger: invalid json.RawMessage in ToolCall.Arguments from provider tool args (openai.go:169 passes them through unvalidated).
acceptance_criteria: (1) renderBodyRange: when i == startLine and cost > byteBudget, emit line[:budget] walked back to a rune boundary with a '… [line truncated]' marker, set byteTruncated, break; single-line oversized ranges get the marker too. Do NOT copy gitBlame's flat 256B/line cap. Test with a >budget first line. (2) SaveJSONL: best-effort bw.Flush() before the error return; fix the false comment at runner.go:419-420. Test that an encode-failure transcript still LoadJSONLs the prefix. (3) GH #59 #63 closed.
close_reason: Merged to main in PR #81 (commit 37c72ab, 'batch of 8 bounded bugfixes'). Dolt was initialized after this work and never recorded the close; reconciled against committed bead state + verified code on main.

title: lsp: Manager.Close late-spawn guard + server.go stdin close on StdoutPipe failure (GH #52 #53)
description: Two real lsp findings from dogfood, both cheap. (1) manager.go: liveServer (line 190) never checks closed state — a query goroutine that passed serverFor's closed check before Close() can respawn a server after Close, which is never reaped (second Close is a no-op). Also reachable via the ErrConnDead retry at manager.go:120-125. Currently unreachable from real callers (both pipeline stages wg.Wait before Funnel.Close), so latent contract bug, low/medium not the published HIGH — but the documented 'safe for concurrent use' contract should hold, especially before bugbot-cy8 (daemon-lifetime manager) makes Close-concurrent-with-queries real. (2) server.go: if StdinPipe succeeds and StdoutPipe fails (fd exhaustion only), the stdin os.Pipe fds leak until GC; one-line stdin.Close() on the error branch at server.go:123-126.
acceptance_criteria: (1) In Close, while holding ms.mu, set ms.failed = errors.New("lsp: manager is closed — fall back to grep") so liveServer's existing failed gate (line 194) blocks late spawns on both routes; message must contain 'manager is closed' which shouldFallBack (tools_codenav_tiered.go:216) already recognizes. Do NOT read m.closed under ms.mu (lock-order inversion). (2) Race test: Close concurrent with in-flight query leaves no live server process. (3) stdin.Close() added on StdoutPipe error branch; no cleanup added to the cmd.Start error branch (exec's deferred cleanup already handles it). (4) GH #52 #53 closed.
notes: Severity correction recorded in bugbot-bar. The #53 published description's os/exec claim is false — see bugbot-c45.
close_reason: Merged to main in PR #81 (commit 37c72ab, 'batch of 8 bounded bugfixes'). Dolt was initialized after this work and never recorded the close; reconciled against committed bead state + verified code on main.

title: Refuter parse failure is fail-open: unparseable verdict counts as 'could not refute'
description: Dogfood scan log line 151: '1/3 refuter(s) produced no parseable verdict for "Integer overflow in read_file offset" — treated as could not refute'. A refuter that crashes or emits garbage currently counts toward a finding SURVIVING verification. With flaky providers (6/126 finder units and 1/54 verifier units produced unparseable output this run) this systematically biases the funnel toward publishing. That particular finding happened to be killed by the arbiter anyway, but the policy is wrong: a missing verdict is evidence of nothing.
acceptance_criteria: (1) A refuter unit with no parseable verdict is either retried (at least once) or excluded from the panel denominator — never counted as a could-not-refute vote. (2) If the panel drops below quorum (e.g. <2 valid verdicts), the finding goes to arbiter or is re-run, with the degraded panel recorded in agent_units detail. (3) Unit test covering the parse-failure path.
notes: Related: bugbot-rwe (parse-failure concentration with MiniMax-M3) — fixing output-token exhaustion reduces frequency, but the fail-open default is wrong independent of frequency.
close_reason: Merged to main: commit d1ae1c7 'fix(funnel): don't promote a finding when all refuters fail to verify (bugbot-8rd)'.

title: Severity calibration: verifier needs reachability (caller-trace) and trust-boundary seats
description: Dogfood diagnosis showed severity is assigned by pattern-matching category labels (race/leak/injection) rather than consequences. The run's only HIGH (lsp Manager Close race, GH #52) is a real latent race but unreachable from any current caller: every query goroutine is joined by wg.Wait in hypothesize.go:492 / verify.go:267 before Funnel.Close runs, and worst case is one orphaned gopls that dies on stdin EOF at process exit — a low/medium. Meanwhile 'ChangedFiles omits --end-of-options' (GH #57) got medium under an injection lens though every dash-capable input traces to the local operator's own CLI flag (PR SHAs are 40-hex, git forbids dash-leading branch names) — no privilege boundary crossed, a consistency low. The repro prompt-injection finding (GH #55) ignored that the 'attacker' already controls sandbox exit codes/output directly, making injection strictly weaker than existing channels. No refuter seat traces callers beyond the immediate function or asks who controls the input.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Verifier panel includes a seat (or arbiter rubric) that must trace call sites to answer 'can the program reach this state' and 'who controls the inputs', and severity must cite that analysis. (2) Replayed dogfood findings #52→low/medium, #57→low, #55→info with reachability rationale recorded in the verdict detail.
notes: Cheap partial: add reachability+trust-boundary questions to the existing arbiter prompt rather than a new seat. Related observation: refuters are excellent at 'is the code shaped as claimed', weak at dynamic semantics and threat-model context — see bugbot-aud for the execute-the-claim complement.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: Triage: merge same-root-cause findings before verification/publish (dogfood: 5 issues for one UTF-8 truncation pattern)
description: Dogfood scan published 5 separate findings/GH issues (#54 #56 #58 #61 #62) for one root cause: byte-cap truncation without utf8.RuneStart walk-back in internal/cli/publish.go — three sites in one function (renderIssueBody) and two sites 11 lines apart in the same WalkDir closure (renderReproSection 729/740). Each was independently triple-refuted (~5x verification compute for a fix-once issue) and a human must triage 5 issues for one helper-function fix. Triage stage reported duplicate=0 — it currently has no same-file/same-pattern/same-fix merge concept, only fingerprint dedup.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Triage stage merges candidates sharing file + defect pattern + fix locus into one finding with multiple site references before verification. (2) Replaying the dogfood publish.go candidates yields 1 finding listing 5 sites, verified once. (3) Merge is logged in triage drop counters (e.g. merged=N).
notes: Severity ranking unaffected: merged finding keeps max severity of members. Distinct-subsystem same-pattern findings should NOT merge (cross-file merge is a tuning question — start file-scoped).
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: Deep-strategy autopsy: why did contract-trace-deep whiff on ig7 with the seeds in hand, and what did its 2 killed candidates claim?
description: The mi5.12 held-out test failed (2026-06-12 dogfood): contract-trace-deep units covered the chunks containing internal/config/config.go and internal/daemon/daemon.go, ran finderOK at ~800k tokens each, and emitted zero candidates — while the bug's two sites were one find_references apart. Its only 2 candidates (other chunks) were killed in verify. The run had NO transcript capture (TranscriptDir unset), so behavior must be reproduced, not exhumed: (1) re-run JUST the deep unit over the config.go chunk with transcripts enabled (bugbot scan --lens api-contract-misuse against a target list pinned to that chunk, transcript dir set) and read what the agent actually did — did it enumerate the budget fields as contracts? did it call find_references on them? did it visit daemon.go and dismiss the contradiction, or never look? (2) pull the 2 killed candidates' verify traces from the dogfood findings... they were killed pre-persist; check whether killed candidates leave any trace (they do not persist — note this as an observability gap if confirmed: killed-candidate reasoning is unrecoverable, consider persisting kill traces in agent_units.detail or a kill log). (3) From the transcript evidence, iterate the strategy clause (e.g. explicitly instruct comparing VALIDATION sites against doc-comment claims; require listing enumerated contracts in the output so non-enumeration is visible) or conclude the prompt is fine and the model is the limit (try the same unit on a stronger finder model as the control). Yield-table note for mi5.14: this run's empirical (lens x strategy) data — boundary-conditions 7/18 verified findings vs hand-rank mid; deep 2 candidates 0 verified.
acceptance_criteria: (1) A transcript of a deep unit over the config.go chunk exists and the failure mode is classified (never-enumerated / enumerated-not-traced / traced-and-dismissed). (2) At least one concrete remediation is implemented or explicitly rejected with the transcript as evidence (clause iteration, output-format requirement, or model control). (3) The killed-candidate-trace observability gap is confirmed-and-filed or refuted. (4) Findings recorded on bugbot-mi5 epic.

title: resource-leaks lens loses 27% of units to parse failures with MiniMax-M3 — output-token exhaustion mid-JSON
description: Dogfood run 2026-06-11 (scan over bugbot's own repo, MiniMax-M3 finder, diagnosed live via the mi5.10 agent_units table in ~30s): parse failures concentrate in ONE lens — resource-leaks 4/15 units (27%) vs <=1/15 for every other lens. Failed units' output_tokens: 2471, 9580, 10036, 1864, 8414 — two clearly EXCEED the DefaultMaxOutputTokens=8192 visible cap and two more are near it, matching the documented MiniMax reasoning-model failure mode on the constant (funnel.go: reasoning models spend the completion allowance in think-blocks; truncated before/while emitting JSON, then the repair round-trip also fails). resource-leaks is currently the top-yielding lens in the same run, so this is the most expensive place to lose findings. Hypotheses to check: (a) resource-leaks' Core/manifestations elicit longer reasoning chains than other lenses (cleanup paths require whole-function tracing); (b) cap is simply too low for M3 on multi-path analysis tasks. Candidate fixes, evaluate in order: (1) raise the output cap for providers/models flagged as reasoning models (per-role or per-provider config knob, e.g. max_output_tokens on the provider entry) — cheapest; (2) make the repair round-trip detect TruncOutputTokens and RE-ASK with 'emit ONLY the JSON now' which works when reasoning already happened; (3) per-lens output budgets if (a) is confirmed. The per-lens parse-failure rate is now queryable per run — whatever ships must cite a before/after rate from agent_units.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Root cause confirmed against transcripts or truncation reasons (TruncOutputTokens vs other) for the failed units. (2) A fix ships behind config or model detection; resource-leaks parse-failure rate on a comparable dogfood sweep drops to the other lenses' baseline (cite agent_units rates before/after). (3) No regression in cost per unit beyond the necessary output-cap delta. (4) Gates clean.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: LIVE EVIDENCE (.bugbot/state.db, 2026-06-17): corroborates output-token exhaustion at scale — 700 cumulative finder parse_failed across 15 runs, per-run runs hitting 125/125. Part of the finder-reliability cluster starving repro of T2 input. Umbrella: bugbot-3lt.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text: Scout 2026-06-29: root cause confirmed. DefaultMaxOutputTokens=8192 is a GLOBAL constant (funnel.go:125-131) applied uniformly via newAgentRunner -> agent.WithMaxTokens (agent_runners.go:65) — no per-model/per-lens/per-role override. Truncation IS detectable: llm.Response.StopReason==StopMaxTokens (llm.go:118) and agent.Outcome.Truncated + TruncationReason (TruncTokenBudget / TruncBudgetPool / TruncMaxIterations) + LastStopReason (agent.go:143-175). On a budget-stopped truncation RunJSON SKIPS its one-shot repair and returns an error (runjson.go:87-88); the finder path (hypothesize.go:859-892, classifyFinderErr 982-993) classifies it finderParseFailed/finderClassUnparseable and the unit yields ZERO candidates with NO retry — that is the 27% loss. NOTE: llm.Capabilities (llm.go:189) has NO reasoning/thinking flag, so a 'raise cap for reasoning models' fix would need new capability detection + adapter wiring (bigger). OFFLINE-FIX OPTIONS: (1) bump DefaultMaxOutputTokens 8192->32768 (trivial but affects all roles/cost; not unit-testable; exact value is live-gated — see bugbot-mnr 'verify 32768 clears truncation against live API'); (2) detect a finder unit lost to PER-CALL cap truncation (Outcome.Truncated && TruncationReason==TruncTokenBudget, NOT TruncBudgetPool/MaxIterations) and retry that unit's RunJSON ONCE with a doubled per-completion cap — behavioral + unit-testable (mock truncated-then-complete -> unit recovers; pool-exhausted -> no retry, budget safe), edit-heavy in hypothesize.go. BLOCKER for full closure: the 27%->lower rate is a LIVE MiniMax-M3 dogfood measurement (can't verify offline); rwe is coupled to bugbot-mnr. RECOMMENDATION: do (2) as the code fix + close the rate-drop AC in a live session alongside mnr.

title: Streaming funnel: per-candidate pipeline with incremental triage, durable partial progress, free interruption
description: Dustin's architectural direction (2026-06-11 dogfood session, his words): the funnel should be 'a live pipeline, not a herd, that can be interrupted freely' — hypotheses flow to triage in turn, not en masse. Today stages A-D run with hard barriers (run.go): no candidate reaches triage until ALL ~300 finder units finish; a killed scan loses everything in memory, never finalizes the scan_run, and (because TouchScanCoverage runs at run end) even forfeits its coverage credit — today's two aborted dogfood sweeps lost ~30 units of coverage advancement exactly this way. The barrier's stated justification is triage's cross-candidate dedup + cross-lens corroboration merge needing the full set, but that is weaker than it looks: the storage layer ALREADY upserts findings by fingerprint and accumulates corroborating lenses incrementally, so triage can process candidates as they arrive — a later-arriving duplicate merges into the existing cluster and upgrades corroboration after the fact, preserving dedup semantics without the barrier. Verify is already per-candidate. Persist is already per-finding upserts. Target design: (1) candidates stream finder→triage→verify→persist individually; (2) durable progress per unit — coverage touched per finderOK unit completion (not run end), findings persisted as verified, scan_run finalizable at any time; (3) SIGINT/kill = clean partial run: verified findings durable, coverage advanced, scan_run finalized with partial stats and an interrupted marker; (4) scheduling policy needed where streaming creates contention — verifier panels now compete with finders for MaxParallel and budget mid-run (suggest verify-priority so candidates never queue behind breadth), and budget degradation gates apply per-unit exactly as now (gate semantics never depended on stage phases). (5) Ranked output (sortFindings) happens at read time, not as a stage. Eval comparability: recorded-corpus eval must produce identical findings under streaming (order-independence of the funnel's verdicts is the key invariant to test).
design: Decompose roughly: (a) move TouchScanCoverage to per-unit completion (small, independently valuable — fixes interrupted-sweep coverage loss, do FIRST — in flight as phase 1); (b) make FinishScanRun idempotent/partial-capable + context-cancellation finalization path (phase 1); (c) lift triage to an incremental consumer off a candidate channel (the fingerprint-upsert merge machinery already supports late corroboration); (d) verify consumes triage output per-candidate (mostly exists — change the call topology, not the panel logic); (e) status live counters ride the same events. GLOBAL AGENT CAP (Dustin, 2026-06-11 — REQUIRED for phases c/d): today MaxParallel bounds each stage's agents but stages are sequential, so it is effectively a global cap; under streaming the per-stage semaphores COMPOUND (finder N + verifier N + reproducer = 2N+ concurrent LLM calls) and the operator's --concurrency silently stops meaning what it says. Replace per-stage semaphores with ONE funnel-owned global slot pool sized MaxParallel, shared by finder units, verifier candidates (one slot covers a candidate's whole sequential panel+arbiter, preserving today's 'bounds candidates, not candidates×refuters' semantics), and reproducer runs. Starvation policy: verify-side work must not queue behind finder breadth — either priority acquisition (verify-first when both wait) or a small reserved carve-out (e.g. ceil(cap/4) slots verify-only, borrowable when idle); decide at implementation with a test that a candidate arriving mid-discovery gets a slot within one unit-completion. Sandbox execs stay inside their owner's slot. Risks: budget gate fairness between roles mid-run; progress-event consumers assuming stage monotonicity; the eval harness's stage-count assertions; slot accounting on the firstErr abort path.
acceptance_criteria: (1) A candidate emitted by an early finder unit reaches verify while other finder units are still running (integration test with scripted clients asserting interleaved stage activity in agent_units rows). (2) Kill/SIGINT mid-hypothesize: previously verified findings are in the store, coverage is advanced for all completed finderOK units, and the scan_run row is finalized with partial stats + interrupted marker (test via context cancellation). (3) Dedup/corroboration semantics order-independent: recorded-corpus eval yields the same finding set (fingerprints, tiers, corroborating lenses) as the staged baseline. (4) Verifier scheduling policy documented and tested (candidates do not starve behind finder breadth). (5) Status counters tick per stage event (consumes the live-counter bead). (6) Gates clean.
close_reason: The streaming-funnel epic-scale bead is complete across PRs #51 (durable partial progress), #65 (global slot pool), #66 (streaming topology), #67 (live triage ticks), #68 (repro operator) — Dustin's architecture realized: 'a live pipeline, not a herd, that can be interrupted freely.' Candidates stream finder->triage->verify->persist->repro individually; every stage boundary durable (per-unit coverage stamps, per-finding persistence, sealed scan_runs with interrupted/aborted classification); one global agent cap with three priority classes governs all roles under any topology; status renders all four counters live; order-independence pinned at cluster level by the recorded eval corpus and a six-arrival-order chain test; SIGINT yields a clean partial run with verified findings durable. Acceptance criteria all delivered (1: mid-discovery verify test; 2: interrupt durability tests; 3: eval order-independence; 4: idle-class scheduling tests; 5: live counters; 6: gates). Residual known issues filed separately: UpsertFinding ReproPath clobber (new P1 bug bead), advisory single-scan lock (existing bead), streaming makes both more visible.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Phase 1 (durable partial progress) merged: PR #51. Per-finderOK-unit coverage stamps (targeted-scan asymmetry preserved), every-exit-path scan_run finalization (interrupted/aborted flags via ctx inspection, detached 5s finalize context), SIGINT-to-context wiring. Review round fixed a success-path regression (finalize failures silently swallowed; now propagate) and hardened the interrupt test. Next: phase 2a global slot pool, then 2b streaming topology.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Phase 2a (global agent slot pool) merged: PR #65. Funnel-wide slotPool sized MaxParallel with verifier-HIGH/finder-LOW priority classes, both stages migrated off local semaphores, cancellation-vs-handoff protocol race-hammered, ctx-free acquire fast path documented as load-bearing for Interrupted classification. Review round: production code clean; fixed a vacuous MaxParallel integration test (instant-return client never contended the bound — now barrier-forced to peak==MaxParallel exactly), deterministic priority-test sequencing, deleted log-only broken-pool fixtures. Dustin's --concurrency contract now holds by construction under any stage topology. NEXT: phase 2b streaming topology — candidates channel, incremental triage consumer, per-candidate verify under this pool, stage-event rework, eval order-independence as the keystone invariant.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Phase 2b design decisions (locked before dispatch, 2026-06-12): (1) TOPOLOGY — run() becomes a coordinator: hypothesize emits candidates per-unit-completion into a buffered channel; single triage-consumer goroutine applies steps 1-4 per candidate with persistent seen/suppression state; verify dispatcher spawns per-candidate panels on HIGH pool slots; survivors persist immediately on panel completion; closes cascade shutdown. (2) THE CLUSTERING DECISION — triage step 5 (location-cluster merge, one primary verified per cluster) is inherently arrival-order-sensitive when incremental. Resolution: first-arrival-becomes-primary; later cluster members attach as corroboration (staged if the primary's panel is in flight, store-updated if already persisted). SEMANTIC RELAXATION accepted: which member is primary depends on arrival order; the kept invariant is CLUSTER-LEVEL equivalence — same location-clusters found, same corroborating-lens sets — enforced by a both-orders keystone test and the unchanged eval suite. Replicating batch primary selection would require re-barriering and was rejected. (3) Stage events keep their identities with overlap: StageFinished(hypothesize) at finder drain (preserving live-counter resets), verify Started on first forward / Finished at full drain. (4) Streaming strengthens phase 1: findings persisted before a cancellation now survive it. (5) The 2a pool does real cross-role arbitration for the first time — spec demands a test that a candidate forwarded mid-discovery gets a HIGH slot before finder breadth completes.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Phase 2b (streaming topology) merged: PR #66 (b2c2669). Candidates now stream finder→incremental triage→per-candidate verify (HIGH pool slots)→immediate persist. Review verdict was NOT MERGEABLE with three reviewer-reproduced MUST-FIXes, all fixed before merge: (1) the implementer had edited the eval manifest to match weaker clustering — restored, and BOTH structural bugs fixed (any-member membership for transitive chains; cluster LIST per location bucket — single-cluster-per-bucket let dissimilar same-bucket groups overwrite each other, the actual eval regression); replay reproduces the recorded manifest exactly. (2) corroboration TOCTOU (43% flake) closed: atomic drain-and-mark returning late-staged lenses + run() folding post-persist attachments into in-memory findings. (3) budget-degradation flake pinned with a zero-cost verifier (verify spend is now concurrent by design). Documented relaxations: arrival-order primary identity; bridge-after-forwarding joins one cluster. Keystone tests: six-arrival-order transitive chain, same-bucket dissimilar groups, negative guarantees ported from the deleted batch path. REMAINING on this bead: live triage status ticks (now feasible — triage is an event-capable consumer), then bugbot-591 (repro operator) completes the dataflow. Delegation lesson for the record: the implementer edited the benchmark to fit the code — spec'd invariants must name their FIXTURES as untouchable, and 2-member cluster tests are degenerate (primary-only == any-member); chains of 3+ are the discriminating shape.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Live triage ticks merged: PR #67 (55f8453) — KindCandidateTriaged per forwarded cluster primary, LiveTriaged in status with full lifecycle resets. This closes everything on this bead except what bugbot-591 delivers (repro operator, in flight now: third idle pool class + funnel repro hook + agent_units repro rows per its locked spec).

title: status: show live in-flight candidate count during the hypothesize stage
description: UX gap found dogfooding 2026-06-11 (Dustin), then sharpened by his follow-up: the status stage fields should be LIVE COUNTERS, not stage-finish artifacts. Today 'bugbot status' during a long hypothesize stage shows hypothesized=0 even while finder units emit candidates, because Stats.Hypothesized is set only from the StageFinished event (internal/funnel/run.go:360). Dustin's framing: he reads the funnel as a live pipeline and expects hypothesized/triaged/verified to tick per event. Fix scope for THIS bead (presentation only): every stage counter in status ticks as its events occur — hypothesized increments as finder units complete (agent_units.candidates is already persisted live per PR #48, or carry the count on the AgentFinished progress event), triaged/verified/killed tick per candidate as those stages process them under the CURRENT staged execution. The deeper execution-model change (candidates streaming through triage/verify individually, free interruption) is the separate streaming-funnel bead — this one must not change funnel semantics, only make the view truthful in real time.
acceptance_criteria: (1) During a running scan, status renders live ticking counts for hypothesized (per completed finder unit) and, once later stages run, triaged/verified/killed per candidate event. (2) Funnel execution semantics unchanged. (3) CLI test drives a fake mid-stage run and asserts live values. (4) Gates clean.
close_reason: Merged in PR #50. Status stage fields now tick live: finder AgentFinished events carry per-unit candidate counts (emit relocated to the caller; exactly-one-emit verified across all four exit paths), new KindFindingKilled per-candidate event, SnapshotSink accumulates Live{Candidates,Verified,Killed} with the stages: line rendering live suffixes during active stages and the exact pre-change format once settled. Review round caught and fixed the real lifecycle bug: counters now also reset on scan/cycle start and finish, since aborted scans never emit StageFinished and the daemon reuses one SnapshotSink across cycles (lifecycle test vacuity-checked). Triage remains stage-finish-only by design (no per-candidate events exist in triage; threading a sink was judged invasive — revisit inside bugbot-280's streaming work where triage becomes an incremental operator anyway).

title: Flaky on main: TestDegradedLensNames_* — budget thresholds race parallel unit launch, ~20% failure rate
description: Measured 2026-06-11 on clean main (5e71284): go test ./internal/funnel/ -run TestDegradedLensNames -count=20 produced multiple failures in BOTH TestDegradedLensNames_SweepKeepsTaxonomyTop2 (diffintent_test.go:422,428) and TestDegradedLensNames_CommitRunKeepsDiffIntentAndNilSafety (diffintent_test.go:480,483), each failing nondeterministically with 'hard budget reached: skipped finder lens ...' for lenses the test asserts must survive degradation. Root cause shape: the tests set a token budget intending to exercise SOFT degradation, but finder units launch in parallel goroutines and gate against LIVE recorded spend (hypothesize.go:228-243); under unlucky scheduling enough spend records before the asserted lenses' units acquire worker slots that the HARD threshold trips instead, skipping them. The assertion conflates 'lens not in degraded survivor set' with 'lens skipped for any budget reason'. Discovered because BOTH wave-1 Sonnet implementer agents (mi5.12, mi5.4) independently tripped on these tests; the mi5.12 branch already re-tunes the budget constants (600 to 1200/1300, sized so hard exceeds 8 units x 150 tokens) which likely fixes the flake for these two tests — evaluate at mi5.12 merge whether that retune fully eliminates it or whether the tests should instead assert on degradation-kind (soft-skip vs hard-stop messages) so the failure mode is structurally impossible.
acceptance_criteria: (1) go test ./internal/funnel/ -run TestDegradedLensNames -count=50 passes 50/50 on the merged tree. (2) The fix is principled: either thresholds provably sized against worst-case pre-launch spend (documented in a comment), or assertions distinguish soft-degradation skips from hard-stop skips. (3) If mi5.12's retune is judged sufficient, close with reasoning referencing the -count=50 run.
close_reason: Fixed by mi5.12's budget retune (merged in PR #45): test budgets re-sized so the hard threshold provably cannot fire before all units complete (hard > units × 150 tokens), making the previously scheduling-dependent assertions one-sided and robust. Acceptance met: go test -run TestDegradedLensNames -count=50 passes 50/50 on merged main (35335c2). The deeper lesson (assertions must distinguish soft-degradation skips from hard stops) is documented in the test comments.

title: Stochastic probe scheduler: sample (lens x strategy x quirk) per finder unit with learned yields instead of the deterministic grid
description: Dustin's framing (2026-06-11): wide sweeps do not converge to problems quickly; probabilistically injecting probing quirks (deep-analysis and others) may be what effective codebase exploration actually requires. Assessment: the current finder schedule is a DETERMINISTIC grid — every cycle runs the same lens prompts over the same chunk geometry in the same yield order — so the daemon's misses are perfectly correlated across cycles: whatever the grid is blind to, it is blind to forever (the strategy-level twin of bugbot-pav's deterministic file starvation; same root cause, determinism + budget truncation = fixed blind spots; also the mechanism behind the 45M-token ig7 miss). The daemon is a repeated game, so the fix is to make the probe distribution stochastic and ERGODIC: each finder unit samples its strategy (and later, quirk modifiers: comment-skeptic, test-contract-reader, error-path-pessimist, blame-historian) from a weighted distribution with a nonzero exploration floor, so any bug findable by SOME probe eventually gets its probe sampled. This is a multi-armed bandit over probe-space with bugbot-mi5.10 unit records as the reward feed. Design requirements: (1) REPLAYABILITY — RNG seeded per scan run, sampled assignment recorded on each mi5.10 unit row; a stochastic daemon that cannot be replayed cannot be debugged. (2) LEARNING LOOP — yield-weighted sampling (Thompson or epsilon-greedy), non-stationary aware (discount old rewards; the codebase drifts); until mi5.10 data accumulates, hand priors + uniform exploration floor. (3) PRECISION SAFETY — the triage->verify funnel already makes recall-side additions structurally safe (epic thesis), so randomness risks only tokens, which the existing budget machinery bounds. (4) ATTRIBUTION DISCIPLINE — start with few arms (the mi5.12 strategy axis only) and widen the quirk vocabulary as unit volume makes yield attribution converge; flipping many axes at once with small N drowns the signal. Quirk candidates worth noting: comment-skeptic (assume comments lie; verify each claim against code) is cheap and would independently cover the ig7 defect class. mi5.9 cross-model ensembles are another diversity axis the same scheduler could own.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Finder unit assignment is sampled, not enumerated: per-unit (strategy, quirk) drawn from a weighted distribution with a configurable exploration floor; deterministic grid remains available as a mode (eval comparability). (2) RNG seed recorded per scan run and assignment per unit; replaying a run with the same seed reproduces the schedule exactly. (3) Sampling weights derived from mi5.10 per-unit yield data with recency discounting; hand priors documented where data is thin. (4) An A/B eval over recorded corpora: stochastic scheduler vs deterministic grid at equal budget, comparing unique-verified-finding discovery rate across repeated cycles. (5) At least two quirk modifiers ship beyond deep/wide, each as data (clause + applicability), reusing the mi5.12 Strategy machinery.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text:
EVIDENCE EVALUATION (2026-06-24, controller + aethon deep-tooling runs). NOT closeable — genuine unimplemented feature — but now UNBLOCKED and strongly motivated by data.

Deps satisfied: mi5.10 (per-unit observability) and mi5.12 (strategy axis) are closed and the runs prove the yield substrate now EXISTS. agent_units has per-unit (lens, strategy, status, input/output tokens, candidates, leads): controller 666 rows, aethon 951 rows. So AC3 (sampling weights derived from mi5.10 per-unit yield data) has real input to learn from, per (lens x strategy) cell.

Motivation now empirical: the aethon run is the textbook case for this bead. Of 951 finder units, 521 were skipped_hard_budget and 236 skipped_degraded — i.e. ~80% of the deterministic grid never ran under budget truncation, and because the grid is fixed, the SAME cold tail is starved every cycle (the strategy-level twin of bugbot-pav's deterministic file starvation, named in this bead's framing). Controller shed 63 skipped_degraded + 34 rate_limited similarly. A stochastic (lens x strategy x quirk) draw with an exploration floor is exactly the fix for fixed blind spots.

Recommendation: keep OPEN at P2; it is the highest-leverage remaining implementation item in the epic and is now fully unblocked. Implementation still required (sampling + per-run seed record/replay AC2, weight derivation AC3, A/B eval AC4, 2 quirk modifiers AC5). Source the learned priors from the controller+aethon agent_units as the seed corpus.

title: state-trace-deep strategy: trace shared mutable state and resource lifecycles to all access sites (concurrency, resource-leaks lenses)
description: Follow-up to bugbot-mi5.12, prompted by Dustin's observation (2026-06-11): data races are the archetypal cross-site bug — every access site looks valid in isolation (idiomatic lock here, goroutine launch there, unguarded write elsewhere) and the defect exists only in the join, exactly the chunk-blindness that motivated the strategy axis. Supporting dogfood signal recorded on bugbot-mi5.10: the one verified finding from the 2026-06-11 runs was a concurrency bug caught by api-contract-misuse over files the concurrency lens whiffed on — cross-site reasoning, not taxonomy, was the missing ingredient. Add a second Strategy value 'state-trace-deep': seed files are a starting point; the agent (1) enumerates shared mutable state declared in the seed — mutex/RWMutex-guarded fields, package globals, fields accessed from goroutines or callbacks, plus acquired resources with lifecycle obligations (handles, conns, tickers); (2) uses find_references to visit EVERY access/release site; (3) checks discipline consistency across sites — same lock held for all writes and reads of a field, release reachable on every path including error paths, no aliasing escape of guarded state. AppliesTo: concurrency and resource-leaks lenses. Do NOT reuse contract-trace-deep's clause: its procedure (declared contracts, sentinel semantics, validators) mis-directs on these lenses. Ecosystem-general: the discipline framing is universal; per-language manifestation rows already carry the idiom specifics. Weight: hand-estimate after mi5.12's deep axis has mi5.10 yield data — do not pick blind.
acceptance_criteria: (1) state-trace-deep ships as a Strategy value only — no changes to the axis machinery from mi5.12. (2) Applies to concurrency and resource-leaks lenses; unit growth bounded accordingly. (3) Clause directs shared-state/lifecycle traversal, with at least one funnel test asserting the composed prompt and task framing. (4) Weight justified from mi5.10 per-unit yield data on the deep axis (or explicitly recorded as a provisional guess if data is still thin). (5) Eval fixture: a seeded cross-file lock-discipline violation (write without the lock held, lock declared in another file) caught by this strategy and missed by sweep-wide concurrency over the same chunks.
close_reason: Shipped state-trace-deep finder strategy (concurrency + resource-leaks, weight 0.85): pure data addition to the mi5.12 axis, traces shared mutable state and resource lifecycles to all access sites. Merged in PR #72.

title: Per-unit finder observability: persist (lens, chunk, launch order, timing, outcome) for every agent unit
description: GENERAL INTENT: run-level stats aggregate away everything needed to reason about WHERE hypotheses come from. Persist one record per finder unit — lens, chunk index, launch order, files, start/finish timestamps, turn count, token spend (cached vs uncached), status (ok/parse-failed/budget-stopped), candidate count, and leads posted — keyed by scan run, in the store. Applies identically to any lens/ecosystem; verifier and reproducer units should get the same treatment for symmetry. TWO CONSUMERS: (a) empirical yield calibration — this is the data feed bugbot-mi5.2's long-term goal (learned per-language-profile yield tables) requires; without it yield rankings stay hand-coded guesses, and the 2026-06-11 dogfood runs already show the hand ranking mispredicting (the one verified finding came from the 5th-of-6 yield-ranked lens — api-contract-misuse caught a concurrency bug the concurrency lens whiffed on over the same files); (b) answering distribution questions directly — e.g. this session's 'why do the last agents push all the hypotheses' (answer was inferred from mechanism: lens-major launch order + hypothesis-bearing units running more turns and finishing last — but could not be MEASURED because nothing per-unit is persisted). Keep records cheap: one row per unit, no transcript content (TranscriptDir already covers that separately); a retention/pruning policy so the table does not grow unboundedly under daemon operation.
acceptance_criteria: (1) A store table records one row per finder unit per scan run with the fields above; verifier/reproducer units recorded analogously. (2) A CLI surface (e.g. 'bugbot report units <run-id>' or similar) renders per-unit timing and outcomes for a run. (3) Candidate-bearing vs empty units distinguishable, enabling launch-order/duration/lens correlation queries over multiple runs with plain SQL. (4) Cached vs uncached token spend recorded per unit. (5) Bounded growth: retention policy prunes unit rows for old runs. (6) Negligible overhead: no extra LLM calls, write happens once per unit completion.
close_reason: Merged in PR #48. Shipped: agent_units table (migration 007) with one row per finder unit (lens, strategy, launch order, files JSON, status incl. skipped_hard_budget/skipped_degraded with zero tokens, uncached+cached token capture from agent Outcome.Usage, candidates, per-unit leads posted) and per-candidate verifier rows (seat names, refuted counts, arbiter verdict — counts only); 'bugbot report units <run-id>' CLI with finder coverage-fraction footer (the bugbot-pav deferred surface); retention pruning keeping the 200 most recent runs via monotonic ULID ids — NOT started_at, which mis-sorts lexicographically across the second boundary (reviewer-reproduced, regression-tested). Recording is best-effort and row inserts happen outside the launch-loop mutex. This is the empirical feed mi5.2's learned-yield goal and mi5.14's stochastic scheduler depend on. Deviation from acceptance: reproducer units not yet recorded (repro stage untouched this round) — follow-up trivially extends recordVerifierUnit's pattern; turn counts omitted (Outcome does not expose them cheaply).

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: More empirical weight for prioritizing this: the bugbot-ig7 post-mortem (see bugbot-mi5 comments) could not answer the most basic distribution questions about a ~45M-input-token daemon search — which files were actually scanned, whether api-contract-misuse ever ran over internal/config/config.go, how often budget degradation dropped it, how much spend re-hit the same hot-head chunks across truncated sweeps (bugbot-pav). Every causal claim in that post-mortem is inferred from mechanism, not measured. The per-unit rows specified here would have answered all four with plain SQL. Suggest also recording the per-unit FILE LIST hash/coverage so cumulative sweep coverage (bugbot-pav acceptance #4) can be derived from the same table.

title: [bug] publish: deleted/transferred GitHub issue (HTTP 410/404 on PATCH) aborts the whole publish run
description: Found live during the bugbot-zdc re-render nudge: published_issues had issue #43 as state='open' but the GitHub issue had been deleted; ghUpdateIssue's PATCH got HTTP 410 ('This issue was deleted') and runPublish (internal/cli/publish.go:~197-210) returned the error, aborting the entire publish run with exit 1 — one stale row blocks ALL syncing. The same applies to the close path and to 404 (issue transferred/repo renamed). Desired: treat 410/404 on update/close as 'published row is stale' — log it, delete (or mark) the published_issues row so the next plan pass re-creates the issue fresh, and continue with the remaining actions; the publish summary line gains a recreated/stale count. findIssueByMarker already exists for recovery-by-fingerprint and may slot in. Manual remediation used today (recorded for reference): DELETE FROM published_issues WHERE fingerprint='...' then re-publish (created #44).
acceptance_criteria: A published_issues row pointing at a deleted GitHub issue no longer aborts publish: the run logs the stale row, re-creates the issue (new number recorded), and continues processing other findings; 404 on PATCH handled the same way; unit test with a ghRunner fake returning 410 on PATCH then asserting re-create + continued processing; existing publish tests pass.
close_reason: Merged to main in PR #81 (commit 37c72ab, 'batch of 8 bounded bugfixes'). Dolt was initialized after this work and never recorded the close; reconciled against committed bead state + verified code on main.

title: Per-ecosystem sandbox capability probing: detect available test/diagnostic modes and constrain the reproducer to them
description: GENERAL INTENT: the reproducer agent generates test invocations without knowing what the sandbox image can actually run, so it can emit repros that are impossible in the environment — the failure then masquerades as a test result (see bugbot-vig). Every ecosystem has capability-gated diagnostic modes: Go's -race needs cgo + a C compiler; C/C++ sanitizers (tsan/asan/ubsan) need compiler support in the image; Python repros may assume pytest or specific plugins; JS repros assume a runner (jest/vitest/node --test) and node version; Rust needs cargo + toolchain components. DESIGN DIRECTION: a capability table alongside the deps ecosystem registry (sandbox/deps.go pattern, same per-ecosystem shape as mi5.2 manifestation tables). Each entry declares cheap probes (e.g. 'cc exists / trivial CGO_ENABLED=1 go build', 'pytest --version', 'which cargo') run once per image and cached. Results feed two consumers: (a) the reproducer prompt — list available invocations/diagnostic modes so generated repros only use runnable ones, and steer toward deterministic assertions when a detector mode is unavailable (e.g. assert each goroutine observed its own value instead of relying on -race); (b) run stats, so a capability gap is visible instead of silent. Optionally: document recommended image contents per ecosystem so operators can opt into the stronger diagnostic modes (a real -race or tsan repro is strictly better T1 evidence).
