MOTIVATING INSTANCE (Go): finding 0000019eb867 — reproducer emitted go test -race; image had no cc; cgo auto-disabled; repro could never run.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Capability probe framework keyed by ecosystem, probed once per image and cached; Go cgo/-race is the first concrete probe. (2) Reproducer prompt enumerates available invocations; generated repros never use an unavailable mode (verified: with cgo absent, no -race repro is generated and a deterministic-assertion repro is attempted instead). (3) With a capable image, the gated mode is used end-to-end (a race finding reaches genuine T1 under -race). (4) Probe results recorded in run stats; probe failure degrades to 'mode unavailable', never blocks repro. (5) Adding a new ecosystem's capabilities requires only a table entry.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

comments.author: bugbot-agent
comments.text: LIVE TRIGGER (.bugbot/state.db, 2026-06-17): dogfood bugbot.yaml sets sandbox.image=debian:stable-slim — NO Go toolchain. Reproducer emits 'go test'-style plans that exit 127 in-container; reproducer agent_units over 15 runs = 1 exhausted, 1 infra_error, 0 reproduced. A capability probe would let repro detect 'this image cannot run Go' up front and skip with an honest reason / constrain the plan instead of burning attempts on impossible invocations. Umbrella: bugbot-3lt.

title: Cartographer: cached per-package summaries injected into finder context
description: Every finder currently rediscovers repo context through tool calls and pays for it on every turn (the read-caps work in bugbot-3nf fights the symptom). Amortize it: a cheap agent pass writes a short summary per package/module — what it does, what invariants it maintains, what it assumes of callers — stored in the SQLite store keyed by the package's content fingerprint (ingest already fingerprints files/symbols), regenerated only when the fingerprint changes. Finder tasks are then assembled with the summaries relevant to their chunk (and its blast-radius neighbors), so agents start with global context instead of buying it per-run. Language-agnostic by construction. Watch prompt-cache interaction: summaries belong in the task message, not the system prompt, so the shared system prefix stays cache-stable across units.
design: Package = repo-relative directory (path.Dir of each target; root normalized to ""). Cache key = packageFingerprint: SHA-256 (ingest.HashBytes) over sorted "path\x00<file-fp>\n" of the package members from the run fps map, so the summary invalidates exactly when any member file content changes. Storage: new package_summaries table (migration 008), Get/UpsertPackageSummaries mirror filestate.go batching. Generation: funnel.cartograph runs ONCE per scan before the finder fan-out, reuses the recorder-wrapped FINDER client (spend ledgered+pooled as finder), one bounded client.Complete per uncached package (no tool loop), input bounded by DefaultCartographerMaxFiles/HeadLines/InputBytes, output by DefaultCartographerMaxTokens; gated by budget.finderOverHard(). Injection: cartography.contextFor appends a "REPO CONTEXT" block to the finder TASK (user) message only — never the system prompt — preserving the cache-stable prefix (bugbot-3nf lesson). Direct dependents via new ingest.PackageImporters (one-pass inverted Go import graph; non-Go contributes no edges). Feature gated by Options.Cartographer / scan.cartographer (env BUGBOT_SCAN_CARTOGRAPHER), DEFAULT OFF → byte-identical when disabled.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Summaries persisted keyed by package fingerprint; unchanged packages never re-summarized. (2) Finder task includes summaries for the chunk's packages + direct dependents. (3) Measured on eval: either reduced finder tokens-per-unit or improved hit rate, with neither regressing. (4) Summary generation respects the run budget and degrades to no-summaries gracefully.
notes: Shipped in commit c866b74 (fast-forwarded to main). Default OFF deliberately: acceptance #3 needs a LIVE eval (real model runs) before flipping on — filed as follow-up. Acceptance #1 (fingerprint cache), #2 (chunk packages + direct dependents injected), #4 (graceful degrade) are unit-tested in internal/funnel/cartographer_test.go, internal/store/cartographer_test.go, internal/ingest/blast_test.go. Implemented by a delegated subagent in a worktree; verified by main agent (build+vet+gofmt+golangci-lint 0 issues+full test suite green). KNOWN LIMITATION: only the default sweep-wide finderTask carries the block; the contractTraceDeep/stateTraceDeep strategy tasks do not (their BuildTask was left unchanged). bugbot-6pu (read_symbol) was ALREADY shipped in PR #26 — no work needed; verified its tests green on main.
close_reason: Implemented + merged to main (c866b74) behind Options.Cartographer / scan.cartographer, default OFF. Acceptance #1/#2/#4 satisfied and unit-tested (fingerprint-keyed cache with no re-summarize on unchanged packages; finder TASK-message injection of the chunk packages + direct importers via new ingest.PackageImporters; graceful degrade on store/LLM error and budget exhaustion). Acceptance #3 (live-eval token/recall measurement) NOT done here — it requires real model runs and a default-on decision; filed as a follow-up bead. Gates clean: build, vet, gofmt, golangci-lint (0 issues), full test suite all green on main.

title: Cross-language boundary lens: audit polyglot seams (schemas, FFI, subprocess/env contracts)
description: The strategic differentiator of going language-general. In a polyglot repo the highest-density bug habitat is the boundary BETWEEN languages: producer in one language, consumer in another (JSON/proto/OpenAPI schema vs both implementations), FFI/cgo signatures, subprocess argument conventions, shared env-var contracts. Single-language static tools are categorically blind here — each stops at its language border — but an LLM agent reads both sides natively. Two parts: (a) seam discovery in the repo model — mechanically enumerate schema files + their consumers, FFI/extern declarations and their binding counterparts, exec/subprocess call sites and the scripts they invoke, shared env-var reads; (b) a boundary lens whose unit of work is one seam, with the finder task naming both sides and asking for asymmetries (field added on one side, encoding mismatch, error-code contract drift). Blocked by mi5.2 for the lens plumbing; seam discovery itself is independent and can start early.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Snapshot exposes an enumerated seam list with both-side file references. (2) Boundary finder unit receives one seam with both sides named in the task. (3) Polyglot eval fixture (e.g. Python writer / Go reader with a seeded field-type mismatch) caught by this lens and missed by single-language lenses. (4) Seam count and coverage reported in run stats.
close_reason: Shipped cross-language-boundary seam lens + ingest.EnumerateSeams (shared data-file + env-var seams), one custom finder unit per seam with both sides named; Stats.SeamsFound/Covered. Intra-language config-field seams deferred (owned by mi5.11). Merged in PR #72.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Seam taxonomy addition from the bugbot-ig7 post-mortem: seams are not only cross-LANGUAGE — a config knob is an intra-language cross-PACKAGE seam (definition site + doc comment, validator clause, N consumption sites). The ig7 miss happened because 8-file chunking never put the validator and the consumer docs in one unit; a contract-seam unit ('here is field budgets.per_day_tokens: its declaration, its validation, its three consumers — find asymmetries') contains the join by construction. Config-struct fields are mechanically enumerable just like schema files and FFI bindings. Suggest part (a) seam discovery enumerate single-language contract seams (config fields, exported option structs) alongside polyglot ones — same unit-of-work machinery, and it makes seam discovery testable on single-language repos before any polyglot fixture exists. Deterministic cousin: bugbot-mi5.11 mines the doc-vs-validator contradiction lexically as a lead; this lens is the semantic generalization.

title: New defect-class lenses Go never needed: memory-safety, exception-safety, dynamic-typing
description: Go's runtime absorbed defect classes that dominate elsewhere; going language-general means new top-level lenses, not translated wording. (a) memory-safety (C/C++/unsafe Rust): use-after-free, double-free, buffer overflow, uninitialized reads, dangling iterators/references — the HIGHEST-yield lens on a C++ repo and absent today; manifestations vary by C++ standard, feeding from ingest's cppstd detection (bugbot-93z.6). (b) exception-safety (Python/Java/C++/JS): cleanup skipped by a throw mid-function, objects left half-mutated, swallowed or over-broad catches, exceptions during cleanup masking the original error. (c) dynamic-typing hazards (Python/JS/Ruby): None/undefined propagation far from origin, implicit coercion (== vs ===, string/bytes), duck-typing breaks on rare paths. Each lens activates only when the repo's language profile warrants it and carries per-language yield entries (memory-safety outranks everything on C/C++; inactive on pure Go). Blocked by lens generalization (mi5.2): requires the ontology+manifestation structure and the (lens x language) yield table.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Three new lenses defined in the generalized structure with per-language manifestations. (2) Activation gated on language profile: pure-Go repo runs none of them; C++ repo ranks memory-safety first. (3) Eval fixtures per lens (e.g. seeded use-after-free in C++, seeded swallowed-exception in Python) caught at T2+. (4) Stable lens names registered for dedup fingerprints.
close_reason: Shipped memory-safety/exception-safety/dynamic-typing lenses + per-lens Languages gate (lensAppliesTo): pure-Go repos run none, C++ ranks memory-safety first (yield 100). Existing lenses stay language-free/byte-identical. Merged in PR #72.

title: Refuter diversity + arbiter on split verdicts
description: Today N refuters run the same prompt N times — redundancy, not diversity — and any single refutation demotes the candidate, silently costing real bugs on split panels. Two changes: (a) assign each panel seat a distinct refutation lens — seat 1 attacks reachability (path cannot execute), seat 2 attacks semantics (claimed behavior is actually correct), seat 3 hunts existing guards (caller/type/prior check already prevents it) — diverse refuters catch failure modes identical ones cannot, making the disagreement-demotes rule meaningful; (b) on a split verdict (some refute, some cannot), spawn one arbiter agent that reads both sides' reasoning plus the code and issues the deciding verdict. Splits are rare so arbiter cost is marginal. Seat prompts compose with persona like everything else; keep post_lead absent from all verifier-side agents (existing bias-loop rationale in verify.go).
acceptance_criteria: (1) Refuters>1 yields distinct seat specializations, composed with the persona-seeded verifier base. (2) Unanimous verdicts behave exactly as today. (3) Split verdicts route to an arbiter whose verdict decides survival; arbiter reasoning lands in the finding trace. (4) Stats count arbiter runs/outcomes. (5) Eval: precision on recorded corpus does not drop; at least one fixture shows a real bug surviving a 1-refute split that previously demoted it.
close_reason: AC1-4 shipped in PR #46 (merged fd25ffa). AC5 now satisfied by the scripted eval gate (PR #96, confirmed passing 2026-06-24): TestBenchmarkSuite shows split-arbiter-keeps (real reachable nil-deref, 1-refute split) survives with tp=1 — a real bug surviving a 1-refute split that pre-arbiter majority would demote — while precision stays 1.000 and clean-fp=0 (split-arbiter-refutes + clean-code canaries yield 0 findings). Precision does not drop; the required fixture exists. Recorded-corpus precision tracking is covered by mi5.19.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Implemented: PR #46 (CI green except CodeQL pending, merge pending Dustin's review approval). Sonnet implementer + adversarial review: no MUST-FIX across all 7 named seams (per-seat runner limits derive from live pool; all 6 aggregation paths integration-tested under -race; post_lead provably absent from arbiter; prompt-constant extraction verified byte-identical at len=1886). Two SHOULD-FIXes applied: VerifyFinding's deliberate asymmetries now documented in code (re-verification gains seat-diverse prompts but keeps majority-only/no-arbiter — biased toward demotion on splits, intentional since a re-check means the code changed/went stale and re-discovery is cheap); vacuous self-comparing seat test replaced. Known cosmetic gap (not fixed): when a split panel's arbiter fails to parse and majority-fallback decides, the finding trace does not mention the attempted arbitration (ArbiterFailures stat still counts it).

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Merged: PR #46 squashed to main (fd25ffa). Bead stays OPEN solely for acceptance #5: precision on the recorded eval corpus must not drop, plus a fixture showing a real bug surviving a 1-refute split that previously demoted — needs an eval run, pairs naturally with the mi5.12 dogfood run.

title: Multi-axis agent probing strategy: new lenses, seeds, and diversity beyond defect-class chunking
description: Brainstorm outcome (2026-06-11 session): current finder design slices only by defect taxonomy over file chunks, which is blind to bugs that do not live in a file (regressions vs commit intent, cross-module flow/state-machine violations, producer/consumer contract mismatches). Proposed angles, in recommended order: (1) deterministic-tool seeding — run go vet/staticcheck/-race/fuzz in the existing sandbox and post hits to the existing leads table so LLMs assess rather than find; (2) diff-intent/regression lens — finder reads the triggering commit message+diff and hunts intent/implementation gaps and broken caller assumptions (bugbot's unique advantage as a commit-triggered daemon); (3) refuter diversity (distinct refutation lenses per panel seat: reachability/semantics/guards) plus an arbiter agent on split verdicts instead of auto-demotion; (4) cached cartographer pass — per-package summaries stored+fingerprint-invalidated, injected into finder tasks to amortize context cost; (5) flow lenses (unit of work = end-to-end scenario) and contract-pair lenses (mechanically enumerated producer/consumer pairs); (6) coverage-gap targeting (churn x no-coverage) feeding a reproduce-first mode that writes property tests directly (findings born T1); (7) cross-model ensembles per lens via existing llm role mapping, dedup via existing fingerprints. Parallelism opinion: axis-parallelism compounds where worker-parallelism saturates; the triage->verify->repro funnel makes recall additions structurally safe as long as new axes are yield-ranked for budget degradation like lenses are.
design: Language-general refinement (supersedes Go-centric framing in description): (A) Two-layer lenses — universal defect ontology per lens + per-language manifestation table keyed by ingest.Language, composed into the prompt the same way persona already is; manifestations are data (same shape as the sandbox deps ecosystem registry), and Yield becomes a (lens x language-profile) table since current rankings explicitly encode Go and drive budget degradation (wrong lenses shed on non-Go repos). Chunk by language affinity so each finder unit gets one coherent manifestation block. Long-term: learn per-language yields from the eval package's recorded corpora. (B) New lenses that only exist outside Go: memory-safety (C/C++/unsafe Rust — highest yield on C++ repos, absent today; C++ standard detection feeds its manifestations), exception-safety (cleanup skipped by throw, half-mutated state, swallowed catches), dynamic-typing hazards (None/undefined propagation, coercion, duck-typing breaks). (C) Cross-language boundary lens — unit of work is a discovered polyglot seam (schema file + consumers in two languages, FFI/cgo bindings, subprocess/env contracts); single-language static tools are categorically blind here, making this the strategic differentiator. Seams are mechanically discoverable from the repo model. (D) Language-general infrastructure: analyzer-seeding registry mirrors the deps ecosystem table with SARIF as the single ingestion parser (symmetric with existing SARIF output); blast-radius tier 2 generalizes via a treesitter import/include query per grammar replacing the Go-only import parser; reproduce-first composes with per-ecosystem SetupCmds. (E) Already language-free, unchanged: diff-intent lens, cartographer, refuter diversity + arbiter, contract pairs, spec-miner (needs buildsys.go-style test-framework detection). Revised priority: 1) diff-intent (zero coupling), 2) lens generalization as keystone/prerequisite, 3) analyzer registry via SARIF-in, 4) refuter diversity + arbiter, 5) boundary lens once 2+ languages are solid, 6) cartographer, 7) coverage-gap + reproduce-first, 8) model ensembles.
acceptance_criteria: Each numbered angle is either (a) split into its own implementation bead with design notes, or (b) explicitly rejected with rationale recorded in a comment on this bead. No implementation required by this bead itself.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Post-mortem of a real miss (2026-06-11, bugbot-ig7): config validator requires budgets > 0 (config.go:584-588) while daemon.go/cycle.go/worldstate.go/funnel.go document 0 = unlimited. Daemon spent ~45M input tokens on internal/**/*.go without finding it; two greps find it in seconds. Three compounding causes, all already named by this epic's thesis: (1) NO LENS OWNS THE CLASS — all 7 lenses hunt within-chunk code defects; api-contract-misuse is closest but prompts at function preconditions, not doc-vs-validator contradiction across packages. (2) CHUNKING HIDES THE JOIN — 8-file heat-coherent chunks mean config.go and daemon.go essentially never co-occur in one prompt; each file is self-consistent in isolation, the bug exists only in the join, and no unit of work ever contains the join. This is direct empirical confirmation of the epic's 'bugs that do not live in a file' framing. (3) BUDGET DEGRADATION drops api-contract-misuse anyway at 70% spend (top-2 survivors). Token-spend lesson: more tokens through the same per-chunk taxonomy slice the same blind spot more times — spend scales within-file coverage only. Plus an independent seeding flaw: deterministic heat ordering with no coverage cursor starves the cold tail under budget truncation every cycle (filed bugbot-pav). Mitigations filed: bugbot-mi5.11 (deterministic contradiction-miner seed), bugbot-pav (coverage cursor), seam-type note on bugbot-mi5.6, evidence note on bugbot-mi5.10.

title: [bug] Add C and C++ to tree-sitter grammarTable (.c/.h, .cc/.cpp/.cxx/.hpp/.hh/.hxx)
description: C/C++ has clangd LSP, build detection, cmake/meson repro guidance, and (pending 93z.6) version-aware personas, but no entry in grammarTable (internal/treesitter/grammars.go) — so when clangd is not installed or lacks compile_commands.json (where it degrades badly), C++ code-nav falls through tree-sitter straight to grep. Same inconsistency shape as bugbot-93z.1 (JS). C and C++ are DISTINCT registry grammars (like typescript vs tsx): .c/.h map to the c grammar, .cc/.cpp/.cxx/.hpp/.hh/.hxx to cpp. Unlike the JS case the tags queries CANNOT be shared verbatim: node names differ between the c and cpp grammars (cpp adds class_specifier, qualified_identifier, template_declaration, field_identifier for methods). Queries must compile against each grammar — a query referencing a node type the grammar lacks fails at query-compile time and silently kills the tier (the exact trap from 93z.1). Defs: function_definition (via function_declarator), type/struct/class/enum specifiers, typedef. Refs: call_expression (identifier + field expressions). .h is claimed as C in extLang — acceptable; C++ headers usually use .hpp/.hh, and the c grammar parses C-compatible declarations in .h either way. Note extLang lacks .hxx? verify and align if so.
acceptance_criteria: DetectLanguage-classified C and C++ files produce tree-sitter definitions and references. Unit tests: a .c file (function def + call), a .cpp file with a class, a method definition, and a template (proves the cpp grammar + queries handle real C++); Supports() covers all the new extensions; existing grammar tests pass. Queries verified to compile for BOTH grammars (test exercises each).
close_reason: Merged in PR #37, content verified on main post-merge (grammars.go carries cppGrammar). C (.c/.h) and C++ (.cc/.cpp/.cxx/.hpp/.hh/.hxx) tree-sitter grammars with SEPARATE query texts — cpp's class_specifier/qualified_identifier/template vocabulary doesn't exist in plain c and queries fail at compile time against a grammar lacking their node types (the ts/tsx lesson, avoided by test-first iteration; node vocabularies confirmed by probing the registry grammars). Captured: C functions (direct + pointer declarators), typedefs, struct/enum/union tags; C++ adds classes, in-class methods (field_identifier), out-of-class Class::method (qualified_identifier), template functions via the underlying function_definition. Deliberately not captured (documented inline): constructors/destructors and operator overloads (false-positive risk), function-pointer-returning declarators, K&R definitions. .h → c grammar (extLang classifies .h as C; documented tradeoff for C++-header .h files). Discovery during implementation: the static binary subsets grammars via Makefile GRAMMAR_TAGS build tags — grammar_subset_c/grammar_subset_cpp added and make build verified, otherwise the shipped binary would silently lack the new grammars. .hxx was already in extLang.

title: [bug] TestManagerConcurrentQueriesAcrossCrash flaky: dead-conn EOF on retry after crash-once restart
description: internal/lsp TestManagerConcurrentQueriesAcrossCrash (manager_test.go:289) fails intermittently with 'Definition across crash: language server connection is dead: EOF' at manager_test.go:310. Measured on linux (cachyos, 2026-06-11): 7/20 failures at 1a56944 (pre rust-analyzer/JS merges) and 2/20 at c9347e9 — PRE-EXISTING flake, unrelated to those merges; rate varies with machine load. It cost a false bisect during the bugbot-64p merge train (initially looked like PR #29 broke main). Shape of the race to investigate: 8 concurrent Definition calls against a FAKE_LSP_CRASH_ONCE_FILE server; first instance crashes mid-flight; every caller retries once against the restarted instance (manager.go maxRestarts=1, query/queryOnce/liveServer around manager.go:104-226). Hypotheses: a retry can observe the old dead conn again before the replacement server is swapped in (consuming its single retry on the same dead instance), or two goroutines race liveServer's restart accounting so one gets the dying server. Decide whether the bug is in Manager restart logic (real production race — worth fixing) or test-only timing; fix accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: go test -race -run TestManagerConcurrentQueriesAcrossCrash -count=100 ./internal/lsp/ passes 100/100 on a loaded machine. Root cause documented in the close reason: manager race vs test race. If a Manager bug, a regression test pins the fixed behavior.
close_reason: Merged to main in PR #81 (commit 37c72ab, 'batch of 8 bounded bugfixes'). Dolt was initialized after this work and never recorded the close; reconciled against committed bead state + verified code on main.

title: Python sandbox dep strategy: wheelhouse prefetch + offline install (pip/uv)
description: Plug Python into the per-ecosystem dep resolver (bugbot-93z.8). Python is the furthest from Go's shape: no vendoring convention, pip's HTTP cache (~/.cache/pip) is NOT directly usable by --no-index installs, and deps must be INSTALLED into the sandbox environment before pytest runs (SetupCmd from 93z.8). Strategy: (1) FETCH (primary): prefetch 'pip download -r requirements.txt -d /depcache' (a wheelhouse) in the network-enabled hardened container into the bugbot-owned cache, sentinel keyed on the requirements/lock hash; in-sandbox SetupCmd 'pip install --no-index --find-links=/depcache -r requirements.txt' running network-none (installs into the container user site or a venv under /tmp — settle empirically; HOME=/tmp already redirects user-site). (2) HOST CACHE: skip for pip (cache shape mismatch) BUT support uv if cheap: uv's cache IS reusable ('uv sync --offline' with UV_CACHE_DIR ro-mounted... verify uv tolerates a read-only cache; uv is fast becoming the standard and bugbot's eval fixtures already include Python). (3) Requirements source priority: uv.lock > requirements.txt > pyproject [project.dependencies] (pip-compile on the fly is out of scope — no lock/requirements -> degrade to off with hint, same rationale as JS). (4) Offline enforcement: --no-index / PIP_NO_INDEX=1 / UV_OFFLINE=1. Sandbox image: needs python3+pip (debian:stable-slim has python3 but not pip — integration tests override image, e.g. python:3-slim; note the default-image gap in design).
design: Settled design (final, as merged): v1 is pip wheelhouse FETCH driven by root requirements.txt only. UV DECISION: explicitly deferred — uv's --offline behavior against a read-only-mounted UV_CACHE_DIR is unverified, and verifying it was not worth blocking the Python deployment; revisit when a uv-using repo matters (the ecosystem-table shape makes it an additive entry). HOST → OFF for Python: pip's HTTP cache (~/.cache/pip) stores URL-keyed wheel/response files, not a wheelhouse — --no-index requires a local directory of wheel files, so mounting the HTTP cache into a network-none container silences the error without resolving anything; treated as off rather than silently broken. pyproject-only and uv.lock-only repos → off. EMPIRICAL FINDING (integration-tested): pip install --user under the backend's HOME=/tmp lands in /tmp/.local on the writable tmpfs, and python:3-slim includes user-site on sys.path automatically — no venv required under the read-only rootfs; venv-under-/tmp fallback documented in deps.go if a future image breaks user-site. Mount /depcache (distinct from Go's /modcache per the table's ContainerPath uniqueness rule), Shared=false (:Z, bugbot-owned), PIP_NO_INDEX=1 as the GOPROXY=off analogue, sentinel keyed on sha256(requirements.txt). Image note: default debian:stable-slim lacks pip — Python deployments must set sandbox.image (e.g. python:3-slim); doctor warning tracked as bugbot-tf1. NOTE: first implementation (branch bugbot-93z11-python-deps) was built against the mis-merged pre-#38 main and reimplemented the SetupCmds mechanism; the merged version is the v2 port onto the real ecosystem table, mechanism files byte-identical to main.
acceptance_criteria: A Python repo with one external dep and requirements.txt passes pytest under network-none via wheelhouse prefetch + offline install (integration-tagged test); pip download runs only in the prefetch container, never network-none; no-requirements repo degrades to off with hint; sentinel keyed on requirements hash; uv decision (supported or explicitly deferred with rationale) recorded in --design; gates green.
close_reason: Merged (squash f7ecd3f, content verified on main post-merge: deps.go carries pythonEcosystem). Python repos with a root requirements.txt now build and test under network-none: one-time pip-download wheelhouse prefetch in the network-enabled hardened container (sentinel on sha256(requirements.txt)), read-only /depcache mount, PIP_NO_INDEX=1, and an in-sandbox SetupCmd installing --user --no-index from the wheelhouse before the test command. Acceptance proven by podman integration tests: pytest passes network-none on python:3-slim via the wheelhouse, and the negative control (no wheelhouse) fails — the wheelhouse is load-bearing and network-none is real. All decisions in --design (uv deferred with rationale; HOST→off because pip's HTTP cache is not a wheelhouse; --user/HOME=/tmp empirical finding with venv fallback documented). Process note: the first implementation was built against the mis-merged pre-#38 main (see bugbot-93z.8 comments) and reimplemented the mechanism; merged version is the v2 port with mechanism files byte-identical to main. Enables T1 repro + patch-prover on dependency-bearing Python repos; remaining sibling work: 93z.9 (Rust), 93z.10 (JS), then 93z.12 docs matrix.

title: JS/node sandbox dep strategy: committed node_modules, npm/pnpm cache mounts, ignore-scripts prefetch
description: Plug npm/pnpm into the per-ecosystem dep resolver (bugbot-93z.8). JS differs from Go/Rust in two load-bearing ways: (A) deps must be MATERIALIZED as node_modules inside the sandbox before tests run — a cache mount alone is not enough, so this rides on the in-sandbox SetupCmd concept from 93z.8 ('npm ci --offline --cache /depcache' / 'pnpm install --offline --store-dir /depcache' runs network-none before the test cmd); (B) SECURITY: npm lifecycle scripts (postinstall etc.) are arbitrary code execution — the network-ENABLED prefetch container MUST use --ignore-scripts (scripts may then run during the network-none in-sandbox install, where arbitrary code execution is already the sandbox's threat model; document this boundary explicitly in the security posture comment). Scope: (1) VENDORED-equivalent: committed node_modules/ — copyTree already copies it; verify with a test and treat as 'nothing needed' (note pnpm's symlinked store layout may not survive copyTree's symlink handling — verify). (2) HOST CACHE: npm _cacache (~/.npm) or pnpm store (pnpm store path) ro-mounted; pick npm first, pnpm if cheap (pnpm-workspace detection already exists in buildsys.go). (3) FETCH: 'npm ci --ignore-scripts --cache /depcache' (or 'pnpm fetch') in the network-enabled hardened container into the bugbot-owned cache, sentinel keyed on package-lock.json/pnpm-lock.yaml hash. (4) Offline enforcement: --offline flags + npm_config_offline env. No lockfile -> degrade to off with a logged hint (npm install without a lockfile is non-reproducible; do not prefetch unbounded). Workspaces (turbo/nx/pnpm) detection exists; monorepo install topology is OUT of scope — single-package repos first, note the follow-up.
acceptance_criteria: A non-vendored npm repo with one external dep and a package-lock.json passes 'npm test' under network-none via prefetch + offline in-sandbox install (integration-tagged test); prefetch container provably runs --ignore-scripts (unit test on the constructed Spec/cmd); committed-node_modules repo needs no setup (test); no-lockfile repo degrades to off with hint; sentinel keyed on lockfile hash; gates green.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: Rust/Cargo sandbox dep strategy: cargo-vendor detection, registry cache mount, cargo fetch prefetch
description: Plug Cargo into the per-ecosystem dep resolver (bugbot-93z.8). (1) VENDORED: detect 'cargo vendor' output — a vendor/ dir alone is NOT sufficient; cargo only uses it when .cargo/config.toml contains the source-replacement stanza (source.crates-io -> vendored-sources). Detect both and treat as vendored; if vendor/ exists without the config stanza, surface a hint rather than silently failing. (2) HOST CACHE: mount the host registry READ-ONLY — SECURITY: mount ONLY $CARGO_HOME/registry (and git/ for git deps), NEVER the whole ~/.cargo, which contains credentials.toml and bin/. Container env: CARGO_HOME=/tmp/cargo won't see the ro registry — investigate cargo's split: registry can be symlinked/mounted under a writable CARGO_HOME, or use CARGO_HOME=/depcache with the registry ro-mounted inside it and cargo's lock dirs redirected; settle the layout empirically in a container. (3) OFFLINE ENFORCEMENT: CARGO_NET_OFFLINE=true (the GOPROXY=off analog — cache misses fail fast instead of hanging under network-none). (4) FETCH: prefetch via 'cargo fetch' in the network-enabled hardened container with CARGO_HOME at the bugbot-owned cache, sentinel keyed on Cargo.lock hash, written only on success (mirror the go.sum pattern). No in-sandbox setup step needed (cargo builds from registry cache directly — same shape as Go). Sandbox image note: needs rust toolchain; integration tests override image (rust:slim) like the Go tests use golang:alpine.
acceptance_criteria: A non-vendored Cargo repo with one external crate passes 'cargo test' under network-none via host-cache mount (integration-tagged test, podman); cargo-vendored repo detected only when the source-replacement config exists (unit tests for both halves); credentials.toml/bin are demonstrably NOT mounted (test asserts mount paths); CARGO_NET_OFFLINE set in all strategies; fetch sentinel keyed on Cargo.lock; gates green.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: Generalize sandbox dep strategy into a per-ecosystem resolver (deps.go is Go-only)
description: The bugbot-2ry dependency strategy (internal/sandbox/deps.go ResolveDeps: vendored/off/host/fetch -> Resolution{ROMounts, Env, Prefetch}) is hardcoded to Go: go.mod marker, vendor/modules.txt detection, GOMODCACHE resolution, 'go mod download' prefetch. Refactor into a per-ecosystem resolver registry (data-driven table, matching this epic's other layers) keyed off root markers, so Rust/JS/Python beads plug in entries instead of forking the logic. Each ecosystem entry contributes: (1) a vendored-deps predicate ('deps already in tree, nothing needed'), (2) a host-cache resolver (host path + container mount point + env), (3) a prefetch command + bugbot-owned cache layout keyed on the lockfile hash (mirroring the go.sum sentinel), (4) offline-enforcement env (the GOPROXY=off fail-fast analog). DESIGN POINTS TO SETTLE: (a) sandbox package must stay stdlib-only (no ingest import — 2ry checked markers directly; keep that pattern); (b) Go's resolver semantics must be byte-identical after the refactor (pin with existing tests); (c) NEW CONCEPT some ecosystems need that Go does not: an in-sandbox SETUP step (npm ci --offline / pip install --no-index) that must run network-none BEFORE the test command — decide whether Resolution grows an optional SetupCmd and how repro/patch-prover/sandbox_exec call sites prepend it (this is the hard part; the per-language beads depend on it); (d) multi-ecosystem repos (go.mod + package.json): compose resolutions or pick primary — decide and document.
design: Settled design (orchestrator, pre-implementation): (1) Spec.SetupCmds [][]string — ordered in-sandbox setup commands run before Cmd in the SAME network-none container; CLI backend composes argv = [sh -c '<q(setup1)> || exit 125; ...; exec "$@"' sh] + Cmd with shell-quoting helper; exit-125 convention deliberately reuses repro interpret()'s existing 125/126/127 environment_error classification so a failed setup (npm ci --offline) can NEVER be misread as a demonstrated bug — patchVerdict gets the same guard if missing. sh requirement documented; only applies when SetupCmds non-empty so Go path needs no shell. (2) Resolution.SetupCmds [][]string merged across ecosystems. (3) Registry: package-level ordered table []ecosystem{name, detect(repoDir), resolve(repoDir, opts)}; stdlib-only marker stats (no ingest import); Go entry extracted from current ResolveDeps with byte-identical behavior (existing tests unchanged). (4) Multi-ecosystem: resolve ALL matching entries, merge (append ROMounts — container paths must be registry-unique, Exec validates; append Env; chain Prefetch sequentially; append SetupCmds); Resolution.Strategy = first matching entry's, documented in deps.go. Call sites: repro execute, patch-prover execSandbox, agent sandbox_exec tool + funnel wiring all pass SetupCmds through.
acceptance_criteria: ResolveDeps dispatches through an ecosystem table; Go entry behavior byte-identical (existing unit+integration tests pass unchanged); Resolution supports an optional in-sandbox setup command consumed by all three Spec call sites (repro execute, patch-prover, sandbox_exec) with tests; multi-ecosystem composition rule documented in deps.go; security posture comments updated (per-mount Shared semantics preserved); gates green incl. integration-tagged sandbox tests under podman.
close_reason: RE-LANDED in PR #38 (after the PR #34 mis-merge documented in comments — #34 was created from the wrong head branch via cwd drift and merged a no-op; four green signals passed because no-op merges are green; detection came from a downstream agent's report contradicting expected main content). Content verified on main post-merge: sandbox.go Spec.SetupCmds, deps.go ecosystems table, command.go buildSetupScript. All original decisions stand as recorded in the prior close reason (exit-125 setup convention reusing interpret()/patchVerdict environment-failure classification; byte-identical Go extraction; table-order merge with first-match Strategy; review NITs applied). Process correction recorded in orchestrator memory: explicit --head on gh pr create, git -C in multi-worktree sessions, post-merge content verification. Unblocks 93z.9/.10/.11 — for real this time.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: CORRECTION: PR #34 did NOT merge this work. gh pr create ran in a directory whose checked-out branch was bugbot-1et-dsn-hardening (persistent-cwd instability), so PR #34 was an empty re-merge of already-landed DSN content wearing the resolver title — CI green because a no-op merge is green. The real reviewed branch bugbot-93z8-ecosystem-resolver (58cf140, gates + podman integration green, adversarial review passed with NITs applied) is intact on origin and is being re-landed via a fresh PR with an explicit --head. Downstream impact: the Python dep-strategy implementation (93z.11 branch) was built against a main that lacked the resolver and reimplemented a divergent SetupCmds mechanism — it must be reworked onto the real table once this lands. 93z.9/.10 remain blocked until the re-land merges.

title: Setup wizard: bugbot init --interactive for new-repo onboarding
description: Plain 'bugbot init' dumps the 140-line commented StarterYAML and leaves the user to edit it blind. Add an interactive mode that detects first and asks only what detection cannot answer: (1) provider — probe which key env vars are set (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, ...) and default accordingly; role models accept-defaults-with-Enter; (2) sandbox runtime — auto-pick whichever of podman/docker is installed; (3) scan excludes — propose repo-specific entries from a quick ingest pass (vendor/build dirs actually present) instead of the generic list; (4) feature toggles — repro, patch_prover, publish, each with a one-line cost/benefit; (5) write bugbot.yaml, run the doctor checks (bugbot-64p), print next steps. TTY-gated: non-terminal stdin errors with a pointer to plain init. Same O_EXCL no-clobber behavior as init.
design: Prompts via stdlib bufio on cmd.InOrStdin — linear flow, no TUI dependency (charmbracelet/huh considered, rejected for dep weight on a quicker task). Config rendered from the commented template with substitution points (text/template over StarterYAML), NOT yaml.Marshal of Config, which would strand every comment. Detection reuses ingest.DetectBuildSystems / DominantLanguages and the doctor check funcs from bugbot-64p — that dependency is why this bead is blocked on it. Flag spelled --interactive on the existing init command (no new top-level verb; init remains the non-interactive path).
acceptance_criteria: bugbot init --interactive on a fresh repo with a TTY produces a valid bugbot.yaml (config.Load passes) reflecting the answers, with template comments preserved. Every prompt has a default; Enter-through-everything yields a config equivalent to StarterYAML modulo detected runtime/provider. Non-TTY stdin exits with a clear error. Existing bugbot.yaml is never overwritten. Ends with the doctor checklist. Prompt flow unit-tested with scripted stdin.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: bugbot doctor: non-interactive environment and config diagnostics
description: Onboarding failures currently surface late and cryptically (scan fails at runtime when an API key env is unset or podman is missing). Add a 'bugbot doctor' command that runs a checklist and reports pass/warn/fail per item: (1) config loads+validates, show resolved path; (2) each configured provider's api_key_env is set — never print values; (3) sandbox runtime binary on PATH and responding (podman/docker info), configured image present locally or 'pull needed'; (4) repo facts: git repo, DominantLanguages, DetectBuildSystems, warn when a detected language lacks repro/build-detection tiers (cross-ref bugbot-93z); (5) informational: gh CLI for publish, LSP servers installed for detected languages (DefaultServers registry). Exit nonzero iff any hard check fails (config invalid, provider key missing, runtime absent); repo-fact and informational items never fail the exit code.
design: Checks implemented as a slice of named check funcs returning (status, detail) so the wizard bead can reuse them programmatically. Runtime probe via exec with short context timeout. Language-tier warnings read the same tables the funnel uses rather than a duplicate list.
acceptance_criteria: bugbot doctor in a correctly configured repo exits 0 with all-green checklist. Unset api_key_env, missing runtime binary, and invalid config each produce a clear failing line and nonzero exit. Secret values never appear in output. Runtime probe respects a timeout (wedged podman must not hang doctor). Unit tests with injected lookups for PATH/env/runtime; no container required in CI.
close_reason: Merged in PR #31: bugbot doctor implemented as a slice of named check funcs over an injectable doctorEnv (lookupEnv/lookPath/runCommand/snapshot seams + out writer), so the setup wizard (bugbot-8ng) can reuse the checks programmatically — that design is in internal/cli/doctor.go. Hard-fail set is exactly config-invalid / provider api_key_env unset / runtime absent-or-unresponsive; repo facts and informational items never affect exit. Key decisions: (1) all external probes bounded — 5s sandboxProbeTimeout for info AND image inspect, 30s repoFactsTimeout for git+snapshot, WaitDelay=2s on the production runCommand so a grandchild holding pipes can't outlive ctx kill (the latter three came from the adversarial review round — Sonnet implemented the timeout only where the spec named it); (2) doctor reads live tables via new exported ingest.ExtensionsForLanguage and repro.HasGuidance — repro guidance refactored into one specificGuidance map powering both langGuidance and HasGuidance so drift is structurally impossible (prompt text byte-identical); (3) lsp.DefaultServers() consumed dynamically (composed cleanly with the rust-analyzer merge); (4) image-absent is WARN 'pull needed', not a failure; secrets: only env var NAMES printed, with a test pinning value absence. Follow-up filed: bugbot-o0e (language check builds a full content-sniffing snapshot — too heavy for an informational line; also extend the secrets test to an error path).

title: C++ standard detection for version-aware personas (C++11 vs C++20)
description: A 'senior C++ engineer' persona papers over the C++11/C++20 divide — idioms, footguns, and review instincts differ by standard (ranges/concepts/coroutines vs pre-move-semantics code). Detect the repo's C++ standard and qualify the persona ('senior C++17 engineer'). Sources, in confidence order: compile_commands.json -std= flags (highest fidelity, per-TU); CMakeLists CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD / target_compile_features(cxx_std_NN); meson cpp_std=; Makefile CXXFLAGS -std= (best-effort grep). Architectural note: Language is deliberately a coarse fixed vocabulary (lang.go comment) — do NOT add LangCPP11/LangCPP20 variants; carry the dialect as a separate annotation on the snapshot/profile that PersonaLanguages consumes (e.g. a Language->qualifier map). Same mechanism could later serve Python 2/3 or Java 8/21 qualifiers. Depends on C++ build-system detection: the markers live in the build files that bead teaches ingest to find and parse.
acceptance_criteria: A repo with CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 20 (or equivalent -std=c++20 in compile_commands.json) yields a persona containing 'C++20'. Repos with no detectable standard fall back to unqualified 'C++'. Conflicting signals resolve by the documented confidence order. Persona output remains deterministic. Unit tests cover each marker source and the fallback.
close_reason: Merged in PR #36. DetectCPPStandard(repoDir) probes four sources in confidence order: compile_commands.json at root or build/ (-std=c++NN/-std=gnu++NN from command/arguments; most-common across TUs, ties to the HIGHER standard — rationale: mixed-standard repos target the higher one for non-legacy code; chronological ranking via explicit standardOrder map because 98>03 numerically but C++03 is newer), CMakeLists CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD/target_compile_features cxx_std_NN, meson cpp_std=, Makefile CXXFLAGS-ish lines only (avoids stray -std= in recipes). gnu++NN normalizes to C++NN. Personas: generic map[Language]string qualifier on PersonaLanguages (NO Language enum variants per lang.go's coarse-vocabulary design — mechanism reusable for Python 2/3, Java 8/21); a C++-dominant repo with CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 20 yields 'senior C++20 engineer'. Wiring decision: Snapshot gains Root (populated by Repo.Snapshot) so Persona(snap) kept its signature — zero change at funnel/run.go; verify_finding.go passes nil qualifiers (single-finding context has no root). Review round fixed one real bug: bare f.Read short-read on multi-MB compile_commands.json would truncate JSON and silently demote detection — now io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader) under the 4MiB cap. Determinism pinned by 50-iteration test.

title: C++ build-system detection: CMake, Meson, Makefile/ninja
description: C/C++ has detection, personas, and clangd LSP, but DetectBuildSystems knows nothing about C++ build systems and there is no repro guidance. C++ has no uniform test entry point, so tier the support: (1) CMake (CMakeLists.txt) — primary target, repro via ctest or direct test binary after cmake -B build && cmake --build; (2) Meson (meson.build) — meson test; (3) raw Makefile / build.ninja — detect and record, but repro falls back to the generic prompt (heterogeneous targets, do not guess). Add langGuidance for LangCPP/LangC keyed to the detected build system (GoogleTest/Catch2 conventions for CMake repos). Sandbox: needs cmake/ninja/gcc-or-clang in the image; configure+build cost per repro run is significant — note timeout implications in design. Detection ordering vs Bazel matters (Bazel C++ repos have both markers; Bazel should win, which the existing priority order already handles).
design: Settled design (orchestrator): constants BuildSystemCMake(CMakeLists.txt)/BuildSystemMeson(meson.build)/BuildSystemMake(Makefile|GNUmakefile)/BuildSystemNinja(build.ninja) appended AFTER python in DetectBuildSystems priority (generic markers must not displace language-specific primaries; Bazel-wins ordering preserved by position). Guidance: langGuidance(lang, systems []ingest.BuildSystem) — C/C++ + cmake → ctest-after-cmake-B-build guidance (GoogleTest/Catch2); + meson → meson test; raw make/ninja → genericGuidance (heterogeneous targets, never invent commands). HasGuidance becomes variadic HasGuidance(lang, systems ...BuildSystem) so doctor's existing call still compiles; doctor checkLangTier updated to pass its detected systems. Reproducer detects build systems once in New (ingest.DetectBuildSystems(repoDir)), threads to systemPrompt. Sandbox image needs cmake/ninja/compiler + configure+build cost per repro — recorded here for the image/timeout follow-up, no code in this bead.
acceptance_criteria: DetectBuildSystems returns cmake/meson/make build systems from their root markers, ordered after Bazel. Repro guidance for a C++ finding in a CMake repo describes a ctest-runnable test. Raw-make repos get generic guidance, not invented commands. Unit tests for marker detection and ordering.
close_reason: Merged in PR #32 (edfa72b). DetectBuildSystems gains cmake/meson/make/ninja at priority positions 8-11 — appended after python so generic markers never displace language-specific primaries (go.mod+Makefile keeps go_module primary; Bazel C++ repos keep bazel first), with ordering pinned by table tests. Repro guidance tiered by detected build system: CMake → cmake -B build -S . && cmake --build build, then ctest --test-dir build -R <TestName> --output-on-failure (GoogleTest/Catch2 conventions noted); Meson → meson setup/compile/test; raw make/ninja → deliberately generic (heterogeneous targets, never invent commands — pinned by negative prompt tests). langGuidance/systemPrompt take []BuildSystem; Reproducer detects once in New. HasGuidance is variadic so doctor compiled unchanged, and doctor now passes its detected systems so a CMake C++ repo no longer warns about missing guidance. NOT in this bead (recorded in design field): sandbox image needs cmake/ninja/compiler and configure+build cost affects repro timeouts — relevant to the image/timeout story when C++ repro is exercised for real; 93z.6 (C++ standard detection) remains open and now has its buildsys foundation.

title: Java to full pipeline: build detection and repro guidance
description: Java is detected and persona-named but missing everything below: no pom.xml/build.gradle(.kts) build detection, no repro guidance, no LSP. Scope: (1) DetectBuildSystems entries for Maven (pom.xml) and Gradle (build.gradle, build.gradle.kts, settings.gradle*); (2) langGuidance for LangJava (JUnit test + mvn test -Dtest=X / gradle test --tests X). LSP is explicitly OUT of scope: jdtls is heavyweight (JVM, workspace dirs, slow warmup) and deserves its own bead with an evaluation of whether it pays for itself in the agent loop. Sandbox: needs JDK+maven/gradle in the image — same single-image constraint as C#.
acceptance_criteria: DetectBuildSystems distinguishes Maven vs Gradle repos. Repro guidance for LangJava names the detected build tool's single-test invocation. jdtls follow-up bead filed with evaluation criteria. Kotlin (.kt) build detection falls out of the Gradle markers — note in design whether LangKotlin repro guidance rides along.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Dep-strategy note (from the 93z.8-93z.12 batch): sandbox repro for Java also needs a dep-resolver entry once 93z.8 lands — Maven ~/.m2/repository or Gradle ~/.gradle/caches ro-mount + 'mvn dependency:go-offline' / 'gradle --refresh-dependencies' prefetch. Gradle's cache is lock-heavy and may fight a read-only mount — needs empirical verification; note alongside the JDK image constraint already recorded here.

title: C# to full pipeline: build detection, repro guidance, LSP
description: C# is detected and persona-named but has nothing underneath: no build-system detection (.sln/.csproj/Directory.Build.props), no repro guidance, no LSP, no tree-sitter. Unlike C++, the ecosystem is uniform: dotnet test works almost everywhere, making the build/repro layers cheap. Scope: (1) DetectBuildSystems entries for *.sln / *.csproj / Directory.Build.props — note these need a glob, not the current exact-name has() helper; (2) langGuidance for LangCSharp (xUnit/NUnit test file + dotnet test --filter); (3) LSP via csharp-ls (lightweight) over OmniSharp. Tree-sitter grammar optional, separate decision. Sandbox constraint: repro requires the dotnet SDK in the single configured sandbox image — decide whether to grow the default image or document an override.
acceptance_criteria: DetectBuildSystems returns a dotnet build system for a repo with a root .sln or .csproj (including globbed names). Repro agent prompt for a C# finding includes dotnet test guidance. csharp-ls registered in DefaultServers with graceful absence. Sandbox image decision recorded in --design or a follow-up bead.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Dep-strategy note (from the 93z.8-93z.12 batch): sandbox repro for C# also needs a NuGet entry in the per-ecosystem dep resolver once 93z.8 lands — ~/.nuget/packages ro-mount + 'dotnet restore' prefetch keyed on packages.lock.json (or restore in-sandbox from the mount). Not a blocker for this bead's build-detection/repro-guidance scope, but the sandbox image decision recorded here should account for it.

title: Add rust-analyzer to LSP DefaultServers
description: Rust has Cargo build detection and cargo-test repro guidance, but no entry in DefaultServers (internal/lsp/server.go:34), so Rust repos get no LSP code-nav. Add a rust-analyzer config (.rs -> languageId rust). Note rust-analyzer indexing is slow on large workspaces — verify the manager's warmup/indexing handling (lsp/manager.go comment about gopls indexing) behaves acceptably.
acceptance_criteria: A Rust repo with rust-analyzer installed resolves go-to-definition/references through the LSP tier. Graceful fallback (existing behavior) when rust-analyzer is not installed. Unit test for the registry entry; integration test if the existing fakeserver pattern supports it.
close_reason: Merged in PR #29 (c9347e9): rust-analyzer registered in lsp.DefaultServers serving .rs with languageId rust and no args (stdio is its default), placed after pylsp with doc comment updated. Graceful not-installed fallback is the existing exec.LookPath path in startServer — no new logic needed. Slow first-index on big workspaces is covered by DefaultQueryTimeout (60s) returning the 'may still be indexing; fall back to grep' error; later queries hit the still-running server. TestDefaultServersRustAnalyzer pins uniqueness of the .rs claim, cmd, languageId, and empty Args. Fakeserver integration skipped: the fake hardcodes .go and WithServers replaces the registry, so it cannot exercise DefaultServers cheaply.

title: Add JavaScript to tree-sitter grammarTable (.js/.jsx/.mjs/.cjs)
description: JavaScript has LSP support, repro guidance, and npm build detection, but .js/.jsx/.mjs/.cjs are absent from grammarTable (internal/treesitter/grammars.go:90), so JS files get no tree-sitter code-nav tier — an inconsistency, not a missing feature. The tsx grammar parses modern JS (and JSX) fine; reuse the existing tsDefQuery/tsRefQuery via the javascript or tsx registry grammar. Watch the gotchas documented in the TS/TSX comment: registry grammars are keyed by sample filename and JSX fails under plain typescript.
acceptance_criteria: DetectLanguage-classified JS files (.js, .jsx, .mjs, .cjs) produce tree-sitter definitions and references. Unit test covers a .jsx file with JSX syntax (proves the chosen grammar parses it). Existing TS/TSX tests still pass.
close_reason: Merged in PR #30 (ef6d36d): .js/.jsx/.mjs/.cjs map to the existing tsxGrammar pointer in grammarTable. Decision rationale: the shared TS tags queries capture interface_declaration/type_alias_declaration (TS-only node types) that fail query compilation against the registry's plain javascript grammar, and plain typescript cannot parse JSX — tsx is the superset that parses plain JS, JSX, and TS. Pointer sharing makes .tsx+JS extensions one navigation family via the rank.go pointer-equality scan; plain .ts stays its own family (pre-existing split). Tests: TestJSXFileDefsAndRefs (real JSX in .jsx produces defs+refs), TestPlainJSFileDefsAndRefs, Supports() coverage for all four extensions. Review round fixed one wrong comment (claimed .ts joined the family).

title: Language support improvements: close per-layer coverage gaps
description: Language support spans seven independent layers (detection in ingest/lang.go, personas in ingest/profile.go, tree-sitter nav in treesitter/grammars.go, LSP registry in lsp/server.go, repro guidance in repro/agent.go, build-system detection in ingest/buildsys.go, sandbox dependency strategy in sandbox/deps.go) and coverage is uneven: only Go gets the full pipeline (Python/TS get most layers; the deps layer from bugbot-2ry is Go-only). This epic tracks closing the gaps, ordered: (1) fix inconsistencies in already-claimed languages (JS tree-sitter, Rust LSP), (2) C#/Java to full pipeline, (3) C++ build systems + version-aware personas, (4) generalize the sandbox dep resolver and plug in Rust/JS/Python so non-vendored repos build under network-none (93z.8-93z.12), (5) one-line detection batch for minors. Most layers are data-driven tables by design; the deps layer becomes one via 93z.8.
acceptance_criteria: All child beads closed. Each language layer table documents its coverage tier. No language is persona-named while silently missing a layer the funnel relies on for that repo type.

title: Finder tool: read_symbol — LSP/tree-sitter-backed 'read just this function body' to cut whole-file reads
description: Follow-up from bugbot-3nf token-burn work and user question. Finders already have read_file offset/limit windowing, grep, and LSP code-nav (definition/references/implementations return locations + bounded snippets). Missing: a tool that takes a symbol (or file+position), resolves its definition via the existing tiered navigator (lsp.Manager with tree-sitter fallback, internal/agent/tools_codenav.go), and returns ONLY that declaration's full body as numbered lines. This would let finders pull a 40-line function instead of an 800+-line capped file read, compounding the bugbot-3nf read-cap savings cache-safely (append-only).
acceptance_criteria: New finder tool (e.g. read_symbol or read_definition) returns the enclosing declaration body for a symbol via LSP with tree-sitter fallback, bounded (reuse codeNav caps); unit tests with the scripted navigator pattern from tools_codenav_test.go; tool description nudges finders to prefer it over whole-file reads; eval precision gate stays green; offline token simulation (pattern from bugbot-3nf's measurement harness) shows reduction on the recorded corpus or a justified estimate.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #26 (commit 1877608). read_symbol tool: exact declaration bodies via new treesitter Backend.DefinitionBodies (gts.Tag.Range = full node; existing Definition keeps NameRange so find_definition unchanged); LSP intentionally bypassed for supported languages (Go/Py/TS/TSX); positional 100-line window + caveat for others. KEY REVIEW FINDING: @definition capture EXCLUDES decorators (Python def/class, TS methods) — fixed with bounded backward scan over contiguous @-lines (Tag carries no parent-node handle, so range-level fix impossible); decorated-symbol tests pin it. Caps 400 lines/32KB, actionable truncation, symlink-safe containment with out-of-root canary test. Measured ~80% per-lookup token saving (honest estimate, weak-floor asserted). Eval precision 1.000.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Context from 3nf: finders self-feed whole files via read_file then re-send them every turn; read caps (800/96KB) bound this but a symbol-body read would eliminate most of it at the source. See compact_measure_test.go for the cost model to evaluate against.

title: Build-system detection + build-graph-aware blast radius (non-sandbox subset of bugbot-0ko)
description: The non-blocked subset of bugbot-0ko: (a) detect the build system (Bazel WORKSPACE/MODULE.bazel, go.work, pnpm/turbo/nx workspaces) beyond current go.mod/Cargo.toml/package.json/pyproject markers in detectSuiteCmd (internal/sandbox/patch.go:161), and (c) build-graph-aware blast radius using the native tool's reverse-dep query (go list, bazel query rdeps) in BlastRadius (blast.go:40) instead of the basename-only textual fallback. Part (b) sandbox dependency strategy stays on bugbot-0ko, blocked by bugbot-2ry.
acceptance_criteria: Build system detected for Bazel and a JS monorepo (turbo/nx/pnpm) in addition to current markers; blast radius uses the build tool's reverse-dep query when available, falling back to current behavior; unit/integration test on a small multi-module fixture; gates green; no dependency on sandbox network/dep changes.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #20 (commit be0941a). DetectBuildSystems (internal/ingest/buildsys.go) detects Bazel/go.work/pnpm-turbo-nx + existing markers in deterministic priority; detectSuiteCmd maps Bazel→bazel test //..., pnpm→pnpm test, go.work-without-root-go.mod→nil (documented limitation). Blast radius gained an ADDITIVE bazel query rdeps native path (injectable queryRunner seam, LookPath precheck, 30s timeout, single-quoted labels) that can only widen results — Go import graph + textual fallback always still run. KEY FINDING: go.work cross-module dependents already covered by existing import suffix-matching — proven by test, no exec path needed for Go.

title: Build-system awareness for complex repos (Bazel/monorepo): sandbox build + blast radius
description:
DOGFOOD + scoping finding: bugbot's sandbox build/test and blast-radius are effectively single-module-Go. Two gaps for complex/real repos: (1) SANDBOX BUILD — workspace is a wholesale tree copy (sandbox/workspace.go copyTree) run network-none; deps must be vendored (we had to 'go mod vendor' bugbot to dogfood it, see bugbot-2ry). Build-system detection is only the go.mod/Cargo.toml/package.json/pyproject markers in detectSuiteCmd (patch.go:161). A Bazel/Buck/Pants monorepo, or any repo with external deps and no vendoring, can't build in the sandbox. (2) BLAST RADIUS — BlastRadius (blast.go:40) is a Go-import graph with a basename-only textual fallback for other languages; it has no notion of a build graph (BUILD.bazel targets, workspace packages). On a monorepo this both over- and under-scopes the targeted scan.

SCOPE (likely an epic — split when picked up): (a) detect the build system (Bazel WORKSPACE/MODULE.bazel, pnpm/turbo/nx workspaces, go.work) and choose the right build/test invocation; (b) sandbox dependency strategy — mounted read-only module cache, or a gated one-time online fetch step before the network-none run, or 'bazel build' inside the sandbox with a warm cache (depends on bugbot-2ry); (c) build-graph-aware blast radius using the native tool ('bazel query rdeps', go list) instead of the textual fallback. This is the gating capability for bugbot to run on large real-world repos rather than self-contained modules.
acceptance_criteria: Build system detected for at least Bazel and a JS monorepo (turbo/nx/pnpm) in addition to current markers; sandbox can build+test a repo with external deps via a documented strategy (cache mount or vendor); blast radius uses the build tool's reverse-dep query when available, falling back to current behavior; integration test on a small multi-module fixture; gates green. May be delivered as sub-beads.
close_reason: All three parts delivered across sub-work: (a) build-system detection + (c) build-graph blast radius via bugbot-52g (PR #20); (b) sandbox dependency strategy via bugbot-2ry (PR #25 — vendor detection, RO module-cache mounts, gated prefetch; verified against an external-dep repo under podman). Acceptance met in full: Bazel + JS-monorepo detection, documented+tested sandbox dep strategy, native reverse-dep blast radius with fallback, multi-module fixtures, gates green.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Split non-blocked subset (build-system detection + build-graph blast radius) into bugbot-52g for this parallel-agent run; part (b) sandbox dep strategy remains here, blocked on bugbot-2ry.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Parts (a) detection and (c) build-graph blast radius shipped via bugbot-52g / PR #20. Remaining scope here: part (b) sandbox dependency strategy — still blocked on bugbot-2ry.

title: Agent improvement: repo/language-aware expert seeding + context injection
description:
Scoping finding: agents are STATICALLY prompted and Go-hardcoded. finderSystemBase opens 'You are a meticulous senior Go engineer' (prompt.go:16); verifier base and repro agent.go:17 are likewise Go-specific. No README/framework/language detection feeds the prompts; no few-shot examples; the ONLY dynamic context injection that exists is the leads blackboard appending to finderTask (prompt.go:96-104) — that's the proven precedent to build on.

IDEAS TO EXPLORE (pick the highest-leverage subset when implemented): (1) LANGUAGE/PERSONA: derive the dominant language(s) from the snapshot (ingest already classifies File.Language) and seed the persona accordingly ('senior Rust engineer' etc.) instead of hardcoded Go — directly unblocks non-Go repos and pairs with the Go-hardcoding noted in bugbot-<bazel> and the repro agent. (2) REPO CONTEXT: inject a compact repo profile into the system or task prompt — languages, build system, key frameworks, and a short README/AGENTS.md excerpt — so finders reason with project conventions (mirror the leads-injection mechanism; bound the size hard, sanitize like leads notes). (3) EXPERT SEEDING per lens: richer, calibrated specialization text or few-shot good/bad examples per lens. (4) Ties to the planned-but-unbuilt repo-specific lens generation (no scaffolding exists yet). Measure against the eval suite — any prompt change must hold precision 1.0 and not regress recall.

NOTE: language-aware persona (idea 1) is the smallest and removes a hard Go assumption that blocks every non-Go repo; consider splitting it out as a fast first win.
acceptance_criteria: At minimum the finder/verifier/repro personas are language-aware (derived from snapshot language mix, not hardcoded Go); optionally a bounded repo-context block injected via the leads-style mechanism; eval suite still gates precision 1.0 with no recall regression on the Go corpus; a non-Go fixture shows the adapted persona; approach + measured effect documented. May be split into sub-beads (language-persona first).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #22 (commit 6dc624a) — the language-persona first win. Personas derive from snapshot language mix via ingest.DominantLanguages (file-count weighted, top-3, LangOther/LangShell excluded, deterministic tie-break); finder/verifier bases became persona-taking functions, ALL other wording byte-identical (reviewer verified by compiling old vs new text). VerifyFinding + repro agent key off finding-file language; repro prompt has per-language test guidance (Go verbatim, +Python/JS-TS/Rust, generic fallback). Eval routing keys on lens names not persona text — precision gate held at 1.000/recall 1.000/zero clean FPs. Adversarial review: clean. REMAINING IDEAS kept open here as future scope: bounded repo-context block injection (leads-style), per-lens few-shot seeding, repo-specific lens generation. Reviewer nice-to-have: golden byte-identity test for prompt bases would guard future drift.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Dependency note: the language-aware PERSONA sub-win (derive 'senior <lang> engineer' from snapshot File.Language) has NO dependency on build-system work (bugbot-0ko) — it's a self-contained prompt change and the highest-leverage fast first win (removes the hard Go assumption blocking every non-Go repo). Only the deeper repo-context-injection idea (build system, frameworks) would benefit from bugbot-0ko's detection. Split the persona out and do it standalone.

title: Reproduction-only daemon mode: promote open unreproduced findings toward T1/T0
description:
Today the daemon's promoteNewFindings (postcycle.go:179-209) only attempts reproduction on THIS cycle's freshly-scanned T2 findings; an open T2/T3 finding that was never reproduced (e.g. budget ran out before repro, or repro was disabled that cycle) stays stuck forever. Add a mode that periodically pulls OPEN, unreproduced findings (tier>=2, status=open, no ReproPath) from the store and runs them through the existing repro+patch-prover pipeline to promote toward T1/T0.

WHY CHEAP: the pieces exist. repro.PromoteAll(ctx, st, []store.Finding) already takes an ARBITRARY finding slice with no freshness assumption (promote.go:55). store.ListFindings(FindingFilter{Status:StatusOpen}) already queries the store (used at postcycle.go:69). The daemon already constructs + wires the Reproducer. This is a query + a new post-cycle step (or a dedicated low-frequency cycle), not new machinery.

DESIGN: add a store query for open findings with tier>=2 AND ReproPath=='' (and not NeedsHuman, to avoid retrying known-unfixable). Run them via PromoteAll on a configurable cadence (reuse SweepInterval or a new repro_interval; or a bounded batch each post-cycle to avoid spending the whole budget on backlog). Respect the per-day budget. Optionally a standalone 'bugbot repro' CLI subcommand (none exists today) for one-shot backlog drain over the store, mirroring scan --repro's wiring but sourcing findings from the store instead of a scan. Gate behind the same sandbox-availability + EnableRepro checks.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon (or a new bugbot repro command) reproduces open tier>=2 findings lacking a ReproPath, promoting demonstrated ones to T1 (and T0 when patch-prover is on); backlog drain is budget-bounded (does not blow per-day on old findings); NeedsHuman findings are skipped; unit test with seeded open-unreproduced findings asserts they get attempted; status pane / stats reflect backlog repro; gates green.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #21 (commit 9cc7304). Daemon gained a third scheduler timer (ReproBacklogInterval default 1h) draining open T2/T3 ReproPath=='' !NeedsHuman findings through PromoteAll, batch-bounded (BacklogBatch default 3), day-budget-gated at firing time. KEY DESIGN (review must-fix): rotation instead of retry-burn — failed attempts touch the finding (upsert bumps updated_at) and OpenBacklog orders oldest-updated-first so unreproducible findings cycle to the back; no schema change needed. New 'bugbot repro' one-shot drain command (--max), NOT day-budget-gated by design (operator-initiated, like scan --repro). Backlog ledger spend uses empty scan-run id (spans historic runs; TotalsSince day-budget unaffected). NextBacklog added to progress events.

title: Harden store DSN construction against pragma override via path (bugbot self-finding)
description: bugbot found this in its OWN store layer during the dogfood self-scan (bugbot-9bd, T1 reproduced finding at internal/store/store.go:78). 'path' is concatenated raw into the SQLite DSN: dsn := path + "?_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)...". A storage path containing query characters ('?', '&', '_pragma=', '_txlock=') would inject or OVERRIDE bugbot's pragmas — e.g. silently disabling foreign_keys or WAL, weakening the durability/integrity guarantees the rest of the store relies on. TRIAGE: real true-positive, but the path is operator-controlled config (storage.path) not attacker input, so severity is correctness/robustness not security. Fix: percent-encode the path before appending the query, or use a url.URL with opaque path + encoded query, or reject paths containing query metacharacters with a clear config error. Validates bugbot's precision on real unseeded code.
acceptance_criteria: A storage.path containing '?'/'&'/'_pragma' cannot alter the intended pragmas (test: such a path is rejected or escaped so journal_mode/foreign_keys remain as configured); existing store tests pass; the self-reported finding's fingerprint resolves.
close_reason: Merged in PR #33: store.Open rejects database paths containing '?' or '#' with a clear config error, placed after the empty-path check and BEFORE MkdirAll so rejection has zero filesystem side effects. Decision: reject rather than percent-encode — the modernc driver's unescaping behavior for bare (non-file:-scheme) DSNs is not contractual, so encoding risks silently opening a differently-named file, which is worse than refusing; '?'/'#' appear in no legitimate database path and storage.path is operator config where a loud early error is correct UX. '&' alone remains allowed (inert outside a query string — pinned by test so we don't over-restrict). Tests cover the real attack shape (path embedding _pragma=journal_mode(DELETE)), the '#' fragment-truncation variant, and PRAGMA journal_mode/foreign_keys queried live to prove the configured pragmas are in effect. The originating self-scan finding's fingerprint will resolve via the reconciler on the next dogfood self-scan — validates bugbot's precision on real unseeded code (origin: bugbot-9bd T1 finding).

title: Human-facing GitHub issue body: clear bugbot attribution, collapse agent-internal metadata, inline repro
description:
USER FEEDBACK from live self-scan: the published issue is good but mixes human-relevant content with agent-internal noise (lens, tier jargon, raw verification trace) and isn't clearly marked as bugbot-authored. Do a full human-readability pass over renderIssueBody (internal/cli/publish.go:364).

DECISIONS (from scope discussion):
1. ATTRIBUTION: footer line '🤖 Filed by Bugbot — automated finding; verify before acting' plus the existing 'bugbot' config label. Clear, not shouting (no top banner).
2. COLLAPSE agent-internal fields into a '<details><summary>Bugbot metadata</summary>' footer block rather than dropping them — nothing lost, but the issue reads like a human wrote it. Lead the body with: title (H2), severity, location, description, then fix-witness diff (from bugbot-n8f, when FixPatch present) and inline repro (item 4). Move into the metadata footer: lens, corroborating lenses, tier label, fingerprint, AND richer provenance a maintainer needs to assess trust — model(s) used, provider, commit SHA scanned, scan timestamp. The verification trace stays in its own separate collapsed <details> (it's useful but verbose).
3. PROVENANCE PLUMBING: store.Finding has CommitSHA + CreatedAt but NOT model/provider. Add model+provider to the finding (or thread via the publish call) so the metadata footer can show 'found by MiniMax-M3'. Smallest viable: record the finder/verifier model on the finding at persist time, or look it up from config at publish time (config is the simpler source — publish already has cfg). Prefer config lookup to avoid a schema migration unless per-finding model attribution is wanted.
4. INLINE REPRO (was the original bead scope): the repro is currently referenced as a local artifact path ('Reproduction script: .bugbot/repro/<id>') meaningless on GitHub. Inline the repro test source + run command in a collapsed <details> code block; gh issues have no file attachments via api so inline is the pragmatic default. Patch-prover diff already renders inline (verify it does in publish, not just markdown report).

Net effect: a maintainer sees a clean, actionable bug report with a fix to try and a repro to run; the bugbot bookkeeping is one collapse-click away and clearly labeled.
acceptance_criteria: renderIssueBody leads with human content (title/severity/location/description/fix-diff/inline-repro); lens, tier, fingerprint, corroborating lenses, model/provider, commit, timestamp live in a collapsed 'Bugbot metadata' details block; a footer line attributes the issue to Bugbot; verification trace remains separately collapsed; repro test source renders inline (no bare local path); model/provider provenance shown (config-sourced is fine); publish_test asserts the metadata is collapsed and the attribution footer present; gates green.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #19 (commit 539193f). renderIssueBody now leads with human content (title/severity/location/description/fix-diff/inline-repro from artifact dir); lens/tier/fingerprint/corroborating/model+provider(config-sourced)/commit/timestamp in collapsed 'Bugbot metadata' details; attribution footer; trace separately collapsed. Review hardening: dynamic-length fences (CommonMark) against fence breakout, <details> sanitization in reasoning, FixPatch 20KB + Description 10KB caps + 60KB total clamp preserving marker line and footer; repro walk skips patch.diff/non-source. Provenance via publishProvenance struct from config — no schema migration.

title: Sandbox needs a dependency strategy for repos with external modules
description: DOGFOOD FINDING (bugbot-9bd): the network-none sandbox can only build/test a repo with deps if they are vendored (had to 'go mod vendor' bugbot before the dogfood run). Real repro/patch-prover/sandbox-exec on most repos needs either vendored deps, a pre-populated module cache mounted read-only, or a one-time online module-fetch step before the network-none run. Document and/or automate.
acceptance_criteria: Documented strategy (vendor detection or mounted module cache) so repro/sandbox-exec works on a non-vendored module; sandbox-exec/repro verified against a repo with external deps.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #25 (commit f0d20d8). Spec gained ROMounts/RWMounts; ResolveDeps strategies: vendored (auto, GOFLAGS=-mod=vendor), off (default), host (host GOMODCACHE ro-mounted at /modcache, GOPROXY=off fail-fast), fetch (one-time go mod download in network-enabled hardened container into bugbot-owned user-cache keyed on go.sum, sentinel written only on success, then ro-mounted). KEY SECURITY DECISIONS: LLM tool args cannot reach mounts/network/env (review-traced); shared host mounts render :ro with NO SELinux relabel (:Z would corrupt the shared host cache labels — per-mount Shared flag, EACCES under enforcing SELinux is the documented conservative failure, fetch is the opt-out). Verified empirically under podman: external-dep module passes go test network-none with cache mount, fails without (integration-tagged tests). Config: sandbox.dep_strategy.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Implemented dependency strategy (branch bugbot-2ry-sandbox-deps). Spec gains ROMounts (read-only) + RWMounts (trusted prefetch only), rendered -v host:ctr:ro,Z / rw,Z with absolute-path + combined-uniqueness validation; security posture comments extended (RO mounts expose host content -> callers must mount only public/cache content). New internal/sandbox/deps.go: ResolveDeps(repoDir, DepOptions) -> Resolution{ROMounts, Env, Prefetch, Strategy}. Strategies: vendored (auto, GOFLAGS=-mod=vendor), off (default), host (mount host GOMODCACHE ro at /modcache + GOMODCACHE/GOFLAGS=-mod=mod/GOPROXY=off), fetch (one-time 'go mod download all' in network-enabled hardened container into os.UserCacheDir()/bugbot/modcache/<repohash>, keyed on go.sum sentinel so no re-fetch, then mount ro). Kept sandbox stdlib-only: direct go.mod/vendor marker check, no ingest import (no cycle). Config: Sandbox.DepStrategy + BUGBOT_SANDBOX_DEP_STRATEGY + enum validation. Wired via repro.Options.DepStrategy (Reproducer resolves once in New, prefetch runs once in PromoteAll; PatchProver carries depMounts/depEnv) and funnel.SandboxOpts.DepStrategy (resolved in funnel.New, prefetch once via sync.Once in ensureDepPrefetch before sandbox_exec). buildSandboxOpts feeds all three SandboxOpts sites. Integration verified against real podman: host-cache strategy makes a network-none 'go test' with github.com/google/uuid succeed; negative control fails without strategy -> satisfies acceptance.
