Docs: README table + bugbot.yaml starter comment. Gates: build/vet/race/golangci-lint(0)/gofmt all clean.

title: Repro/patch-prover agent spend is not recorded in the spend table
description: Live validation (bugbot-7ib) found the spend table only has finder+verifier rows; reproducer-role and patch-prover agent token usage is never RecordSpend-ed, so day-budget accounting and the status pane undercount real spend whenever --repro runs. The repro stage runs outside the funnel's spend recording path.
acceptance_criteria: repro and patch-prover agent usage recorded with role=reproducer (or distinct patch-prover role) per scan run; day-budget getter includes it; test with scripted client asserting spend rows; gates green.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #16. Decision: ledgerRecorder (llm.Recorder over store.RecordSpend) in cli, wrapped at reproducer-client construction in scan + daemon paths; recorder outermost in the client stack so retried completions ledger once on success only; daemon's long-lived client retagged per cycle via daemon.Deps.ReproTagger (SetScanRun before PromoteAll; stale-tag-on-empty-cycle harmless since nothing records between cycles). Patch-prover usage flows through the same client under the reproducer role (distinct role deemed unnecessary). Review: merge-ready, zero must-fixes. Test scar worth remembering: store.Open(':memory:') with concurrent access fails — each pooled SQLite connection gets its own empty in-memory DB; use t.TempDir() file-backed stores in concurrent tests. Validation: dogfood scan immediately after merge confirms reproducer rows in spend ledger.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: VALIDATED on rebuilt binary: fixture scan --repro now ledgers reproducer|33 completions|98290 input tokens alongside finder+verifier. (First validation attempt showed zero reproducer rows due to a STALE bin/bugbot predating the merge — lesson: always 'make build' before validating a just-merged fix; 'cmp bin/bugbot <(go build)' or a version stamp would catch it.)

title: Pane of glass: bugbot status world-state summary + bugbot leads drill-down
description: bugbot status today only shows live activity (status.json). Extend it into the single pane of glass with a store-backed world-state block: findings by tier/status, needs-human queue count, pending leads count + short preview, publish sync state, today's spend vs budget, last completed run summary. Add 'bugbot leads' as the full blackboard drill-down (status: posted/consumed, poster->target, file:line, note, age; --pending filter). Decision from scope discussion: hub + drill-downs pattern; one command per table without a summary is the anti-pattern; live TUI deferred to follow-up bead.
acceptance_criteria: bugbot status renders the world-state block from the store even when no scan is running (activity block says idle); shows tier/status counts incl. needs-human, pending-leads preview, published open/closed counts, day spend vs per_day budget, last run line; bugbot leads lists newest-first with --pending filter and friendly empty output; store helpers covered by tests; command-level tests; gates green.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #15 (squash ea3b513). Decisions from the scope discussion: hub + drill-downs — bugbot status is THE pane of glass (live-activity block + new store-backed world-state block: findings by tier with needs-human callout, pending-leads preview max 3, GitHub sync counts, day spend vs per_day budget with one-decimal percent, last-run line), rendered when idle too; bugbot leads is the full blackboard drill-down (--pending filter); one-command-per-table without a summary explicitly rejected as the anti-pattern; live TUI deferred to bugbot-7td. Store gained ListLeads/CountFindings/CountPublishedIssues/LatestScanRun. Status never creates the store as a side effect (os.Stat guard, pinned). Review round caught truncateNote byte-slicing UTF-8 (LLM-authored notes; now rune-aware + pinned) and the duplicate day-spend line (world-state is now the single source since it adds budget percent). Validated against the live-validation fixture store: one screen shows the whole PRs-11-14 story. Process scar for memory: this PR's WIP commit accidentally captured .beads/issues.jsonl via 'git add -A', causing a merge conflict, a wrong-side rebase resolution (--theirs in rebase = the BRANCH), and a squash that regressed the beads export on main (restored by re-export in bb8257c).

title: False-positive memory: dismissals feed refuters as few-shot context
description: Make precision compound with use instead of resetting every scan. New 'bugbot dismiss <id> --reason' records a human dismissal (pattern, file, lens, fingerprint, reason) in the store; at verify time, refuters for a candidate get the K most relevant past dismissals (same lens, similar pattern/file) injected as few-shot 'previously dismissed because...' context. Re-surfacing a dismissed fingerprint is suppressed outright (suppression already exists — extend it with reasons). Relevance can start dumb (same lens + same file or package) and get smarter later. Synergy: the hash-anchored KV store planned in bugbot-dtq is the natural substrate; build on it.
acceptance_criteria: dismiss command persists reason + metadata; suppressed fingerprints never re-surface (existing behavior preserved, now with visible reason in report); refuter prompt for a candidate includes relevant dismissals when they exist (unit test asserts injection + relevance filter); recorded-corpus replay unaffected; tree green + lint clean.

title: Patch-prover: minimal candidate fix as a tier-0 confidence witness
description: Repro already demonstrates a bug with a failing sandboxed test; go one further: an agent writes a MINIMAL candidate fix patch, and the sandbox proves the failing test passes with the patch applied (and the rest of the suite still passes). A finding shipped with failing-test + working-patch is nearly review-free — promote to a new tier 0 ('demonstrated + fix witness'). The patch is a witness, not a recommendation: emit it in the report as a diff block with clear 'starting point, not reviewed' framing. Also a brutal implicit refuter: if no plausible minimal fix exists after N attempts, flag the finding for human review (might be misdiagnosed). Scope: Go targets first, reuse the repro sandbox machinery.
design: Patch-prover as a follow-on substage inside repro after att.Promoted: new repro/patch.go ProvePatch loop (config repro.patch_max_attempts, default 3). Patch mechanism = whole-file replacement via sandbox WriteFiles (no diff machinery exists); agent returns PatchPlan{files: map[path]full-content, summary}. CRITICAL validation: patch files must NOT touch the repro test files (else the agent can 'fix' the bug by editing the test) and must not be _test.go files at all. Two sandbox runs per attempt: (1) targeted repro cmd with test+patch applied -> exit 0 required (NOTE inversion: exit 0 = success here, opposite of repro), (2) full 'go test ./...' with test+patch applied -> exit 0 = suite green witness. Unified diff for the report computed via host 'git diff --no-index' between original and patched content, capped ~32KB, stored in new findings column fix_patch TEXT (migration 006 — 005 is reserved by bugbot-dx1) so report rendering stays pure; patched files + patch.diff also land in the artifact bundle. Success -> tier 0 ('T0 Fix-witnessed') — update tierName/tierLabel/writeCounts/SARIF props. Exhaustion -> needs_human INTEGER column (also migration 006) + attempt summary appended to Reasoning; rendered as meta line. Config: repro section {patch_prover: false, patch_max_attempts: 3} + env overrides; wired through cli runRepro and daemon promoteNewFindings. Report framing: 'candidate fix — a witness, starting point only, NOT reviewed'.
acceptance_criteria: Repro stage optionally attempts a fix patch after a demonstrated failure; sandbox verifies failing-test-passes + suite-still-green with patch applied; promoted findings carry tier 0 and the diff in report output; fix-not-found after N attempts marks the finding needs-human with the attempt summary; integration test gated like existing sandbox tests; tree green + lint clean.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #14 (squash 4b26aef), Sonnet implementer + review round + REAL-PODMAN verification. Decisions: patch = whole-file replacement via sandbox WriteFiles (no diff machinery exists); two-run proof per attempt (targeted repro cmd then full suite, both exit 0, patchVerdict deliberately separate from interpret() because semantics are inverted); env failures stop attempts WITHOUT needs_human. Four-rule plan validation pinned by tests: no repro-file collision (the load-bearing, language-independent guard), no test files (cross-language isTestPath: _test.go, test_*.py/*_test.py, *.test.*/*.spec.*, *_spec.rb, *Test.java/*Tests.cs, test dirs), no escapes, existing files only (new files out of scope). Tier 0 'fix-witnessed' across all renderers; fix_patch (32KB-capped git diff --no-index) + needs_human in migration 006; exhaustion appends attempt summary to Reasoning idempotently. GENERALIZED per user direction: suite cmd is repro.suite_cmd with marker detection (go.mod/Cargo.toml/package.json/pyproject/setup.py); unknown toolchain SKIPS (SkippedNoSuiteCmd) rather than guessing — wrong suite cmd would silently weaken the witness. Review must-fixes: diff-error text no longer leaks into the rendered diff block; flagNeedsHuman idempotent. Verified end-to-end against real podman (TestIntegration_PatchProver: genuine bug -> failing repro -> patch -> targeted pass -> suite green -> T0+diff, 23s) before AND after rebase. Known limits on bead: FindingFilter tier-0 sentinel collision documented (T0 not exact-filterable via --tier); LLM-driven live validation (does a real model write good minimal fixes?) pending next calibration run.

title: PR review mode: blast-radius scan posts inline review comments
description: Catch bugs pre-merge where they are cheapest. 'bugbot review --pr N': resolve the PR's base/head via gh, run the existing --since blast-radius targeted scan over the head checkout, then post verified findings as inline PR review comments (gh api) anchored to the diff positions, with the reasoning trace collapsed in a <details> block. The funnel's adversarial verification is the differentiator vs typical AI reviewers — only T2+ goes to the PR; suspected T3 goes to a single summary comment (or is withheld, config). Re-running on a new push must update/resolve stale comments via fingerprint, not duplicate. CI-friendly: nonzero exit on new T2 findings (configurable) so it can gate merges.
design: New cobra command internal/cli/review.go following scan.go pattern. PR resolution via 'gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/N' (gh auto-fills owner/repo placeholders — no resolution code). Require repo HEAD == PR head SHA (CI-friendly; error with guidance otherwise). Changed files via repo.ChangedFiles(baseSHA, headSHA) -> ChangedPaths -> funnel.Targeted (blast radius included). Inline comments: parse 'git diff base...head' hunks to compute RIGHT-side commentable lines; findings on commentable lines -> inline review comments (POST pulls/N/comments with commit_id/path/line/side), others + T3 suspected -> single summary issue comment. Lifecycle: hidden HTML marker '<!-- bugbot:fp=... -->' in each comment body; re-run lists existing comments, builds fp->id map, PATCHes changed bodies, creates new, marks stale ones resolved via body edit (no GraphQL thread resolution). Summary comment keyed by '<!-- bugbot:summary -->' marker, updated in place, includes spend/reliability stats from funnel.Stats. gh execution behind an injectable runner interface so tests fake responses. Config: new review section (fail_on: verified|none, suspected: summary|withhold), flags override. Exit: nonzero on new T2 findings per fail_on; also keep MostFindersFailed unreliable-scan error.
acceptance_criteria: bugbot review --pr N posts inline comments on a real or fixture PR mapped to correct diff lines; re-run after a push updates rather than duplicates (fingerprint match); summary comment includes spend/reliability stats; configurable exit gate; unit tests with faked gh responses; tree green + lint clean.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #10 (squash commit 8092e0d). Decisions: all GitHub I/O via 'gh api' behind an injectable ghRunner func-type seam (auth delegated to gh, tests fake with canned JSON; {owner}/{repo} placeholders mean zero repo-resolution code). Local HEAD must equal PR head SHA — no auto-checkout, error gives exact fetch/checkout commands (CI checkout is the target env). Commentable lines computed locally from 'git diff -M base...head' hunk parsing (three-dot, matching GitHub's anchor base); T2 on commentable lines -> inline review comments, blast-radius T2 outside diff + T3 -> single summary issue comment. Lifecycle keyed by hidden first-line markers: bugbot:fp=<fingerprint> per finding, bugbot:summary for the summary, bugbot:resolved tags tombstones. planSync is a pure reconciler (create/update/skip/resolve) -> applyPlan is the only writer (no-op under --dry-run). Stale comments PATCHed to a SHA-free resolved notice, never deleted; detected by marker so no re-PATCH churn on later pushes. CI gate (review.fail_on verified|none, flag + BUGBOT_REVIEW_* env): nonzero on NEW T2 fingerprints not on the PR; review round caught and fixed a tombstone gate bypass — resolved fingerprints count as ABSENT so fix-then-revert trips the gate again and the comment un-resolves to the full body. Known deferred (reviewer minors): same-fp collision across review+issue comment kinds orphans the review comment (migration edge); no snapshot progress sink (bugbot status can't watch a review run); review runs persist findings into the local store (ephemeral in CI — acceptable). Acceptance met: faked-gh tests cover create/idempotent-rerun/update/stale-resolve/reappear-gate/summary-in-place/T3 routing/exit gate/dry-run/pagination; CI + all local gates green.

title: GitHub issue publishing: findings -> gh issues with lifecycle sync
description: Close the loop from findings to action. New 'bugbot publish' command (and daemon post-cycle hook) that files T2+ findings as GitHub issues by shelling out to gh: title from finding title, body with reasoning trace, repro test (when present), file:line permalink, lens/tier labels, and the finding fingerprint embedded in an HTML comment for dedup. Lifecycle sync both ways: re-publishing an already-filed fingerprint updates instead of duplicating; when daemon re-verification auto-closes a finding (code changed, bug gone), close the GH issue with a 'fixed in <sha>' comment. Respect a config allowlist (publish.tiers, publish.labels, dry-run flag). Design note: also consider SARIF as a sibling exporter (separate bead bugbot output for code-scanning UI).
design: New 'bugbot publish' command following report.go openStore pattern (store + gh only, no LLM/funnel). Migration 005 published_issues(fingerprint PK, issue_number, state open|closed, created_at, updated_at) + suppressions-style CRUD. Pure reconciler planPublish(openFindings tier<=min, publishedMap, fixedFindings) -> actions create/update/close-skip, mirroring prcomments planSync/applyPlan split; all gh I/O via existing ghRunner seam using gh api JSON endpoints (POST repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues, PATCH issues/N, POST issues/N/comments). Issue body: first line <!-- bugbot:fp=... --> marker (DB is the primary dedup; marker is recovery/debug), meta, description, reasoning in details block, repro link, permalink built from 'gh repo view --json url' + Finding.CommitSHA + file#Lline. Close path: reconcile step closes GH issues whose finding is StatusFixed with 'no longer reproduced as of <head>' comment + PATCH state=closed; same reconciler invoked from daemon postcycle behind config publish.enabled via new optional daemon dep. Config publish{enabled:false, tier_min:2, labels:[bugbot], close_on_fixed:true} + BUGBOT_PUBLISH_* env. --dry-run prints actions, zero gh writes. Conflict guardrails vs parallel bugbot-n8f: do NOT touch findings table schema, report/ package, or scan.go; postcycle hook goes after reverifyOpenFindings (n8f owns promoteNewFindings).
acceptance_criteria: bugbot publish files issues for open T2+ findings via gh with fingerprint-comment dedup (re-run files zero duplicates); finding auto-close closes the linked issue with the fixing sha; dry-run prints without filing; works without gh installed (clear degradation error); unit tests with a fake gh shim binary; tree green + lint clean.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #13 (squash 3f8519d), Sonnet implementer + review round. Decisions: pure planPublish reconciler (create/recover/update/skip/close) + applyPublish as only gh layer, mirroring prcomments architecture; published_issues migration 005 keyed by fingerprint with a CHECK-constrained four-state machine. The review round's three must-fixes were all cross-system partial-failure bugs and drove the state design: 'pending' recorded BEFORE gh create (interrupted create recovers next run via fingerprint-marker search — adopt or create, never duplicate); 'closing' recorded after the auto-close comment lands (PATCH failure resumes without re-commenting every cycle); daemon publisher latches off via atomic.Bool after one missing-gh warning (errGHRequired wraps a sentinel detectable via errors.Is). Update heuristic: finding.UpdatedAt > published.updated_at (no gh reads; no-op PATCH on metadata touch accepted). Reasoning capped 30KB under GitHub's 65536 body limit. tier_min semantics: f.Tier <= tier_min, so tier_min=0 publishes only fix-witnessed findings post-bugbot-n8f. Daemon hook after reverifyOpenFindings behind publish.enabled; manual 'bugbot publish' always works; --dry-run zero writes. NOT yet validated against live GitHub — needs a real-repo smoke (file + auto-close one issue) in a future session.

title: Anthropic auth via Claude coding-plan (OAuth) in addition to API keys
description: The anthropic provider currently authenticates only via api_key_env (pay-per-token API key). The user's primary Anthropic access is a Claude coding-plan subscription (the auth Claude Code uses), so the Anthropic half of the cache probe (originally bugbot-7f0: real cache hit-rates from the agent loop, 1h-TTL decision) is blocked — no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY on the host. Add subscription auth support: config gains an auth mode on the anthropic provider type (api_key | oauth-token), where oauth mode sends the coding-plan OAuth bearer token (e.g. sourced from an env var the user populates via 'claude setup-token', or by reading Claude Code's stored credentials — evaluate which is robust and least surprising) with the required anthropic-beta OAuth header instead of x-api-key. Everything downstream (capabilities, cache breakpoints, usage parsing) stays identical. NOTE for implementer: verify current header/beta-flag requirements for OAuth bearer tokens against the live Claude Code behavior rather than trusting training data; also check Anthropic ToS posture on third-party subscription use and surface it in the bead if it's a concern — this is a personal tool using the user's own plan.
design: Settled design (orchestrator, with Claude API reference loaded): config.Provider gains auth: api_key (default, empty = api_key) | oauth-token, plus auth_token_env naming the env var holding the coding-plan OAuth token (separate field from api_key_env — mirrors the SDK's ANTHROPIC_API_KEY vs ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN split and avoids overloading a misnamed field). oauth-token valid only on type=anthropic. Validation: oauth mode requires auth_token_env and must NOT set api_key_env (the API rejects requests carrying both credentials — sending both is a real failure mode, so config forbids it outright). Wire facts (verified against current Claude API reference, not training data): OAuth bearer goes on Authorization: Bearer via the Go SDK's option.WithAuthToken, PLUS header anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20 — required for /v1/messages even though some endpoints tolerate its absence; x-api-key must not be sent. Token sourcing: user populates the env var via 'claude setup-token' (long-lived) or 'ant auth print-credentials --access-token' (short-lived); bugbot just reads the env var — no credential-file reading (least surprising, no coupling to Claude Code's storage format). Everything downstream (capabilities, cache breakpoints, usage parsing) unchanged. Doctor's provider check reads whichever env var the mode names. ToS posture: personal tool using the user's own coding-plan subscription, same auth mechanism Claude Code itself uses — noted, not a blocker. Live smoke + cache-hit-rate observation deferred to post-merge (needs the user's real token).
acceptance_criteria: Config accepts an anthropic provider in oauth mode without api_key_env; adapter sends correct Authorization + beta headers (unit test on serialized request, no live call needed for the test); api_key mode unchanged (existing tests pass); clear error message when the token env is missing/expired; docs/template.go example added; live smoke: one completion against the coding plan succeeds and reports cache fields, then run the deferred Anthropic cache-hit-rate observation and record the 1h-TTL decision on this bead.

title: Live probe: verify MiniMax cached_tokens and Anthropic cache hit-rates; consider 1h TTL
description: bugbot-2ix (PR #4) parses prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens on openai-compatible endpoints but it is unverified whether MiniMax actually returns it (Capabilities.PromptCaching left false/conservative for compatible endpoints). Also unverified live: Anthropic breakpoint placement actually yields expected cache hit-rates in a real agent loop. One cheap live scan confirms both — check spend ledger cache_read_tokens > 0 after a 2+ iteration run. If MiniMax caches, consider a config override to set PromptCaching=true for known-caching compatible endpoints. Separately evaluate extended cache TTL (1h vs default 5m) for long sweeps where cycles outlive the 5m window. Live invocation recipes are in bd memories (live-eval-invocations, minimax-key-and-endpoint).
acceptance_criteria: Documented (bead comment) whether MiniMax returns cached_tokens, with raw usage JSON evidence; Anthropic hit-rate observed and recorded from a live loop (cache_read_tokens vs input_tokens per iteration); decision recorded on PromptCaching config override and on 1h TTL (implement or explicitly decline with rationale).
close_reason: MiniMax half complete with evidence; Anthropic half moved to bugbot-dbg (no Anthropic key on host — coding-plan OAuth support needed first). FINDINGS: (1) MiniMax DOES automatic prefix caching and reports prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens — direct probe, identical 7684-token request twice: cached 114 then 7666 (~99.8%); raw JSON in bead comments. (2) End-to-end on live scans: 78% and 75% cache-read rates flowing through ledger + scan summary ('Cache: read=N' line) — caching is working and accounted. (3) Budget pool live-validated: 400k budget, 453k spend (1.13x vs the old 2.7x overshoot); in-flight finders stopped at turn boundaries with honest 'partial coverage' skip notes. (4) Finder fixes validated: single-lens run completed with parseable output and zero failures (baseline: 6/6 unparseable); trustworthy 'No findings'. DECISIONS: Capabilities.PromptCaching stays false for openai-compatible (nothing gates on it; cached_tokens parsing works regardless — revisit only if a caller starts gating). 1h-TTL: deferred to bugbot-dbg (Anthropic-specific; MiniMax caching is automatic, no TTL knob). Residual: full 6-lens coverage of even a 6-file package needs ~1.5-2M cycle budget with M3; budget sizing guidance worth a note in config docs.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Scope adjustment (2026-06-09): host has only MINIMAX_API_KEY — the Anthropic half (live hit-rates, 1h TTL decision) moves to new bead bugbot-dbg (coding-plan OAuth auth support), which carries the deferred observation in its acceptance. This bead closes on the MiniMax evidence + calibration scan. MiniMax cached_tokens: CONFIRMED via direct probe — identical 7,684-token-prompt request twice: usage payload 1: {"prompt_tokens":7684,"prompt_tokens_details":{"cached_tokens":114}}; payload 2: {"prompt_tokens":7684,"prompt_tokens_details":{"cached_tokens":7666}} (~99.8% hit, automatic server-side). Adapter already parses this field. Live calibration scan over internal/lsp in progress.

title: Code-nav fallback tier: tree-sitter backend for repos without working builds
description: LSP servers vary in build requirements: pyright/tsserver work from source, but gopls needs resolvable modules, clangd needs compile_commands.json, JVM servers need full builds. On target repos where the build is broken or deps unfetchable, the LSP backend (bugbot-tmb) degrades to plain grep. Add a middle tier backed by tree-sitter (user direction 2026-06-09: tree-sitter is the intended approach; the earlier 'telescope' mention meant this): parse source directly with per-language grammars and the standard tags.scm definition/reference queries (the same mechanism behind GitHub basic code nav). Gives real parsing with no build and distinguishes call sites from comment/string mentions — the main noise source in grep fallback. Honest limit: syntactic only, no cross-file semantic resolution; find_definition returns ranked candidates for ambiguous names and the result text says so. IMPLEMENTATION CONSTRAINT: bugbot builds CGO_ENABLED=0 (make build) and Go tree-sitter bindings require cgo — use WASM grammars via wazero (keeps static build, in-process) or shell out to ast-grep/tree-sitter CLI as a single external binary; evaluate both, prefer the wazero route if grammar coverage suffices. ctags remains a simpler plan-B if tree-sitter integration proves heavy. Backend selection: LSP healthy -> tree-sitter -> plain grep, behind the same tool names so the model is agnostic; result text carries a confidence caveat naming the tier that answered.
acceptance_criteria: find_definition and find_references answer via tree-sitter queries when the LSP server for the language is missing or unhealthy, with a tier caveat and ranked candidates for ambiguous names; reference results exclude comment/string mentions (unit test proves it vs grep); backend selection covered by unit tests (LSP up, LSP down -> tree-sitter, grammar missing -> plain grep); CGO_ENABLED=0 build still works (make build green); integration test on a fixture repo with deliberately broken go.mod proving definitions still resolve; tree green + lint clean.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #7 (merged). Route decided: github.com/odvcencio/gotreesitter — pure-Go tree-sitter runtime (no cgo, no WASM layer); WASM/wazero and external-binary routes evaluated and rejected (heavier / reintroduces missing-binary problem). Tier: LSP healthy -> tree-sitter -> grep behind same tool names; falls back ONLY on never-answerable LSP errors, never on timeout; find_implementations stays LSP-only. References match AST call nodes so comment/string mentions excluded (tested). Review criticals fixed: per-tagger mutex (race was reproduced, test is load-bearing), recover seam for the dep's known GLR panic caps, per-file tag cache (mtime+size invalidation), distinct tsx grammar. Binary cost contained via upstream grammar_subset build tags in make build: 86.2MB -> 64.8MB. Plan-B ctags never needed.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Implementation done (Opus agent, commit b2cbf65): route = github.com/odvcencio/gotreesitter (pure-Go tree-sitter, no cgo/WASM), tags-query backend, tiered navigator falling back only on never-answerable LSP errors. Review found CRITICAL confirmed data race (shared Tagger/Parser mutable state, reproduced under -race with 50 concurrent calls) + CRITICAL missing recover() on parse path (dep has known GLR panic caps); HIGH whole-repo reparse per query (no per-file tag cache) + HIGH .tsx mapped to plain typescript grammar (JSX silently fails); MEDIUM ~24MB embedded grammars for 4 languages (upstream has subset build tags to investigate). Clean: dependency hygiene (pure Go, benign transitives), symlink/sandbox safety (WalkDir doesn't follow; render re-checks containment), fallback triggers vs real LSP error strings, deterministic ranking, caps/dedup consistent with LSP tier. Fix agent dispatched for all findings.

title: Eval fixture hygiene: seed all real defects, stop documenting bugs in comments
description: Baseline triage showed two fixture-quality issues: (1) resource-leak fixture contains a REAL unseeded defect (make([]byte, n) with caller-controlled n panics on negative input) so honest findings score as false positives — fixtures must either seed every genuine defect they contain or eliminate the unseeded ones; (2) the fixture's doc comment literally describes its own seeded bug ('On the short-read error path it returns without closing f: a leaked file descriptor') — the model can read the answer off the comment, inflating recall. Sweep all builtin fixtures for both problems.
acceptance_criteria: Every builtin fixture audited: no genuine defect is unseeded (either added to Seeded with tolerance or code fixed); no comment/docstring reveals a seeded bug; scripted suite updated accordingly and still gates 1.0; re-record the corpus afterward and note score deltas on bugbot-9w5 (recall measured without comment-hints is the honest number).
close_reason: Done in PR #105 (squash-merged, commit cd7156f). Fixed the one unseeded genuine defect (resource-leak fixture make([]byte,n) negative-n panic -> make([]byte,max(n,0)); seeded FD leak intact) and scrubbed all answer-leak doc comments (7 seeded-bug descriptions + clean fixture's 'false-positive canary' meta-text + split-FP reachability hint) to neutral purpose comments, line counts preserved. Scripted suite still gates 1.0 (TestBenchmarkSuite, TestBuiltinCases_PerCaseExpectations). Oracle Rev18t APPROVE. OUT OF SCOPE / FOLLOW-UP: the live real-model corpus re-record + recall-without-comment-hints score deltas on bugbot-9w5 (needs real models + spend) \u2014 the recorded corpus still reflects pre-fix state (resource-leak precision 0.5; the live model did flag the make-panic, corroborating the fix).

title: Activity visibility: live progress pane and bugbot status
description: Bugbot gives no sign of life while working: scan prints only a final summary after potentially many minutes of agent activity, and the daemon logs sparsely. Operators need to see that the tool is running and what it is doing. Design: a progress-event seam (callback/channel) emitted from the funnel and daemon — stage transitions, agent run start/finish (role, lens, file chunk, candidate), live token spend, cycle schedule — consumed by pluggable renderers: (1) default TTY pane: in-place ANSI multi-line status (current stage, active agents, candidates surviving per stage, spend ticker) degrading to plain log lines when not a TTY; (2) the existing slog stream; (3) a status snapshot persisted (status file under .bugbot or store table) so a separate terminal can run 'bugbot status' against a running daemon and see current cycle, last findings, spend today, next sweep ETA. Avoid heavyweight TUI deps if feasible (plain ANSI first; bubbletea only if clearly warranted).
acceptance_criteria: bugbot scan on a TTY shows live per-stage progress (stage, active agent count, lens/candidate labels, running token spend) updating in place, and plain sequential lines when piped; daemon emits the same events through slog; bugbot status run from another terminal reports a running daemon's current activity, today's spend, open findings count, and next poll/sweep times (and says so clearly when no daemon is running); event seam is a typed interface in funnel/daemon usable by future sinks; no flicker/garbage on narrow terminals; tests cover event emission ordering and the non-TTY fallback rendering.
close_reason: Built and verified (13 packages green under -race, lint 0 issues). Decisions: emission contract is never-block/never-fail/never-panic with a single Emit choke point (nil-safe, time-stamped); pane is plain ANSI with COLUMNS fallback 80 and ModeCharDevice TTY detection — zero new deps, no bubbletea; spend ticks reuse the funnel's existing spendRecorder via an onRecord hook; snapshot writes are temp+rename atomic, ~1s rate-limited, forced on terminal events; status staleness = old last_updated OR dead pid (signal 0); repro per-attempt emission deliberately skipped to contain the diff — promotions visible at cycle level (noted for follow-up if wanted); runRefuters now returns per-candidate verifier tokens for agent_finished events.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Starting implementation. Read funnel/{funnel,run,hypothesize,verify,triage,lens,helpers}, daemon/{daemon,run,cycle,postcycle,clock}, cli/{scan,daemon,root}, store/{scanruns,spend,findings,store}, repro/promote, config, llm/recorder. Plan: new internal/progress package (events.go, sink.go, pane.go, log.go, snapshot.go, term.go), funnel Options gains Progress sink emitting at run/stage/agent/spend/budget boundaries, daemon Deps gains Progress sink for cycle/schedule/reverify/promote, CLI wires PaneRenderer (TTY) / LogRenderer (non-TTY) + Snapshot for scan, LogRenderer-over-slog + Snapshot for daemon, new 'bugbot status' command reading status.json.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Implementation complete. New package internal/progress (events.go, sink.go, term.go, format.go, log.go, pane.go, snapshot.go) with full tests. Funnel Options gained Progress sink; emits scan_started/finished, stage_started/finished w/counts, agent_started/finished (finder=lens, verifier=candidate title), spend_tick (via recorder onRecord hook), budget_degraded/stopped. runRefuters signature changed to also return summed tokens (VerifyFinding updated). Daemon Deps gained Progress, threaded into per-cycle funnel via newFunnel; emits cycle_scheduled/started/finished, reverify, promote. CLI scan: TTY->PaneRenderer else LogRenderer, both + SnapshotSink, pane stopped before final summary. CLI daemon: SlogRenderer + SnapshotSink with daySpend getter. New 'bugbot status' command reads status.json (missing/stale-by-time/stale-by-dead-pid/fresh, exit 0 always, open findings count via store). All gates pass: build, vet (+tags), test -race, gofmt clean, golangci-lint 0 issues. DEVIATION: repro PromoteAll per-attempt emission SKIPPED to keep diff contained (Reproducer built by CLI before daemon progress sink exists; would need Options/signature churn). Promotion still visible via daemon's cycle-level KindPromote count event. Width detection is syscall-free ($COLUMNS fallback 80) per spec; TTY detection via os.ModeCharDevice.

title: Leads blackboard: cross-lens agent messaging
description: Finders trip over out-of-lens suspicions that currently evaporate (nil-safety finder notices dodgy locking). Add a leads table (poster role/lens, target lens, file/line, note, scan_run, status posted|consumed) and a post_lead tool for finders. The funnel injects pending leads into the matching lens's next task context (next chunk or next cycle in daemon mode) and marks them consumed. Deliberately asynchronous blackboard, NOT direct agent chat: persistent, deterministic, testable — and verify-stage refuters stay isolated by design since their independence is what kills false positives.
design: Migration 004_leads.sql: leads(id, scan_run_id, poster_lens, target_lens, file, line, note, status posted|consumed, created_at, consumed_at) with UNIQUE(target_lens,file,line) upsert as the near-identical dedup (re-post refreshes note, preserves created_at) — same key style as suppressions. Store CRUD: AddLead, PendingLeads(lens), ConsumeLeads(ids) following suppressions.go template. post_lead agent tool: FINDER-only (verifiers excluded by design — refuter independence is what kills FPs; documented in tool wiring); args {target_lens, file, line, note}; target_lens validated against BuiltinLenses names; posting to own lens allowed but pointless (documented). Injection: hypothesize claims pending leads per lens up-front (single-threaded, before parallel unit launch), marks consumed at claim time (a failed finder loses the lead — accepted trade-off, documented), injects a LEADS section into every chunk task for that lens. Stats: LeadsPosted/LeadsConsumed rendered in scan output + review footer. Cross-cycle: store is the carrier; funnel test seeds a lead via store then asserts next Sweep's concurrency-lens task contains it and marks consumed. Eval suite must stay precision 1.0.
acceptance_criteria: Migration adds leads table; post_lead tool for finder runners only (verifiers excluded by design, documented); funnel injects pending leads into matching lens prompts and marks consumed; dedup of near-identical leads; cross-cycle test: lens A posts a concurrency lead, next concurrency run receives it (daemon carries it across cycles); leads visible in scan stats; eval suite unchanged or better.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #12 (squash f385cdf), Sonnet implementer + skeptical review round. Decisions: migration 004 leads table with UNIQUE(target_lens,file,line) upsert as near-identical dedup; re-posting a consumed lead flips status back to posted (re-raised suspicion gets fresh attention), created_at preserved, consumed_at NULLed. post_lead tool finder-only with poster lens injected at construction (no impersonation); target validated against ALL builtin lenses so leads can target inactive lenses and stay pending until that lens runs; filepath.IsLocal blocks absolute+traversal paths; note capped 500 bytes. Hypothesize claims pending leads per lens single-threaded before parallel unit launch, consumes at claim time (failed finder loses the lead — documented trade-off), injects CROSS-LENS LEADS section into every chunk task for the lens; notes newline-flattened at render (review round caught the injection vector: an embedded newline could fabricate extra lead lines in a future finder's prompt). PendingLeads/ConsumeLeads errors degrade (note + continue), never abort. Refuters excluded at both verify.go and verify_finding.go with rationale comments, pinned by TestVerify_RefuterToolset_NoPostLead. Stats leads_posted/leads_consumed in scan + review footer. Cross-cycle proven by two-sweep funnel test driving the real agent tool-call loop. Acceptance fully met; eval gates precision 1.0. Live behavior (do real finders actually post useful leads?) unvalidated — fold into next live calibration run.

title: Debate verification: advocate/refuter/judge experiment
description: Experiment: replace independent-refuter majority voting with a structured debate — an advocate argues the candidate is real, a refuter argues it is not, a judge agent rules. Literature suggests debate improves judgment quality but it costs more tokens and trades away refuter independence, so it must EARN its place: implement as a pluggable verify strategy behind config and judge it with the eval harness (scripted + recorded corpora) against the majority-refutation baseline on precision, recall, and spend. Adopt, keep-as-option, or reject based on numbers; record the decision either way.
acceptance_criteria: Verify strategy is pluggable (majority|debate) via config with majority remaining default; debate implementation with bounded turns; eval comparison runnable as one command producing a side-by-side table (precision/recall/tokens per strategy); decision recorded on this bead with the numbers; if rejected, code stays behind config as an experiment, not deleted silently.

title: Memory: hash-anchored KV store + agent memory tools
description: Agents re-derive the same codebase facts every cycle — pure token waste in daemon mode. Add a memory table (key, value, provenance: role+scan_run, file anchors with content hashes, optional TTL/confidence) plus memory_get/memory_put/memory_search agent tools. Entries auto-invalidate when any anchored file's hash changes (reuses the finding re-verification machinery). Prompts must treat recalled entries as hints to verify, never ground truth — memory poisoning (one wrong fact propagating across cycles) is the failure mode; provenance + hash invalidation + hint framing are the mitigations.
acceptance_criteria: Migration adds memory table; three tools with schemas wired into finder/verifier runners; invalidation test: change an anchored file, entry no longer returned; TTL expiry test; provenance recorded and shown; prompt updates frame memory as unverified hints; poisoning posture documented in ARCHITECTURE.md; eval suite unchanged or better.

title: Refutation memory: machine-learned triage suppressions
description: Suppression memory only learns from human dismissals today. When refuters kill a candidate, persist the refutation (reason, anchored to the implicated file's content hash). Triage pre-kills matching future candidates for free while the anchor holds — and the entry expires the moment the file changes, so a stale refutation can never hide a bug introduced later. Extends the suppression concept from human-taught to machine-learned with automatic expiry.
acceptance_criteria: Refuter majority-kills write refutation entries keyed by candidate fingerprint pattern + file hash; triage consults them before verification and stats count the pre-kills separately; expiry test proving a changed file re-enables full verification; eval case: same bogus candidate found twice costs verifier tokens only once; a seeded real bug introduced after a refutation of similar shape is NOT suppressed (the critical safety test).

title: Git history tools + churn heat prioritization
description: Two wins from the existing git-CLI plumbing: (1) git_blame and git_log tools for agents — bug context, regression candidates, recent-change awareness; (2) a heat model (churn x recency, later x coverage-gap) that ranks files so sweep chunking spends finder budget where bugs statistically cluster instead of uniformly.
acceptance_criteria: git_blame/git_log tools with bounded output and fixture-repo tests; heat scoring in ingest or funnel orders sweep chunks (highest heat first) with unit tests; degradation when history is shallow; ordering observable in scan stats/logs; eval suite unchanged.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #27 (commit 78c41f5). git_blame (100-line clamp) + git_log (max 50) tools: 32KB bounds, 10s timeouts, -- separator (review probed flag injection + pathspec magic: repo-contained, documented), ERROR: degradation; wired to finders AND verifiers. ChurnHeat: one git log -n500 --since=180d call, score Σexp(-age/30d); Sweep sorts heat-desc/alphabetical-tiebreak (shallow history degrades automatically); Targeted untouched; KindHeatOrdered progress event + Stats fields. REVIEW MUST-FIXES: -c core.quotepath=off (non-ASCII paths were silently zero-heat) + 10s timeout on heat call. BONUS: real data race found+fixed in hypothesize.go (parallel finders appending post_lead into shared slice backing capacity; three-index append fix; reviewer-verified, revert-experiment-confirmed regression test). Follow-ups: bugbot-eg0 (git output injection surface), bugbot-cif (heat config knob).

title: Verifier sandbox-exec tool: evidence-based refutation
description: Only the repro stage executes code today; refuters argue rhetorically. Expose a gated sandbox execution tool to the verify stage so a refuter can empirically demonstrate a refutation (run the guard path, run the existing test suite) instead of asserting it. Converts verification from rhetoric to evidence — the core precision philosophy. Sandbox runs are expensive: gate by config flag and candidate severity, bound per-candidate executions, and record spend.
design: Funnel gains optional sandbox: Options.Sandbox (sandbox.Sandbox, nil = feature off) wired from cli/scan.go + daemon when config enables it. New config section verify: {sandbox_exec: bool (default false), sandbox_min_severity: string (default high), sandbox_max_execs: int per candidate (default 3)} + env overrides. New agent tool sandbox_exec exposed ONLY to refuter runners and ONLY when enabled + candidate severity >= min: args {cmd argv, files map[path]content like repro.Plan}, runs sandbox.Exec(Spec{RepoDir, Cmd, WriteFiles, Network:none}), returns exit/stdout/stderr/duration with tool-result output capped ~16KiB (sandbox's 1MiB cap is prompt-hostile). Per-candidate exec counter enforced inside the tool (refuters for one candidate run sequentially in its goroutine, so a plain counter per candidate works); exceeding returns a budget-exhausted tool error, never kills the run. Stats: SandboxExecs, SandboxExecMillis aggregated under existing result-mutex pattern, rendered in scan output + eval suite. Verifier prompt updated: prefer empirical demonstration over rhetoric when the tool is present. Eval: scripted client extended to optionally emit scripted tool calls (or funnel-level test with fake sandbox) demonstrating refute-via-execution; fake sandbox.Sandbox impl for tests. Network stays none always — tool never accepts a network arg.
acceptance_criteria: Sandbox exec tool exposed only to verifier-role runners behind a config flag with a severity gate; per-candidate execution count bounded; output capped; spend/duration recorded in scan stats; eval case demonstrating an empirical refutation flow; network stays none; full tree green.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #11 (squash 3e95208), implemented by a Sonnet agent + skeptical review round. Decisions: new config section verify{sandbox_exec off-by-default, sandbox_min_severity high, sandbox_max_execs 3} + BUGBOT_VERIFY_* env overrides; funnel Options.SandboxOpts{Sandbox, Enabled, MinSeverity, MaxExecs} with zero-value=off; sandbox_exec tool only for refuters on candidates at/above min severity (unknown severities rank 4 = conservatively excluded); args {cmd, files} mirror repro.Plan; network hardcoded none, additionalProperties:false blocks smuggling image/timeout; non-zero exit = normal result; per-candidate atomic exec counter (refuters sequential per candidate) returns tool error on exhaustion; 16KiB per-stream prompt cap over the backend 1MiB cap; infra errors -> tool errors (refuter falls back to rhetoric). Stats SandboxExecs/SandboxExecMillis aggregated via atomics folded under result mutex; rendered in scan + PR-review footer. Refuter prompt: prefer empirical demonstration, execution confirming bug = do NOT refute (inversion guarded). Daemon VerifyFinding deliberately excluded (re-verify runs per finding per code change — container spend) — documented + pinned by TestVerifyFinding_NoSandboxTool. Review round must-fixes applied: explicit warning when enabled-but-no-runtime (scan/review/daemon), atomic test fakes. Eval-case acceptance satisfied at funnel level: TestSweep_SandboxExec_StatsAggregated drives a scripted tool-call client through the real agent runner loop with a fake sandbox; eval ScriptedClient left untouched (extending it for tool calls was disproportionate). Live podman validation not yet done — fold into next live calibration run.

title: Code-nav tools: LSP-backed find_definition/find_references/find_implementations (multi-language)
description: Code navigation tools for agents, LSP-first (general, not Go-only — user direction 2026-06-09, supersedes the go/packages v1 bugbot-24n). Refuters currently argue from grep output, which is noisy and misses guards. Add find_definition / find_references (callers) / find_implementations tools to the agent harness backed by a hand-rolled LSP client (JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio, Content-Length framing, ~5 methods — no official Go client lib; x/tools client is internal/, community libs stale). The real work is server lifecycle: per-language spawn (gopls, typescript-language-server, pyright, clangd), initialize handshake + capability negotiation, crash restart, clean shutdown, discovery by file extension, graceful degradation when servers are missing. Simplifiers: agents are read-only (no didChange sync) and tool errors already round-trip to the model. Wire into funnel readOnlyTools(); update refuter prompt to prefer find_references when checking whether callers already guard a claimed bug.
acceptance_criteria: LSP client speaks JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio with correct Content-Length framing and UTF-16 position conversion (non-ASCII test case required). Tools registered with JSON schemas and wired into finder/verifier tool sets. Server lifecycle managed: spawn, initialize handshake, bounded warm-up wait (never hang; degrade to a clear 'unavailable' tool error), crash restart, clean shutdown. Language server discovery by extension with graceful degradation message when binary missing. Unit tests with a fake/scripted server for protocol + lifecycle; integration tests gated on real server availability (gopls; skip cleanly when absent) like sandbox tests. Refuter prompt updated. go build/vet/test and gofmt clean; golangci-lint clean.
notes: PR #5 open, branch lsp-code-nav (commit fdd6167). Close when merged. Gotcha: gopls zero-references on interface methods not called via interface. Follow-up beads: repro tool set lacks code-nav; daemon-lifetime LSP manager for warm indexes; bugbot-0ze tree-sitter fallback.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #5 (merged to main 9edf9a6). LSP-first per user direction, superseding go/packages v1. Hand-rolled 5-method JSON-RPC client (internal/lsp), lifecycle: lazy per-language spawn, capability gating, crash-restart-once-then-disable, bounded 60s queries, polite shutdown with kill escalation. Tools take {file,line,symbol} — tool resolves column itself. UTF-16 verified vs live gopls (emoji/CJK) and clangd. Post-review hardening: symlink containment on result paths, 16KB header bound, -race concurrency tests. Known gotcha: gopls returns zero references for interface methods never called through the interface. Follow-ups: bugbot-0ze (tree-sitter fallback), bugbot-yc8 (repro tools), bugbot-cy8 (daemon-lifetime manager), bugbot-ya3 (conn robustness lows).

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Implementation complete on PR #5 (https://github.com/dpoage/bugbot/pull/5). All gates green locally (build/vet/test/gofmt, CI-exact golangci-lint 0 issues, -race clean on lsp+agent, integration vs real gopls AND clangd incl. UTF-16 emoji/CJK case). Merges cleanly with PR #4 in either order (merge-tree verified, 0 conflict markers). Design: new internal/lsp package (jsonrpc framing / conn with server-request answering / server lifecycle / manager with crash-restart-then-disable); tools take {file,line,symbol} and resolve the column themselves so the model never guesses columns. Gotcha for future sessions: gopls returns ZERO references for an interface method never called through the interface type — 'no references' output is not proof of no callers. Independent code review in flight; close bead when PR merged.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Code review of PR #5 complete. Findings fixed in fb1189c: H1 symlink content-leak on result paths (relPath now mirrors fsRoot.resolve symlink containment), M3 unbounded header read loop (16KB budget), L6 misleading indexing-timeout message on parent deadlines, M2+L7 missing concurrency/-race coverage (4 new tests: concurrent conn calls w/ shuffled responses, transport death racing senders, concurrent lazy spawn, queries across crash-restart; FAKE_LSP_STALL_ON knob replaces the nanosecond-timeout hack). L4+L5 (read-loop reply wedge, stderr-tail vs EOF race) deferred to bugbot-ya3. Reviewer confirmed clean: UTF-16 conversion, Close() wiring on all exit paths, dead-channel/pending-map race safety, frame parsing edge cases, symbol resolution, input-path sandboxing. All gates re-run green after fixes.

title: Code-nav tools v1: find_definition/find_callers via go/packages (Go)
description: Refuters currently argue from grep output, which is noisy and misses guards. Add call-graph navigation tools to the agent harness: find_definition, find_callers (and find_implementations for interfaces), with language-agnostic tool names/schemas and a Go backend via golang.org/x/tools/go/packages (new dep, acceptable). Wire into finder and verifier runners when the target repo is Go. Update the refuter prompt to prefer find_callers when checking whether callers already guard a claimed bug.
acceptance_criteria: Tools registered for Go repos with JSON schemas; table-driven tests on a fixture package covering functions, methods, and interface implementations; graceful degradation message for non-Go targets; refuter prompt updated; eval suite precision unchanged or better; full tree green.

title: Implement internal/repro: Tier-1 reproduce stage
description: Build the Reproduce stage per bugbot-v2f.8. Reproducer agent (read-only tools) generates a minimal failing test for a T2 finding, executes it in the sandbox (network none), interprets exit codes, guards against compile-error false reproductions, writes artifacts (test files + run.sh + README.md) on success, promotes finding to T1 with repro_path. PromoteAll runs bounded-parallel over T2 findings. Wire a --repro flag into internal/cli/scan.go.
acceptance_criteria: internal/repro package with Reproducer.New, Attempt, PromoteAll, Summary; go vet ./internal/repro/... ./internal/cli/...; go test -race ./internal/repro/... green; success/zero-exit-revision/compile-guard/exhaustion unit tests with fake llm.Client + sandbox.NewMock + real store in t.TempDir; optional integration test behind build tag. gofmt clean.
close_reason: Implemented internal/repro: Reproducer{New,Attempt,PromoteAll,Summary,Plan,Attempt,FindingOutcome,Options}. Reproducer agent uses read-only tools (read_file/list_dir/grep) + RunJSON with a plan schema {files,cmd,expect}; system prompt demands a minimal assertion-bearing failing test that fails on buggy code and passes when fixed. Execution via sandbox Spec network=none. interpret() promotion rules: exit0=>not demonstrated, timeout=>not, non-zero with build-failure markers=>not (false-repro guard), else demonstrated. Non-demonstrating attempts feed category-specific feedback back for revision up to MaxAttempts. On success writes ArtifactDir/<id>/ bundle (repro files + run.sh + README.md) and PromoteAll updates store to T1 + repro_path via a SQL-free wrapper over GetFindingByFingerprint+UpsertFinding (no new store method needed). Failure leaves finding at T2 and removes partial artifacts. PromoteAll bounded by MaxParallel (default 2). Wired --repro into cli/scan.go with sandbox.Detect graceful skip. Tests: success, zero-exit->revision->success, compile-error guard, exhaustion (all real store in t.TempDir, sandbox.NewMock, scripted fake client) + integration test behind build tag 'integration' with real podman/golang image and hand-written plan. go vet + go test -race green, gofmt clean.

title: Reporting layer: report pkg (md/SARIF emitters, sinks) + report CLI
description: Build internal/report (Markdown + SARIF 2.1.0 emitters over []store.Finding+metadata, Sink interface with FSSink/StdoutSink, SinksFromConfig registry) and rewrite internal/cli/report.go (list/show/dismiss/emit). Package must stay independent of funnel to avoid import cycles for daemon reuse.
acceptance_criteria: go vet + go test -race ./internal/report/... green; gofmt clean on new files; SARIF unmarshals with required non-empty fields and severity->level mapping; markdown golden test deterministic; dismiss writes suppression (IsSuppressed true) and list --status dismissed shows it; ID-prefix resolution handles exact/prefix/ambiguous.
close_reason: Built internal/report (report.go core/sort, markdown.go, sarif.go hand-rolled 2.1.0 structs, sink.go FSSink+StdoutSink+SinksFromConfig, query.go CollectOpen+ResolveID) and rewrote internal/cli/report.go with list/show/dismiss/emit. Dismiss uses store.AddSuppression (atomic suppress+flip) per spec. SinksFromConfig accepts fs/stdout and treats legacy markdown/sarif as FSSink aliases (config default ships 'markdown') collapsing to one FSSink. Package is funnel-independent (operates on store.Finding + small findingLister iface) so daemon/scan can reuse via report.CollectOpen. Tests: markdown golden, SARIF shape+severity mapping+repo-relative URIs, FSSink latest copies, full dismiss flow vs real store, ID-prefix resolution (exact/prefix/ambiguous), CLI end-to-end. go vet + go test -race + gofmt all green on owned packages; go build ./... succeeds.

title: Daemon mode: scheduler, budgets, backoff, re-verification
description: bugbot daemon: scheduler interleaving the slow whole-repo sweep with commit/PR-triggered targeted investigations (blast-radius scoped); token budgets per cycle and per day with graceful degradation (fewer lenses, defer deep verification); idle backoff when repo unchanged; re-verify open findings whose implicated code changed and auto-close fixed ones.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon detects a new commit in a fixture repo and runs a targeted investigation; budget exhaustion halts LLM spend with a clear log line; a fixed seeded bug is auto-closed on the next cycle; idle repo produces near-zero spend.
close_reason: Daemon built and verified (6 tests, -race, injectable clock so no wall-time sleeps: commit->targeted-scan flow, idle backoff growth, day-budget skip with zero funnel calls, auto-close of fixed findings, graceful shutdown). Decisions: re-verify seam = new additive funnel.VerifyFinding reusing runRefuters/majorityRefuted at default count (majority-refuted = auto-close-as-fixed signal); last-seen commit in file_state sentinel row @daemon/last-seen — slash prefix cannot collide with repo paths, avoids a migration; MarkFixed not dismiss for resolved findings so reintroduced bugs can reopen; spend-read errors fail open (per-cycle budget still bounds); hash-unchanged findings never touched; survivors re-anchored to new hash to avoid per-cycle re-verification.

title: Evaluation harness: seeded-bug benchmarks, precision/recall
description: Ground truth for tuning precision: mutation/bug-injection benchmark over healthy fixture repos measuring detection rate AND false-positive rate on unmutated code; regression corpus of real confirmed bugs; runnable in CI via recorded LLM transcripts (from harness transcript capture) so results are cheap and comparable.
acceptance_criteria: Single command produces precision/recall numbers per lens and per stage; offline mode runs from recorded fixtures with no API calls; results comparable across runs for prompt/pipeline regression detection.
close_reason: Eval harness built and verified (28 tests, -race; suite runs at precision 1.000, tp=5 fp=0 fn=0). THE command: go test ./internal/eval/ -run TestBenchmarkSuite -v. Decisions: recorded replay is two-level (role, run-sequence)->session because funnel shares one client per role across runners; MaxParallel=1 default in eval since parallelism breaks sequence-matched replay; bug Kind is a label not a match gate (file+line matching only) so cross-lens detection still counts; vacuous-perfect convention avoids NaN on clean cases; suppressed case asserted to die in triage not verification. Real-model transcript corpus is future work; mechanism proven with synthetic recordings. RunSuite ready for a future bugbot eval command.

title: Reporting: markdown + SARIF emitters, sinks, report CLI, suppression
description: Report generation and management: markdown and SARIF emitters (reasoning chain, tier, repro artifact link); pluggable sink interface (filesystem, stdout now; GitHub issues/webhook later); bugbot report list/show/dismiss CLI where dismiss writes suppression memory.
acceptance_criteria: Emitted SARIF validates against the SARIF 2.1 schema; dismiss causes the finding to be suppressed in subsequent scans; markdown report contains file:line, tier, reasoning chain, and repro link when present.
close_reason: Reporting built and verified (golden markdown, SARIF shape tests, FSSink + latest copies, dismiss->IsSuppressed flow against real store, ID prefix resolution). Decisions: dismiss uses AddSuppression (suppression row + status flip in one txn, the path UpsertFinding honors forever) not UpdateStatus; package store-decoupled via 1-method findingLister so daemon reuses CollectOpen cycle-free; SARIF validation by-construction with schema validation deferred to CI; legacy markdown/sarif sink names aliased to fs, config default/template updated to fs by orchestrator.

title: Reproduce stage: sandboxed failing-test generation (Tier 1)
description: For verified findings, a reproducer agent writes a minimal failing test or repro script and executes it in the sandbox. Demonstrated failure promotes the finding to Tier 1 with the artifact attached; failed repro demotes to T2 but does not delete.
acceptance_criteria: Seeded-bug fixture yields at least one T1 finding with a runnable repro artifact; failed repro attempts demote rather than drop; artifacts stored and linked from reports.
close_reason: Repro stage built and verified (unit suite race-clean with scripted client + sandbox mock; integration test against real podman + golang:1.26-alpine behind integration tag, offline-capable fixture). Decisions: promotion ruleset is conservative — non-zero exit with build/dep markers treated as failed attempt not demonstration (false-repro guard errs toward demotion); failed repro demotes nothing (T2 stays); Attempt is store-free, PromoteAll owns persistence and surfaces persist-failures as errors; store untouched — promotion via GetFindingByFingerprint + UpsertFinding re-read to avoid clobbering; default parallelism 2 due to workspace copy cost.

title: Regression-hunter daemon digest: 'new since last green' per-cycle report
description: Part (b) of bugbot-ovt (the regress COMMAND landed in PR #108). The daemon should emit a per-cycle/weekly digest listing findings INTRODUCED since the last green run. Hook an emitRegressDigest into internal/daemon/postcycle.go after runPublisher; resolve 'last green' via a scan_runs query (most recent finished sweep) and possibly a stored marker. Reuse the regress attribution helpers from PR #108 (cli.anchorAbsentAtRef / ingest.ReadFileAtRef) to label introduced-this-period findings.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon emits a per-cycle digest listing introduced-this-period findings; 'last green' is tracked; mechanism documented; test covers a finding introduced since the prior green run.

title: dead_hypotheses kill trace is headed 'Survived adversarial verification'
description: buildReasoning (internal/funnel/verify.go:267) always emits the header 'Survived adversarial verification (N/M refuter verdicts could not disprove it)', and bugbot-cvc reuses the identical call for the KILL trace persisted to dead_hypotheses (verify_stream.go:331). So a killed hypothesis's reasoning_trace reads 'Survived adversarial verification (0/3 ... could not disprove it)' followed by three 'refuted' lines — internally consistent in the counts but the word 'Survived' is misleading for an operator auditing a kill. Surfaced while writing the bugbot-fg9a wiring test (PR #102), which pins the current reuse.
acceptance_criteria: A dead_hypotheses (killed) row's reasoning_trace header accurately reflects refutation (not 'Survived'); survivor traces unchanged; fg9a wiring test updated to match.
notes: Low-pri cosmetic/clarity. Fix options: (a) give buildReasoning a kill/survive mode that switches the header (and update the fg9a test's expected string), or (b) a dedicated kill-trace header. fg9a's TestSweep_KilledCandidate_PersistsDeadHypothesis asserts byte-equality with buildReasoning(...), so any header change must update that test.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #104 (squash-merged, commit 987ed31). buildReasoning gained a 'killed bool' param: verifier-killed hypotheses' dead_hypotheses.reasoning_trace now reads 'Refuted by adversarial verification (M/N refuter verdicts refuted it)' instead of the misleading 'Survived ...'. Survivor traces byte-unchanged. Call sites: kill->true, survive->false, reverify->majorityRefuted(verdicts). Added TestTrace_KilledHeader; fg9a byte-equality test updated to killed=true (still byte-equal). Oracle RevWmqa APPROVE (after restoring a trailing newline it had stripped); full funnel suite + -race -count=10 green.

title: Funnel-level test for persistKilled kill-path wiring (dead_hypotheses)
description: bugbot-cvc added persistKilled at the candKilled branch of funnel/verify_stream.go, persisting verifier-killed candidates to the dead_hypotheses store. Store round-trip + prune are unit-tested (internal/store/dead_hypotheses_test.go), but the WIRING at the kill site (correct field mapping; the identical buildReasoning(verdicts,seatNames,arbiterReasoning,arbiterRan) call as survivors) is currently verified only by build + oracle code review (RevCvc). Add a funnel-level test that drives a candidate to the candKilled branch and asserts exactly one dead_hypotheses row is written whose structured columns (seat names, refuted count, arbiter verdict) match the verdicts and whose reasoning_trace equals buildReasoning(...).
acceptance_criteria: A funnel test exercises the kill path and asserts a single dead_hypotheses row with correct fingerprint/lens/file/line/title/severity, structured kill-verdict breakdown, and the buildReasoning trace; best-effort insert failure still does not abort the run.
notes: Source: oracle review RevCvc of bugbot-cvc (2026-06-29). Low priority hardening; the path is correct per code review.
close_reason: Done in PR #102 (squash-merged). Added TestSweep_KilledCandidate_PersistsDeadHypothesis: a Sweep that kills the bogus candidate (verifierRouting refutes 'Add overflows') asserts exactly one dead_hypotheses row, NOT one for the survivor; pins structured columns (Title/File/Line/Severity/Lens/Fingerprint/ScanRunID, SeatNames [reachability,semantics,guards], RefutedCount=3, TotalSeats=3, arbiter flags false) and reasoning_trace byte-equal to the identical buildReasoning(verdicts,seatNames,'',false) survivors use. Oracle RevFg9a APPROVE; -race -count=10 clean. NOTE follow-up: buildReasoning emits a 'Survived adversarial verification' header even for kills (cvc reuse) — misleading prose in dead_hypotheses, candidate for a low-pri cosmetic bead.

title: Decouple repro-as-evidence from repro-as-promotion for below-quorum findings
description: bugbot-sw7 confirmed NeedsHuman has a dual meaning (patch-prover-exhausted AND below-quorum verifier survivor) and that both are deliberately excluded from the repro backlog (verify_stream.go) so automation never routes around the human gate. Cost: a below-quorum finding never gets a repro WITNESS that could aid the human reviewer. A repro attempt currently does double duty: produce evidence AND promote (sets ReproPath, T2/T3->T1, can cascade to patch-proving/publishing). To let below-quorum findings gain a non-promoting repro witness, decouple the two roles: run repro for evidence but gate the promotion/cascade behind human confirmation for NeedsHuman findings.
acceptance_criteria: Below-quorum (NeedsHuman) findings can receive a repro attempt that records a witness artifact WITHOUT auto-promoting tier or triggering patch-proving/publishing; patch-prover-exhausted findings retain current behavior; decision and mechanism documented.
notes: Source: oracle review RevSw7 of bugbot-sw7 (2026-06-29). Larger design change; current exclude+document behavior is the correct scope for sw7.
close_reason: Done in PR #107 (squash-merged). Added a ReproWitness field parallel to ReproPath (migration 017) to decouple repro-as-evidence from repro-as-promotion: an in-run repro attempt on a below-quorum (NeedsHuman, no ReproPath) finding now records the artifact bundle as ReproWitness WITHOUT promoting tier, setting ReproPath, or triggering patch-prover/publish. PromoteOne/PromoteAll branch on NeedsHuman (witnessFinding vs promoteFinding); the claim check allows exactly one witness attempt; daemon backlog still excludes NeedsHuman (human gate preserved); patch-prover-exhausted findings (ReproPath set) unaffected. Oracle RevW1bh APPROVE (store SQL verified balanced; tests non-tautological). NOTE: implemented by a sub-implementor that aborted mid-run; orchestrator verified + added the 3 tests. Nit (no action): publisher keys on Tier not ReproPath, so a witness is publication-neutral.

title: Tool-health Phase 3: cross-run trend store + daemon auto-mitigation + repro/patch-prover wiring
description: Follow-up to bugbot-r9f (tool-health signal, shipped PR #98). Phases 1+2 delivered objective infra-failure capture + the flag-gated report_tool_issue complaint tool + KindToolUnhealthy alerts. Phase 3 remaining: (1) Cross-run trend: a tool_health store table mirroring agent_units (internal/funnel/observability.go pattern, PruneAgentUnits-style bounding) so 'codenav failed in 8/10 runs' style alerts are possible across runs. (2) Daemon auto-mitigation keyed on the OBJECTIVE signal only (never model-supplied severity): disable a chronically-broken OPTIONAL tool, de-rate affected lenses, or flag NeedsHuman. (3) Wire the objective sink + report_tool_issue into the reproducer and patch-prover runners in internal/repro (Phases 1+2 scoped wiring to the funnel finder/refuter/arbiter sites only; the repro stage's sandbox is the other high-value infra-failure surface). (4) Optional: a second objective producer — classify codenav LSP-crash (vs the normal unsupported-language grep fallback) as a ToolHealthError so finder-side health is exercised.

title: Bazel sandbox: make disk_cache writable so multi-exec repro builds are fast (perf; read-only /bazel-cache floods warnings + forces recompiles)

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text: bugbot-2tl.3 (sandbox build) addresses the disk_cache-writability problem for repos that adopt the generated offline image: the warm-cache-as-layer trick bakes a warmed /bazel-cache as an IMAGE LAYER, which is writable through the per-run container overlay even though bugbot mounts everything read-only. So a repo using 'bugbot sandbox build' gets a writable disk_cache per run (warm start, no read-only-cache warnings, no forced recompiles) without changing the read-only mount model. This bead's broader fix (a generally writable /bazel-cache mount for ad-hoc/non-image repro) is still open.

title: agent: run_tests buildArgv ignores pkg/run filters for cmake/meson compound
description: RunTestsTool.buildArgv (internal/agent/tools_run_tests.go) switches on baseCmd[0]. For C/C++ the baseCmd is a compound ['bash','-c','cmake ... && ctest ...'] so baseCmd[0]=='bash' hits the default passthrough: the advertised pkg/run narrowing (Def at tools_run_tests.go:85) is a silent no-op exactly for the slowest, most timeout-prone ecosystem. The buildArgv doc comment also claims a 'ctest' shape that is not implemented (no case "ctest"). Surfaced by oracle review of bugbot-ziq/p4l.
acceptance_criteria: buildArgv either narrows the bash-wrapped ctest/meson compound (e.g. ctest -R <run>, meson test <run>) or detection emits a non-wrapped narrowable baseCmd; the buildArgv doc comment matches the implemented runners. Tests cover pkg/run narrowing for cmake and meson.
close_reason: Merged to main (squash a5f3569). buildArgv narrows bash-wrapped ctest/meson (ctest -R, meson test NAME), word-boundary aware both runners, shell-injection-safe via shellSingleQuote; doc comment fixed. Oracle APPROVE + meson-symmetry fix applied.

title: Surface merged_root_cause counter in cli/scan.go merge-display line
description: bugbot-wf1 added Stats.MergedRootCause (json merged_root_cause) and surfaced it in cli/prcomments.go and internal/eval/suite.go, but NOT in the cli/scan.go 'Location merges'/merged=N progress line, because cli/scan.go is being actively rewritten by bugbot-0nc.17 (RunE decompose) and editing it now would create a merge conflict across epics. Deferred per orchestration of bugbot-ed4.
acceptance_criteria: After bugbot-0nc.17 lands, cli/scan.go's merge-counter display line includes merged_root_cause so a pure root-cause-merge run (within=0, cross=0, root=N) reports merged=N to the operator.
close_reason: Merged to main (squash 337ca15). printResult's Location merges line now includes root_cause; a root-cause-only merge run (within=0 cross=0 root=N) reports merged. Test TestPrintResult_MergeRootCauseOnly. Oracle APPROVE.

title: NeedsHuman flag overloaded: below-quorum verifier findings reuse patch-prover marker
description: bugbot-dox set NeedsHuman=true on findings that fall below the refuter-examined quorum floor. NeedsHuman was originally the patch-prover 'could not find a minimal fix' marker. Overloading it means (a) report/markdown.go and cli/worldstate.go render patch-prover-specific copy ('the patch-prover could not fix — possibly misdiagnosed') for a finding the prover never touched, and (b) repro_hook.go and daemon/backlog.go exclude NeedsHuman findings from the repro backlog, so below-quorum findings silently get no repro attempt. Surfaced by adversarial oracle review (agent OracleVerifier, hole H3) during epic bugbot-ed4.
acceptance_criteria: Either a distinct reason/marker distinguishes 'below-quorum' from 'patch-prover-exhausted', OR the human-facing copy (report/markdown.go, cli/worldstate.go) is made reason-neutral; AND a deliberate decision (documented) on whether below-quorum findings should be excluded from the repro backlog. No misleading patch-prover copy on verifier-flagged findings.
close_reason: Merged to main (squash 40b3a12). NeedsHuman copy made reason-neutral (report/markdown.go, cli/worldstate.go); dual meaning documented at source (store.Finding.NeedsHuman) and exclusion sites. Below-quorum repro exclusion kept as the deliberate decision recorded in verify_stream.go. Oracle APPROVE.

title: Build markers + repro guidance for Zig/Gleam/Elixir (detection landed in 93z.7)
description: 93z.7 added DETECTION ONLY for the minor languages. For the three with a single uniform build marker + test command, add build-system detection + repro guidance: build.zig -> 'zig build test'; gleam.toml -> 'gleam test'; mix.exs -> 'mix test' (Elixir is already detected). Add BuildSystem consts + DetectBuildSystems markers + langGuidance/specificGuidance entries, mirroring the existing per-language guidance. Scala/Dart/Lua remain detection-only.
acceptance_criteria: DetectBuildSystems returns the new markers; langGuidance for LangZig/LangGleam/LangElixir names the single-test invocation; HasGuidance consistent; unit tests for markers + guidance; gofmt/vet/tests clean.
close_reason: Done in PR #106 (squash-merged, commit f42f014). Added BuildSystemZig/Gleam/Elixir consts + DetectBuildSystems markers (build.zig/gleam.toml/mix.exs), wired both exhaustive BuildSystem switches (funnel detectTestCmd + repro detectSuiteCmdFor) to 'zig build test'/'gleam test'/'mix test', and added specificGuidance entries (HasGuidance now true for the three). Scala/Dart/Lua remain detection-only. Unit tests for markers + guidance. Oracle Rev93z18 APPROVE (verified all other BuildSystem consumers have safe defaults; one doc-comment nit fixed). NOTE: implemented partly by an aborted sub-implementor, finished + audited by the orchestrator.

title: Java jdtls LSP: evaluate whether it pays for itself in the agent loop
description: Explicit follow-up carved out by 93z.4, which left Java LSP out of scope. jdtls is heavyweight (JVM, workspace dirs, slow warmup). Evaluate whether registering jdtls in lsp.DefaultServers improves the agent's code-nav enough to justify the warmup cost, given the daemon-lifetime LSP manager (bugbot-cy8). If yes, add the entry (.java -> languageId java) with graceful absence; if no, record the decision.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded with rationale (cost vs. nav benefit). If adopted: jdtls in DefaultServers serving .java with graceful not-installed fallback + registry unit test; warmup behavior verified against manager.go indexing handling.

title: C# tree-sitter grammar for code-nav tier (.cs)
description: Deferred from 93z.3 (tree-sitter was called optional/separate). C# files get LSP (csharp-ls) but no tree-sitter code-nav fallback. Add .cs to internal/treesitter/grammarTable if the grammar registry carries a C# grammar + def/ref tag queries (mirror the JS/TS work in 93z.1). Decide grammar availability first; if absent from the registry, record that and close as wont-fix-until-available.
acceptance_criteria: Either: .cs produces tree-sitter defs+refs with a unit test on a real .cs file (existing tests still pass); or a recorded decision that the registry lacks a usable C# grammar. gofmt/vet/tests clean.

title: util: extract shared micro-helpers (shortSHA, sortedKeys, collapse/truncate)
description: shortSHA is duplicated identically in cli/scan.go:686, daemon/cycle.go:388, progress/format.go:15 (the last even comments that it mirrors the others). sortedKeys[V] in funnel/cartographer.go:532 and repro/artifacts.go:163. Whitespace-collapse pattern in funnel/cartographer.go:138 collapseNewlines, cli/cartography.go:116 previewLine, cli/leads.go:80 truncateNote (the last is UTF-8-safe). shortID (report.go:54) and shortFingerprint (cartography.go:127) are the same truncate-to-12. Coordinate with bugbot-und (truncateUTF8 helper) — converge on ONE rune-safe truncation.
acceptance_criteria: An internal package (e.g. internal/util) hosts ShortSHA/Truncate, SortedKeys[V], CollapseWhitespace, and a rune-safe TruncateRunes; all listed call sites migrated; no helper redefined per-package. bugbot-und's truncateUTF8 is reconciled with the shared rune-safe truncation (one implementation).
close_reason: Created internal/util (stdlib-only leaf): ShortSHA/Truncate/SortedKeys/CollapseWhitespace/TruncateRunes. All per-package duplicates removed and call sites migrated across cli/daemon/progress/funnel/repro (behavior-identical, incl. rune-safe ellipsis truncation). Merged.

title: llm: surgical shared adapter helpers + document capability-asymmetry matrix
description: Anthropic/OpenAI/Google adapters share >60% structure via fully parallel methods (buildParams/toXMessages/toResponse/mapXStop/normalizeErr). Surgical dedup WITHOUT a base-adapter framework: parseToolParameters (anthropic.go:242, openai.go:95, google.go:75 — unmarshal + unwrap properties/required), parseResponseSchema (anthropic.go:151, openai.go:124, google.go:102), normalizeErr (anthropic.go:403, openai.go:238, google.go:252 — already half-shared via normalize.go classifyStatus/parseRetryAfter/newAPIError). Capability ASYMMETRIES to DOCUMENT (not unify): native structured-output is dropped when user tools are present on Anthropic/Google but honored on OpenAI (deliberate — RunJSON.repair drops tools to compensate); CacheCreationInputTokens populated only by Anthropic; default MaxTokens=4096 only on Anthropic.
design: Stay surgical. The win is three small pure helpers + a documented capability matrix, not a provider framework.
acceptance_criteria: (1) parseToolParameters + parseResponseSchema + a normalizeSDKError shape extracted and used by all 3 adapters (behavior identical; covered by existing llm tests + a cross-adapter parse test). (2) A capability matrix documented (comment on Capabilities or README) covering structured-output-with-tools, cache-creation tokens, and default max-tokens. EXPLICIT non-goal: no BaseAdapter callback framework and no generic message-converter builder — both more complex than the duplication they would remove.
close_reason: LLM adapters wired to shared parseToolParameters/parseResponseSchema/normalizeSDKError helpers; capability-asymmetry matrix documented. parseToolParameters used by Anthropic only (OpenAI/Google keep raw unmarshal to preserve wire shape) - intentional. Merged b102908.

title: cli: add command-bootstrap helpers (openStore/buildRoleClients/buildFunnelOptions/closeStore)
