description: config.Load + store.Open + defer Close is reimplemented 8+x (scan.go:71, daemon.go:49, review.go:247, repro.go:55, status.go:46, cartography.go:40, metrics.go:32; only report.go:42 has an openStore helper) with inconsistent error wrapping. Finder/verifier/cartographer LLM client construction is copy-pasted across scan.go:87, daemon.go:65, review.go:250, cartography.go:56. funnel.Options budget-field plumbing (9 fields) is duplicated scan.go:204 / daemon.go:117 / review.go:289 — parity-drift risk (daemon may add a field scan/review miss). Sandbox degraded-warning text duplicated scan.go:131 / daemon.go:103 / review.go:276. defer st.Close() repeated 12x.
acceptance_criteria: A cli bootstrap module exposes cmdOpenStore, buildRoleClients, buildFunnelOptions(cfg, overrides), closeStore, printSandboxDegradedWarning. scan/daemon/review/cartography/report/status/repro migrated; budget-field plumbing lives in exactly one place. Build/vet/test clean; CLI behavior unchanged.
close_reason: cli bootstrap.go: cmdOpenStore/closeStore/buildRoleClients/buildFunnelOptions/printSandboxDegradedWarning; all commands migrated; budget-field plumbing single-sourced. Merged 4c4886e.

title: store: generic row-scan helper, consistent annotateErr, deterministic ORDER BY
description: scanFinding/scanLead/scanAgentUnit plus inline scans in published.go/metrics.go repeat the QueryContext + defer + for rows.Next() + rows.Err() loop ~13x with no generic. annotateErr (sqlite_error.go:107) is applied asymmetrically: spend/metrics/cartographer use it; findings/leads/published/suppressions/filestate/agentunits/scanruns do not. Determinism gaps: published.go ListPublishedIssues orders by fingerprint only; suppressions.go ListSuppressions by created_at DESC only — both missing the secondary id tiebreak that findings/leads/metrics use.
acceptance_criteria: (1) scanRows[T](rows, scanFn) ([]T,error) helper enforcing rows.Err(); list methods migrated. (2) annotateErr applied uniformly on DB errors across accessors (documented convention). (3) ListPublishedIssues and ListSuppressions add a secondary id ordering. Tests assert stable ordering.
close_reason: scanRows[T] generic helper adopted across list methods; annotateErr applied uniformly; rowid tiebreaks for published/suppressions. Chunk-accumulation loops (scanWatermarksChunk/getPackageSummariesChunk) and metrics.RunMetrics aggregate left as documented exceptions (error-annotated, shape-only). Merged c6d8117.

title: funnel: extract bounded-fan-out primitive for stage dispatch
description: The slot-pool fan-out scaffold is hand-rolled 4x with subtle divergences: hypothesize.go ~390-500 (slotLow, runCtx breaker cancel, emit OUTSIDE mu), cartographer.go:333-378 regenSummaries (slotLow, runCtx budget cancel, onResult INSIDE mu, acquire-before-wg.Add ordering differs), verify_stream.go:61-95 (slotHigh, NO derived ctx, direct findingsMu append), repro_hook.go:49-55 (slotIdle, no budget gate, direct AddAgentUnit). Common shape: acquire(class)+defer release, post-acquire ctx/budget recheck, WaitGroup, serialized result handling. Asymmetries: optional callback vs hardcoded persist; derived runCtx present in only 2 of 4 (verify/repro cannot wake slot-blocked goroutines on a budget hard-stop).
design: Keep slotClass and the budget predicate as caller params (no registry). Unify emit/onResult into one optional callback with documented serialization semantics.
acceptance_criteria: A single generic helper (e.g. runUnitsWithBudget[T]) encapsulating acquire/release, post-acquire ctx+budget recheck, WaitGroup, a derived cancellable runCtx, and an optional per-result callback; all 4 stages migrated with caller-supplied slotClass + budget predicates. Behavior preserved — funnel/cartographer/verify/repro tests pass unchanged. HIGHEST-RISK item: schedule LAST; rely on interrupt_test, slotpool_test, cartographer_parallel_test, verify_diversity_test as the safety net.
close_reason: Extracted internal/funnel/fanout.go (newFanout/spawn/spawnSerial/stop/wait); migrated finder loop, cartographer regenSummaries (ordered admission), and verify dispatcher. Repro left as-is with documented reason (pre-acquire claim check + non-fanout callers rely on internal slotIdle acquire; forcing it would change behavior). Behavior preserved; funnel suite green under -race. Merged.

title: cartographer pass has no circuit breaker — hangs against an unreachable provider
description: Discovered while verifying bugbot-2uz against the_cloud. The cartographer pass (internal/funnel/run.go cartograph, runs BEFORE the finder stage) makes one LLM summary call per package. Against an unreachable/misconfigured provider each call burns the retry budget (~3.5s) and there are many packages, so the scan stalls in cartography for a long time BEFORE the finder-stage circuit breaker (bugbot-2uz) can ever fire. Confirmed: with cartographer ON the scan made no progress in 30s (empty state.db); with cartographer OFF the finder breaker trips in ~4s and the scan finishes in 11s.
design: Mirror the finder-stage breaker (bugbot-2uz): track consecutive transport-class failures (llm.APIError StatusCode==0) across cartographer summary calls with zero successes; once a small threshold is hit, stop issuing further summaries and let the pass return (cartography is best-effort, so an early stop just yields fewer summaries). Reuse isTransportError from hypothesize.go (or lift it to a shared helper). Keep it scoped to cartography; do not change finder behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A scan against an unreachable provider with cartographer ON aborts the cartographer pass within a few seconds instead of grinding through every package; cartography stays best-effort (no error returned); a healthy run is unaffected; funnel tests cover the abort.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #100 (commit cf8b9bd). Added a transport-error circuit breaker to the lazy cartographer generation path (ensureContextFor/getSummary->generate), mirroring the finder breaker (bugbot-2uz): count isTransportError (StatusCode==0) failures with zero successes; at max(3,MaxParallel) short-circuit further generations (cached memo/store summaries still render; best-effort, no error). One success disarms permanently. The two identical sf.Do closures were factored into generate(); RefreshCartography/regenSummaries untouched. NOTE: scan path is now LAZY (not the eager pre-finder pass the bead described). Oracle-reviewed APPROVE + ~3800 race-iteration stress clean.

title: finder stage degrades slowly against an unreachable/misconfigured provider
description: With an unreachable provider every finder LLM call exhausts DefaultRetryConfig (4 attempts, ~4s backoff) and the funnel allocates many finder seats for a large repo, so a fully-broken provider takes seats x ~4s to surface MostFindersFailed instead of failing fast (observed ~45s+ on the_cloud with one lens).
design: Add a finder-stage circuit breaker in internal/funnel/hypothesize.go: track consecutive finder units that failed with a transport/connection error (APIError StatusCode 0 / ErrServer transport class, distinct from rate-limit and parse failures) with zero successes; once the count exceeds a small threshold (e.g. max(3, concurrency)), cancel remaining finder work and stop dispatching, recording the early-abort reason so stats still report unreliable. Must NOT trip on rate-limits (already excluded) or normal parse failures.
acceptance_criteria: A funnel test with a finder client that always returns a connection/transport error completes within ~one batch (not seats x retries) and reports the finder stage as unreliable/most-failed; a run where finders succeed is unaffected; go test ./internal/funnel/ passes.
close_reason: finder-stage circuit breaker added; verified on the_cloud it trips in ~4s and the scan finishes in 11s (was 70s+) reporting unreliable. Discovered the cartographer has the same gap -> filed bugbot-1r9

title: heat ranking surfaces config/data files as top finder targets
description: On the_cloud the top heat files were deps.MODULE.bazel, values.yaml, values.schema.json (3 of top 5) — config/manifests, not code, consuming the highest-priority finder seats. ChurnHeat (internal/ingest/heat.go) scores purely by git churn/recency with no language awareness.
design: Exclude non-code files from heat candidates: in ChurnHeat/parseHeat drop paths whose DetectLanguage == LangOther so config/data/manifest files get no heat (fall to 0 / ranked last). Keep LangShell and all real code languages. Composes with the Elixir bead (Elixir becomes non-Other so it is included).
acceptance_criteria: parseHeat over a fixture containing a high-churn config file (.yaml/.json/.bazel) and a normal code file keeps the code file and drops the config file from the heat map; heat_test.go updated; go test ./internal/ingest/ passes.
close_reason: ChurnHeat/parseHeat drop LangOther paths; verified on the_cloud the top-5 heat files are now all code (was 3 config files), heat_files 4010->2885

title: agent: sandboxed test-runner tool for finders/refuters
description: sandbox_exec is refuter-only. Give finders/refuters a constrained test-runner so a weak model can empirically check a hypothesis instead of arguing it.
acceptance_criteria: A sandboxed run-tests tool exists with budget gating; available to chosen roles; integration test under the sandbox build tag.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: store/funnel: lead ranking (confidence) + agreement-based finding confidence
description: Leads are unranked and corroboration is informational only. Add a confidence field on leads (sort PendingLeads by it) and let cross-lens/refuter agreement raise a findings confidence score, focusing scarce context budget on the strongest signals.
acceptance_criteria: AddLead accepts confidence; PendingLeads orders by confidence then created_at; finding confidence reflects corroboration; unit tests cover ordering and scoring.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: Surface llm RequestTimeout in config.yaml (per-attempt request timeout)
description: The per-attempt LLM request timeout (bugbot-ci0 fix) defaults to 5m via DefaultRequestTimeout and is wirable through RetryConfig.RequestTimeout, but is not yet exposed in config.yaml. Surface it (e.g. under a providers/retry or budgets section) with env override + template doc + validation, following the existing config pattern, so operators with slow models or strict latency needs can tune it without a rebuild.
acceptance_criteria: config.yaml field maps to RetryConfig.RequestTimeout for all role clients; env override + StarterYAML doc + validation present; zero/omitted uses DefaultRequestTimeout (5m); covered by a config test.
close_reason: Merged to main in PR #81 (commit 37c72ab, 'batch of 8 bounded bugfixes'). Dolt was initialized after this work and never recorded the close; reconciled against committed bead state + verified code on main.

title: repro: fence sandbox output in feedback prompt as data (GH #55 is overblown — flattening is the wrong fix)
description: GH #55 claims verdict.feedback embeds untrusted sandbox output without the newline-flattening guard used elsewhere. Diagnosis: not a security boundary. The funnel guard (strategy.go:85-89, prompt.go:308-310) protects line-format integrity of one-item-per-line sections, not injection; hostile repo content already enters every agent prompt verbatim via read_file by design, and an attacker controlling the sandbox already forges verdicts directly via exit codes/output — injection grants nothing extra. Flattening would actively break the repro loop: multi-line compiler/test output is load-bearing feedback. If hardening at all: fence v.summary in unique delimiters with a treat-as-data note in feedback() (interpret.go:80-86) and buildTask (agent.go:189-193), and add a comment explaining why newlines are intentionally preserved (pre-empts future scanner re-reports).
acceptance_criteria: (1) Decision recorded: close GH #55 as won't-fix-as-written or apply the fencing variant. (2) If fencing: v.summary wrapped in delimiters with data-not-instructions note; comment at the site explaining preserved newlines. (3) Funnel guard comments clarified as format-integrity, not anti-injection.
close_reason: Merged to main in PR #81 (commit 37c72ab, 'batch of 8 bounded bugfixes'). Dolt was initialized after this work and never recorded the close; reconciled against committed bead state + verified code on main.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Acceptance item 1 satisfied: GH #55 closed as won't-fix-as-written with rationale comment (user-authorized 2026-06-12). Bead remains open only for the optional fencing + documentation work.

title: publish: add truncateUTF8 helper for all byte-cap truncation sites (GH #54 #56 #58 #61 #62)
description: Five sites in internal/cli/publish.go slice at raw byte offsets without rune-boundary walk-back: 522 (description), 534 (fix patch), 578 (reasoning), 729/740 (repro per-file/total caps). Real but cosmetic: gh CLI's JSON marshal coerces the split rune to U+FFFD, so impact is one replacement glyph at an already-truncated point; no fence/details-tag escape is possible (split-rune bytes are 0x80-0xF4, can't synthesize backtick or '<'). The same function already does the correct utf8.RuneStart walk-back at publish.go:604-609 — factor that into a helper and use it at all six sites.
acceptance_criteria: (1) truncateUTF8(s, max) helper that walks back to a rune boundary; applied at the 5 flagged sites plus replacing the inline walk-back at 604-609. (2) Test asserting utf8.ValidString on truncated output (precedent: leads_test.go:142). (3) GH issues #54 #56 #58 #61 #62 closed by the fix.
notes: For the []byte repro sites, byte-budget accounting still works since the helper only shrinks (≤3 bytes drift under cap). One PR, one helper, five issues.
close_reason: Merged to main in PR #81 (commit 37c72ab, 'batch of 8 bounded bugfixes'). Dolt was initialized after this work and never recorded the close; reconciled against committed bead state + verified code on main.

title: Refuters assert stdlib/platform semantics from memory — published issue #53 contains a false os/exec claim
description: GH #53 (stdin pipe leaked when StdoutPipe fails) states 'Without cmd.Start() succeeding, Go's exec package does not close the pipe file descriptors automatically.' That is false: os/exec Start's deferred cleanup closes childIOFiles always and parentIOPipes when start fails (exec.go ~649-658; pipes registered at 550-551/599-600). The real residual is only the StdoutPipe-fails-before-Start path (os.Pipe EMFILE), a 2-fd GC-reclaimable leak — info-level. Three refuters echoed the false stdlib claim; none opened the Go source on disk. Bugbot's published reports teach readers wrong platform facts when reasoning rests on unverified stdlib behavior.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Verifier/refuter prompts instruct: any claim about stdlib/runtime/third-party behavior that the verdict hinges on must be verified by reading the actual source (GOROOT is available in the sandbox/host) or executing a probe — and the verdict must cite what was checked. (2) Replayed #53 either dies or is downgraded to info with a corrected mechanism.
notes: Fix-side: the one-line stdin.Close() on the StdoutPipe error branch is still worth doing (see bugbot fix bead for lsp findings); do NOT add cleanup to the Start error branch — dead code codifying the misconception.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: Repro as a pipeline operator: wire verified findings into the repro backlog continuously, under the global agent cap
description: Completes Dustin's full-dataflow direction (2026-06-11): with bugbot-280 streaming candidates through finder->triage->verify, the repro stage joins the same dataflow. Key insight: repro is ALREADY the architectural template — internal/daemon/backlog.go is a durable queue over the findings store with oldest-first rotation (failed attempts rotate to the back) and idempotent attempts; publish is fingerprint-idempotent downstream. What changes: (1) verified T2 findings persisted mid-run become immediately eligible for repro (today the scan --repro flag runs repro as a post-stage and the daemon drains on idle) — the repro consumer runs continuously as a background-class operator; (2) repro agents draw from bugbot-280's GLOBAL agent cap as the lowest-priority role class (verify > finder > repro), since repro is sandbox-heavy, latency-tolerant, and its rotation loses nothing by waiting — an idle-slots-only policy is acceptable v1; (3) interruption semantics come free from the existing backlog durability. Explicitly out of scope: changing repro attempt logic, sandbox mechanics, or promotion rules — this is call topology + scheduling only. Blocked by bugbot-280's streaming phases (the global cap must exist first).
acceptance_criteria: (1) During a long scan, a finding verified early gets a repro attempt before the finder stage completes (integration test with scripted clients + fake sandbox asserting interleaved roles in agent_units). (2) Repro agents never displace a waiting verifier or finder slot under the global cap (scheduling test). (3) Kill mid-run: repro attempts made are recorded, backlog rotation state intact, nothing double-attempted on resume. (4) Daemon idle-drain behavior unchanged when no scan is running. (5) Gates clean.
close_reason: Merged in PR #68 (775cc71). Repro is the dataflow's idle-class operator: third slotIdle pool class (verify > finder > repro; cancellation protocol preserved and hammered across all classes); survived T2 findings enqueue in-run via reproQueue (never-blocking enqueue, overflow fallback unit-tested directly, exactly-once by construction + 12-finding burst test); one finding = one PromoteOne = one idle slot; CLI --repro = in-run hook + catch-up drain excluding already-attempted fingerprints (review M2: exhausted attempts leave no store marker and were being double-attempted); spend attributed to the scan run via hook-supplied scanRunID (review M1: was landing with empty scan_run_id under comments describing nonexistent machinery). Daemon idle drain unchanged. Acceptance: in-run streaming attempt before discovery completes (test), never-displace (idle class + tests), no-double-attempt (claim check + attempted set + tests), daemon unaffected, gates clean.

title: Advisory single-scan lock: warn or refuse when a second scan targets the same state db
description: Motivated by a real incident (2026-06-11 dogfood): two identical 'bugbot scan' processes ran concurrently against the same config/state db, silently double-spending API tokens and contending for rate limits (~10 min before noticed via ps + agent_units scan_run_id counts). Process-level parallelism is the cousin of bugbot-280's in-process global agent cap, out of scope there. Fix: an advisory lock keyed on the state db — e.g. a 'running scan' row in scan_runs (started, not finished, recent heartbeat) checked at scan start; on conflict, refuse with a clear message naming the running scan_run_id/pid and a --force override (stale-lock tolerant: a scan_run with no heartbeat for N minutes is considered dead — the bugbot-280 phase-1 interrupted-finalization work makes stale rows rarer). Ecosystem-general, store-level, no platform file-locking needed.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Second concurrent scan against the same store refuses by default, names the live run, and --force overrides. (2) Stale (heartbeat-expired or unfinalized-old) locks do not block. (3) Daemon's own poll/sweep cycles are unaffected. (4) Test covers refuse, force, and stale-lock paths. (5) Gates clean.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text: Largely superseded by bugbot-4d2's implementation: store.Open now takes an exclusive cross-process flock (LOCK_EX) on the state db for all writing commands, so a second concurrent scan REFUSES with *ErrLocked (naming the holder pid) rather than double-spending — the core ask here. Kernel auto-releases on process death, so the heartbeat-based stale-lock detection 4zt proposed is unnecessary. NOT yet done: the --force override escape hatch. Decide whether to keep this open just for --force or close as covered.

title: Record reproducer units in agent_units (mi5.10 acceptance gap)
description: bugbot-mi5.10 shipped finder and verifier unit rows (PR #48) but the reproducer stage records nothing — acceptance criterion 1 asked for verifier/reproducer symmetry, the Sonnet implementer silently omitted it, and the review round's named seams did not cover the repro stage so it survived to merge (closed with the deviation documented). Extend internal/funnel/observability.go's recordVerifierUnit pattern to the repro stage: one row per repro agent run with role='reproducer', the finding's lens/file, status from the repro outcome classification mapped to short snake_case, tokens from the agent Outcome, detail = outcome summary (no model text). Same best-effort discipline (never abort a scan) and outside-the-mutex insert if the repro stage has shared state.
acceptance_criteria: (1) One agent_units row per reproducer agent run with role='reproducer' and a status vocabulary documented in the migration or agentunits.go comment. (2) Funnel/repro test drives a repro attempt via fakes and asserts the row (status, nonzero tokens, lens/file). (3) report units renders reproducer rows without breaking the finder-only coverage fraction. (4) Gates clean.
close_reason: Closed by PR #68 alongside bugbot-591: every reproducer attempt records one agent_units row (role='reproducer', status vocabulary reproduced/exhausted/infra_error documented at the producer and store, Candidates=1 on reproduced, files_json=[finding.file]); in-run rows carry the real scan_run_id, daemon-drain rows carry empty scan_run_id (documented, tolerated by report units and pruning). Tokens are zero on the rows — repro spend flows through the CLI's ledger recorder, not Outcome plumbing; noted at the recording site.

title: Agent activity feed: show what each in-flight agent is up to (tool-derived + optional hypothesis notes)
description: Status view / future watch TUI (bugbot-7td) shows only role+lens+elapsed per in-flight agent. Add a per-turn 'current activity' string so the user can see what each agent is actually doing. TIER 1 (mechanical): runner emits KindAgentActivity per turn derived from tool calls ('reading file X', 'grepping Y') — zero token cost, cannot misreport. TIER 2 (flag-gated): a lightweight status_note tool lets the agent self-report its current hypothesis ('suspecting double-close in retry loop'). Tier 2 doubles as a data feed for bugbot-mi5.10's distribution questions: timestamped hypothesis notes measure WHEN/WHERE hypotheses form (turn number, launch order), which run-level stats aggregate away. Risk noted: finder prompt forbids speculation; hypothesis broadcasting could bias agents toward performing hypotheses — frame as non-binding scratchpad note, keep behind a flag.
design: Seam: agents are in-process goroutines (internal/agent/runner.go), so the runner's turn loop can emit progress events directly — no transcript tailing needed. Touchpoints: events.go (new KindAgentActivity), snapshot.go (extend AgentStatus + apply()), runner.go (emit per turn), pane.go/status.go (render). Flows to bugbot-7td watch TUI for free via status.json. Rejected alternative: tailing transcript JSONL files — pointless given same-process access; adds I/O latency and parsing noise.
acceptance_criteria: (1) AgentStatus in internal/progress/snapshot.go carries an activity string + last-update time; status.json includes it. (2) Runner emits a KindAgentActivity event per turn derived from tool calls with no extra LLM cost. (3) Pane/log renderers and bugbot status display the activity line per in-flight agent. (4) Hypothesis self-report tool exists behind a flag, off by default; when on, notes appear as activity and land in the transcript. (5) Activity strings are truncated/sanitized for single-line terminal rendering. (6) No behavior change to finder output schema or verdicts.
notes: CORRECTION to the risk framing in description (2026-06-11): the finder prompt does NOT forbid speculation per se — it forbids REPORTING speculation as a finding (prompt.go:27-38: 'Report ONLY concrete, confirmed bugs', 'do NOT pad with weak or speculative entries', low-confidence entries dropped). Internal hypothesizing is the design (stage is named hypothesize; funnel is hypothesize→refute). Precedent: cross-lens LEADS (prompt.go:174) already inject other agents' suspicions ('they may be wrong') into finder tasks — speculation as a shared artifact is sanctioned. The actual Tier-2 risk is channel confusion: the status_note prompt wording must keep the candidates-array bar unchanged so notes neither become a pressure valve that relaxes reporting discipline nor a back door for unconfirmed findings. Flag-gating stays as the containment.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: Cross-model finder ensembles per lens
description: Different model families have measurably non-overlapping bug-finding distributions. The llm package already maps roles to provider+model; extend config so a lens (or the finder role generally) can fan out to 2+ models, with triage's dedup fingerprints absorbing cross-model duplicates. Natural two-tier variant: a cheap model runs wide sweeps posting leads; the expensive model runs deep dives on lead-rich areas. Evaluate added unique recall per dollar before defaulting it on — this is the most speculative item in the epic and should be driven by eval data from the recorded corpus.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Config supports multiple finder models per lens or role; default remains single-model. (2) Dedup verified to collapse cross-model duplicate candidates. (3) Eval report quantifies unique-finding contribution and token cost per added model. (4) Budget accounting attributes spend per model.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text:
EVIDENCE EVALUATION (2026-06-24). NOT closeable — unimplemented AND currently UNMEASURABLE. Recommend DEFER.

This bead is explicitly the most speculative angle in the epic, and its acceptance is gated on eval data: AC3 "Eval report quantifies unique-finding contribution and token cost per added model" and AC4 "budget accounting attributes spend per model". Both require >=2 model families wired. The dogfood/eval setup is SINGLE-PROVIDER: bugbot.yaml maps all roles to MiniMax-M3 (only provider configured; see dogfood-config memory), and both deep-tooling runs (controller, aethon) were 100% MiniMax-M3. So there is no cross-model recall delta to measure and nothing in the run evidence advances this bead.

Recommendation: DEFER until a second model family is available to the dogfood config (e.g. a second provider/key). Until then the eval that gates it cannot run, so building the config fan-out (AC1/AC2) would ship an unvalidated, default-off path with no way to earn its default-on. The two-tier cheap-wide/expensive-deep variant is the more attractive framing if revisited. Lowest priority in the epic (P3); no objection to rejecting it outright if a second model family is not on the roadmap.

title: Coverage-gap targeting + reproduce-first mode (findings born T1)
description: Two coupled pieces. (a) Targeting: compute per-file test-coverage presence per ecosystem and intersect with churn heat — high-churn x uncovered is the strongest bug prior available without running anything. Use it to rank sweep targets. (b) Reproduce-first: for a small budget-capped set of top-ranked targets, skip hypothesize entirely — an agent reads doc comments / declared contracts and writes property or round-trip tests, run in the sandbox via the per-ecosystem SetupCmds machinery (bugbot-93z.8/93z.11). A failing test IS the finding, born at T1 with the repro attached. Expensive per target, so it only runs on the coverage-gap-ranked shortlist. Coverage detection can start coarse (does any test file reference this module) before integrating real coverage tooling per ecosystem.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Sweep targeting incorporates churn x uncovered ranking with stats showing the intersection size. (2) Reproduce-first runs only on a configurable top-N shortlist within budget. (3) A failing generated test produces a T1 finding with the test attached as repro evidence; a passing suite produces nothing (no speculative candidates). (4) Generated tests never leak outside the sandbox workspace.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text:
EVIDENCE EVALUATION (2026-06-24). NOT closeable — unimplemented feature. Status unchanged by the runs, but feasibility improved.

The reproduce-first half now has working machinery to build on: the reproducer agent + per-ecosystem SetupCmds landed (bugbot-ziq/p4l/uaa etc.), and the aethon run actually produced agent_units with status 'reproduced'=2, so the sandbox repro path is exercised end-to-end on a real bazel monorepo. The missing pieces remain: (a) churn x uncovered targeting (coverage-presence detection per ecosystem intersected with churn heat) is not implemented; (b) reproduce-first mode (skip hypothesize; agent writes property/round-trip tests run in-sandbox; failing test = T1 finding) is not implemented.

Recommendation: keep OPEN at P3. Lower priority than mi5.14; sequence after mi5.14 since both reshape finder targeting/scheduling and should not be built against the soon-to-change deterministic grid. No run evidence makes it closeable.

title: doctor: warn when sandbox image lacks the detected language's toolchain (C++ cmake/g++, Python pip)
description: The default sandbox image (docker.io/library/debian:stable-slim) has no C++ toolchain (cmake/ninja/g++) and no pip — so C++ repro (configure+build, per bugbot-93z.5's guidance) and Python dep installs (bugbot-93z.11 SetupCmds) fail at runtime on the default image, with errors that surface deep in the repro stage. Deployments must set sandbox.image appropriately (e.g. gcc:13 or python:3-slim), but nothing tells the operator upfront. Add a doctor informational/warn check: when dominant languages imply toolchain needs (cpp -> cmake or make + a compiler; python -> pip), probe the configured image for those binaries (single '<runtime> run --rm <image> sh -c "command -v ..."' with the existing 5s-probe-timeout pattern, skipped when the image is not present locally) and WARN naming the missing tools and suggesting an image. Never hard-fail (image may be intentionally minimal with deps handled elsewhere). Also add commented sandbox.image guidance per language family in config/template.go. Cross-ref: 93z.5 close reason recorded the C++ image/timeout concern; 93z.11 design records the pip gap.
acceptance_criteria: On a config pointing at debian:stable-slim with a dominant C++ repo, doctor WARNs naming cmake/compiler absence and suggests an image; Python repo likewise for pip; image-not-local skips the probe with an INFO; probe respects timeout; never affects exit code; unit tests with injected runCommand fakes; template.go gains per-language image guidance comments.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: doctor: lighter language detection than full content-sniffing snapshot
description: bugbot doctor's languages check builds a full ingest.Snapshot — reading and binary-sniffing every tracked file — purely to print a one-line INFO list of dominant languages (internal/cli/doctor.go checkRepo). On large repos that is slow and heavy for an informational diagnostic, and it is why the repo-facts group needs a 30s timeout. Flagged in the adversarial review of PR bugbot-64p (finding 4). Consider an extension-only scan over git ls-files (no content read), or a Snapshot fast-path that skips binary sniffing. Related nit from the same review: TestDoctor_SecretsNeverInOutput only exercises the happy path; extend it to a config-error path while in here.
acceptance_criteria: doctor's languages check no longer reads file contents (verify: doctor on a large repo completes the languages line in well under the 30s repo-facts timeout; no os.ReadFile of tracked files on that path). DominantLanguages results match the snapshot-based ones on this repo. Secrets test covers at least one error path.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: Cross-language dep-strategy verification matrix + docs (README strategy table per ecosystem)
description: Capstone for the dep-strategy children (93z.8-93z.11): the README currently documents the vendored/off/host/fetch strategies for Go only. Extend the strategy table to a per-ecosystem matrix (Go/Rust/JS/Python: what 'vendored' means there, host-cache path, prefetch command, offline-enforcement env, whether an in-sandbox setup step runs), document the per-ecosystem security boundaries (npm --ignore-scripts in prefetch; never mounting ~/.cargo credentials; SELinux Shared-mount semantics), and add a single integration-tagged matrix test that exercises one external-dep fixture per ecosystem under network-none (skipping per-language when the toolchain image is unavailable). Also satisfies the epic acceptance line 'each language layer table documents its coverage tier' for the deps layer.
acceptance_criteria: README matrix covers Go/Rust/JS/Python with strategy semantics + security notes per ecosystem; integration matrix test exists with per-language skips; deps.go header comment lists the ecosystem table's coverage tiers; gates green.
close_reason: Merged to main: commit 573f6de 'docs(sandbox): ecosystem coverage-tier header + generalize dep_strategy config doc (bugbot-93z.12)'.

title: Config knob for sweep heat ordering (currently Options-only, silently always-on)
description: Review finding on bugbot-sro: funnel Options.DisableHeatOrdering exists but is wired from no config field or env var — heat ordering is always-on for every user and un-disableable without code edits. It degrades to alphabetical when git history is unavailable, so this is acceptable short-term. Add a config home (likely a new scan: section or budgets-adjacent field) + env override + wiring in scan.go/daemon.go/review.go.
acceptance_criteria: Config field + env override toggles heat ordering; default on; wired at all three funnel-construction sites; config tests follow existing patterns.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: Prompt-injection surface: git_log/git_blame return attacker-adjacent commit metadata verbatim to agents
description: Review finding on bugbot-sro: git_log returns author+subject and git_blame returns author+line content verbatim to finder/verifier models. In public repos commit messages are attacker-controllable, so a malicious commit subject could carry prompt-injection text. Likely acceptable today (models already read attacker-adjacent file content) but the surface is wider and undocumented. Evaluate sanitization (flatten whitespace like leads notes do in funnel finderTask), an explicit untrusted-content framing line in tool output, or accept-and-document.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded: either sanitization/framing implemented for git tool output (with tests) or an explicit accept-risk rationale documented in the tool source; consistent with how leads notes are sanitized.
close_reason: Merged to main in PR #81 (commit 37c72ab, 'batch of 8 bounded bugfixes'). Dolt was initialized after this work and never recorded the close; reconciled against committed bead state + verified code on main.

title: Detection batch for minor languages: Zig, Gleam, Elixir, Scala, Dart, Lua, Obj-C
description: These languages resolve to LangOther today, making them invisible to personas and language stats. Add extension entries to extLang plus displayName: Zig (.zig), Gleam (.gleam), Elixir (.ex/.exs), Scala (.scala/.sc), Dart (.dart), Lua (.lua), Objective-C (.m/.mm — note .m is a real omission given .h already maps to C; beware MATLAB also uses .m, accept the misclassification or sniff content). Where the ecosystem has a single build marker and uniform test command, optionally add build detection + repro guidance in the same pass: build.zig / zig build test, gleam.toml / gleam test, mix.exs / mix test. Scala/Dart/Lua get detection only for now.
acceptance_criteria: Each listed extension classifies to its language in DetectLanguage and renders in personas via displayName. DominantLanguages counts them. Zig/Gleam/Elixir build markers detected if included in this pass (otherwise file follow-ups). gofmt/vet/tests clean.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: Polish: fix_patch diff headers leak temp-dir paths
description: Live validation (bugbot-7ib) showed computeDiff's 'git diff --no-index' output uses the patched-copy temp path in headers (e.g. 'b/tmp/bugbot-patch-diff-NNN/mathx.go') instead of a clean b/mathx.go. Cosmetic but visible in every report/issue body carrying a fix witness.
acceptance_criteria: fix_patch headers read a/<path> b/<path> with no temp components; unit test on header shape; existing patch tests green.
close_reason: Merged to main in PR #81 (commit 37c72ab, 'batch of 8 bounded bugfixes'). Dolt was initialized after this work and never recorded the close; reconciled against committed bead state + verified code on main.

title: bugbot watch: live TUI dashboard over status.json + store
description: Follow-up to bugbot-awl (pane of glass). The snapshot sink already rate-limits status.json to ~1s for exactly this: a live-refreshing terminal dashboard (bugbot watch) rendering activity, agents in flight, spend ticker, and findings/leads as they land. Build after the static status layout has proven what belongs on screen.
acceptance_criteria: bugbot watch live-refreshes without flicker; degrades to static snapshot when no activity; quits cleanly; no LLM/network use; reuses the status world-state renderers.

title: Regression hunter: findings introduced within a commit range
description: Answer 'what got worse since X' instead of 'what is wrong'. 'bugbot regress --from A --to B' (and a daemon weekly report): restrict scanning to the blast radius of A..B (the --since machinery generalized to a range), then attribute each finding as INTRODUCED (absent at A, present at B — re-verify the anchor against A's content) vs PRE-EXISTING. Daemon emits a per-cycle/per-week 'new since last green' digest. Synergy: the git history tools + churn heat prioritization planned in bugbot-sro provide the plumbing (commit-range diffing, blame); build on it.
acceptance_criteria: regress command scans only the A..B blast radius and labels findings introduced vs pre-existing with the evidence (anchor check at A); daemon digest lists introduced-this-period findings; works when A's tree lacks the file (treated as introduced); fixture test with a bug added in a known commit; tree green + lint clean.
close_reason: Part (a) delivered in PR #108 (squash-merged): 'bugbot regress --from A [--to B]' scopes a targeted blast-radius scan to the A..B range and labels each finding INTRODUCED (anchor absent at A, or line beyond A's EOF) vs PRE-EXISTING (present at A). New ingest.ReadFileAtRef (git show ref:path); ScanFlags From/To reuse runScanCmd + store.ScanTargeted (no new ScanKind); printRegressAttribution prints an INTRODUCED-first block + counts; fixture tests cover absent/in-bounds/beyond-EOF. Oracle RevOvt APPROVE (should-fix on output ordering + nits applied: INTRODUCED-first sort, dropped the dead --since flag, fixed test comment/help). Part (b) the DAEMON 'new since last green' digest is a separate follow-up bead (filed above) \u2014 internal/daemon was intentionally untouched.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text: Scout 2026-06-29: plumbing mostly exists (builds on bugbot-sro). DECOMPOSES cleanly into (a) the regress command (~200-300 LOC, self-contained, offline) and (b) the daemon digest (~50-100 LOC, separable). Seams: generalize the --since machinery to --from/--to (cli/scan.go runScanCmd ~188-204; ChangedFiles(ctx,from,to) in ingest/change.go:63 ALREADY supports A..B); add newRegressCmd() mirroring newScanCmd + register in cli/root.go AddCommand; reuse executeScan() with the range-scoped changed-files list; attribution = new ingest.ReadFileAtRef(ctx,ref,path) via 'git show REF:path' + anchorAbsentAtRef(file,line,A) -> INTRODUCED if file missing/line absent at A else PRE-EXISTING (no schema change; label computed at report time); fixture test via the daemon/fake_test.go fixtureRepo pattern (init/write/commit). Part (b): hook emitRegressDigest into daemon/postcycle.go after runPublisher; 'last green' via scan_runs table query; may need a stored marker. RECOMMENDATION: land (a) first as a tasked implementor (tight spec from this scout map); file (b) as a follow-up. NOT a clean single-task land for an autonomous implementor in one turn \u2014 prefer orchestrator-driven or split.

title: Doc-contract drift lens: verify code honors its documented contracts
description: Bugs linters cannot see by construction: doc comments state contracts ('caller must hold mu', 'returns nil when X', 'safe for concurrent use', 'must be closed by caller') that the implementation or callers silently violate. New built-in lens: finder extracts explicit contract claims from doc comments in the chunk, then uses the code-nav tools (find_references — now cheap) to check callers/implementation against each claim. High-precision target: only EXPLICIT normative statements (must/never/always/safe-for), not vibes. The committed M3 corpus and this repo's own internal/lsp (rich doc contracts) are good first targets.
acceptance_criteria: Lens ships as a built-in; finder prompt extracts only explicit normative claims and verifies via code-nav with the claim quoted in the candidate; fixture eval case with a seeded doc-contract violation (e.g. 'must hold mu' violated) is found and survives refutation; no measurable precision regression on the recorded corpus replay; tree green + lint clean.

comments.author: dpoage
comments.text: BLOCKER for an offline land (scout 2026-06-29): adding a Go-applicable built-in lens BREAKS the on-disk recorded-corpus replay. recorded cases (internal/eval/testdata/recorded/{clean-code,nil-deref-seeded,resource-leak-seeded}) use empty funnel.Options, so replay runs all Go-applicable BuiltinLenses(); the replay store (internal/eval/replay.go) pops one recorded session per finder agent-run IN ORDER and ERRORS when exhausted. A new lens adds a finder run with no recorded session -> replay fails (not just a precision regression). The recorded transcripts do NOT persist the system prompt (record.go:17-20), so the recorded lens set can't be derived from the data to pin it. Therefore 2q3's AC 'no precision regression on recorded corpus replay' requires a LIVE re-record of the corpus with the new lens active (go test -tags record, real API keys). Seams for the lens itself (all offline): BuiltinLenses() internal/funnel/lens.go:162 (add Lens{Name,Core,Languages}); finderSystemPrompt prompt.go:114 splices Core; find_references already wired (funnel.go:56-118); scripted fixture via OnSystemContains(lensName) in cases.go; TestBuiltinLensNames_Stable (lens_lang_test.go:24) pins lens count/order. RECOMMENDATION: schedule with a live eval session so the corpus can be re-recorded; the code change is small but the corpus re-record gates the AC.

title: Repo-specific lens generation: meta-agent writes custom lenses
description: Generic lenses find generic bugs; generated lenses find THIS repo's bugs. A meta-agent runs once per repo (and on significant change): reads README/CLAUDE.md/doc comments/build files, identifies frameworks, conventions, and invariants ('shells out to podman — audit arg construction'; 'store dedups by fingerprint — check every insert computes it'), and emits custom lens definitions (name, finder prompt, scope hints) persisted in the store and versioned. Generated lenses run alongside built-ins, clearly labeled in findings. Human can list/disable them ('bugbot lens list/disable'). Guardrails: cap count, require the meta-agent to cite the evidence (file:line) each lens is derived from, and never let a generated lens widen scan scope beyond config.
acceptance_criteria: Meta-agent produces N<=cap lenses each citing derivation evidence; persisted + versioned in store; sweep runs them alongside built-ins with provenance labels in report; lens list/disable works; regeneration is idempotent on an unchanged repo; tree green + lint clean.

title: Per-lens precision calibration: auto-tune verification spend
description: Track each lens's historical precision (from eval corpus replays + human verdicts: dismissals vs confirmed/published findings) in the store, and let the funnel auto-tune refuter count per lens: noisy lenses get more adversarial verification, consistently-precise lenses get the minimum. Keep it transparent: scan summary shows effective refuter counts and why. Bounded: never below 1 or above configured max; cold-start (no history) uses the configured default. Depends on dismissal data existing (false-positive memory bead).
acceptance_criteria: Store accumulates per-lens outcome counts; funnel derives refuter count per lens within configured bounds; summary/report show the derived counts with the precision stats behind them; deterministic given fixed history (unit test); cold-start behaves exactly as today; tree green + lint clean.

title: SARIF export for GitHub code scanning and tool interop
description: Findings -> SARIF 2.1.0 so results land in GitHub's Security/code-scanning tab and interoperate with other analysis tooling. 'bugbot export --format sarif' (and a scan flag) mapping: lens -> rule, tier -> level (T1/T2 -> error/warning, T3 -> note), fingerprint -> partialFingerprints, reasoning -> message, file:line -> physicalLocation. Keep it dependency-light (hand-rolled struct marshaling; the schema subset we emit is small).
acceptance_criteria: Exported SARIF validates against the 2.1.0 schema (testdata golden file + a validator in tests if feasible); uploadable via gh code-scanning upload on a fixture run; stable fingerprints across runs; tree green + lint clean.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: LSP conn robustness: async server-request replies; prefer stderr-tail crash errors over bare EOF
description: Two low-severity findings from the bugbot-tmb code review (PR #5), deferred. (1) conn.readLoop answers server->client requests synchronously under wmu (conn.go respondToServer): a misbehaving server that stops reading our stdin while still sending requests blocks the write, stops the read loop draining, and wedges the connection until process exit — callers stay bounded by the 60s query timeout but the read goroutine leaks until process death. Fix: route replies through a buffered channel drained by a dedicated writer goroutine, or make reply writes best-effort/non-blocking. (2) markDead is once-guarded and races between readLoop (bare EOF) and the cmd.Wait goroutine (which carries the stderr tail explaining the crash); EOF often wins so the user-facing error drops the diagnostic stderr. Fix: have the EOF path defer briefly to the process-exit error, or prefer a non-EOF deadErr.
acceptance_criteria: Reply write-back cannot stall the read loop (test: server sends requests but never reads stdin; conn keeps draining and Close stays bounded); crash errors include the stderr tail deterministically (test: crashing fake server with stderr output, assert tail present in the query error); -race clean; tree green + lint clean.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: Daemon-lifetime LSP server manager: keep language-server indexes warm across cycles
description: bugbot-tmb (PR #5) spawns and shuts down language servers per scan/cycle via Funnel.Close(). gopls indexing takes seconds-to-minutes on medium repos, so per-cycle spawn re-pays warm-up every cycle. Lift the lsp.Manager to daemon lifetime: construct once at daemon boot, share across cycles, pre-warm at boot, shut down on daemon exit. Was in the original v2 scoping ('daemon mode is the natural fit: start servers at boot, amortize indexing warm-up across cycles').
acceptance_criteria: Daemon constructs one lsp.Manager at boot and reuses it across cycles (verify gopls PID stable across two cycles in an integration test or log assertion); clean shutdown on daemon exit incl. signal paths; one-shot scan behavior unchanged (per-scan lifecycle); crash-restart semantics still bounded; tree green + lint clean.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.

title: Give repro agents the code-nav tools
description: internal/repro builds its own readOnlyTools(dir) (repro.go:258) and was an intentional scope cut in bugbot-tmb (PR #5): repro agents get read/list/grep but not find_definition/find_references/find_implementations. Add a CodeNav instance (with Close hook) to the repro tool set so reproduction agents can navigate precisely too.
acceptance_criteria: Repro agent tool set includes the three code-nav tools backed by the shared lsp.Manager pattern; CodeNav closed on all repro exit paths; existing repro tests pass; tree green + lint clean.
close_reason: Work merged to main. Dolt was initialized after this work; reconciling. Each verified to map to a feature/fix commit reachable from main HEAD via 'git log HEAD --grep=<bead-id>'.
